Japanese: ‥‥はあ‥‥はあ‥‥
English: *gasp*... *gasp*...

Japanese: ‥‥くそっ！なんでオレがこんな目に‥‥
English: Dammit! ...Why me?

Japanese: ‥‥つかまりたくねえ‥‥こんなことで‥‥
English: I can't get caught... Not like this!

Japanese: 誰か‥‥誰かがやったことにするんだ‥‥！
English: I-I've gotta find someone to pin this on...

Japanese: ‥‥そうだ、あいつだ‥‥
English: Someone like... him!

Japanese: あいつがやったことにすれば‥‥
English: I'll make it look like HE did it!

Japanese: ８月３日　午前９時４７分地方裁判所　被告人第２控え室
English: August 3, 9:47 AM District Court Defendant Lobby No. 2

Japanese: （うう‥‥。キンチョーするなあ‥‥）
English: (Boy am I nervous!)

Japanese: ‥‥なるほどくん！
English: Wright!

Japanese: あ、しょ、所長。
English: Oh, h-hiya, Chief.

Japanese: ふう。なんとか、間にあったわね。
English: Whew, I'm glad I made it on time.

Japanese: どうかしら？　初めての法廷は。こ、こんなにドキドキするの、小学校の学級裁判のとき以来です。‥‥それはそれは。ずいぶん、ごぶさたしてるのね。え、ええ、まあ。あの‥‥所長。今日は、すみません。いそがしいのに‥‥。ううん、かまわないわ。カワイイ部下の初舞台だもの。‥‥それにしても。
English: Well, I have to say Phoenix, I'm impressed!

Japanese: 初めての法廷で殺人事件をあつかうなんて、すごい度胸ね。
English: Not everyone takes on a murder trial right off the bat like this.

Japanese: 感心するわ。あなたにも、あなたの依頼人にも。
English: It says a lot about you... and your client as well.

Japanese: は、はい‥‥。
English: Um... thanks.

Japanese: ‥‥借りがあるんですよ。ヤツには。
English: Actually, it's because I owe him a favor.
A favor?

Japanese: あの、依頼人の方に？
English: You mean, you knew the defendant before this case?

Japanese: ええ。
English: Yes.

Japanese: ‥‥実は、ぼくがこうして弁護士になったのも、
English: Actually, I kind of owe my current job to him.

Japanese: ある意味、ヤツのおかげ、みたいなところがあるんです。
English: He's one of the reasons I became an attorney.

Japanese: ‥‥それ、初耳ね。
English: Well, that's news to me!

Japanese: ‥‥ぼく、あいつのチカラになってやりたいんです！
English: I want to help him out any way I can!

Japanese: 助けてやりたい‥‥
English: I just... really want to help him. I owe him that much.

Japanese: （オシマイだぁ！）
English: (It's over!)

Japanese: （もう、何もかもオシマイなんだぁ！）
English: (My life, everything, it's all over!)

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥...あそこで叫んでるの、あなたの依頼人じゃない‥‥？
English: Isn't that your client screaming over there?

Japanese: そ、そうですね。
English: Yeah... that's him.

Japanese: （‥‥死ぬ！　死ぬぞぉ！）
English: (Death! Despair! Ohhhh!)

Japanese: （オレはもう、死んでやるんだぁぁ！）
English: (I'm gonna do it, I'm gonna die!!!)

Japanese: ‥‥死にたがってるわよ。
English: It sounds like he wants to die...

Japanese: はあ。
English: Um, yeah. *sigh*

Japanese: 成歩堂ぉ！
English: Nick!!!

Japanese: よ、よう、矢張。
English: Hey. Hey there, Larry.

Japanese: 有罪だぁ！オレを有罪にしてくれよぉ！
English: Dude, I'm so guilty!! Tell them I'm guilty!!!

Japanese: 死刑でもなんでもカッくらって、サッパリ死なせてくれぇ！
English: Gimme the death sentence! I ain't afraid to die!

Japanese: ど、どうしたんだよ、矢張！
English: What!? What's wrong, Larry?

Japanese: ダメだよ！やっぱりオレ、ダメなんだ！
English: Oh, it's all over... I... I'm finished. Finished!

Japanese: あの女がいない人生なんて‥‥死んだ方がマシだぁ！
English: I can't live in a world without her! I can't!

Japanese: 誰が‥‥いったい誰が、カノジョを‥‥！
English: Who... who took her away from me, Nick? Who did this!?

Japanese: 教えてくれよぉぉぉ！成歩堂ぉぉぉ‥‥！
English: Aww, Nick, ya gotta tell me! Who took my baby away!?

Japanese: （“カノジョ”を殺害した犯人、か‥‥）
English: (Hmm... The person responsible for your girlfriend's death?)

Japanese: （新聞に書いてあるのは、お前の名前なんだよな‥‥）
English: (The newspapers say it was you...)

Japanese: ぼくの名前は成歩堂　龍一（なるほどうりゅういち）。
English: My name is Phoenix Wright.

Japanese: ３ヶ月前に弁護士になったばかり。今日は初めての法廷だ。さて。
English: Here's the story:

Japanese: 今回の事件は、いたってシンプル。
English: My first case is a fairly simple one.

Japanese: マンションの一室で、若い女性が殺害された。
English: A young woman was killed in her apartment.

Japanese: 逮捕されたのは、不運にも彼女とつきあっていた男。
English: The guy they arrested was the unlucky sap dating her:

Japanese: 矢張　政志（やはりまさし）。‥‥小学校からの大親友だ。
English: Larry Butz... my best friend since grade school.

Japanese: “事件のカゲに、ヤッパリ矢張”と言われつづけて２３年。
English: Our school had a saying: "When something smells, it's usually the Butz."

Japanese: なぜか、いつでもキツいトラブルに巻き込まれている。
English: In the 23 years I've known him, it's usually been true.

Japanese: ‥‥ただ１つ言えることは、
English: He has a knack for getting himself in trouble.

Japanese: 悪いのは、こいつじゃない。‥‥ただひたすら、運が悪いだけ。
English: One thing I can say though: it's usually not his fault. He just has terrible luck.

Japanese: ‥‥いいヤツだってことは、誰よりも、このぼくが知っている。
English: But I know better than anyone, that he's a good guy at heart.

Japanese: そして何より、ぼくはこいつに大きな借りがある。
English: That and I owe him one. Which is why I took the case... to clear his name.

Japanese: 助けてやるんだ‥‥ゼッタイに！
English: And that's just what I'm going to do!

Japanese: ８月３日　午前１０時地方裁判所　第２法廷
English: August 3, 10:00 AM District Court Courtroom No. 2

Japanese: これより、矢張　政志の法廷を開廷します。
English: The court is now in session for the trial of Mr. Larry Butz.

Japanese: 検察側、準備完了しております。
English: The prosecution is ready, Your Honor.

Japanese: べ、弁護側も準備完了しています。
English: The, um, defense is ready, Your Honor.
Ahem.

Japanese: 成歩堂くん。
English: Mr. Wright?

Japanese: きみはたしか、今日が初めての法廷だったかな？
English: This is your first trial, is it not?

Japanese: は、はい‥‥。‥‥キンチョーしてます‥‥。
English: Y-Yes, Your Honor. I'm, um, a little nervous.

Japanese: 依頼人が有罪になるか、無罪になるかは、弁護士にかかっています。
English: Your conduct during this trial will decide the fate of your client.

Japanese: 弁護士であるきみが、そんなにキンチョーしていては困りますね。
English: Murder is a serious charge. For your client's sake, I hope you can control your nerves.

Japanese: ‥‥すみません。
English: Thank... thank you, Your Honor.

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥。...‥‥そうですね。裁判を進める前に、
English: Mr. Wright, given the circumstances...

Japanese: 本当に“準備完了”しているか、たしかめさせてもらいましょうか。
English: I think we should have a test to ascertain your readiness.

Japanese: え、ええ‥‥。
English: Yes, Your Honor.

Japanese: （うわあ、アタマの中がマッシロになってきたぞ‥‥）
English: (Gulp... Hands shaking... Eyesight... fading...)

Japanese: カンタンな質問をするから、答えてください。
English: The test will consist of a few simple questions. Answer them clearly and concisely.

Japanese: まず、この事件の被告人の名前を。‥‥言ってみなさい。
English: Please state the name of the defendant in this case.

Japanese: えーと、被告人といえば、ぼくのこと、ですよね？
English: Um... the defendant... is me, right?

Japanese: ちょ、ちょ、ちょっと！何を言ってるの、なるほどくん！
English: Wright! Have you completely lost your mind? Focus!

Japanese: ヒコクニンっていうのは、裁判にかけられている人のことよ！
English: The defendant is the person on trial!

Japanese: あなたは、弁護人！
English: You're his lawyer!

Japanese: え、あ、あれ？そうでしたよね。ははは‥‥。
English: Um, er, eh? Oh yeah, right! Eh heh heh.

Japanese: “ははは”じゃないわよ。
English: This is no laughing matter!

Japanese: なんで弁護士に、こんなコト言わなくちゃならないの‥‥？
English: You did pass the bar, didn't you?

Japanese: よく聞こえませんでしたね。もう１回、尋ねましょう。
English: Sorry, I couldn't hear your answer. I'll ask once more:

Japanese: ヒコクニン‥‥ですか。ええと、綾里　千尋さん‥‥。
English: The, um, defendant? That's... er... Mia Fey?

Japanese: な、なるほどくん。‥‥私、帰ります。
English: Wrong, Wright. Look, I have to leave.

Japanese: そういえば今日、宅急便が届くから‥‥。
English: I have to go home. I'm... I'm expecting a delivery.

Japanese: ま‥‥まあまあ、ゆっくりしていってくださいよ！
English: Aw, c'mon Chief. There's no need to be going so soon, is there?

Japanese: もうっ！
English: Wright!

Japanese: ヒコクニンっていうのは、裁判にかけられている人のことでしょ！
English: Listen: the defendant is the one on trial--your client!

Japanese: 初歩、っていうより、これはもう、常識のレベルよ‥‥。
English: I mean, that's about as basic as you can get!

Japanese: （さすがにマズかったな。とにかく、落ち着かないと‥‥）
English: (I put my foot in it this time! I've got to relax!)

Japanese: 答えがよく聞こえませんでしたね。もう１回、尋ねましょう。
English: Sorry, I couldn't hear your answer. I'll ask once more:

Japanese: ヒコクニン‥‥それは、まあ、矢張くんのことですよね。
English: The defendant? Well, that's Larry Butz, Your Honor.

Japanese: そのとおり。
English: Correct.

Japanese: そんな感じで、落ち着いて答えればよろしい。
English: Just keep your wits about you and you'll do fine.

Japanese: じゃあ、次の質問です。
English: Next question:

Japanese: 今日の裁判は殺人事件ですが、被害者の名前を教えてください。
English: This is a murder trial. Tell me, what's the victim's name?

Japanese: （‥‥ふう。それならわかるぞ。あれだけ調書を読んだからな）
English: (Whew, I know this one! Glad I read the case report cover to cover so many times.)

Japanese: （‥‥って、あれ？）
English: (It's... wait... Uh-oh!)

Japanese: （ど‥‥ど忘れしちまったぞ！名前、なんだっけ！）
English: (No... no way! I forgot! I'm drawing a total blank here!)

Japanese: な、なるほどくん！あ、あ、あ、あなたって人は‥‥
English: Phoenix! Are you absolutely SURE you're up to this?

Japanese: 被害者の名前も知らないの？
English: You don't even know the victim's name!?

Japanese: そ、そんなワケ、ないじゃないですか。
English: Oh, the victim! O-Of course I know the victim's name!

Japanese: ちょっと、忘れちゃっただけです。
English: I, um, just forgot. ... Temporarily.

Japanese: ‥‥な、なんか、アタマがいたくなってきたわ‥‥。
English: I think I feel a migraine coming on.

Japanese: 事件のことは、《法廷記録》を見れば、わかります！
English: Look, the victim's name is listed in the Court Record.

Japanese: Ｒボタンで、好きなときに見ることができるから、
English: Just touch the Court Record button to check it at anytime, okay?

Japanese: いつもチェックしておいてね。‥‥おねがいだから。
English: Remember to check it often. Do it for me, please. I'm begging you.

Japanese: 答えてもらいましょう。この事件の被害者の名前は？
English: Let's hear your answer. Who is the victim in this case?

Japanese: ええと、綾里　千尋さん‥‥。
English: Um... Mia Fey?

Japanese: なな、なんで私が被害者なのっ！
English: W-W-What!? How can I be the victim!?

Japanese: す、すみません。とりあえず、思いついた名前をですね‥‥。
English: Oh! Right! Sorry! I, er, it was the first name that popped into my head, and--

Japanese: 名前を忘れたのなら、Ｒボタンで法廷記録をチェックして。
English: The Court Record button! Remember to use it when you are in a pinch.

Japanese: ‥‥では、もう一度、お尋ねしましょう。
English: Let me ask that one again:

Japanese: たしか、日高　友加さん、だったような‥‥。
English: Oh, um, wasn't it Ms. Block? Ms. Cinder Block?

Japanese: そんなアヤフヤな発言は、ここでは通用しませんよ。
English: The person in question was a victim of murder, not ill- conceived naming, Mr. Wright.

Japanese: なるほどくん。
English: Wright?

Japanese: 自信がないときは、Ｒボタンで法廷記録をチェックして。
English: If you forget something, just touch the Court Record button to help you remember.

Japanese: 間違った発言は、すぐに命とりになるわよ。
English: A mistake in court could cost you the case.

Japanese: もう一度、聞きますよ。
English: I'll ask you again:

Japanese: えーと。被害者の名前は、高日　美佳さんです。
English: Um... the victim's name is Cindy Stone.

Japanese: ‥‥よろしい。
English: Correct.

Japanese: では、彼女はどうやって殺害されたか？
English: Now, tell me, what was the cause of death?

Japanese: 被害者の死因は‥‥？
English: She died because she was...?

Japanese: ええと、たしか、毒でも飲まされたんでしたっけ？
English: Oh, right! Wasn't she, um, poisoned by er... poison?

Japanese: 私に聞いてどうするんですか！
English: You're asking me!?

Japanese: どうでしたっけ？　所長？
English: Um... Chief! Help me out!

Japanese: 法廷記録をチェックなさい。‥‥Ｒボタン。
English: Check the court record. The Court Record button... remember?

Japanese: （‥‥みんな、冷たいなあ‥‥）
English: (Geez. Give a guy a break!)

Japanese: もう一度、聞きましょう。
English: Let me ask again.

Japanese: たしか、首を絞められたんじゃなかったかなあ‥‥？
English: Right... she was strangled, wasn't she?

Japanese: それ、独り言よね？
English: Please tell me that was you talking to yourself.

Japanese: 自分の首でも絞めるんですね。
English: If you wish to hang yourself, Mr. Wright, you're welcome to, but not inside my courtroom.

Japanese: ‥‥しかたない。もう一度、聞きましょう。
English: I suppose there's nothing to do but give you another try:

Japanese: 鈍器で１発、ガツンと殴られて‥‥。
English: She was struck once, by a blunt object.

Japanese: そのとおり。
English: Correct.

Japanese: じゃあ、質問はこれぐらいにして、そろそろ審理を始めましょう。
English: You've answered all my questions. I see no reason why we shouldn't proceed.

Japanese: きみも、だいぶ落ち着いてきたようですからね。
English: You seem much more relaxed, Mr. Wright. Good for you.

Japanese: ‥‥はい。（そうでもないけど）
English: Thank you, Your Honor. (Because I don't FEEL relaxed, that's for sure.)

Japanese: ‥‥さて。
English: Well, then...

Japanese: ちょっといいですか？亜内検事。
English: First, a question for the prosecution. Mr. Payne?

Japanese: なんでしょうか、裁判長。
English: Yes, Your Honor?

Japanese: 今、成歩堂くんが言ったとおり、被害者は鈍器で殴られています。
English: As Mr. Wright just told us, the victim was struck with a blunt object.

Japanese: その“鈍器”ですが、具体的には、どういう‥‥？
English: Would you explain to the court just what that "object" was?

Japanese: 凶器は、この《考える人》の置物です。
English: The murder weapon was this statue of "The Thinker."

Japanese: 死体のそばに転がっておりました。
English: It was found lying on the floor, next to the victim.

Japanese: ‥‥ふむう。証拠品として受理しましょう。
English: I see... the court accepts it into evidence.

Japanese: 証拠品《置物》のデータを法廷記録にファイルした。
English: Statue added to the Court Record.

Japanese: なるほどくん。
English: Wright...

Japanese: 裁判が進むと、こんなふうに《証拠品》が提出されていくの。
English: Be sure to pay attention to any evidence added during the trial.

Japanese: 《証拠品》のデータは、これからあなたの武器になるから、
English: That evidence is the only ammunition you have in court.

Japanese: Ｒボタンで、法廷記録をチェックしておいた方がいいわよ。
English: Touch the Court Record button to check the Court Record frequently.

Japanese: では、亜内検事。証人を呼んでください。
English: Mr. Payne, the prosecution may call its first witness.

Japanese: まず、被告人?矢張くんの話を聞きたいと思います。
English: The prosecution calls the defendant, Mr. Butz, to the stand.

Japanese: あの、所長。ぼくは、どうすれば‥‥？
English: Um, Chief, what do I do now?

Japanese: 今は、依頼人を助けるための《情報》を聞き逃さないこと。
English: Pay attention. You don't want to miss any information that might help your client's case.

Japanese: 反撃のチャンスは、あとでかならず、やってくるわ。
English: You'll get your chance to respond to the prosecution later, so be ready!

Japanese: 彼が、よけいなことを言わないよう、祈りましょう。
English: Let's just hope he doesn't say anything... unfortunate.

Japanese: （‥‥心配だ。矢張のヤツ、すぐにコーフンするからな‥‥）
English: (Uh oh, Larry gets excited easily... this could be bad.)

Japanese: さて。
English: Ahem.

Japanese: 矢張くん。キミは最近、被害者にフラれたそうですね？
English: Mr. Butz. Is it not true that the victim had recently dumped you?

Japanese: なんだとコラ！
English: Hey, watch it buddy!

Japanese: 今世紀最高のカップルに向かってなんてことを！
English: We were great together! We were Romeo and Juliet, Cleopatra and Mark Anthony!

Japanese: （い‥‥いきなりかよ‥‥）
English: (Um... didn't they all die?)

Japanese: ただ最近、電話に出なかったり、会おうとしても断られただけだ！
English: I wasn't dumped! She just wasn't taking my phone calls. Or seeing me... Ever.

Japanese: ナメんなぁ！
English: WHAT'S IT TO YOU, ANYWAY!?

Japanese: 世間では、そういう状況を“フラれた”と呼んでいます。
English: Mr. Butz, what you describe is generally what we mean by "dumped."

Japanese: 実際、彼女はきみをほったらかして遊びまわっていた。
English: In fact, she had completely abandoned you... and was seeing other men!

Japanese: 殺害される前の日も、海外旅行から帰ったばかりだったんだよ。
English: She had just returned from overseas with one of them the day before the murder!

Japanese: なんだとぉ！
English: Whaddya mean, "one of them"!?

Japanese: そんなのウソだぁ！オレァ、信じねえぞぉ！
English: Lies! All of it, lies! I don't believe a word of it!

Japanese: 裁判長。被害者のパスポートです。
English: Your Honor, the victim's passport.

Japanese: 亡くなる前日まで、彼女はニューヨークにいたようですね。
English: According to this, she was in Paris until the day before she died.

Japanese: 証拠品《パスポート》のデータを法廷記録にファイルした。
English: Passport added to the Court Record.

Japanese: ふむう。たしかに帰国の日付は、死亡の前日、だ‥‥。
English: Hmm... Indeed, she appears to have returned the day before the murder.
Dude... no way...

Japanese: 彼女はモデルでしたが、収入は大したことはありません。
English: The victim was a model, but did not have a large income.

Japanese: どうやら彼女には、“パパ”がたくさんいたようです。
English: It appears that she had several "Sugar Daddies."

Japanese: ‥‥パパ？
English: Daddies? Sugar?

Japanese: お小づかいをくれるおじさんたちのことです。
English: Yes. Older men, who gave her money and gifts.

Japanese: 彼女は、彼らから金を“援助”してもらって、遊んでいたワケだ。
English: She took their money and used it to support her lifestyle.

Japanese: なんだって‥‥
English: Duuude!

Japanese: ‥‥被害者の高日　美佳はそういう人間だった。
English: We can clearly see what kind of woman this Ms. Stone was.

Japanese: きみは彼女についてどう思うかな？　矢張くん。
English: Tell me, Mr. Butz, what do you think of her now?

Japanese: ‥‥なるほどくん。
English: Wright...

Japanese: この質問には、答えさせない方がいいかもしれないわね‥‥。
English: I don't think you want him to answer that question.

Japanese: （‥‥矢張のことだ。トンでもないことを口走るかも‥‥）
English: (Yeah... Larry has a way of running his mouth in all the wrong directions.)

Japanese: （‥‥どうしよう？）
English: (Should I...?)

Japanese: 矢張くんは、被害者が他の男性とつきあっていたのは知らなかった！
English: My client had no idea the victim was seeing other men!

Japanese: したがって、今の質問は、事件とは関係ありません！
English: That question is irrelevant to this case!

Japanese: ‥‥むぐぐ‥‥
English: Oof! *wince*

Japanese: ナニ言ってんだ成歩堂ぉ！関係ないこと、あるかぁ！
English: Dude! Nick! Whaddya mean, "irrelevant"!?

Japanese: ‥‥ナメやがって、あの女！
English: That cheatin' she-dog!

Japanese: （ま、とりあえず、ようすを見ておくか‥‥）
English: (Might be better not to get involved in this one...)

Japanese: ‥‥どうかな？　矢張くん。
English: Well, Mr. Butz?

Japanese: く、くそっ！ナメやがって、あの女！
English: Dude, no way! That cheatin' she-dog!

Japanese: 死んでやる！もう、死んでやるぞお！
English: I'm gonna die. I'm just gonna drop dead!

Japanese: 天国で、あのオンナを問いつめてやるんだあ！
English: Yeah, and when I meet her in the afterlife... I'm going to get to the bottom of this!

Japanese: ‥‥では、審理をつづけましょうか。
English: Let's continue with the trial, shall we?

Japanese: とにかく、被告人の動機はおわかりいただけたと思います。
English: I believe the accused's motive is clear to everyone.

Japanese: ‥‥とてもよく。
English: Yes, quite.
(Oh boy. This is so not looking good.)

Japanese: じゃあ、次の質問に移りましょう。
English: Next question!

Japanese: 事件があった日、きみは彼女の部屋に行かなかったかな？
English: You went to the victim's apartment on the day of the murder, did you not?
Gulp!

Japanese: どうなのかな？
English: Well, did you, or did you not?

Japanese: ‥‥え、な、何がどうなんだよ！
English: Heh? Heh heh. Well, maybe I did, and maybe I didn't!

Japanese: （あのようすじゃ、あいつ、部屋に行ってるな‥‥）
English: (Uh oh. He went.)

Japanese: （どうしよう‥‥）
English: (What do I do?)

Japanese: （よし。合図を送るぞ‥‥。）
English: (I know! I'll send him a signal...)

Japanese: （《正直に答えろ》）
English: (TELL THE TRUTH)

Japanese: ああ！　たしかにその、行ったよ。行ったさ！
English: Er... Yeah! Yeah! I was there! I went!

Japanese: 静粛に！
English: Order!

Japanese: ‥‥それで？　矢張くん！
English: Well, Mr. Butz?

Japanese: う‥‥。そ、そんな目で見るなよな！
English: Dude, chill!

Japanese: アイツ、留守でさ‥‥。会えなかったワケよ、結局。
English: She wasn't home, man... So, like, I didn't see her.

Japanese: 裁判長。被告人はウソをついております。
English: Your Honor, the defendant is lying.

Japanese: ‥‥ウソ？
English: Lying?

Japanese: 我々には、今のウソを立証する証人がいます。
English: The prosecution would like to call a witness who can prove Mr. Butz is lying.

Japanese: （合図を送るぞ‥‥）
English: (I'll send him a signal...)

Japanese: （《うまくにげるんだ》）
English: (LIE LIKE A DOG)

Japanese: ‥‥いや。その、覚えがねえなあ‥‥。
English: Um, well, see, it's like this: I don't remember.

Japanese: “覚えがない”‥‥そうですか。
English: You "don't remember"?

Japanese: じゃ、しかたない‥‥。思い出させてあげましょう。
English: Well then, we'll just have to remind you!

Japanese: （なんか、イヤな予感‥‥）
English: (I got a bad feeling about this...)

Japanese: こちらには、決定的な証人がいるんですよ。
English: We have a witness that can prove he DID go to the victim's apartment that day!

Japanese: ‥‥それは話が早い。どんな証人ですか？
English: Well, that simplifies matters. Who is your witness?

Japanese: 死体の発見者です。
English: The man who found the victim's body.

Japanese: 彼は、死体を発見する直前に、
English: Just before making the gruesome discovery...

Japanese: 殺人現場から逃げていく被告人?矢張くんを目撃しているのです！
English: He saw the defendant fleeing the scene of the crime!

Japanese: 静粛に！　静粛に！
English: Order! Order in the court!

Japanese: ‥‥亜内検事。その証人を呼んでください！
English: Mr. Payne, the prosecution may call its witness.

Japanese: ははっ。
English: Yes, Your Honor.

Japanese: （と、とんでもないことになってきたぞ‥‥）
English: (This is bad...)

Japanese: 事件当日、現場のマンションで新聞の勧誘をしていた、
English: On the day of the murder, my witness was selling newspapers at the victim's building.

Japanese: 山野　星雄（やまのほしお）さんを入廷させてください！
English: Please bring Mr. Frank Sahwit to the stand!

Japanese: 山野さん。新聞勧誘員をしておられる‥‥？
English: Mr. Sahwit, you sell newspaper subscriptions, is this correct?

Japanese: ‥‥は。は。さようでございます、はい。
English: Oh, oh yes! Newspapers, yes!

Japanese: では、さっそく《証言》をしていただきましょう。
English: Mr. Sahwit, you may proceed with your testimony.

Japanese: あなたが事件当日、見たことを話してください。
English: Please tell the court what you saw on the day of the murder.

Japanese: ～事件の当日、目撃したこと～
English: -- Witness's Account --

Japanese: 勧誘しておりましたら、とある部屋から、男が出てきたんです。
English: I was going door-to-door, selling subscriptions when I saw a man fleeing an apartment.

Japanese: 男はあわてていて、ドアを半開きにしたまま、行ってしまいました。
English: I thought he must be in a hurry because he left the door half-open behind him.

Japanese: おかしいと思いまして、私、部屋をのぞいてみたんでございます。
English: Thinking it strange, I looked inside the apartment.

Japanese: すると、なんと、女の人が死んでいるじゃございませんか！
English: Then I saw her lying there... A woman... not moving... dead!

Japanese: 私、腰が抜けてしまいまして。怖くて、部屋に入れませんでした。
English: I quailed in fright and found myself unable to go inside.

Japanese: 私、すぐに警察を呼ぼうと思ったんでございます。
English: I thought to call the police immediately!

Japanese: でも、彼女の部屋の電話は通じませんでしたので、
English: However, the phone in her apartment wasn't working.

Japanese: それで、近くの公衆電話から通報いたしました。
English: I went to a nearby park and found a public phone.

Japanese: 時間はハッキリ覚えております。お昼すぎの、２時でした。
English: I remember the time exactly: It was 1:00 PM.

Japanese: 逃げた男は、間違いなく、そこの被告人のカタでございます。
English: The man who ran was, without a doubt, the defendant sitting right over there.

Japanese: ふむう‥‥。
English: Hmm...

Japanese: （矢張‥‥！　なんで正直に話してくれなかったんだ！）
English: (Larry! Why didn't you tell the truth?)

Japanese: （こんなにハッキリ見られてちゃ、弁護なんか、しようがない！）
English: (I can't defend you against a testimony like that!)

Japanese: ところで、現場にあった電話はなぜ通じなかったのかな？
English: Incidentally, why wasn't the phone in the victim's apartment working?

Japanese: はい。事件があった時間、マンションは停電中だったんです。
English: Your Honor, at the time of the murder, there was a blackout in the building.

Japanese: しかし、停電中でも、たしか電話は使えるはずでは‥‥？
English: Aren't phones supposed to work during a blackout?

Japanese: はい、そのとおりです。
English: Yes, Your Honor...

Japanese: しかし、機種によっては、子機の使用はできないのです。
English: However, some cordless phones do not function normally.

Japanese: 現場で山野さんが手にしたのは、そういうタイプの子機でした。
English: The phone that Mr. Sahwit used was one of those.

Japanese: 裁判長。
English: Your Honor...

Japanese: 念のため、停電の記録を提出します。
English: I have a record of the blackout, for your perusal.

Japanese: 証拠品《停電記録》のデータを法廷記録にファイルした。
English: Blackout Record added to the Court Record.

Japanese: では、弁護人。
English: Now, Mr. Wright...

Japanese: ‥‥は、はい！
English: Yes! Er... yes, Your Honor?

Japanese: 《尋問》をおねがいします。
English: You may begin your cross-examination.

Japanese: じ、尋問、ですか‥‥？
English: C-Cross-examination, Your Honor?

Japanese: ‥‥さあ、なるほどくん。いよいよ本番よ。
English: Alright, Wright, this is it. The real deal!

Japanese: ‥‥あの、尋問って、どうすれば‥‥？
English: Uh... what exactly am I supposed to do?

Japanese: 今の証言のウソを暴くのよ、もちろん。
English: Why, you expose the lies in the testimony the witness just gave!

Japanese: えっ！　‥‥ウソ、ですか‥‥？
English: Lies! What?! He was lying!?

Japanese: あなたの依頼人が無実なら‥‥
English: Your client is innocent, right?

Japanese: あんな目撃証言なんか、ウソに決まってるでしょう！
English: Then that witness must have lied in his testimony!

Japanese: ‥‥それとも、あなたは依頼人の無実を信じてないの？
English: Or is your client really... guilty?

Japanese: ‥‥！　でも‥‥どうやって？
English: !!! How do I prove he's not?

Japanese: カギをにぎっているのは、《証拠品》よ！
English: You hold the key! It's in the evidence!

Japanese: あの証人の証言と証拠品のデータとの間には、
English: Compare the witness's testimony to the evidence at hand.

Japanese: 何か決定的な食い違い、すなわち《ムジュン》があるはず。
English: There's bound to be a contradiction in there!

Japanese: まず、法廷記録と証言で、ムジュンしている部分を探すの。
English: First, find contradictions between the Court Record and the witness's testimony.

Japanese: そして、ムジュンしている証拠品を見つけたら、
English: Then, once you've found the contradicting evidence...

Japanese: それを、あの証人につきつけてやりなさい。
English: present it and rub it in the witness's face!

Japanese: ‥‥は、はあ。
English: Um... okay.

Japanese: Ｒボタンで法廷記録を見ながら、証言と食い違う部分を探して！
English: Touch the Court Record button and point out contradictions in the testimony!

Japanese: ～事件の当日、目撃したこと～
English: -- Witness's Account --

Japanese: 勧誘しておりましたら、とある部屋から、男が出てきたんです。
English: I was going door-to-door, selling subscriptions when I saw a man fleeing an apartment.

Japanese: 男はあわてていて、ドアを半開きにしたまま、行ってしまいました。
English: I thought he must be in a hurry because he left the door half-open behind him.

Japanese: おかしいと思いまして、私、部屋をのぞいてみたんでございます。
English: Thinking it strange, I looked inside the apartment.

Japanese: すると、なんと、女の人が死んでいるじゃございませんか！
English: Then I saw her lying there... A woman... not moving... dead!

Japanese: 私、腰が抜けてしまいまして。怖くて、部屋に入れませんでした。
English: I quailed in fright and found myself unable to go inside.

Japanese: 私、すぐに警察を呼ぼうと思ったんでございます。
English: I thought to call the police immediately!

Japanese: でも、彼女の部屋の電話は通じませんでしたので、
English: However, the phone in her apartment wasn't working.

Japanese: それで、近くの公衆電話から通報いたしました。
English: I went to a nearby park and found a public phone.

Japanese: 時間はハッキリ覚えてます。お昼すぎの、２時でした。
English: I remember the time exactly: It was 1:00 PM.

Japanese: 逃げた男は、間違いなく、そこの被告人のカタでございます。
English: The man who ran was, without a doubt, the defendant sitting right over there.

Japanese: ‥‥証言は、ここまで。
English: That's all of it.

Japanese: この中のどこかに、決定的なムジュンが含まれているはずよ。
English: There must be a contradiction in there somewhere.

Japanese: Ｒボタンで法廷記録を開いて、それを見ながら証言を聞きなさい。
English: Examine the Court Record button if something strikes you as being suspicious.

Japanese: 証拠品とのムジュンを見つけて、つきつけてやりましょう！
English: Then, find the evidence that contradicts his testimony, and present it to him!

Japanese: 部屋から男が出てくるなんてめずらしいことでもないのに、
English: Isn't a man leaving an apartment a common sight?

Japanese: どうしてその男が気になったんですか？
English: I find it odd you would take notice of him...

Japanese: はあ‥‥。
English: Er... heh.

Japanese: なんか、妙なヒトだったんでございますよ。
English: I don't know. He just seemed strange to me, that's all.

Japanese: 怒っているような、それでいておびえているような‥‥。
English: Like he was mad, and yet frightened at the same time.

Japanese: まるで、殺人現場から逃げ出すハンニンみたいな感じで‥‥。
English: Just like... a criminal fleeing the scene of a crime!

Japanese: 証人！　よけいなことは言わないでください！
English: The defense requests that the witness refrain from conjecture!

Japanese: ま。とにかく、アヤシイ人物だった、と。
English: Of course. What the witness means is that the man he saw looked suspicious.

Japanese: ‥‥それで、どうなりましたかな？
English: So, what happened next?

Japanese: 半開き‥‥ですか。
English: Half-open... you say?

Japanese: ええもう、半開きでございます。
English: Yes, yes, the door was open halfway. Yes.

Japanese: しばらく見ておりましても、誰も閉めるようすがありません。
English: I watched for a moment, but no one came to close the door.

Japanese: なんか不用心だな、と、心配になりまして‥‥。
English: "That's odd, in a big city like this," I thought...

Japanese: なるほど。それで、どうしましたか？
English: I see. And what happened next?

Japanese: なぜ、そんなことを？
English: What gave you the idea to do that?

Japanese: だって、なにぶん半開きでございましたから。
English: Well, the door was half-open, you see.

Japanese: のぞきたくなるのが人情でございましょ？
English: Isn't it only human to want to... peek?

Japanese: 山があれば、人は登ります。それと同じことでございますよ。
English: We climb mountains because they are there! It's the same thing.

Japanese: ああ、そりゃそうです。誰だってのぞくでしょうね。
English: Truer words have never been spoken! Anyone would look inside!

Japanese: （なんかムリヤリ押し切られたような気がするけど‥‥）
English: (Hmm... why did Payne cut him off so quickly?)

Japanese: とにかく、部屋をのぞいた、と。それで、どうなりましたか？
English: So, you looked into the apartment. What happened then?

Japanese: 間違いなく、死んでいたんですか？
English: Are you sure she was dead?

Japanese: さ、さあ。それはわかりませんけど‥‥。
English: W-Well, no, I guess I wasn't.

Japanese: でも、ピクリとも動かないし、血もどくどく流れてましたから。
English: But, she wasn't moving at all, and there was blood everywhere.

Japanese: （なるほど‥‥致命傷だったワケか‥‥）
English: (I guess that would look fatal to anyone...)

Japanese: わかりました。それで、どうしましたか？
English: Very well, what happened next?

Japanese: では、部屋のものには、いっさい手を触れていない‥‥？
English: So, you didn't touch ANYTHING in the apartment?

Japanese: そうですね、ハイ。
English: Um, yes. I mean no! Nothing.

Japanese: ‥‥わかりました。それで、どうしましたか？
English: Okay. What happened next?

Japanese: “思った”ということは、行動には移さなかったわけですか！
English: You "thought" to call the police? Does that mean you didn't actually call them!?

Japanese: まあまあ。落ち着いて証言を聞いてください。
English: Please, please... Listen to the rest of the testimony.

Japanese: あなたは電話をかけようと思った。それで、どうなりましたか？
English: You thought to call the police... What happened next?

Japanese: 現場にあった電話が、通じなかった‥‥？
English: The phone in her apartment wasn't working?

Japanese: ‥‥はあ。さようでございますね、ハイ。
English: Yes. I mean, no, no it wasn't. Right.

Japanese: でも、あなたは部屋には入らなかった、と‥‥？
English: But you said you didn't go into the apartment... or did you!?

Japanese: ああ、そのことでございますか。
English: Oh, oh, that? I can explain that!

Japanese: 玄関のタナの上に、電話の子機がありまして。
English: There was a cordless phone on a shelf in the entranceway.

Japanese: それを使ってみたんでございますが‥‥
English: I reached inside and tried using that to call...

Japanese: その子機が不通だった、というわけですな。
English: And that phone wasn't working, correct?

Japanese: ‥‥それで、どうしましたか？
English: What happened next?

Japanese: なぜ、公衆電話を‥‥？
English: Why use a public phone?

Japanese: はあ。私、携帯電話を持っておりませんし‥‥。
English: Well, you see, I don't have a cell phone.

Japanese: それに、時間帯のせいか、付近の部屋の方々も、皆さん留守で‥‥。
English: And, being the middle of the afternoon, there was no answer at the nearby apartments.

Japanese: ははあ。‥‥ところで、通報した時間は‥‥？
English: Ah, right... what time did you call again?

Japanese: 午後２時！間違いありませんか？
English: 1:00 PM! Are you certain?

Japanese: はい。さようでございます。
English: Yes. Absolutely.

Japanese: （‥‥うーん、妙に自信がありそうだな‥‥）
English: (Hmm... He seems really confident.)

Japanese: 午後２時‥‥？
なるほどくん。なんかおかしくないかしら？
English: Wright. Doesn't that seem strange to you?

Japanese: この証言とムジュンする証拠品をつきつけてみましょう！
English: Present some evidence to contradict him!

Japanese: ゼッタイ、間違いないですか！
English: Are you absolutely, 100% positive?

Japanese: ございませんとも。ええもう、それは絶対に。
English: Yes, it was him. No mistake about it.

Japanese: 間違いありませんね？
English: The witness says he's certain!

Japanese: 死体を見つけたのが、２時。間違いありませんか。
English: You found the body at 1:00 PM. You're sure?

Japanese: ええ。２時でしたね。たしかに。
English: Yes. It was 1:00 PM, for certain.

Japanese: しかし、それはおかしいんですよ。
English: Frankly, I find that hard to believe!

Japanese: この解剖記録のデータと、あきらかにムジュンしています。
English: Your statement directly contradicts the autopsy report.

Japanese: 被害者が死んだのは、午後４時より後なんです。
English: The autopsy notes the time of death at sometime after 4PM.

Japanese: ２時に死体を見つけられるはずは、ゼッタイにありません！
English: There was nobody to... er... no "body" to find at 1:00 PM!

Japanese: どうして、２時間もズレがあるんでしょうか‥‥？
English: How do you explain this three-hour gap?
Oh, that! Oh, er...

Japanese: それは、ささいなコトです。単なる記憶ちがいでして‥‥
English: This is trivial! The witness merely forgot the time!

Japanese: ‥‥私には、そうは思えません。
English: After his testimony, I find that hard to believe.

Japanese: 山野さん。
English: Mr. Sahwit...

Japanese: どうして、死体を見つけた時間を２時だと‥‥？
English: Why were you so certain that you found the body at 1:00 PM?

Japanese: ‥‥ええと‥‥その、ど、どうしてでしょうね‥‥。
English: I... er... well, I... Gee, that's a really good question!

Japanese: みごとなツッコミよ！なるほどくん！
English: Great job, Wright! Way to put him on the spot!

Japanese: そうやって、ムジュンを指摘していけばいいの。
English: That's all you have to do: point out contradictions!

Japanese: ウソは、かならず次のウソを生み出すはず。
English: Lies always beget more lies!

Japanese: そのウソをまた見抜いて、あいつを追い詰めましょう！
English: See through one, and their whole story falls apart!

Japanese: ‥‥あっ！そうそう、思い出しました！
English: Wait! I remember now!

Japanese: ‥‥では、もう一度《証言》してもらいましょうかな？
English: Would you care to give your testimony again?

Japanese: ～死体を発見した時間について～
English: -- The Time of Discovery --

Japanese: 死体を見つけたとき、時間が聞こえてきたんでございます。
English: You see, when I found the body, I heard the time.

Japanese: あの音、時報みたいな感じで‥‥。たぶん、テレビだったと思います。
English: There was a voice saying the time... It was probably coming from the television.

Japanese: あ、でも、時報にしては、２時間もズレていたんですよね？
English: Oh, but it was three hours off, wasn't it?

Japanese: たぶん被害者の方は、ビデオを見ていたのではないでしょうか。
English: I guess the victim must have been watching a video of a taped program!

Japanese: その音を聞いたから、２時だったと思い込んでしまったんでしょうね。
English: That's why I thought it was 1:00 PM!

Japanese: どうも、ごメイワクをおかけいたしました‥‥。
English: Terribly sorry about the misunderstanding...

Japanese: ‥‥ふむ。なるほど。ビデオで時報の音を聞いた‥‥。
English: Hmm... I see. You heard a voice saying the time on a taped program.

Japanese: では、弁護人。《尋問》をおねがいします。
English: Mr. Wright, you may cross- examine the witness.

Japanese: なるほどくん。
English: Wright!

Japanese: もう、やり方はバッチリよね？
English: You know what to do!

Japanese: ‥‥まかせてください。
English: I've got this one.

Japanese: ～死体を発見した時間について～
English: -- The Time of Discovery --

Japanese: 死体を見つけたとき、時間が聞こえてきたんでございます。
English: You see, when I found the body, I heard the time.

Japanese: あの音、時報みたいな感じで‥‥。たぶん、テレビだったと思います。
English: There was a voice saying the time... It was probably coming from the television.

Japanese: あ、でも、時報にしては、２時間もズレていたんですよね？
English: Oh, but it was three hours off, wasn't it?

Japanese: たぶん被害者の方は、ビデオを見ていたのではないでしょうか。
English: I guess the victim must have been watching a video of a taped program!

Japanese: その音を聞いたから、２時だったと思い込んでしまったんでしょうね。
English: That's why I thought it was 1:00 PM!

Japanese: どうも、ごメイワクをおかけいたしました‥‥。
English: Terribly sorry about the misunderstanding...

Japanese: ‥‥さあ、どこかヘンなところはなかったかしら？
English: Notice anything suspicious?

Japanese: “聞こえた”‥‥？“見た”わけじゃないんですか？
English: You said "heard"... Not "saw"?

Japanese: はい、おそらく‥‥。
English: Yes, heard.

Japanese: とにかく、死体しか目に入らなかったもので‥‥、
English: All I saw was the body lying there...

Japanese: たぶん、時計を見るよゆうはなかったと思います‥‥。
English: I didn't think to look at anything else, least of all my watch.

Japanese: はあ‥‥。でも、ちょっとヘンですね。
English: Hmm... Isn't that a little strange?

Japanese: それならなぜ、“音”は聞こえたんでしょう？
English: So you're saying you "heard" something.

Japanese: 死体に気をとられていたのなら、音も耳に入らないような気が‥‥。
English: But if you were so shocked by the body, you wouldn't hear anything at all!

Japanese: 証人は、実際に音を聞いているのです。
English: The witness did say he actually heard the time.

Japanese: “聞こえなかったかも”というギロンには、意味がありません！
English: It's ludicrous to suggest he "wouldn't hear anything"!

Japanese: ‥‥ふむう。それはまあ、そのとおりですね。
English: Hmm... I have to agree with the prosecution.

Japanese: 証人は、証言をつづけてください。
English: Witness, continue your testimony.

Japanese: テレビ、ですか。ラジオではなかった？
English: Are you sure it was a television and not... a radio?

Japanese: さ、さあ。ラジオだったかもしれません。
English: Well, no, I guess it might have been a radio.

Japanese: ちなみに、現場にラジオはありませんでした。
English: Incidentally, there was no radio on the premises.

Japanese: 大型のテレビが１台、あっただけです。
English: There was only one large television.

Japanese: なるほどくん。
English: Wright!

Japanese: “テレビの時報を聞いた”っていう証言‥‥、
English: I can't put my finger on it, but something about this seems fishy.

Japanese: 私、ちょっと引っかかるわね‥‥。
English: Something about "hearing" the television...

Japanese: とにかく、山野さん。現場で時報を聞いたわけですね？
English: The witness has testified. He heard the time.

Japanese: そのズレを説明できるんですか！
English: How do you explain the gap!

Japanese: どうですか？　証人？
English: Well, witness? Can you explain this?

Japanese: ‥‥ビデオ、ですか？
English: A... video?

Japanese: ええ。それなら、時報にズレがあっても、おかしくないでしょう？
English: Yes, that would explain why the time was wrong!

Japanese: ‥‥たしかに、そうですね‥‥。
English: True, true...

Japanese: なるほどくん。
English: Wright!

Japanese: 問題は、もっと他にあるんじゃないかしら‥‥。
English: I think the problem lies someplace else...

Japanese: とにかく、あなたが現場で時刻を聞いたのは、間違いないんですね？
English: We're agreed that you heard the time at the scene, then.

Japanese: ２時という時間は、本当に間違いないんですか？
English: Are you sure the voice you heard said it was 1:00 PM?

Japanese: ‥‥そうですね。今でも耳に残っておりますよ。
English: Yes, I can practically hear it now. It was quite clear.

Japanese: 亜内検事。この証言の裏づけ捜査はしましたか？
English: Mr. Payne, has the prosecution verified this testimony?

Japanese: ‥‥すみません。
English: My apologies, Your Honor.

Japanese: 『時報を聞いた』という証言は、私も今、初めて聞いたので‥‥。
English: I, too, have only just learned that the witness "heard" the time.

Japanese: もうしわけありません。今まで、すっかり忘れておりまして‥‥。
English: Oh, I'm really sorry. I only remembered it just now.

Japanese: 気をつけてくださいよ！
English: Well, you just watch it!

Japanese: （‥‥と、こんなところにゆさぶりをかけても意味ないな）
English: (Hmm... Not much point pressing him on that one, was there?)

Japanese: ちょっと待ってください！
English: Hold it right there!

Japanese: そもそも、マンションは停電だったはずですよね。
English: The prosecution has said there was a blackout at the time of the discovery!

Japanese: この記録がそれを証明しています！
English: And this record proves it!

Japanese: ‥‥！...!テレビにしろ、ビデオにしろ、使えるワケがないじゃないですか！
English: You couldn't have heard a television... or a video!
Gah!!!

Japanese: そ‥‥、それは‥‥。
English: I... well... urk!

Japanese: ‥‥たしかに、そのとおり。
English: The defense has a point.

Japanese: 山野さん。‥‥どういうことですかな？
English: Do you have an explanation for this, Mr. Sahwit?

Japanese: ‥‥いや、私もその、どういうことなのか‥‥。
English: No, I... I find it quite puzzling myself! Quite!
Aah!

Japanese: そ、そうだ！お、思い出しました！
English: W-wait! I remember now!

Japanese: ‥‥山野さん。
English: Mr. Sahwit?

Japanese: 証言は、最初から正確におねがいしますよ。
English: The court would prefer to hear an accurate testimony from the very beginning.

Japanese: だんだん、あなたという人物があやしく見えてきます。
English: These constant corrections are harming your credibility.

Japanese: なんか、妙にクネクネしているしねえ。
English: That, and you seem rather... distraught.

Japanese: ‥‥！！...!も、もうしわけございません。
English: M-my apologies, Your Honor!

Japanese: ‥‥でも、なにぶん、死体を見たショックで‥‥。
English: It... er, it must have been the shock of finding the body!

Japanese: もういい、わかりました。
English: Very well, Mr. Sahwit.

Japanese: では、さらに《証言》をおねがいしましょう。
English: Let's hear your testimony once more please.

Japanese: ～《時間を聞いた》ことについて～
English: -- Hearing the Time --

Japanese: やっぱり、“聞いた”のではなく、“見た”んでした！
English: Actually, I didn't "hear" the time... I "saw" it!

Japanese: 現場には、置き時計があったじゃないですか。
English: There was a table clock in the apartment, wasn't there!

Japanese: ほら、犯人が殴るときに使った凶器ですよ！
English: Yeah, the murder weapon! The killer used it to hit the victim!

Japanese: おそらく、あれで時間がわかったんだと思います‥‥。
English: That must have been what I saw.

Japanese: 時計を見た‥‥。まあ、それなら理解できますね。
English: You saw a clock? I guess that would explain it.

Japanese: では、弁護人。《尋問》を。
English: The defense may cross- examine the witness.

Japanese: わかりました。
English: Gladly.

Japanese: ～《時間を聞いた》ことについて～
English: -- Hearing the Time --

Japanese: やっぱり、“聞いた”のではなく、“見た”んでした！
English: Actually, I didn't "hear" the time... I "saw" it!

Japanese: 現場には、置き時計があったじゃないですか。
English: There was a table clock in the apartment, wasn't there!

Japanese: ほら、犯人が殴るときに使った凶器ですよ。
English: Yeah, the murder weapon! The killer used it to hit the victim!

Japanese: おそらく、アレで時間がわかったんだと思います‥‥。
English: That must have been what I saw.

Japanese: ‥‥さあ、ムジュンした部分を探しましょう！
English: Now, find the contradiction!

Japanese: ‥‥なんか、不自然なカン違いですね。
English: That strikes me as a very suspicious mistake.

Japanese: はい。自分でもそう思うんですけど‥‥。
English: Yes, I can see how you'd be a little doubtful...

Japanese: でも、急に置き時計のことを思い出しちゃったんですよ。
English: I'm really sorry. I only just remembered that table clock!

Japanese: “置き時計”‥‥？
English: A "table clock"?

Japanese: “置き時計”‥‥。そんなものがあったんですか？
English: A "table clock"? Was there a clock at the scene?

Japanese: ‥‥私も初耳ですね。
English: This is the first I've heard of it!

Japanese: 凶器‥‥ですか？
English: The... murder weapon?

Japanese: ええ。凶器の置き時計でございますよ。
English: Yes, the table clock that was used as a weapon!

Japanese: ‥‥シツレイですが、ちゃんと話は聞いておられましたか？
English: That's what I just said. Did you doze off in the middle of my testimony or something?

Japanese: （‥‥何か、引っかかるな‥‥）
English: (Something's fishy here...)

Japanese: なんで最初からそう証言しなかったんですか？
English: Why didn't you tell us that in the first place?

Japanese: なぜか、思い出せなかったんですよ‥‥。
English: I guess it just slipped my mind!

Japanese: 自分でもよくわかりません。もうしわけありませんでした。
English: I'm not really sure how it happened myself...

Japanese: とにかく、現場で置き時計を見た、ということですね。
English: The witness says he saw the table clock. End of story.

Japanese: ちょっと待ってください。
English: Wait just a moment!

Japanese: 凶器はこのとおり、“置物”なんですよ。
English: The murder weapon wasn't a clock. It was this statue!

Japanese: これのどこが時計なんですかッ！
English: Now how is this supposed to be a clock?

Japanese: うおおおっ！
English: Whaa!?

Japanese: ‥‥あんた、なんなんだ！さっきから、エラそうに。
English: Y-you with your "objections," and your "evidence"... Just who do you think you are!?

Japanese: 山野さん。質問に答えてください。
English: Just answer the question, Mr. Sahwit.

Japanese: オレ‥‥わ、私は見たんだ。そいつは置き時計なんだよ！
English: Hey, I... I saw it there, okay! That's a clock!

Japanese: 裁判長！ちょっとよろしいですか。
English: Your Honor! If I may...

Japanese: どうしましたか？　亜内検事。
English: Yes, Mr. Payne?

Japanese: この置物は、証人の言うとおり、実は置き時計なんです。
English: As the witness stated, this statue is indeed a clock.

Japanese: 首がスイッチになっていまして、時間をアナウンスするタイプです。
English: The neck is a switch. You just tilt it, and it says the time out loud.

Japanese: 時計には見えないので、《置物》として提出したんです‥‥。
English: As it doesn't look like a clock, I submitted it as a statue. My apologies.

Japanese: なるほどね‥‥。
English: I see.

Japanese: 凶器は、実は《置き時計》だった、と。
English: So the murder weapon was a table clock after all.

Japanese: どうかな？　成歩堂くん。
English: Well, Mr. Wright?

Japanese: 山野さんの証言は間違っていない。これはたしかに、時計なんだから。
English: It appears that the witness's testimony was correct. This is a clock.

Japanese: ‥‥山野さんの証言について、もう問題はないかね？
English: Do you have any problems with his testimony now?

Japanese: そうですね。
English: I guess not.

Japanese: 現場に時計があったのなら、問題はないと思います。
English: There was a clock on the scene, so, no problem.

Japanese: ちょ、ちょっと待って！
English: Wright! Are you out of your mind!?

Japanese: あの“時計”、どう見たって“置物”じゃない。
English: That clock doesn't look like a clock at all!

Japanese: あの証人は、あれが“時計”だって知ってるはずがない！
English: The witness couldn't have possibly known it was a clock just by seeing it!

Japanese: なぜなら彼は、“現場の部屋には入らなかった”と、
English: He said himself, he never entered the apartment!

Japanese: そう証言しているんだから！
English: It was in his testimony!

Japanese: ‥‥あっ！そういえばそうですね。
English: Hey! You're right!

Japanese: どうかしましたか、弁護人。やはり、問題がある、と‥‥？
English: Is something the matter? Does the defense have anything to add?

Japanese: ‥‥は、はい！
English: Yes... Yes I do!

Japanese: 裁判長！大きな問題が残っています！
English: Your Honor, there is a gaping hole in the witness's testimony!

Japanese: その置物が時計だと知るためには、実際に手に取るしかありません。
English: The only way he could have known the weapon was a clock is to hold it in his hand.

Japanese: しかし、証人は“部屋には入らなかった”と証言している。
English: Yet the witness testified that he never entered the apartment!

Japanese: これは、あきらかにムジュンしています！
English: Clearly, a contradiction!

Japanese: ‥‥ふむう‥‥。たしかに。
English: Hmm... indeed!

Japanese: なぜ、証人が時計のことを知っていたか？　それは‥‥
English: The witness knew it was a clock, because he...

Japanese: 実は、証人は被害者と知り合いだったのではないですか？
English: Tell me, isn't it true that you knew the victim?

Japanese: ズバリ、彼女の“パパ”たちの１人だった！　違いますか？
English: In fact, you were one of her "sugar daddies"! Be frank with us, Mr. Sahwit!

Japanese: ‥‥フン！
English: Hmph.

Japanese: “ズバリ”と言われましてもねえ。
English: "Frank"? I'm always "Frank"!

Japanese: 裁判長。
English: Your Honor.

Japanese: 彼女の交友関係は、すべてチェックしました。
English: We have complete records of the victim's relationships.

Japanese: そのリストの中に、山野　星雄の名前はありません。
English: Mr. Frank Sahwit does not appear anywhere.

Japanese: あれ。そうでしたか‥‥。
English: Huh? Oh, really?

Japanese: “あれ”じゃないでしょう！
English: Please, Mr. Wright... Is "Huh" the best response you can muster up?

Japanese: テキトーなことは言わないように。
English: Try to refrain from making off-the-cuff accusations in the future.

Japanese: わ、わかりました。‥‥考えなおします。
English: Y-yes, Your Honor. Let me think this over.

Japanese: あなたはウソをついている！
English: You're lying!

Japanese: あなたはあの日、現場の部屋に入ったのです！
English: You were inside the apartment on the day of the murder!

Japanese: な、なんだと！し、知らねえぞオレは‥‥
English: Oh yeah? Prove it! Prove I went in there!

Japanese: そうだ！　あの置き時計で被害者を殴ったのは、あなただったんだ！
English: I'll do better than that! I can prove you were the one who killed her!

Japanese: 彼女を殴ったときのはずみで、あの時計は鳴った！
English: You struck her with the clock, and the shock of the blow triggered the clock's voice!

Japanese: あなたは、その音を聞いたんです！
English: That was the sound you heard!

Japanese: 静粛に！
English: Order in the court!

Japanese: ‥‥おもしろい。成歩堂くん、つづけなさい。
English: Intriguing. Please continue, Mr. Wright.

Japanese: はい。
English: Yes, Your Honor.

Japanese: ‥‥山野さん。
English: Mr. Sahwit.

Japanese: きっとあなたは、かなり驚いたはずです‥‥。
English: The sound must have left quite an impression on you.

Japanese: 被害者を殴った瞬間、置物が急にしゃべり出したわけですから。
English: Understandable, since the murder weapon spoke just as you hit the victim!

Japanese: その声が、強烈に印象に残ってしまった。
English: That voice was burned into your mind.

Japanese: だから、あなたは時間だけを妙にハッキリ覚えていたんです！
English: That's why you were so certain about the time!

Japanese: ど、ど、どういうつもりですか！
English: W-w-what's the meaning of this?

Japanese: そ、そんないいかげんな言いがかりは‥‥。
English: This is all baseless conjecture!

Japanese: いいかげんかどうか‥‥、
English: Baseless...?

Japanese: 証人の顔を見てください！
English: Just look at the witness's face!
Ngh... grrrah!

Japanese: 証人‥‥。どうなんですか？
English: Would the witness care to elaborate?

Japanese: あなたが殴ったんですか？
English: Did you strike the victim with the clock?

Japanese: わ、ワタシは、その‥‥‥‥け、決して‥‥
English: I... I...! That... that day... I... I never!

Japanese: ‥‥あの、ワタシが、聞いた、いや、見たのは‥‥うぐぐ‥‥
English: Look... I... the clock... I heard, no! I mean, I saw...Saw... nggg!

Japanese: うおおおおおおおおおおおおおっ！
English: Gwaaaaaaaaaaaaah!

Japanese: うるせえんだよ！細かいことをぐちぐちと！
English: Shutupshutupshutup! I hate you!

Japanese: あ、アイツだ‥‥！オレは見たんだよ‥‥。
English: I-it was him, I tell you! I saw him!

Japanese: し、死刑だ！あの男に、死刑を‥‥！
English: H-he killed her and he should burn! Burn! Give him death!

Japanese: 静粛に！　静粛に！
English: Order! Order in the court I say!

Japanese: 裁判長！お、お待ちください！
English: Your Honor, a-a moment please!

Japanese: ただ今の弁護人の主張には、証拠のカケラもありません！
English: There isn't a shred of evidence supporting the defense's claims!

Japanese: 成歩堂くん！
English: Mr. Wright!

Japanese: はい。
English: Your Honor?

Japanese: 証人が聞いたという時報が、この時計の音だったという証拠。
English: You claim the sound the witness heard came from the clock...

Japanese: そんな証拠は、あるのですか？
English: Do you have any evidence?

Japanese: （これは重要な問題だ！よく、考えないとな‥‥）
English: (The whole case is riding on this! I'd better think it through carefully!)

Japanese: ‥‥そうですね。
English: Yes, Your Honor.

Japanese: 山野さんが聞いたのは、この置き時計の音だったのです。
English: The sound Mr. Sahwit heard was definitely this clock.

Japanese: それを証明することは、カンタンだと思います。それは‥‥
English: A fact which is clear if you simply...

Japanese: その置き時計の電池を調べればいいのです！
English: All you have to do is examine the batteries!

Japanese: 電池は‥‥ちゃんと入っている。時計も動いているようですね。
English: Indeed? The batteries are... in the right way. The clock seems to be working fine.

Japanese: ‥‥どういうことですか？弁護人。
English: What exactly did you mean, Mr. Wright?

Japanese: つまり、時計は動いていた、ということです！
English: Yes, the clock was working fine!

Japanese: だから？
English: Yes, and...?

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥‥‥...なんだか、しゃべっているうちに、よくわからなくなってきました。
English: Umm, I'm sorry, I think I got confused back there with all those testimonies.

Japanese: 弁護人！
English: Mr. Wright!

Japanese: ここまで来て、そのオチはないでしょう。
English: I expect more from a lawyer in this court. Even if it is your first day.

Japanese: ペナルティを与えます！シッカリ考えるように！
English: I'm afraid I have to penalize you! Try to think things through more carefully.

Japanese: わ、わかりました。とにかく！
English: Y-yes, Your Honor. As I was saying...

Japanese: 被害者の隣室の住人に、聞き込みをすればいいのです！
English: All you have to do is talk to the victim's neighbors!

Japanese: ‥‥聞き込み？
English: Talk to the neighbors...?

Japanese: 事件のあったとき、誰かが時計が鳴った音を聞いたはずです！
English: I'm sure one of them heard the clock tell the time when the incident occurred!

Japanese: なるほど‥‥。
English: I see...

Japanese: どうですか、亜内検事？
English: Does the prosecution have anything to say, Mr. Payne?

Japanese: ‥‥聞き込みは、すでにやっております。
English: We have already made all the necessary inquiries.

Japanese: 事件のあった頃、付近の部屋のみなさんは留守でした。
English: Everyone living near the victim's apartment was out at the time of the murder.

Japanese: しかし、もし彼らが時計の音を聞いていたとしても、
English: Furthermore, even if a neighbor had heard the clock,

Japanese: その音を山野さんが聞いたかどうかは、別の問題です。
English: that would not prove that Mr. Sahwit had heard anything.

Japanese: うーん‥‥。そりゃあそうですね。
English: Hmm... That is true.

Japanese: どうやら、違ったようですね。弁護人！
English: I believe you may be wrong, Mr. Wright.

Japanese: ‥‥ペナルティを与えます。
English: You'll receive a penalty for that, unfortunately.

Japanese: す、すみません。考え直します！　でも、とにかく！
English: I-I'm sorry, Your Honor! Let me think about it again!

Japanese: この場で、置き時計を鳴らしてみればいいんですよ。
English: Let's sound the clock now, here in this court.

Japanese: 裁判長。その時計を貸してください。
English: Your Honor, may I have the clock?

Japanese: いいですか。‥‥よく、聞いてください。
English: I ask the court to listen very carefully...

Japanese: 《おそらく、９時２５分だと思う》
English: [I think it's 8:25.]

Japanese: な、なんか、ヘンなアナウンスをする時計ですね。
English: That certainly is a strange way to announce the time.

Japanese: まあ、《考える人》ですから。
English: Well, he is "The Thinker," after all.

Japanese: ‥‥で。このアナウンスがどうかしましたか‥‥？
English: So, we've heard the clock. What are your conclusions, Mr. Wright?

Japanese: 亜内検事。今、ほんとうは何時ですか？
English: Mr. Payne... can you tell me what time it is now?

Japanese: あっ！
English: Ack!

Japanese: ２時間、遅れているんですよ。この置き時計は‥‥。
English: As you can see, this clock is exactly three hours slow!

Japanese: 殺人現場で、山野さんが聞いた時刻と同じく、ね。
English: Precisely the discrepancy between what Mr. Sahwit heard and the actual time of death!

Japanese: さあ、どうですか！
English: So, Mr. Sahwit...

Japanese: 言い逃れできますか、山野さん‥‥？
English: Try to talk your way out of this one!
...Hah! Hah hah!

Japanese: あんた、１つ見逃してるよ！
English: You forgot one thing!

Japanese: （‥‥え。な、なんのことだ‥‥？）
English: (Uh oh... what's he talking about...?)

Japanese: たしかに、その時計は２時間、遅れてるみたいだな。
English: While it may seem like that clock IS running three hours slow...

Japanese: だがな‥‥。
English: It proves nothing!

Japanese: その時計が、事件当日にも遅れていたかどうか‥‥
English: How do you know it was running three hours slow on the day of the murder!?

Japanese: その証拠がないかぎり、証明したことにはならねえぜ！
English: If you can't prove that, you don't have a case!

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥！...!（‥‥そんなこと、証明できるワケがないじゃないか！）
English: (He's right! How am I going to prove that!?)

Japanese: （クソッ！　あと１歩だっていうのに‥‥！）
English: (Dammit! I was so close!)

Japanese: 成歩堂くん。
English: Mr. Wright?

Japanese: ‥‥どうやらあなたは、それを証明するのはムリなようですね。
English: It seems you lack the critical evidence to support your claim.
Yes, Your Honor.

Japanese: となると、山野さんの言うとおり、彼を告発することはできません。
English: This means I cannot let you indict the witness.

Japanese: ‥‥ザンネンですが、ね。
English: Unfortunately...

Japanese: ではこれにて、山野　星雄氏に対する尋問を、終了します！
English: This ends the cross-examination of Mr. Frank Sahwit.

Japanese: ヘッ！　ヒトがわざわざ証言しに来てやったっていうのに、
English: I come all the way down here to testify, and look what happens!

Japanese: ハンニン呼ばわりかよ！
English: They treat me like a criminal! A criminal!

Japanese: まったく弁護士ってのは、ヒドい連中だぜ！
English: You lawyers are all slime!

Japanese: （くそっ！　‥‥山野め！）
English: (Grr! I almost had him!)

Japanese: （すまない！　矢張‥‥。もう少しだったのに）
English: (Sorry, Larry... I failed you.)

Japanese: （ぼくにはもう、どうしようもない‥‥）
English: (There's nothing I can do about it now...)

Japanese: 待ちなさい！　　山野　星雄！
English: Not so fast, Mr. Sahwit!

Japanese: ち、千尋さん！
English: Mia! I mean, Chief!

Japanese: なるほどくん！
English: Listen up, Wright!

Japanese: ダメよ！　ここであきらめちゃ！‥‥考えましょう！
English: Don't throw this one away, not like this! Think!

Japanese: でも、もうムリだよ千尋さん！
English: But, Chief, it's over.

Japanese: 事件があった日に、時計が遅れていたかどうかなんて、
English: I can't prove the clock was slow the day of the murder!

Japanese: 今さら、証明しようがない！
English: Nobody can prove that!

Japanese: そ、‥‥そうね‥‥。
English: Um... well, yes.

Japanese: じゃあ、いっそのこと発想を逆転させましょう！
English: But that doesn't mean you can't still win! Try thinking out of the box!

Japanese: 《あの時計が、事件当日も２時間遅れていたかどうか》‥‥
English: Don't waste time doubting the facts. Assume the clock was three hours slow and...

Japanese: ‥‥そう考えるのではなく、
English: Think through it!

Japanese: そもそも、あの時計が、《なぜ２時間も遅れていたのか？》
English: Ask yourself, "why was the clock three hours slow"?

Japanese: その理由を考えてみるの！
English: Figure out the reason, and you'll have your proof!

Japanese: なるほどくん！
English: Right, Wright?

Japanese: あの時計がなぜ、２時間遅れていたか、わかる？
English: Can you think of a reason as to why the clock would be three hours slow?

Japanese: わ、わかりませんよ、そんなの！
English: H-how am I supposed to know that!?

Japanese: そんなはずはないわ！
English: I know you can figure it out!

Japanese: あなたは、あの時計が遅れていた理由を示す証拠品を‥‥、
English: There must be some evidence in the Court Record...

Japanese: 法廷記録の中に、ちゃんと持っているはずよ！
English: Something that can show why that clock was three hours slow!

Japanese: それをアイツにつきつけてやりなさい！
English: Find it, and he won't have a foot to stand on!

Japanese: 成歩堂くん。
English: Mr. Wright?

Japanese: は、は、はい！
English: Y-y-yes, Your Honor!

Japanese: 事件があった日に、時計がすでに遅れていた‥‥、
English: You say the clock was already running slow on the day of the murder...

Japanese: それを証明することができると言うのですか！
English: Do you have evidence to prove this?

Japanese: （イチかバチか、ハッタリだ！）
English: (This is it... all or nothing!)

Japanese: ‥‥はい。
English: Yes, Your Honor.

Japanese: ある証拠品で、それを証明できると思います！
English: I believe I have the evidence that can prove my claim!

Japanese: ほお！　‥‥できるもんなら、やってみな！
English: Hah hah! I'd like to see THAT!

Japanese: では、時計が遅れていた理由を示す証拠品を提示してもらいましょう！
English: Let's see this evidence that proves why the clock was running slow!

Japanese: ‥‥それが、どうかしましたか？
English: Um, excuse me. This proves your claim... how?

Japanese: 時計とは関係ないような気がしますが‥‥。
English: I can't see what that evidence has to do with the clock.

Japanese: （しまった！　しくじったか！）
English: (D'oh! That wasn't it!)

Japanese: ‥‥も、もう一度、チャンスをください！
English: One more chance... Give me just one more chance!

Japanese: 時間をムダにしたくありません。
English: Alright, Mr. Wright, but time is not on your side.

Japanese: ‥‥早くしなさい。
English: Be quick about it.
Wait!

Japanese: はい、それならたぶん、わかると思います！
English: Maybe I can prove it!

Japanese: あなたは、それを証明する証拠品を持っているはずよ！
English: You must have evidence somewhere that can prove it, Wright!

Japanese: それをつきつけてやりましょう！
English: Find it and let them have it!

Japanese: どうですか、成歩堂くん。
English: Well, Mr. Wright?

Japanese: 事件があった日には、時計はすでに遅れていた‥‥
English: You say the clock was already running slow on the day of the murder...

Japanese: それを証明することができると言うのですか？
English: Have you found evidence to support this claim?

Japanese: ‥‥もちろん。
English: Of course.

Japanese: ある証拠品で、それを証明できると思います。
English: There is a piece of evidence in the Court Record that can prove my claim beyond a doubt!

Japanese: ほお！　‥‥できるもんなら、やってみな！
English: Hah! Tough words! Let's see you pull this one off!

Japanese: 被害者は、事件の前日、帰国したばかりでした。
English: The victim had just returned home from abroad the day before the murder.

Japanese: ニューヨークと日本では、１４時間の時差があります！
English: As we all know, the time difference between here and Paris is nine hours!

Japanese: 日本で午後４時のとき、向こうは前日の午前２時。
English: When it's 4:00 PM here, it's 1:00 AM the next day there.

Japanese: 時計で見れば、その差はちょうど、２時間になります！
English: The clock wasn't three hours slow, it was nine hours fast!

Japanese: 被害者は、帰国後、時計の時差をまだ戻していなかったのです！
English: The victim hadn't reset her clock since returning home!

Japanese: だから、あなたが聞いた時間は、２時間ズレていたんだ！
English: That's why the time you heard when you struck her dead in her apartment was wrong!

Japanese: どうですか！　山野さん！
English: Proof enough for you, Mr. Sahwit? Or should I say... Mr. Did It!

Japanese: せ、静粛に！　静粛に！
English: O-order! Order, I say!

Japanese: ‥‥さて。
English: Well...

Japanese: ここへ来て、状況は一変したと言っていいでしょう。
English: This case has certainly turned out differently than we all expected.

Japanese: 亜内検事。山野　星雄は‥‥？
English: Mr. Payne... your client?

Japanese: は、あの‥‥先ほど緊急逮捕いたしました。
English: He... er... he was arrested and has been taken away, Your Honor.

Japanese: よろしい。
English: Very well.

Japanese: ‥‥成歩堂くん。
English: Mr. Wright?

Japanese: はい。
English: Yes, Your Honor.

Japanese: ‥‥正直なトコロ、驚きました。
English: I have to say, I'm impressed.

Japanese: こんなに早く、依頼人を救い出した上、
English: I don't think I've ever seen someone complete a defense so quickly...

Japanese: 真犯人まで探し出すとは‥‥。
English: and find the true culprit at the same time!

Japanese: ありがとうございます‥‥。
English: Thank you, Your Honor.

Japanese: とにかく、今となっては形式にすぎませんが、
English: At this point, this is only a formality, but...

Japanese: 被告人、矢張　政志に判決を言い渡します。
English: This court finds the defendant, Mr. Larry Butz...

Japanese: ‥‥では、本日はこれにて閉廷！
English: And with that... The court is adjourned.

Japanese: 山野という男は、空き巣の常習犯だったらしい。
English: It turns out that Frank Sahwit was a common burglar!

Japanese: 新聞の勧誘をしながら、留守の家を狙っていたという。
English: He posed as a newspaper sales- man to check and see when people were out of the house!

Japanese: ‥‥あの日。
English: That day...

Japanese: 矢張が部屋を訪ねたとき、被害者は留守だった。
English: When Larry went to her apartment, the victim wasn't home.

Japanese: 彼が立ち去った後、山野は“仕事”をするため、部屋に侵入。
English: After he left, Mr. Sahwit let himself in to do his dirty work!

Japanese: そして部屋を物色中、被害者が帰ってきたのだ。
English: While he was searching her place, the victim returned!

Japanese: 逆上した山野は、そばにあった置物を手に取って‥‥
English: Flustered, Mr. Sahwit grabbed the nearest blunt object he could find...

Japanese: ８月３日　午後２時３２分地方裁判所　被告人第２控え室
English: August 3, 2:32 PM District Court Defendant Lobby No. 2

Japanese: （‥‥うう、ブジに終わったなんてまだ信じられないよ‥‥）
English: (Whew... I still can't believe we won!)

Japanese: なるほどくん！やったわね！
English: Wright! Good job in there!

Japanese: おめでとう！
English: Congratulations!

Japanese: あ、ありがとうございます！‥‥ホント、所長のおかげです。
English: Th-thanks, Chief. I owe it all to you.

Japanese: ううん。そんなことないわ。
English: Not at all, not at all! You fought your own battles in there.

Japanese: とにかく私、ひさしぶりにスカッとしたわ！
English: It's been a while since I've seen a trial end on such a satisfying note!

Japanese: （こんなにニコニコしている所長、初めてだ）
English: (I've never seen the chief looking this happy...)

Japanese: （そうだ！　矢張のヤツもよろこんでるだろうな‥‥）
English: (If she's this glad, imagine how Larry must feel!)

Japanese: 死ぬんだぁ‥‥。
English: My life is over...

Japanese: な、なんでおまえがそんなカオしてるんだよ！
English: Larry! You're supposed to be happy! What's wrong now!?

Japanese: 成歩堂ぉ‥‥。オレ、そろそろ死ぬからさぁ。
English: Aww, Nick... Don't worry 'bout me! I'll be dead and gone soon!

Japanese: い、いやいやいや。
English: Good! Wait, no! I mean... Bad! Bad bad bad!

Japanese: おまえは無罪になって、事件は終わったんだよ、矢張。
English: Larry, you're innocent! The case is closed.

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥...でもよぉ、‥‥美佳は帰ってこないんだよなぁ‥‥。
English: But... but my Cindy-windy's gone, man! Gone forever!
(Larry, she was a... Nah... Never mind.)

Japanese: おめでとうございます、ヤッパリさん。
English: Congratulations, Harry!

Japanese: ヤ、“ヤッパリ”‥‥？
English: H-Harry...?

Japanese: どうかしましたか？ヤッパリさん。
English: Yes, you! I can practically see the headlines now: "Harry Butz, Innocent!"

Japanese: いや、あの。なんつーかその、ホント、ありがとッス。
English: Heh... um... thanks! I really owe you one.

Japanese: オレ、一生忘れねーから。
English: I won't forget this, ever! Let's celebrate! Dinner? Movie? My treat!

Japanese: ‥‥まあ。
English: Oh, no, I couldn't.

Japanese: （おい、助けたのはぼくだぞ‥‥）
English: (Hey, I was the one who got you off the hook!)

Japanese: あ、そうだ。
English: Oh, hey!

Japanese: こ、これ、プレゼント！‥‥受けとってください！
English: H-here, take this! It's a present!

Japanese: あら。‥‥私に？
English: A present? For me?

Japanese: あの‥‥。でもそれ、証拠品じゃあ‥‥？
English: Wait... Wasn't this the evidence that...

Japanese: 実はコレ、オレがあいつのために作ってやった時計なんスよ。
English: Actually, I made this clock for her!

Japanese: あいつのと、オレのとで２個、作ったんス。
English: I made one for her and one for me.

Japanese: まあ、あなたが作ったの、これ？
English: R-really? You? You made this?

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥...そうね。‥‥じゃ、記念にいただくわ。
English: Well, thank you. I'll keep it as a memento.

Japanese: ‥‥でもよぉ、成歩堂‥‥。
English: Yo, Nick...

Japanese: オレ、こんなにアイツのこと想っていたのに、
English: Can you believe it? I was so into that chick...

Japanese: あの女にとってオレ‥‥、ただのオモチャだったんだよな。
English: And... and she was just playing me for a fool!

Japanese: すげぇ、カナシイよぉ‥‥。
English: Don't that make you wanna just cry? *sob*

Japanese: 矢張‥‥。
English: Larry...

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥...私、そうは思わないけど。
English: Are you so sure?
Ex-squeeze me?

Japanese: 彼女は彼女なりに、あなたのことを想っていたはずですよ。
English: I think she thought quite a lot of you, in her own way.

Japanese: ハッ！‥‥な、なぐさめなんて‥‥。
English: Nah, you don't gotta sympathize with me, 'sokay.

Japanese: あら、それはどうかしら。
English: Oh, I'm not just sympathizing, really.

Japanese: ねえ、なるほどくん？‥‥わからず屋の彼に、
English: Isn't that right, Wright? Don't you have something to show your friend?

Japanese: 彼女の気持ちがわかる証拠を見せてあげて。
English: Something that proves how she felt about him?

Japanese: は、はい？‥‥あ、そ、そうですね。
English: H-huh? Oh, yeah, right!

Japanese: （い、いきなり言われてもなあ‥‥）
English: (What the heck is she talking about?)

Japanese: ほ、ほおら。これがその証拠だよ、矢張。
English: Here you go, Larry. Proof.

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥...は、はは。‥‥もういいんだよ、成歩堂。
English: Eh... heh heh? It's okay, Nick. Don't worry about me.

Japanese: オレ、あの女のコトは、忘れることにするからさ。
English: I'll forget about her soon enough.

Japanese: じゃ、オレ、帰るわ。‥‥ホント、ありがとな。
English: Look, I'm gonna head home. Thanks a ton, eh?

Japanese: （‥‥この証拠品‥‥、ダメだったみたいだな‥‥）
English: (Guess that wasn't the right thing to show him...)

Japanese: 矢張、これ‥‥。
English: Check this out, Larry. Proof Positive you weren't just some chump to her.

Japanese: な、なんだよ‥‥。この時計がどうかしたか？
English: Huh...? Where'd you get that clock?

Japanese: これ、お前の手製のオリジナル時計なんだよな。
English: This is the clock you made for her, Larry!

Japanese: 彼女、こいつといっしょに旅行に行ってたんだぜ。
English: She took it with her when she traveled.

Japanese: は、はん。たまたま時計がなかったんだろ？
English: Hmm, she probably just needed a clock, that's all.

Japanese: それだけで、わざわざこんな重いものを持っていくか‥‥？
English: You think so? It's a pretty heavy clock to take traveling.

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥...まあ、何をどう考えるかは、お前しだいだけど。
English: Well, make of it what you will.
Hey, Nick.

Japanese: 今回のコト、おマエにたのんでよかったよ。
English: I'm glad I asked you to be my lawyer.

Japanese: ‥‥ホント、ありがとな。
English: Really, I am. Thanks.

Japanese: （‥‥これでよかったのかな‥‥）
English: (Hope that made him feel a little better...)

Japanese: ‥‥なるほどくん。
English: Wright?

Japanese: 証拠品って、こういうものよ。
English: I hope you see the importance of evidence now.

Japanese: 見る角度によって、その意味合いはどうにでも変わってしまうわ‥‥。
English: Also, hopefully you realize, things change depending on how you look at them.

Japanese: 人間だって、そう。
English: People, too.

Japanese: 被告人が“有罪”か“無罪”か？私たちには、知りようがない。
English: We never really know if our clients are guilty or innocent.

Japanese: 弁護士にできるのは、彼らを信じることだけ。
English: All we can do is believe in them.

Japanese: そして彼らを信じるということは、自分を信じるということなの。
English: And in order to believe in them, you have to believe in yourself.

Japanese: ‥‥なるほどくん。
English: Wright...

Japanese: 強くなりなさい。‥‥もっと。
English: Listen. Learn. Grow strong.

Japanese: 自分が信じたものは最後まで、あきらめてはダメ。
English: Never let go of what you believe in. Never.

Japanese: ‥‥じゃあ、私たちも帰りましょうか。
English: Well, I think our work here is done! Shall we be off?

Japanese: そうですね。
English: Yeah, I guess so!

Japanese: 今夜は私、パーッとごちそうしちゃおうかな。
English: Say, how about dinner. On me?

Japanese: ヤッパリさんの無罪をお祝いしましょう！
English: We'll drink a toast to innocent Butz!

Japanese: ‥‥はいっ！
English: Yeah!

Japanese: あ、そうだ。‥‥ヤッパリさんといえば、
English: Oh, speaking of Harry...

Japanese: あなた、彼のおかげで弁護士になった、って言ってたわよね。
English: You were saying part of why you became a lawyer was because of him.

Japanese: え、は、はあ。‥‥“ある意味”ですけど。
English: Er, yeah. Part, at least.

Japanese: 今度、聞かせてね、その話。‥‥ものすごく興味があるの。
English: You'll have to tell me more about it sometime! Maybe... over drinks?

Japanese: ‥‥こうして、ぼくの最初の事件は幕を閉じた。
English: And so, my first trial came to a close.

Japanese: 矢張のヤツ“トモダチはいいなあ”を連呼しつつも、
English: Larry slapped me on the back and said, "Gee, Nick, it's good to have friends!"

Japanese: あの《考える人》の時計以外に、依頼料を払う気はないようだ。
English: But I'm pretty sure he's not going to pay me. Unless you count the clock he gave Mia.

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥‥‥...そして、あの時計は、
English: I didn't know it then...

Japanese: 再び、とんでもない事件を引き起こすことになり、
English: but that clock was soon going to be at the center of another incident.

Japanese: “矢張のことを話す”というぼくの約束は、
English: And my promise to tell the chief about me and Larry...

Japanese: 永久に果たされることはなかった。
English: would be one promise that I wouldn't be able to keep.

Japanese: おわり
English: THE END

Japanese: そこまで！
English: That's enough!

Japanese: 本法廷は、これ以上の審議の必要性を認めません。
English: This court sees no reason to further prolong the trial.

Japanese: 弁護人の反証は、検察側の主張をくつがえす力を持っていない！
English: The defense has failed to give the court sufficient reason to doubt the prosecution's claim.

Japanese: 被告人、矢張　政志に、この場で判決を言い渡します。
English: This court finds the defendant, Mr. Larry Butz...

Japanese: 被告人はこれより、その身柄を司直に預けるものとし、
English: The accused will surrender to the court immediately,

Japanese: １ヶ月以内に、高等裁判所において通常裁判を受けることとします。
English: to be held pending trial at a higher court within a month from today's date.

Japanese: では、本日はこれにて閉廷！
English: That is all. This court is adjourned!

Japanese: プルルル‥‥‥‥プルルル‥‥‥‥
English: Brrring... Brrrrring...

Japanese: ピッ
English: *beep*

Japanese: はい‥‥綾里ですけど。
English: Hello? This is Maya.

Japanese: もしもし。私よ。
English: Hey Maya, it's me.

Japanese: あ、チヒロお姉ちゃん！どうしたの？　めずらしいね。
English: Mia! What's up? You haven't called in a while.

Japanese: ごめんね。最近、忙しくって。‥‥どう？　元気？
English: Sorry, I've been so busy. How you been?

Japanese: もう！　かわいい妹を１人でほったらかして、ひどいなー。
English: Well, LONELY. And it's all YOUR fault.

Japanese: なーんて。
English: Nah, I'm just teasing.

Japanese: だいじょうぶだよ！　もう、慣れちゃったから‥‥１人ぐらし。
English: I've been great! I'm finally getting used to having my own place.

Japanese: そう‥‥ありがとう。
English: That's good to hear.

Japanese: ‥‥あのね。実はちょっと、たのみたいことがあるの。
English: Actually, I'm calling because I have a favor to ask.

Japanese: あー、わかった！　また、証拠品あずかれって言うんでしょ？
English: I know, I know. You want me to hold evidence for you?

Japanese: よくわかったわね。
English: Sharp as always!

Japanese: ‥‥今度の事件、ちょっと話が大きくなっていてね。
English: There's a lot of buzz about the upcoming trial...

Japanese: 裁判の日まで、そばに置いておきたくなくて。
English: I just don't feel safe keeping the evidence here.

Japanese: へえ‥‥。で？　で？　今度は何？
English: I gotcha. So, what is it this time?

Japanese: 置き時計よ。
English: It's... a clock.

Japanese: ‥‥時計？
English: A clock?

Japanese: そう。考える人の形をしてて、なんと、しゃべるのよ！
English: Yeah, it's made to look like that statue, "The Thinker." And it tells you the time!

Japanese: あなた、好きでしょ？こういう、オモチャみたいなの。
English: I thought you might like it. You always liked toys.

Japanese: あー。またあたしのコト、コドモ扱いしたー。
English: Hey! I'm not a little girl anymore, Sis!

Japanese: ふふふ。‥‥ごめんなさい。
English: Now, now. You know I'm only teasing.

Japanese: ‥‥あ。でも、今はこの時計、しゃべらないんだったっけ。
English: Ah, I should probably tell you, the clock isn't talking right now.

Japanese: えー、なんでー？‥‥つまんない。
English: Huh? It's not working? That's lame!

Japanese: さっき、時計の機械を抜いちゃったから。
English: I had to take the clockwork out. Sorry.

Japanese: かわりに、書類が入っているの。
English: I put some papers inside it instead.

Japanese: 書類？‥‥それが“証拠品”なの？
English: Papers? Is that the evidence, then?

Japanese: うー。なんか、話がソレっぽくなってきたね。
English: Hmm, well... there's a possibility that it might turn out that way, yes.

Japanese: ‥‥じゃあ、今晩の９時ごろ、事務所に来てくれるかしら？
English: Can you come by the office tonight, say 9:00, to pick it up?

Japanese: 裁判の打ち合わせ、たぶん、そのころまでかかるから。
English: I'll be in a pretrial meeting until then.

Japanese: お姉ちゃん！　また、何かおいしいのゴチソウしてよね！
English: Okay, Sis, but I expect dinner! Something good!

Japanese: あ、みそラーメンがいいなみそラーメン。
English: Like... burgers! I could really go for a good burger.

Japanese: はいはい。じゃ、いつものお店、行こうね。
English: Okay, okay. We'll hit the usual joint.

Japanese: やったー！　ヤクソクだよ！
English: Alright! It's a deal!

Japanese: じゃ、お姉ちゃん。また、あとでね！
English: Okay, Sis, see you soon!

Japanese: うん。待ってるわよ、マヨイ。
English: Yep. I'll be waiting, Maya.

Japanese: ピー
English: *beep*

Japanese: 《ただ今の通話、録音しました。９月５日、午前９時２７分》
English: [Conversation recorded. September 5, 9:27 AM]

Japanese: ９月　５日　午後８時５７分綾里法律事務所
English: September 5, 8:57 PM Fey & Co. Law Offices

Japanese: ‥‥じゃあ、ミス?チヒロ。もらっていくよ‥‥書類を。
English: Now, Miss Fey, I'll take what's mine... the papers.

Japanese: 悪いけど、渡せないわ。‥‥ここには、ないもの。
English: I'm sorry, but I can't give you what I don't have.

Japanese: ミス?チヒロ‥‥、ウソはヘタみたいだね。
English: Miss Fey, you are a poor liar.

Japanese: ちゃんとあるじゃないか、そこに‥‥。
English: Why, I see it right over there...

Japanese: 書類を飲み込んだ《考える人》が、さ。
English: That must be "The Thinker" that swallowed those papers.

Japanese: ‥‥ど、どうしてそれを！
English: How could you know...?

Japanese: ははっ。ドンチュノウ？　知らないのかい？
English: Ho hoh. You are not cogniferous of my background?

Japanese: ぼかぁ、情報を集めるのが仕事なんだよ。
English: Gathering information is my business, you see.

Japanese: 油断‥‥したわ。
English: I... I should have been more careful.

Japanese: ‥‥フン。
English: Ho hoh.

Japanese: それと‥‥ちょっと気の毒なんだけど、
English: My dear Miss Fey... I am so very sorry.

Japanese: キミには、約束してもらわなきゃならないことがあるんだ。
English: But I am afraid I must ask you for one more thing.

Japanese: エターナル?サイレンス‥‥永遠の沈黙、をね。
English: Your eternal silence... Farewell, Miss Fey.
Red... White... Blue...

Japanese: ９月　５日　午後９時　８分綾里法律事務所
English: September 5, 9:08 PM Fey & Co. Law Offices

Japanese: ふう。すっかり遅くなっちまった。
English: Uh oh, I'm late.

Japanese: ‥‥おかしいな。所長、帰っちゃったのかな。
English: Huh, that's strange. The chief must have gone home already.

Japanese: 妹さんが遊びにくるから、ゴハン食べに行こうって‥‥。
English: She said her sister was coming over so we should all go out for dinner...

Japanese: ‥‥‥...なんだ‥‥このニオイ‥‥。
English: What's that smell...?
Blood...?

Japanese: ち、千尋さん！（所長室か！）
English: Mia! (Maybe she's in her office!)

Japanese: ‥‥！...!（さ、さっきの女の子は‥‥どこだ！）
English: (That girl just now... where'd she go!?)

Japanese: （そこのソファに寝かせておいたのに‥‥！）
English: (I put her right there on that sofa...!)

Japanese: （まさか‥‥逃げた、のか？）
English: (Uh oh... I hope she didn't run on me.)

Japanese: うわっ！
English: Yipes!

Japanese: （‥‥ふう。　おどかすなよ‥‥）
English: (Don't scare me like that...)

Japanese: ‥‥あのさ。
English: Umm...

Japanese: 名前‥‥教えてくれないかな？
English: Excuse me but, who are you?

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥...だいじょうぶ。ぼくは、この事務所の人間だから。
English: It's okay. I work here.

Japanese: マヨイ‥‥。
English: Maya...

Japanese: アヤサト?マヨイ。
English: Maya Fey.

Japanese: あやさと‥‥まよい‥‥。
English: Maya... Fey?

Japanese: （“マヨイ”だって‥‥？）
（千尋さんが書き残したのは、この子のことだったのか‥‥？）
English: (So Mia was writing this girl's name!)

Japanese: （ちょっとこの子に、あの領収書をつきつけてみよう）
English: (Maybe I should show her the receipt?)

Japanese: （法廷で覚えたテクニックが、実生活で役に立つとは‥‥）
English: (I never thought there'd be a use for evidence like this outside the courtroom!)

Japanese: ファン‥‥ファン‥‥‥‥ファン‥‥ファン‥‥
English: *whee-ooo* *whee-ooo*... *whee-ooo* *whee-ooo*...

Japanese: ！!パ、パトカーだ！（ここへ向かってるみたいだ‥‥）
English: The police! (Sounds like they're coming this way!)

Japanese: 動くな！　警察だ！
English: Freeze! Police!

Japanese: 自分は、刑事課のイトノコギリという者ッス。
English: Alright, I'm Detective Dick Gumshoe, see?

Japanese: （イトノコギリ‥‥ヘンな名前だな）
English: (Gumshoe...? What an odd name.)

Japanese: このビルの向かいのホテルの客から、
English: We received a report from the building across the way, see.

Japanese: 殺人を目撃したという通報が入ったので、かけつけたッス。
English: Got a person saying they saw a murder.

Japanese: （‥‥そうか。あの女の人だな）
English: (It must have been that woman I saw.)

Japanese: とりあえずアンタたち、そこを動いちゃダメッスよ。
English: Anyway, I don't want either of you moving one inch, 'kay?

Japanese: （‥‥やれやれ‥‥）
English: (Great. Just great.)

Japanese: （マヨイ‥‥ちゃんか。まさか、この子が‥‥？）
English: (Maya... Wait, she wouldn't have... nah.)

Japanese: うおおおおおおおおおおおおおッ！
English: Whoaaaaaaaaaaaah!

Japanese: ちょっといいッスかッ！
English: Scuze me!

Japanese: きゃっ！
English: Eek!

Japanese: この“マヨイ”という文字に心当たりはないッス？
English: This word "Maya" here mean anything to you?
Um... that...

Japanese: あ‥‥あたしの名前‥‥
English: That's my name...

Japanese: なんスとぉぉぉッ！
English: WHAAAAT!?

Japanese: これは、ヒガイシャが血で書いたメモッス！
English: The victim drew this here note in her own blood, see?

Japanese: 息を引き取るまぎわ、ハンニンを告発したッス！
English: With her dying breath, she wrote down the killer's name!

Japanese: あ‥‥あたしじゃありま
English: K-killer...? I'm not--

Japanese: こりゃもう、キマリッス！署まで来てもらうッス！
English: Case closed! You're coming down to the precinct, ma'am.
W-what?

Japanese: 千尋さんの妹?真宵ちゃんは、緊急逮捕された。
English: Mia's younger sister, Maya, was arrested on the spot.

Japanese: ぼくも取り調べを受けて、解放されたのは、明け方だった。
English: I was taken in for questioning and didn't get out until the next morning.

Japanese: まぶたは重かったが、眠れるわけがない。
English: My eyes were heavy... but I couldn't sleep.

Japanese: 留置所の面会が始まる時間を待って、
English: I sat around, waiting for visiting hours to begin at the detention center.

Japanese: ぼくは、すぐに真宵ちゃんに会いに行った。
English: I had to talk to Maya as soon as possible.

Japanese: （‥‥とにかく、早く警察を呼ぼう！）
English: (I'd better hurry up and call the police!)

Japanese: 所長室へ通じるドアだ。半開きになっている。
English: The door to the chief's office. It's slightly open.

Japanese: ドアノブには触れない方がいいだろう。
English: I'd better not touch the door knob.

Japanese: 受付に使っているデスクだ。いつもぼくが座っている。
English: The reception desk. I usually sit here.

Japanese: 来客用のソファだ。革張りの高級品らしい。
English: This sofa is for clients. It's leather... a real luxury model.

Japanese: 大きな絵画だ。モダン?アートというのだろうか。
English: A large painting. I guess you'd call this "modern art."

Japanese: ぼくには、ただの失敗作にしか見えない。
English: I, on the other hand, call it a mistake.

Japanese: 法律関係の本が、イヤになるほどビッシリ並んでいる。
English: You couldn't cram more legal books in here, even if you wanted to.

Japanese: 事務所を訪れる客たちは、かならずこの本棚にビビる。
English: Few can gaze upon the shelves without feeling insignificant.

Japanese: 小さなライティングデスクには、事務用品が整然と並んでいる。
English: A small writing desk cluttered with office supplies.

Japanese: ‥‥今は、この血のニオイが気になる！
English: I smell blood... and that can't be good!

Japanese: 千尋さん‥‥所長の無事をたしかめないと！
English: I have to check and see if Mia... the Chief's okay!

Japanese: 名前、教えてくれないかな。
English: Excuse me but, who are you?

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥...だいじょうぶ。ぼくは、この事務所の人間だから。
English: It's okay. I work here.

Japanese: マヨイ‥‥。
English: Maya...

Japanese: あやさと?まよい。
English: Maya Fey.

Japanese: （“マヨイ”だって‥‥？）
（千尋さんが書き残したのは、この子のことだったのか‥‥？）
English: (So Mia was writing this girl's name!)

Japanese: （ちょっとこの子に、あの領収書をつきつけてみよう）
English: (Maybe I should show her the receipt...)

Japanese: （法廷で覚えたテクニックが、実生活で役に立つとは‥‥）
English: (I never thought there'd be a use for evidence like this outside the courtroom!)

Japanese: （かなり弱っているみたいだけど、聞かないわけにはいかないな‥）
English: (She seems to be in shock. I don't want to disturb her, but I have to know...)

Japanese: あの‥‥きみ。
English: Um... excuse me?

Japanese: いったい、何があったんだい？
English: Can you tell me what happened?

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥...‥‥あたしが来たら、部屋が暗くて‥‥
English: I came in... The room was dark.

Japanese: もう、お姉ちゃんは‥‥
English: And Sis... Sis...!

Japanese: （亡くなった後だった、というわけか）
English: (So she was already dead.)

Japanese: きみは、所長の‥‥？
English: So, you're the chief's...?

Japanese: ‥‥妹です。
English: Sister. I'm her younger sister.

Japanese: こんな時間に‥‥遊びに来たの？
English: And you were here... visiting? This late at night?

Japanese: はい‥‥。証拠品をあずかって欲しい、って。
English: Yes. She said she wanted me to keep some evidence for her.

Japanese: 証拠品‥‥って？
English: Evidence...?

Japanese: はい‥‥。あ、あの‥‥、
English: Yes... I-it was that clock...

Japanese: 《考える人》の置時計を‥‥。
English: It was "The Thinker."

Japanese: ‥‥すみません。特に心当たり、ないです。
English: I'm sorry, I've never seen that before.

Japanese: これ、所長のそばに散らばってたんだけど‥‥
English: This was lying next to the chief.

Japanese: はい。あたしも見ました。
English: I know. I saw it there too.

Japanese: たぶん、電気スタンドのカケラかな、って。
English: I thought they might be pieces of the light stand.

Japanese: うん。たぶんね。
English: Hmm. Maybe.

Japanese: （でも、ガラス製の電気スタンドなんて、めずらしいな‥‥）
English: (Never heard of a glass light stand before...)

Japanese: あの、《考える人》のことなんだけど‥‥
English: Um, about "The Thinker"...

Japanese: ！!い、いや！
English: The Th-Thinker...

Japanese: ‥‥お姉ちゃんは、あれで‥‥！
English: That was... Oh, Sis! *sob*

Japanese: （今、凶器のことを聞くのはさすがにヒドいな‥‥）
English: (Hmm, probably shouldn't have asked her about the murder weapon...)

Japanese: 千尋さん、亡くなる前に自分の血でメッセージを‥‥
English: Before Mia died, she wrote a message with her own blood.

Japanese: 領収書のウラにね。
English: She wrote it on the back of this receipt.

Japanese: ！!あ、あ、あたしの名前！
English: Th-th-that's MY name!

Japanese: ど、どうして！お姉ちゃんが‥‥？　どうして！
English: W-why!? Why would she write my name?

Japanese: お、落ち着いてくれ！
English: Please, just calm down.

Japanese: なんで‥‥お姉ちゃんが、あたしを‥‥？
English: W-why would Sis write my name?

Japanese: （う。そ、そんな目で見られてもなあ‥‥）
English: (Uh-oh. Now I've done it...)

Japanese: ９月　６日　某時刻綾里法律事務所
English: September 6 Fey & Co. Law Offices

Japanese: （事務所は警官たちでいっぱいだ‥‥）
English: (The office is filled with police officers.)

Japanese: （みんな、忙しそうに動きまわっているぞ‥‥）
English: (They're all busily searching for clues...)

Japanese: コラ！
English: Hey! You there!

Japanese: ここは立入禁止ッス！
English: This is a crime scene, pal! No trespassing!

Japanese: ‥‥ん？たしかアンタは‥‥、
English: Umm... Sorry, don't I know you from somewhere?

Japanese: ヤッパリくん‥‥だったッス？
English: Wait, you're that Butz guy, aren't you!

Japanese: ナルホドです！（アイツと間違えないでくれ！）
English: No, no, Phoenix Wright. (How could anyone mistake me for Larry!?)

Japanese: あ、そうそう。なんかそんな名前だったッス！
English: Ah, guess I got the wrong name, Mr. Wright. Sorry 'bout that.

Japanese: “ヤッパリ”は、たしか殺人犯の名前だったッス。
English: That Butz guy, he was a killer! And you're no killer! Right?

Japanese: （アイツたしか、ムザイになったはずだけど‥‥）
English: (He WAS proven innocent...)

Japanese: えーと。そういうあなたは、たしか‥‥
English: Umm... right. And you were...

Japanese: イトノコ刑事‥‥でしたよね。
English: Um... Gumshoe, wasn't it? Dick Gumshoe?

Japanese: そッス。イトノコッス‥‥。
English: Right! At your service.

Japanese: ってアンタ！　人の名前、勝手に縮めちゃダメッス！
English: Hang on! That's Detective Gumshoe to you, pal!

Japanese: えーと、カメノコ刑事‥‥でしたっけ。
English: Um... Suedeshoes, wasn't it?

Japanese: そッス。カメノコッス‥‥。
English: That's me! Don't step on my blue suede shoes...

Japanese: って、それはタワシッス！
English: Wait! That's a song, pal!

Japanese: 自分は、イトノコ‥‥
English: My name's Dick Gumshoe...

Japanese: いやいやいや。
English: W-wait!

Japanese: そもそも、人の名前を勝手に縮めちゃダメッス！
English: That's Detective Gumshoe to you, pal!

Japanese: えーと、ノコノコ刑事‥‥でしたっけ？
English: Um... Gumtree, was it? Detective Gumtree?

Japanese: ノ、ノコノコ‥‥。
English: G-Gumtree...?

Japanese: ‥‥アンタ、ちょっとおふざけがすぎたッス‥‥。
English: Gum doesn't grow on trees, pal!

Japanese: 本官をナメると、イタい目にあうッスよ‥‥！
English: And I'm the one in charge here so don't push your luck!

Japanese: （うわあ、急に自分のコト“本官”とか言い出したよ‥‥）
English: (Whatever you say, Detective...)

Japanese: 自分は、糸鋸　圭介（いとのこぎりけいすけ）ッス！
English: Anyway, get the name right.

Japanese: なんぴとたりとも、自分を“イトノコ”なんて‥‥
English: And don't go calling me "Dick"...

Japanese: おい、イトノコ！こっち、早くたのむよ！
English: Hey, Dick! Get over here!

Japanese: ハ、ハッ！
English: Y-yes, sir!

Japanese: す、すぐ行くッス！
English: B-be right there!

Japanese: あー、オホン！
English: Umm... ahem!

Japanese: たしかアンタ、弁護士だったッスな。
English: You're her lawyer, right, pal?

Japanese: 用なら、早くしてほしいッス！
English: If you got business here, you'd better do it quick!

Japanese: （しめた！　ぼくを真宵ちゃんの弁護士だと思っているぞ‥‥！）
English: (Whew! He thinks I'm Maya's lawyer...!)

Japanese: （‥‥だいたい、聞くべきことはおさえたかな‥‥）
English: (I guess I've asked all the questions I need to.)

Japanese: もういいッス？
English: You all done, pal?

Japanese: あ。ありがとうございました。‥‥そろそろ、帰ります。
English: Um, yes, thank you. I'll be heading out now.

Japanese: ‥‥あ、そうそう。
English: Oh, wait.

Japanese: あんたに、１つ言っておくッス。
English: One more thing I wanted to mention to you.

Japanese: おおかた、これからあの目撃証人に会おうとか、
English: I don't suppose you're planning on talking to that witness.

Japanese: そんなフラチなことを考えてるかもしれないッスが、
English: Anyway, you'd better not!

Japanese: 会ってもムダッスよ！
English: No influencing the witness with your lawyerly ways, pal!

Japanese: ‥‥...（そういえば、彼女のコト、忘れてたな‥‥）
English: (Come to think of it, I had completely forgotten about her...)

Japanese: 目撃証人、ですか‥‥？
English: The... witness?

Japanese: 松竹　梅世（しょうちくうめよ）のことッス！
English: Yeah, Miss April May.

Japanese: あいにくッスが、
English: I'm sorry 'bout this...

Japanese: 自分は、彼女の情報はゼッタイもらさないッス！
English: But I can't tell you anything about her!

Japanese: （名前は松竹　梅世、か）
English: (Well, you just told me her name. Miss May, huh?)

Japanese: もう彼女、家に帰らせてあげたんですよね？
English: So you've sent her home already, then?

Japanese: ワレワレをナメちゃダメッス。
English: Ahah! You're trying your lawyerly tricks on me now!

Japanese: 彼女には、裁判が終わるまで、勝手な外出は禁止してるッス！
English: She's not to go outside her room until the trial!

Japanese: （‥‥てことは、まだ彼女はこの向かいのホテルにいる、と）
English: (So... she's still in the hotel across the way.)

Japanese: はあ。さすが警察ですね。
English: I guess I should know better than to try to get a detective to leak information.

Japanese: わかればいいッス！
English: You got that right, pal!

Japanese: （よし！　ホテルにも行ってみよう！）
English: (Time to pay a visit to Miss May!)

Japanese: 所長室へ通じるドアだ。
English: The door to the chief's office.

Japanese: ドアの向こうでは、警官たちが捜査をしているようだ。
English: The police are in there searching for clues.

Japanese: ‥‥入らない方がいいな。
English: I'd better not go in.

Japanese: 千尋さんお気に入りの観葉植物だ。
English: Mia's favorite potted plant.
