English: Uh... uh... uwaaaaaaah!

Japanese: （今、法廷中の人間が、コイツの素顔を見た！）
English: (That did it! The court's seen the real Miss April May now!)

Japanese: （トドメを刺すなら、今だ！）
English: (Now to deal the final blow!)

Japanese: （トドメを刺すなら、今だ！）
English: (Now to deal the final blow!)

Japanese: 梅世さん！
English: Miss May!

Japanese: な、何よこのタコ！気やすく話しかけるんじゃないよ！
English: What is it you little shrimp! Talk to me in that tone of voice will you!?

Japanese: あなたがやったんですね？
English: You killed her, didn't you!

Japanese: 静粛に！‥‥静かにしてください！
English: Order! There will be order!

Japanese: な、なんでそうなるのよ！
English: What? How can you possibly say that!? Are you mad?

Japanese: 梅世は、盗聴してただけ‥‥
English: A-all I did was a little wiretap...

Japanese: ‥‥あっ！
English: Oops!

Japanese: 盗聴は認めるわけですね？
English: So you admit you tapped her phone!

Japanese: ‥‥く、くくくぅぅぅ‥‥！
English: Heh... heh... hrrah!

Japanese: で、でもねぇ！
English: B-but wait!

Japanese: 殺人なんてヒドいコト、梅世はしてないんだからね！
English: I didn't do ANYTHING bad like murder! I'm a good girl!

Japanese: じゃあ！
English: Really?

Japanese: ‥‥それを証明できますか？（できるワケ、ないだろう‥‥）
English: Can you prove it!? (No way can she prove it!)

Japanese: カッコつけてんじゃないよ！
English: You think you're so smart, Mr. Lawyer...

Japanese: できるわよ！決まってんでしょ！
English: But I can prove it! And I will!

Japanese: そ、そんなバカな！
English: You can't be serious! No way!

Japanese: ハン！　サムいオトコね。いまどき“そんなバカな！”って！
English: Way, I say! Way! Oh, and I assure you I'm serious, Mr. Lawyer!

Japanese: ‥‥えへん！
English: Hmph!

Japanese: いい？　事件があったの、夜の９時ごろでしょ？
English: Okay? So, the killing happened around 9:00 at night?

Japanese: 梅世、そのときボーイさんからルームサービスを受け取ったの。
English: Why, that's just when I was getting room service from that sweet bellboy...

Japanese: る、るうむさーびす？
English: R-room service!?

Japanese: 梅世、アイスコーヒーをたのんだの。
English: Ice coffee, I believe it was?

Japanese: 知ってる？　アイスコーヒー。
English: Ice coffee? You know? Like normal coffee, but COLD.

Japanese: 早く飲まないと、氷が溶けてただのコーヒーになっちゃうの。
English: If you don't drink it quick, the ice melts and then you have... regular cold coffee.

Japanese: あ、あいすこぉひぃ？
English: I-ice coffee...!?

Japanese: ウソだと思うんなら、ボーイさんに聞いてみたらぁ？
English: Think I'm making this up? Ask the bellboy! *wink*

Japanese: つまり彼女は、現場に行っていないということだ。
English: Ergo, the witness was not on the scene at the time of the murder!

Japanese: ‥‥！!すると、どういうことに‥‥？
English: So, where does that leave us...?

Japanese: まことにイカンながら‥‥、
English: It is my great displeasure to inform you...

Japanese: 証人はどうやら、被害者の電話を盗聴していたらしい。
English: That the witness appears to have been tapping the victim's telephone.

Japanese: しかし！　それはこの殺人事件とはまったく関係ない、別の犯罪だ。
English: However! That is a separate crime, with no bearing on the current case whatsoever!

Japanese: 被告人?綾里　真宵の殺人を目撃したことは事実なのだッ！
English: Her testimony stands! She saw the defendant, Maya Fey, commit murder!

Japanese: （くそ‥‥このままじゃ逃げられる‥‥！）
English: (No! They're going to let her just walk away!)

Japanese: （梅世と殺人事件を結びつけられないかぎり、勝ち目はない‥‥）
English: (There's no way I can win this unless I tie Miss May to the murder somehow...)

Japanese: 弁護人。‥‥どうしますか？
English: Well, does the defense have anything to say?

Japanese: え、ええと‥‥。（すると、残された手は‥‥？）
English: Um, well... (Come on, think of something!)

Japanese: ルームサービスを運んだ、ボーイを尋問させてください！
English: The defense would like to call the hotel bellboy as a witness!

Japanese: そこに何か、トリックがあるはずです！
English: There's something suspicious there, and I'm going to get to the bottom of it!

Japanese: うわ。やっぱりサムいヒトぉ。いまどき“トリック”って‥‥。
English: I think you've sunken quite low enough already.

Japanese: 私は、その尋問に反対する！
English: I object to calling the bellboy!

Japanese: な、なぜですか！その理由は！
English: W-why? What's your reason?

Japanese: “盗聴は、殺人とは関係ない”というのが、私の考えだからだ。
English: Because I hold that the wiretapping had nothing to do with the killing!
However...

Japanese: キミが１つ、条件を飲むなら、ボーイの尋問に同意してもよい。
English: If you agree to one condition, I'll consent to calling this witness.

Japanese: 条件‥‥？
English: Condition...?

Japanese: ボーイを尋問した結果、松竹　梅世のアリバイがくずせなかった場合、
English: If Miss April May's alibi is not called into question after you examine the bellboy...

Japanese: 当然、松竹　梅世は犯人ではないということになる。
English: Then you will recognize that Miss April May was not the killer, thus she is innocent!

Japanese: その場合は、きみは綾里　真宵の《有罪》を認める。
English: Therefore you must accept the verdict of "guilty" for Ms. Maya Fey!

Japanese: ‥‥それが条件だ。
English: That is my condition.

Japanese: （なんだそりゃあ！）
（ボーイの証言から、何かを引き出せなければ‥‥）
English: (I'd better find something suspicious in that bellboy's testimony...)

Japanese: （真宵ちゃんは、その場で《有罪》というワケか‥‥）
English: (Otherwise Maya will be declared "guilty" on the spot!)

Japanese: （‥‥どうする？）
English: (What should I do...?)

Japanese: （えーい、とりあえずやってみるか！）
English: (Alright! I've got nothing to lose! Except for... well, everything!)

Japanese: わかりました。条件を飲みましょう！
English: Understood. I accept your condition.

Japanese: ‥‥フッ。
English: Hmph.

Japanese: バカが‥‥。ワナにかかったか。
English: Fool... You fell right into my trap!

Japanese: （‥‥なんだって！）
English: (Uh oh!)

Japanese: あ、あの、やっぱり‥‥
English: Uh... um, wait...

Japanese: （‥‥くそっ！　さすがにそんな条件は飲めない！）
English: (Grr! I can't accept those conditions!)

Japanese: ‥‥わかりました。ボーイの尋問はやめます。
English: Very well. The defense will refrain from calling the bellboy as a witness.

Japanese: では引きつづき、この証人に対する尋問をつづけてください。
English: I see. You may continue your cross-examination, Mr. Wright.

Japanese: わかりました。尋問をつづけます。
English: Right. On with the cross-examination.

Japanese: ‥‥しかし。これ以上何を聞くというのか？
English: What exactly do you have left to examine, Mr. Wright?

Japanese: 松竹　梅世は盗聴を認めている。
English: Miss April May has admitted to the wiretap, yes.

Japanese: だが、それは殺人とは関係のないことだ。
English: But that bears no relevance to the case at hand: murder!

Japanese: 関係があることは証明できないからな！
English: There's no way you can prove any connection!

Japanese: （そうなると‥‥もう、材料がないぞ！）
English: (Uh oh! Think! This can't be the end... but I'm out of evidence!)

Japanese: では、この証人に対する尋問は、終了ということになりますね。
English: Then I believe the cross- examination is over.

Japanese: 御剣検事。次の証人はいますか？
English: Mr. Edgeworth, does the prosecution have any other witnesses to call?

Japanese: ‥‥いいや。これが最後の証人、だ。
English: None, Your Honor. She's the last.

Japanese: （なんだって！このままじゃ、真宵ちゃんは）
English: (What!? B-but that means...)

Japanese: （間違いなく《有罪》だ！）
English: (Maya's guilty...!?)

Japanese: ま、待ってください！
English: W-wait! Your Honor!

Japanese: なんですか？　弁護人。
English: Yes, Mr. Wright?

Japanese: やっぱり、ボーイの尋問を‥‥やります！
English: The defense would like to call the bellboy after all!
Tsk tsk tsk...

Japanese: そう来ると思ったよ。
English: As I thought!

Japanese: ‥‥？...?しかし。もしボーイを尋問した結果、
English: May I remind you, dear Mr. Wright. Should you question the bellboy...

Japanese: 松竹　梅世のアリバイをくずせなかった場合は‥‥
English: and Miss April May's alibi prove to be solid...

Japanese: 自動的に、綾里　真宵が《有罪》ということになる。
English: then, by default, your client Ms. Maya Fey will be pronounced "guilty"!

Japanese: それで、かまわないな！
English: Are you prepared to accept my condition?
(Edgeworth...)

Japanese: （完全に追いつめられてしまったみたいだ‥‥）
English: (He's got me backed into a corner...)

Japanese: （しかし、他にもう道はない‥‥）
English: (But I don't see any other way to take this!)

Japanese: かまいません！
English: I accept!

Japanese: よろしい！
English: Very well!

Japanese: では、ホテルのボーイを入廷させてください！
English: The court calls the hotel bellboy to the stand!

Japanese: なぜ、盗聴を‥‥？
English: Why did you tap her phone?
Answer the question!

Japanese: どうして答えなくちゃいけないのぉ？
English: Do I have to?

Japanese: 殺人事件とはムカンケイでしょお？
English: Isn't this a murder trial? Isn't tippity-tapping er... irrelevant?

Japanese: （くそ‥‥御剣から指示されたとおりに答えているな‥‥！）
English: (Gah! She's saying exactly what Edgeworth wants her to say.)

Japanese: いいですか。
English: Miss May.

Japanese: あなたは殺人事件の被害者を盗聴していたんですよ？
English: You were tapping the victim's phone!

Japanese: ものすごくアヤシイじゃないですか！
English: I hardly call that "irrelevant"!

Japanese: 弁護人の言い方は気になりますが、気持ちはわかります。
English: While this court does not condone the defense's tone of speech, he has a point.

Japanese: どうなのですか？　証人。
English: Well, Miss May? Do you have an explanation for the court?

Japanese: あなたが殺人とは無関係だと、証明はできませんか‥‥？
English: Can you prove you had nothing to do with this murder, even though you tapped her phone?

Japanese: （フン、できるわけないさ！）
English: (Hah! I'd like to see her pull THAT off!)

Japanese: 弁護士さん、なぁにそのカオ？
English: Mr. Lawyer, I saw that evil, evil grin!

Japanese: “フン、できるわけないさ！”とか思ってるんでしょ？
English: You were probably thinking "I'd like to see her pull THAT off," weren't you!

Japanese: （むむ！　やるな‥‥）
English: (Damn... she's good!)

Japanese: ザンネンでしたぁ！‥‥できるに決まってんでしょ！
English: Well you're not the first man who's thought that! And of course... I can, and will!

Japanese: 梅世さん‥‥。あなたがやったんですね？
English: Miss May, confess. You did it, didn't you?

Japanese: な、バカなこと言わないでよ！
English: Are you out of your mind!? Oh wait, I forgot... you're a lawyer! You must be!

Japanese: 事件が起こった時間、
English: At the time of the murder...

Japanese: 梅世はルームサービスを受け取っていたんだから！
English: I was in my hotel room, getting room service!

Japanese: 梅世にできるワケ、ないでしょ！
English: How could I have killed her?

Japanese: なんだったら、ボーイさんに聞いてみてよ！
English: If you don't believe me, just ask the bellboy!

Japanese: 梅世さん‥‥。なぜ、盗聴を？
English: Miss May... Why did you tap her phone?

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥。...答えてください！
English: Answer the question!

Japanese: どうして答えなくちゃいけないのぉ？
English: Do I have to?

Japanese: 殺人事件とはムカンケイでしょお？
English: Isn't this a murder trial? Isn't tippity-tapping er... irrelevant?

Japanese: （くそ‥‥御剣から指示されたとおりに答えているな‥‥！）
English: (Gah! She's saying exactly what Edgeworth wants her to say.)

Japanese: いいですか。
English: Miss May.

Japanese: あなたは殺人事件の被害者を盗聴していたんですよ？
English: You were tapping the victim's phone!

Japanese: ものすごくアヤシイじゃないですか！
English: I hardly call that "irrelevant"!

Japanese: 弁護人の言い方は気になりますが、気持ちはわかります。
English: While this court does not condone the defense's tone of speech, he has a point.

Japanese: どうなのですか？　証人。
English: Well, Miss May? Do you have an explanation for the court?

Japanese: では、質問を始めさせていただく。
English: I believe we're ready for the witness to testify.

Japanese: ‥‥いかにも、ボーイのイメージに忠実な男だ‥‥。
English: He certainly does look like a bellboy.

Japanese: ハイ。
English: Yes, sir.

Japanese: 仕事中にご招待をいただいて、参上したしだいでございます。
English: I received your summons in the middle of work, sir. I'm happy to be of service.

Japanese: では、ティーセットも重いでしょうから、
English: That tea set looks rather heavy, so without further ado,

Japanese: さっそく《証言》をおねがいします。
English: the witness may begin his testimony.

Japanese: かしこまりました。
English: Very good, sir!

Japanese: ～梅世のルームサービスについて～
English: -- Miss May's Room Service --

Japanese: ワタクシ、板東ホテルのボーイ長をしております。
English: I am the head bellboy at the fine Gatewater Hotel, in business for four generations!

Japanese: あれは、夜の８時すぎでしたか。梅世サマからお電話がありました。
English: I believe I received a call after 8:00 in the evening from our guest, Miss May.

Japanese: きっかり９時に、アイスコーヒーを持ってきてほしいと。
English: She asked for an ice coffee to be brought to her at 9:00, on the dot, sir.

Japanese: それでワタクシ、きっかり９時に持っていったしだいでございます。
English: I brought it to her at precisely the requested time, of course.

Japanese: アイスコーヒーは、間違いなくそのご婦人にお渡しいたしました。
English: And I delivered the ice coffee to our guest Miss May, herself.

Japanese: では、弁護人。《尋問》を‥‥。
English: I see. The defense may begin its cross-examination.

Japanese: は、はい。わかりました！
English: R-right! I'm ready. (I hope...)

Japanese: （このボーイの尋問で、）
English: (This is it...)

Japanese: （梅世が殺人に関係している可能性を証明できないと、）
English: (If I can't prove Miss May was involved with the murder now...)

Japanese: （真宵ちゃんは‥‥オシマイだ！）
English: (Maya will be finished!)

Japanese: ～梅世のルームサービスについて～
English: -- Miss May's Room Service --

Japanese: ワタクシ、板東ホテルのボーイ長をしております。
English: I am the head bellboy at the fine Gatewater Hotel, in business for four generations!

Japanese: あれは、夜の８時すぎでしたか。梅世サマからお電話がありました。
English: I believe I received a call after 8:00 in the evening from our guest, Miss May.

Japanese: きっかり９時に、アイスコーヒーを持ってきてほしいと。
English: She asked for an ice coffee to be brought to her at 9:00, on the dot, sir.

Japanese: それでワタクシ、きっかり９時に持っていったしだいでございます。
English: I brought it to her at precisely the requested time, of course.

Japanese: アイスコーヒーは、間違いなくそのご婦人にお渡しいたしました。
English: And I delivered the ice coffee to our guest Miss May, herself.

Japanese: （‥‥このボーイが、ウソをつく理由はないはずだ‥‥）
English: (This bellboy wouldn't have any reason to lie...)

Japanese: （しかし、この証言から何か見つけないと‥‥！）
English: (But I have to find something to use in his testimony!)

Japanese: （もう一度だ。テッテイ的にゆさぶってやる！）
English: (One more time. I'll press him until he spills the beans... or his tea!)

Japanese: あの、仕事は何をしているんですか？
English: What exactly is it you do at the hotel?

Japanese: なんでもいたしますよ。
English: Why, anything required of me, sir.

Japanese: フロントでチェックイン?チェックアウトの管理から、
English: I check in guests, I check out guests.

Japanese: お部屋のおそうじにベッドメイキング‥‥、
English: I clean rooms, I make beds.

Japanese: ついでにルームサービスもお運びいたします。
English: I even deliver room service, sir.

Japanese: 梅世サマのチェックインもモチロン、ワタクシが。
English: I checked Miss May in personally.

Japanese: いつも、そんな気取ったしゃべり方なんですか？
English: Are you always so... so prim?

Japanese: 弁護人。
English: Mr. Wright.

Japanese: つまらないことは聞かなくてよろしい。
English: You will refrain from asking frivolous questions...

Japanese: 梅世さん本人の声でしたか？
English: Are you sure it was Miss May on the phone?

Japanese: 間違いございません。
English: Absolutely, sir.

Japanese: ど、どうしてそう言いきれるんですか！
English: H-how can you be so certain!?

Japanese: お客様のチェックインは、ワタクシ自身が担当いたしました。
English: I checked Miss May in personally, sir.

Japanese: そのとき、お美しい顔を拝見して、お声も拝聴しております。
English: Not only did I see her in all her stunning radiance, but I also heard her voice.

Japanese: そのときから目をつけて‥‥、いや、その、えへん。
English: And then I saw THEM, and I... Ahem. Er... ahem!

Japanese: とにかく、よく覚えておりました。
English: The point being, I remembered her quite well, sir.

Japanese: それで、どうなりましたか？
English: Yes, what then?

Japanese: “きっかり９時”‥‥ですか？
English: 9:00 "on the dot," you say?

Japanese: ハイ。何度も念を押されました。
English: Yes. I confirmed that detail several times.

Japanese: テレビを見ていたそうで、番組が終わったら飲みたいと‥‥。
English: She was watching a program on the TV, and wished to drink after she finished, sir.

Japanese: （‥‥９時。犯行の時間か）
English: (9:00... the time of the murder!)

Japanese: “きっかり９時”に？
English: "Precisely" 9:00, then?

Japanese: きっかりしっかりはっきりくっきり９時でございました。
English: Precisely, exactly, and most definitely, sir. 9:00 PM.

Japanese: ‥‥その、妙な自信は、いったいどこから‥‥！
English: How can you be so sure!?

Japanese: 梅世サマから、それはもう強く念を押されていましたから。
English: Miss May was quite insistent that it be brought then.

Japanese: “ボーイさぁん、おねがいよぉ。梅世ぉ、９時に飲みたいのぉ”
English: "Oh, bellboy? Tee hee! I'd like, like, ice coffee at exactly 9:00!"

Japanese: ‥‥みたいな感じで。
English: Something like that, sir.

Japanese: それでワタクシ、時報とともにノックしたしだいでございます。
English: Therefore, I knocked on her door at the crack of 9:00, sir.

Japanese: （なんか、作為を感じるな‥‥）
English: (Why would she be so particular about the time?)

Japanese: 松竹　梅世本人に間違いありませんか？
English: You are sure it was Miss April May herself?

Japanese: モチのロンでございます。
English: Ab-SO-lutely, sir.

Japanese: “モチのロン”‥‥？
English: "Ab-SO-lutely"...?

Japanese: “おふこおす”“もちろん”を、ワタクシ風にコジャレてみました。
English: Yes, sir. As in, "so very absolutely," sir. It's an endearing mannerism of mine.

Japanese: ‥‥その、妙な自信は、いったいどこから‥‥！
English: How come you're so very certain!?

Japanese: あの、ルームサービスをお持ちした際にですね‥‥。
English: Well, when I brought the room service, sir...

Japanese: せせ、せ、せっぷんを、その。い、いただいたものですから。
English: S-she... the guest, sir, favored me w-with a, um, an "embrasser," sir.

Japanese: せ、せっぷん？
English: "Embrasser"!? Is that French for "embrace"?

Japanese: ええ。キッスのことです。ホッペのここに、チュッと。
English: It's French for "kiss," sir. But not a french kiss, sir! More of a peck on the cheek.

Japanese: な、な、な、なんでそんなことを‥‥？
English: Wh-why would she have done that...?

Japanese: きっとワタクシに、その。ホの字だったのではないかと。
English: I believe, perhaps, she was momentarily swayed by my prim demeanor, sir.

Japanese: ワタクシ、もう忘れられなくて。
English: It was a moment I shall never, ever forget, sir.

Japanese: （ゼッタイ、不自然だ‥‥）
English: (Sounds pretty fishy to me...)

Japanese: （梅世め、自分を印象づけようと工作していたな‥‥）
English: (I think our Miss May was up to something and wanted the bellboy to remember her!)

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥...‥‥ダメだ！（何も見つからない！）
English: It's no good! (There's nothing there!)

Japanese: （もう、おしまいか‥‥？）
English: (Is... is that it!?)

Japanese: クックックッ。
English: Tsk tsk.

Japanese: やっとわかってもらえたかね。
English: Finally, you understand.

Japanese: このボーイには、ウソをつく理由がないのだよ‥‥。
English: This bellboy has absolutely no reason to lie!

Japanese: ‥‥さて。
English: Now...

Japanese: そろそろ、タイクツな尋問は終わりにしてもらえるかな‥‥？
English: If you have any decency, you will end this rather tedious cross-examination here!

Japanese: そうですね。
English: Hmm. It was a bit tedious.

Japanese: ‥‥では、証人ごくろうさまでした。
English: The witness may leave the stand.

Japanese: （いいのか、このままで！）
English: (I can't let this happen... can I?)

Japanese: （こ、ここまできてあきらめてしまったら‥‥）
English: (If I give up now, I lose everything...)

Japanese: （なんのために弁護士になったかわからないじゃないか‥‥）
English: (If I just give up the case, I'd be giving up the very reason I became a lawyer!)

Japanese: ちょ、ちょっと待ってください！
English: W-wait! Please wait!

Japanese: な、なんですか弁護人。
English: Yes? Does the defense have something to add?

Japanese: さ、最後に１つだけ、質問をさせてください！
English: One last question... let me ask one last question!

Japanese: 今さら何を言う！
English: Your Honor, I must object.

Japanese: そんな、ムダなことを‥‥
English: This charade of justice has gone on long enough!

Japanese: まあまあ、御剣くん。
English: Now, now, Mr. Edgeworth.

Japanese: ‥‥成歩堂くん。１つだけ質問を許します。
English: Alright Mr. Wright. I'll give you one more question, that's all.

Japanese: （これが‥‥）
English: (Okay.)

Japanese: （ほんとうに、これが最後のチャンスだ！）
English: (This is really it, now. This is my last chance!)

Japanese: （何を聞けばいい‥‥！）
English: (What do I ask him about...!?)

Japanese: チェ‥‥チェックインのことを話してください！
English: T-tell me about check-in!

Japanese: 梅世さんがチェックインしたときのことを‥‥
English: Tell me about when you checked-in Miss May.

Japanese: ああ、ハイ。かしこまりました。
English: Oh, alright. Very well, sir.

Japanese: とにかく、キレイな方だな、と。
English: My first thought was that she was a beautiful, beautiful person.

Japanese: お近づきになりたいものだと思いましたが、ザンネンでした。
English: She's just my type of girl, so it was a disappointment, really.

Japanese: そうですか‥‥。
English: I see...

Japanese: ‥‥？?何が“ザンネン”だったんです？
English: Excuse me... what exactly was a "disappointment"?

Japanese: さすがのワタクシも、横にコイビトがいらっしゃっては‥‥。
English: Well, I am not without charm, sir, but even I'd have little chance with her lover there.
(What did he say!?)

Japanese: い、今、なんて言いました！
English: What did you say!?

Japanese: え！　‥‥いや、ワタクシはその‥‥！
English: Ah! Oh... er... rather, quite!

Japanese: ボーイさん！もしかして梅世さんは‥‥、
English: Bellboy! Tell us the truth now...

Japanese: ２人でチェックインしたんですか！
English: Did Miss May check in with another person?

Japanese: も、もう一度、ルームサービスのことを聞かせてください。
English: T-tell me again about er... room service!

Japanese: ま、またでございますか。
English: A-again, sir?

Japanese: きっかり９時に、３０３号室の梅世サマにお届けしました。
English: At exactly 9:00, I delivered room service to Miss May in room 303.

Japanese: 届けたのはアイスコーヒー。１８００円でございます。
English: The guest had requested ice coffee... $18 was the charge, as I recall.

Japanese: ‥‥そうですか‥‥。
English: I see...

Japanese: ‥‥‥？...?せ、１８００円？ずいぶん高いですねえ。
English: E-eighteen dollars? Doesn't that seem a bit expensive?

Japanese: え、ええ、まあ‥‥。２人ぶんでございましたから‥‥。
English: Y-yes, well, ice coffee for two, you know. And we don't skimp on the ice, sir.
(What did he say!?)

Japanese: い、今、なんて言いました！
English: What did you say!?

Japanese: え！　‥‥いや、ワタクシはその‥‥！
English: Ah! Oh... er... rather, quite!

Japanese: ボーイさん！梅世さんの部屋には、
English: Bellboy! Tell us the truth now...

Japanese: ２人、泊まっていたんですか！
English: Was someone else staying in Miss May's room?

Japanese: ベ‥‥ベッドメイキング‥‥
English: Bed... bed making!

Japanese: ベッドメイキングしたときのことを話してください。
English: Tell me about making beds that day.

Japanese: 何を聞くのかと思えば、そんなこと‥‥
English: I was wondering what you were going to ask, but bed making? A new low!

Japanese: まあまあ、御剣くん。
English: Now, now, Mr. Edgeworth.

Japanese: ‥‥証人は、質問に答えなさい。
English: The witness will answer the defense's question.

Japanese: い、いえ。別に。いつもどおりでございましたよ。
English: Yes, well, it was quite like any other day's bed making.

Japanese: シーツとまくらカバーの取りかえ、そしてベッドメイク。
English: I changed the sheets, the pillowcases, and then I proceeded to make the bed.

Japanese: それを２人ぶん。‥‥それだけでございます。
English: I had to bring pillows for two, of course, but they're quite light, you see.

Japanese: そうですか‥‥。ありがとうございます。
English: I see. Thank you.
(Pillows... for two!?)

Japanese: ボーイさん！今、なんて言いました！
English: Bellboy! What did you just say?

Japanese: え！　‥‥いや、ワタクシはその‥‥！
English: Eh!? Ah, yes, pillows are light... sir?

Japanese: もしかして、梅世さんの部屋には‥‥、
English: Bellboy! Tell us the truth now...

Japanese: ２人、いたんですか！
English: Was someone else staying in Miss May's room?

Japanese: と、とりあえず異議を申し立てる！
English: I object! That was... objectionable!

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥...とりあえず、異議は却下します。‥‥答えなさい、証人！
English: Objection overruled. The witness will answer the question.

Japanese: は、はあ。‥‥まあ、そうでございました。
English: Er... yes, I see.

Japanese: なぜ、それを証言しなかったんですか！
English: Why did you not mention this in your testimony!?

Japanese: ま、まあ‥‥そ、その。き、聞かれませんでしたから‥‥。
English: W-well, sir, you er... you didn't ask!

Japanese: （‥‥アヤシイ！）
English: (Nice try!)

Japanese: それにしても、フツー言うもんじゃないですか！
English: That's the sort of thing you're normally supposed to mention!

Japanese: い、いえ、その‥‥実は‥‥。
English: Ah, yes, quite. Indeed...

Japanese: そこの、検事さんに‥‥
English: It was the, er, good barrister there, Mr. Edgeworth, who...

Japanese: ！!聞かれないかぎり、そのことは言うな、と‥‥。
English: He asked me not to mention it if I wasn't specifically asked, sir.

Japanese: く‥‥くぅっ！
English: Oof!

Japanese: この‥‥バカボーイがああああッ！
English: Y-you fool!

Japanese: ‥‥か‥‥勝った‥‥
English: I've done it... I've won!

Japanese: 松竹　梅世は、ツインルームに男性と２人でチェックインした。
English: Miss April May checked into a twin room... with a man.

Japanese: 間違いありませんね？
English: Correct?

Japanese: ハイ。
English: Yes, sir.

Japanese: そして、あなたがルームサービスを運んだとき、
English: Then, when you brought them room service,

Japanese: あなたは、その男を見ていない。
English: you didn't see that man in the room...?

Japanese: そのとおりでございます。
English: That's right, sir.

Japanese: ふむう‥‥。
English: Hmm...

Japanese: 裁判長！
English: Your Honor!

Japanese: この時点で、被告人?綾里　真宵に判決を下すのは不可能です。
English: We have just learned of another person involved who may have been the murderer!

Japanese: なぜなら、犯人のウタガイが強い人物が、他にいるからです。
English: In this new light, I hold that it's impossible to judge the defendant.

Japanese: そうですね？　御剣検事。
English: You agree, Mr. Edgeworth?

Japanese: 誰だッ！　その人物とは！
English: Who! Who is this "other person"!

Japanese: ‥‥その人物とは、
English: Simple, it was...

Japanese: それは、松竹　梅世です！
English: None other than Miss April May!

Japanese: ハッ！
English: Eh!?

Japanese: 弁護人は、今までの話を聞いていなかったのか！
English: Have you heard nothing that has transpired so far!?

Japanese: 事件が起こったときの彼女のアリバイは、成立している！
English: She has an alibi! She was in the hotel at the time of the murder!

Japanese: ‥‥あ。そうでしたね。
English: Oh... right.

Japanese: 弁護人！
English: Mr. Wright!

Japanese: す、すみません！も、もう一度、おねがいします！
English: S-sorry, Your Honor! Give me one more chance!

Japanese: ズバリ、ボーイです！
English: It was the bellboy and none other!

Japanese: そ、それはまた、意外な人物ですな‥‥。
English: Well, this comes as some surprise...

Japanese: いいかね。
English: Your Honor, Mr. Wright...

Japanese: 梅世のアリバイを証明したのはボーイなのだ。
English: It was the bellboy who confirmed Miss May's alibi...

Japanese: ということは、ボーイのアリバイも成立している、ということだ！
English: And this in turn confirms the bellboy's alibi! He was in the hotel!

Japanese: ‥‥そ、そういう考え方もできますね‥‥。
English: Well, s-sure... if you put it that way...

Japanese: ‥‥ぜひ、そういう考え方をしてもらいたいな！
English: I do put it that way, and I trust you will too!

Japanese: でも、２人が共犯だとしたら！
English: But what if they were in cahoots!

Japanese: そんな証拠があるんですか？
English: You have evidence of this?

Japanese: いや、それはないです！
English: Um... no, Your Honor.

Japanese: 梅世といっしょにチェックインした男です！
English: The man who checked in with Miss May!
Oof!

Japanese: いいですか！
English: Your Honor!

Japanese: すでに証明されたとおり、松竹　梅世は盗聴をしていた。
English: As has been previously revealed, Miss April May was tapping the victim's phone.

Japanese: 彼女自身は、犯行の時間にアリバイがある。
English: Yet Miss May herself has an alibi at the time of the murder.

Japanese: しかし！　梅世といっしょだったこの男なら、犯行は可能でした！
English: However, that does not clear the man that was with her!

Japanese: 事件があった時間、ボーイはこの男を見ていません！
English: The bellboy saw no one else in the room at the time of the murder!!!

Japanese: 今さら、そんなツゴウのいい言いぶんが‥‥
English: M-my, what a convenient little setup... but it's too late...
"Too late"?

Japanese: この男の存在を、今まで隠していたのは、
English: I suppose you'd like it if it was too late, wouldn't you...

Japanese: 御剣検事！あなた自身ですよ！
English: After all, it was you who hid the presence of the other man from this court!
Oof!

Japanese: キサマごとき‥‥シロウトに‥‥
English: Upstart... amateur...!

Japanese: この御剣が‥‥‥‥信じられん‥‥。
English: T-these accusations are... ludicrous!

Japanese: そこまで！
English: Enough!

Japanese: ‥‥弁護人の主張を認めます。
English: The court acknowledges the defense's argument.

Japanese: 検察側は、この男について、キチンと調査をするように。
English: I expect the prosecution and defense to look into this matter fully!

Japanese: いいですか！
English: Am I understood?

Japanese: ‥‥了解した。
English: Yes... *gasp* Yes, Your Honor.

Japanese: 本日の綾里　真宵の審理は、ここまでとします。
English: That is all today for the trial of Maya Fey.

Japanese: ‥‥では、本日はこれにて閉廷！
English: Court is adjourned!

Japanese: ９月７日　午後２時２４分地方裁判所　被告人第１控え室
English: September 7, 2:24 PM District Court Defendant Lobby No. 1

Japanese: 弁護士さん！
English: Mr. Wright!

Japanese: スゴい！　カッコよかったです！
English: You were amazing in there!

Japanese: そ、そう？
English: R-really?

Japanese: もうあたし、シビレっぱなしでした！
English: I think I might be your newest fan!

Japanese: いやぁ、あ、そう。ははは。
English: Oh, I was just "doing my job" you know... heh heh.

Japanese: あ、でも。あの検事さんもステキだったかなあ。
English: Then again, that other attorney was pretty cool, too...
Huh?

Japanese: 白目をむいて、クチビルをぶるぶるふるわせた、あの表情。
English: That face of his! With his eyes wide, and trembling lips!

Japanese: グッときちゃいますよね！
English: It sent shivers up my spine!

Japanese: ‥‥そ、そうかなあ。
English: Hmm... if you say so.

Japanese: で、どうなんですかあたし！
English: So, what happens with me?

Japanese: もう、帰れるんですか？
English: Do I get to go home now?

Japanese: い、いや。ダメだと思うよ、まだ。
English: Well, no. I don't think so. Not yet.

Japanese: うう。そうなんですか‥‥。
English: Oh... I see.

Japanese: でも、今日の法廷で、大きな手がかりを見つけたから。
English: But I got a great lead in today's trial!

Japanese: “てがかり”？
English: A "lead"?

Japanese: 梅世といっしょだった男のことだよ、もちろん！
English: That man with Miss May! He's the key!

Japanese: あ！　なるほど‥‥。
English: Oh! I get it.

Japanese: ‥‥そういえば、梅世さん、どうなったんですか？
English: What happened to Miss May after that, anyway?

Japanese: とっつかまったみたい。いいキミだね。
English: I heard they arrested her. I guess she's learning her charms won't work everywhere.

Japanese: 留置所に行けば会えるハズだ。あとで行ってみようかな。
English: She's probably at the detention center now. I may have to go down there later.

Japanese: とにかく。
English: Anyway.

Japanese: 事件はまだ、解決したわけじゃないんだ。
English: This case is far from closed.

Japanese: は、はい！
English: Yes sir!

Japanese: ぼくはあの男のことを調べてみる。
English: I'm going to find out more about this man.

Japanese: そいつが、お姉ちゃんを‥‥？
English: Do you think he was the one who...?

Japanese: ‥‥たぶん、そうだ。
English: Maybe so.
Sis...

Japanese: だいじょうぶ。明日までに、かならず見つける。
English: Don't worry, I'll find him by tomorrow. I promise.

Japanese: ‥‥おねがいします！
English: I'm counting on you!

Japanese: 明日の法廷の武器にするため、
English: I asked for a full record of April May's testimony.

Japanese: ぼくは松竹　梅世の証言をまとめた書類をもらった。
English: I thought it might come in handy during the trial tomorrow.

Japanese: しかし‥‥。
English: But now that I have it, I'm not so sure.

Japanese: 松竹　梅世の証言は結局、ウソばかりだったため、
English: Most of her testimony was all lies...

Japanese: 証言書に書かれていたのは、次の一文だけだった。
English: In fact, there's only one part that got left on the record.

Japanese: 証拠品《梅世の証言書》を法廷記録にファイルした。
English: May Testimony added to the Court Record.

Japanese: ‥‥こんなものが、武器になるのかねえ‥‥？
English: I don't know how much good this will do me at all, now.

Japanese: とにかく、調査にでかけよう！
English: Anyway, time to hit the pavement and do some investigating!

Japanese: いつまでも、真宵ちゃんを留置所に置いとくワケには行かないからな！
English: Maya doesn't belong in that detention center, and it's up to me to get her free!

Japanese: つづく
English: To be continued.

Japanese: そこまで！
English: That's enough!

Japanese: 本法廷は、これ以上の審議の必要性を認めません。
English: This court sees no reason to further prolong the trial.

Japanese: 弁護人の反証は、検察側の主張をくつがえす力を持っていない！
English: The defense has failed to give the court sufficient reason to doubt the prosecution's claim!

Japanese: 被告人、綾里　真宵に、この場で判決を言い渡します。
English: This court finds the defendant, Ms. Maya Fey...

Japanese: 被告人はこれより、その身柄を司直に預けるものとし、
English: The accused will surrender to the court immediately,

Japanese: １ヶ月以内に、高等裁判所において通常裁判を受けることとします。
English: to be held pending trial at a higher court within a month from today's date.

Japanese: では、本日はこれにて閉廷！
English: That is all. The court is adjourned!

Japanese: ９月　７日　某時刻綾里法律事務所
English: September 7 Fey & Co. Law Offices

Japanese: ‥‥どうやら今日は、捜査はしていないみたいだ。
English: Looks like forensics is taking the day off today.

Japanese: イトノコ刑事の姿も見えない。
English: Detective Gumshoe's nowhere in sight.

Japanese: 警察の人たちがさんざん捜査したあとだから、
English: The police really gave this place a working over.

Japanese: 重要な手がかりが残っているとも思えないけど。
English: I doubt there are any valuable clues left.

Japanese: ‥‥まあ、いちおう調べてみるか‥‥。
English: Suppose it can't hurt to take a look around, though.

Japanese: 千尋さんお気に入りの観葉植物だ。
English: Mia's favorite potted plant.

Japanese: これから、ぼくが水をやることになるのか‥‥。
English: I guess I'll have to water it now...

Japanese: 法律関係のムズカシイ本がいやというほど並んでいる。
English: You couldn't cram more legal books in here, even if you wanted to.

Japanese: 所長が手がけた事件の記録も、キチンとファイルされている。
English: All the cases that the chief worked on are filed here.

Japanese: ‥‥何冊か、ぬけているような気もするけど‥‥。
English: Hmm. Are some of the files missing...?

Japanese: まあ、気のせい、かな。
English: Nah, I'm imagining things.

Japanese: ぼくの気持ちとウラハラに、外はいい天気だ。
English: The sky is blue, and so am I...

Japanese: 目の前に、例のホテルが見える。
English: There's that hotel, right across the way.

Japanese: 千尋さんのデスクだ。
English: Mia's desk.

Japanese: いつもどおり、キレイに片づいている。
English: Perfectly clean, as always.

Japanese: 足りないのは、千尋さんの姿だけ、だ‥‥。
English: The only thing it's missing is... Mia.

Japanese: 古い映画のポスターだ。
English: An old movie poster.

Japanese: 千尋さんが初めて泣いた映画なのだそうだ。
English: Apparently, this was the first movie that made Mia cry when she saw it.

Japanese: 今度、ぼくも見てみるか。
English: I'll have to check it out one of these days.

Japanese: 手がかりなるものはない
English: No clues here.

Japanese: この部屋だけを見ていると、
English: It's funny, looking at this room, it seems... so normal.

Japanese: ここで殺人事件があったとは思えない。
English: Hard to imagine a murder took place here.

Japanese: 星影の大センセイは、ここに手がかりがあると言っていた。
English: Mr. Grossberg said there would be clues...

Japanese: ‥‥もう一度、調べてみるか‥‥。
English: Maybe I should have another look.

Japanese: 千尋さんお気に入りの観葉植物だ。
English: Mia's favorite potted plant.

Japanese: これから、ぼくが水をやることになるのか‥‥。
English: I guess I'll have to water it now...

Japanese: 所長が手がけた事件の記録がキチンとファイルされている。
English: All the cases the chief ever worked on are filed here.

Japanese: “あいうえお”順にならんでいる。‥‥ちょっと、調べてみよう。
English: They're in alphabetical order. Let's take a look...

Japanese: どのファイルにするかな‥‥
English: Which file should I look at...?

Japanese: “あ”行で気になる項目は‥‥と。
English: Let's see if there's "A" record in this file that catches my "I."

Japanese: 《綾里　舞子》か。‥‥千尋さんと真宵ちゃんの母親だ。
English: "A"... "B"... "F"! "Misty Fey." That's Mia and Maya's mother.

Japanese: ‥‥読んでみようか？
English: Hmmm... Should I take a look?

Japanese: 今回の事件には、あまりやくに立ちそうもないな。
English: I don't think it has much to do with the current case.

Japanese: 気にはなるけど、まあ、読まなくてもいいだろう。
English: I'd be interested to know what it contains... but I'll leave it alone for now.

Japanese: “『綾里の名を傷つけてしまった』そう言って母は、姿を消した”
English: "'I have tarnished the Fey name.' Leaving only these words, my mother vanished."

Japanese: “その母を傷つけた人物‥‥。私は、彼らを探し出すために”
English: "I was determined to find the ones who had made my mother blame herself in this way."

Japanese: “綾里の霊力を使って、死者たちと語らった”
English: "Using the E.S.P. that runs in my family, I held an audience with the dead."

Japanese: “そしてついに、２人の男の名前をつき止めた”
English: "Finally, the names of two men surfaced."

Japanese: “母の情報を大金で売った、星影　宇宙ノ介弁護士”
English: "One was Marvin Grossberg, a lawyer who sold my mother's information for riches."

Japanese: “そして、その情報をマスコミに売りつけた男”
English: "The other was the man who sold that information to the press."

Japanese: “脅迫をメシのタネにしている寄生虫のような、その男の名は”
English: "This parasite, who makes his fortune on threats and coercion..."
"His name is..."

Japanese: ここから先が、ゴッソリとなくなっている‥‥。
English: Hmm. The record stops there.

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥...千尋さん‥‥星影先生のこと、知ってたんだ。
English: So Mia knew Grossberg...

Japanese: “か”行で気になる項目といえば、そりゃあもう《小中》だな。
English: "T"... "U"... I know, "W"! "White"!

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥。...‥‥！!《こ》の部分が、まるごとなくなっている。
English: The entire "W" section is missing!

Japanese: ‥‥持ち去られたのか‥‥？
English: Was it taken...?

Japanese: “さ”行で思い当たる項目は‥‥
English: Let's see... "J" through "S"...

Japanese: べつに、ない。
English: Nothing much in here.

Japanese: どうしよう。テキトーに読んでみようか‥‥？
English: Maybe I'll just skim some of this?

Japanese: ‥‥めんどくさいな。やめやめ。
English: Nah... this is taking too long. Later.

Japanese: こういうの読むの、ニガテなんだよね‥‥。
English: *yawn* I've never been good at reading these things.

Japanese: ‥‥ううう。
English: *sigh*

Japanese: まあ、いちおう、ザッと目をとおしておくか‥‥。
English: Well, no harm in flipping through a bit, I guess...

Japanese: いちばん大きい項目は、と。“し‥‥自殺”か。‥‥ヤな感じ。
English: The biggest part's here at the end in "S"... "Suicide"? Eww...

Japanese: 政治家や警察官の自殺記事ばっかり、集めてあるな。
English: She has a collection of suicide reports. There's politicians... policemen...

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥...‥‥！...!ほとんどの記事の見出しに、エンピツで文字が書かれている。
English: There's writing on most of these in pencil.

Japanese: ‥‥“小中？”‥‥か。千尋さんの字だな、これは
English: "White"...? This is Mia's handwriting.

Japanese: そうか‥‥。この記事に書かれている人たちは、
English: Wait, I get it! Mia thought he was involved in these "suicides"!

Japanese: みんな、小中の脅迫によって‥‥。
English: White drove them all to...

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥...この新聞記事は、使える！
English: I can use these newspaper clippings.

Japanese: よし‥‥とりあえず、いちばんヒサンなのをチョイスして‥‥と。
English: Hmm... let's find the most disturbing one...

Japanese: 《新聞記事》を法廷記録に挟んだ。
English: Newspaper Clipping added to the Court Record.

Japanese: もう一度、千尋さんのファイルをチェックしてみようかな？
English: Shall I check Mia's files once more?

Japanese: どのファイルを調べようか？
English: Which file to check?

Japanese: かんじんな部分がぬけたファイルを調べても、イミないな。
English: I don't see much point in checking files that are missing all the key parts.

Japanese: はい、やめやめ。
English: *yawn* I give up.

Japanese: “さ”行で思い当たる項目は、ないんだよな。
English: Hmm... not much in the "J-S" section.

Japanese: 自殺の記事を読んでも、気がめいるばかりだし‥‥、
English: I'm not sure reading suicide accounts will be very good for my mood...

Japanese: もう、いいだろう。
English: I think that's enough reading for today.

Japanese: “た”行で気になる項目は‥‥
English: Let's see if there's anything else interesting in the first file...

Japanese: そうそう、《ＤＬ６号事件》！‥‥だけど‥‥。
English: Right, the "DL-6 incident"! Wait...

Japanese: 《て》の部分だけ、スッポリぬけ落ちている！
English: The entire "D" file is missing!

Japanese: 誰かが、持ち去ったらしいな‥‥。
English: I'll bet someone "borrowed" this.

Japanese: ぼくの気持ちとウラハラに、外はいい天気だ。
English: The sky is blue, and so am I...

Japanese: 目の前に、例のホテルが見える。
English: There's that hotel, right across the way.

Japanese: 千尋さんのデスクだ。
English: Mia's desk.

Japanese: いつも通り、キレイに片づいている。
English: Perfectly clean, as always.

Japanese: たりないのは、千尋さんの姿だけ、だ‥‥。
English: The only thing it's missing is... Mia.

Japanese: 古い映画のポスターだ。
English: An old movie poster.

Japanese: 千尋さんが初めて泣いた映画なのだそうだ。
English: Apparently, this was the first movie that made Mia cry when she saw it.

Japanese: 今度、ぼくも見てみるか。
English: I'll have to check it out one of these days.

Japanese: 手がかりなるものはない
English: No clues here.

Japanese: ９月　７日　午後　３時１１分留置所　面会室
English: September 7, 3:11 PM Detention Center Visitor's Room

Japanese: あらあ！
English: Well hello!

Japanese: こーんなムサっ苦しいところまで、わざわざ会いに来てくれたのねぇ！
English: I didn't expect anyone to visit me in such a dank place as this...

Japanese: 梅世、カンゲキしちゃうなぁ。
English: It's really quite... moving.

Japanese: ‥‥って、フザけんじゃないわよ！
English: Not! You stinking lawyer! I hope you die!

Japanese: なんなの？　梅世をワラいに来たってワケ？
English: Have you come to laugh? Yes, laugh at the fallen Miss May!

Japanese: い、いや。ちょっと聞きたいことがあって‥‥。
English: No, not really, there's something I wanted to ask.

Japanese: あいにくこっちには、聞かせたいことなんて、ないの。
English: Unfortunately, there is nothing I want to BE asked.

Japanese: とっとと帰んな！こぉの、ギザギザアタマぁ！
English: Haven't you done enough questioning, you...! Spiky-head!

Japanese: （‥‥やれやれ‥‥）
English: (Here we go again...)

Japanese: ‥‥あそこの看守さんがビビってるよ。
English: Please, you're scaring the security guard.

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥...それでぇ？　弁護士さん。梅世に聞きたいことって、なぁに？
English: So? What is it you wish to ask of me, then? Hmm?

Japanese: （とりあえず、そのネジれた性格のことを聞きたいよ‥‥）
English: (For starters, how did you get to be so totally whacked!?)

Japanese: 面会のようすを監視する看守だ。
English: This guard monitors the visitor's room.

Japanese: さっきから、まったく動かない。さすが、プロだな。
English: He hasn't moved an inch. A real pro, this guy.

Japanese: ‥‥というより、梅世に見とれて動けない、と言うべきかも‥‥。
English: Or maybe he just doesn't get to see a lot of women like Miss May in here.

Japanese: ごていねいに、カメラで見張っているようだ。
English: Smile for the camera...

Japanese: 手がかりなるものはない
English: No clues here.

Japanese: いっしょにホテルに泊まっていた“男”のことなんだけど‥‥
English: About the man who stayed with you in your hotel room...

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥。...とりあえず、そいつの話を聞いてみたいんだ。
English: Can you tell me about him?

Japanese: どこにいるんだ？‥‥教えてくれないかな。
English: Where is he? C'mon...

Japanese: ‥‥やよ。
English: No way, Jose.

Japanese: （‥‥何か聞き出すには、材料が必要だな‥‥）
English: (Hmm... maybe if I had something to get her to talk...)

Japanese: きみたちは、なぜ千尋さんの盗聴を‥‥？
English: Why did you place a wiretap on Mia's phone?

Japanese: あん、やめてよ“盗聴”なんて。人聞きが悪いでしょ。
English: Aww, when you say it like that it sounds so... cold. So criminal!

Japanese: 人のこと盗み聞きしておいて、“人聞きが悪い”もないだろ。
English: Um... tapping people's phones is a crime, Miss May.

Japanese: うわあ。それで、うまいコト言ったつもりなのぉ？
English: Oh, and I suppose you learned that in Lawyer School, hmm?

Japanese: サムぅい。
English: Creep!

Japanese: （ダメだ。‥‥話にならない！）
English: (This woman is impossible to talk to...)

Japanese: あのさ。
English: Say...

Japanese: なんでそんなに、ネジくれた性格をしてるのかな。
English: Why are you so... angry?

Japanese: 見た目はけっこう、その。悪くないのに‥‥。
English: I mean, you don't look like a bad person...

Japanese: うるっさいわね！この、スットコドッコイ！
English: Ooh, that does it! Bottom-feeding, scum-sucking lawyer!

Japanese: ス、スットコ‥‥
English: B-bottom...?

Japanese: （彼女のボキャブラリーにはある種、シビれるものがあるな）
English: (I can't tell, does she have a thing against lawyers... or just against me?)

Japanese: ねぇ、知ってるぅ？
English: Hey, guess what.

Japanese: あたし、あなたのコト、あんまり好きくない？　みたいな。
English: Actually, I, um, really hate your guts.

Japanese: ‥‥協力するワケ、ないじゃなぁい。
English: So get lost. Because, well, I'm not cooperating.

Japanese: （‥‥それは、ごもっとも）
English: (Thanks, I noticed.)

Japanese: うわあ、また来たぁ！しつっこいヒト、きらわれるよぉ？
English: YOU AGAIN!? Can't you take a hint and stay gone?

Japanese: よけいなお世話だよ！きみが話してくれないからだろ！
English: Hey, the only reason I'm back here is because YOU won't talk to me!

Japanese: あ、ひっどぉい！
English: Oh, so it's MY fault now?

Japanese: ギザギザアタマのヒトって、心までギザギザなんだぁ！
English: You don't just have spiky hair, you also have a spiky heart!

Japanese: （この事件が解決したら、ボーズ頭にしようかな‥‥？）
English: (That does it. When this case is done I'm shaving my head.)

Japanese: 面会のようすを監視する看守だ。
English: This guard monitors the visitor's room.

Japanese: さっきから、まったく動かない。さすが、プロだな。
English: He hasn't moved an inch. A real pro, this guy.

Japanese: ‥‥というより、梅世に見とれて動けない、と言うべきかも‥‥。
English: Or maybe he just doesn't get to see a lot of women like Miss May in here.

Japanese: ごていねいに、カメラで見張っているようだ。
English: Smile for the camera...

Japanese: 手がかりなるものはない
English: No clues here.

Japanese: きみがいっしょに泊まっていた“男”のことなんだけど。
English: About the man who stayed with you in your hotel room...

Japanese: 話を聞きたいんだ。‥‥どこにいるのかな？
English: Can you tell me about him? Where is he?

Japanese: 教えなぁい。
English: I'm not telling!

Japanese: 殺人事件だぞ。かばっている場合じゃないだろう？
English: Look, he's being accused of murder! I don't think you want to be protecting him.

Japanese: じゃ、あなた、死んじゃった所長さんのコト、ヒトに売るのぉ？
English: Hmm. Would you have sold out the late Ms. Fey to the cops?

Japanese: ‥‥売らない。
English: No.

Japanese: でしょお？
English: See?
(Damn!)

Japanese: （何か聞き出すには、材料が必要だな‥‥）
English: (Hmm... maybe if I had something to get her to talk...)

Japanese: きみたちは、なぜ千尋さんの盗聴を‥‥？
English: Why did you place a wiretap on Mia's phone?

Japanese: あん、やめてよ“盗聴”なんて。人聞きが悪いでしょ。
English: Aww, when you say it like that it sounds so... cold. So criminal!

Japanese: 人のこと盗み聞きしておいて、“人聞きが悪い”もないだろ。
English: Um... tapping people's phones is a crime, Miss May.

Japanese: うわあ。それで、うまいコト言ったつもりなのぉ？
English: Oh, and I suppose you learned that in Lawyer School, hmm?

Japanese: サムぅい。
English: Creep!

Japanese: （ダメだ。‥‥話にならない！）
English: (This woman is impossible to talk to...)

Japanese: あのさ。
English: Say...

Japanese: なんでそんなに、ネジくれた性格をしてるんだ？
English: Why are you so... angry?

Japanese: 見た目はけっこう、その。悪くないのに‥‥。
English: I mean, you don't look like a bad person...

Japanese: うるっさいわね！この、スットコドッコイ！
English: That does it, you bottom- feeding, scum-sucking lawyer!

Japanese: ス、スットコ‥‥
English: B-bottom...?

Japanese: （彼女のボキャブラリーにはある種、シビれるものがあるな）
English: (I can't tell, does she have a thing against lawyers... or just against me?)

Japanese: ‥‥この写真、どうかな？
English: Any thoughts about this photograph?

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥。...誰それ。あなたのお母さん？
English: Who's that? Your mother?
(*sigh* Looks like I've just been wasting my time.)

Japanese: これ、見てくれないか。
English: Have a look at this.

Japanese: ‥‥だからぁ。梅世、あなたに話すコトなんて、何も‥‥
English: Look, I've said several times, I'm not telling you...
Where did you...?

Japanese: （やっぱり！反応があったぞ‥‥）
English: (Ah hah! A reaction!)

Japanese: この人だね？‥‥梅世さん。
English: This is him, isn't it?

Japanese: な、な、な、何がぁ？
English: What? Who? When? Why...?

Japanese: 決まってるだろう。
English: It IS him.

Japanese: 事件のあった夜、きみのホテルの部屋にいた人物だよ。
English: This is the man who stayed in your hotel room the night of the murder!

Japanese: ち、ち、ち‥‥ちがうわよぅ。
English: No! No, that's not right.

Japanese: （むむ‥‥しぶといぞ‥‥）
English: (Nice try, Miss Cooperative.)

Japanese: そ、そのヒトだったっていう証拠でも、あるの？
English: D-do you have proof that was him? Hmm?

Japanese: そ、そうよ！証拠を見せなさいよぅ！
English: Y-yeah! Proof! Show me proof!

Japanese: （もうひと息だな‥‥）
English: (I'm so close!)

Japanese: この写真だけど‥‥
English: How about this photograph?

Japanese: ‥‥もう、ム?ダ！
English: You're wasting your time!

Japanese: これ以上、言うことなんかないもん！
English: Miss May's lips are sealed!

Japanese: こいつを見てくれないか。
English: Could you have a look at this?

Japanese: ‥‥な、何よそれ？
English: What's that?

Japanese: ホテルのボーイの宣誓書だよ。
English: The bellboy's affidavit! He told us everything he saw.

Japanese: きみといっしょにチェックインした男は‥‥
English: He told us about the man you checked in with.

Japanese: コイツだったってことを、ボーイは証言してくれるそうだ。
English: He told us who he was.

Japanese: ‥‥！...!（よし。あとひと押しだ！）
English: (Now I'm getting somewhere!)

Japanese: （‥‥ここはイチかバチか、ハッタリをかますぞ！）
English: (This is it, all or nothing! Time to do a little bluff.)

Japanese: トボけたってムダだよ！
English: No use playing dumb! (If, indeed, that's an act.)

Japanese: きみがしゃべらないなら、こいつをマスコミに公表してやる！
English: If you don't talk, I'm taking this photo to the press!

Japanese: な、なんですってェ！
English: Whaaaaat!?

Japanese: 殺人事件を目撃したはずなのに、その男は、隠れている。
English: Even though he should be a witness to murder, this man is in hiding.

Japanese: マスコミは“あやしい人物”とかあれこれ書きたてるだろうね！
English: I'm sure the press would have a field day with his reputation!
Ooooh! Fine! I'll talk!

Japanese: ‥‥梅世の‥‥負け‥‥ね。
English: You... you win, Lawyer.

Japanese: （やったぁ！‥‥スカッとしたぞ！）
English: (Yes! Man, that felt good!)

Japanese: （生きててよかったあ！）
English: (It's great to be alive!)

Japanese: 何よ？‥‥そのイヤミなガッツポーズ。
English: Why are you pumping your fists in the air?

Japanese: コホン。
English: *cough*

Japanese: ‥‥じゃ。教えてもらおうか。この男のことを。
English: Now, tell me about the man you were with.

Japanese: その人‥‥梅世の上司。
English: That man... He's my boss.

Japanese: 情報処理会社《コナカルチャー》社長、小中　大（こなかまさる）。
English: Redd White, the president of the information gathering conglomerate, Bluecorp.

Japanese: （コナカ‥‥マサル‥‥ね）
English: (Redd... White?)

Japanese: 情報処理会社、って？
English: "Information gathering?"

Japanese: そうね‥‥。大きな探偵会社、とでも考えればいいわ。
English: Well... I suppose you could call them a detective agency.

Japanese: ‥‥ふうん。
English: Hmm...

Japanese: 事件のあった夜、きみの部屋にいっしょにいたのは、この男だね？
English: So this is the man that was with you the night of the murder?

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥...しゃべるの、コワい‥‥。
English: I'm... I'm scared to talk.

Japanese: ‥‥梅世、あの女弁護士みたいになりたくないよぉ‥‥。
English: I don't want to end up like her!

Japanese: （‥‥‥！）(...!)‥‥わかった。くわしいことは本人から聞くよ。
English: It's okay, I'll just ask Mr. White himself.

Japanese: コナカルチャーの場所を教えてくれないか。
English: Can you tell me where Bluecorp is located?
(Mr. Redd White, at last!)

Japanese: （ついに、手がかりをつかんだぞ！）
English: (Finally, a lead on this guy!)

Japanese: （梅世に犯行がムリだった以上、こいつこそ、犯人のはず‥‥！）
English: (If April May couldn't have done it, that leaves him!)

Japanese: （さっそく、行動だ！）
English: (Time to take action!)

Japanese: 証拠品《ボーイの宣誓書》をゴミ箱に捨てた。
English: Discarded the Bellboy's Affidavit.

Japanese: （よし。相手はこれでも女の子だ。やさしくした方がいいだろう）
English: (Okay, okay... she's vulnerable now. I should be gentle.)

Japanese: いいかい。
English: Miss May...

Japanese: この宣誓書があれば、警察を動かすことだってできるんだよ。
English: It would be easy for me to hand this affidavit to the police.

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥...さわぎを大きくしたら、上司にもメイワクがかかるだろう？
English: If they get involved, wouldn't your boss be even more... er... inconvenienced?

Japanese: とりあえず、ぼくはこの人と話がしたいだけなんだ。
English: All I want to do is have a little chat with him.

Japanese: ‥‥教えてくれないかな。
English: You won't tell me?

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥...言いたいことは、それだけ？
English: Is that all you have to say?

Japanese: え。
English: Yes.

Japanese: 警察、動かせるもんなら、やってみればぁ‥‥？
English: If it's so easy for you to "get the police involved," then why don't you?

Japanese: ボクちゃんには、ムリだと思うけどぉ？
English: Somehow I think you're not up to it, hmm?

Japanese: （‥‥やさしくしたら、つけ上がってきやがった！）
English: (Show them a little kindness and they jump all over you!)

Japanese: （完全に逆効果だったみたいだな‥‥）
English: (That had the opposite effect to what I hoped...)

Japanese: あーあ。残念だったわねぇ‥‥。
English: Aww. *smirk* Poor little lawyer...

Japanese: もうちょっとだったのに。
English: And you were so close!

Japanese: （‥‥だめだ）
English: (This isn't working...)

Japanese: （もう、この宣誓書は効果がないぞ‥‥）
English: (I think I've worn out this piece of paper's usefulness.)

Japanese: くそっ！
English: Dammit!

Japanese: 証拠品《ボーイの宣誓書》をゴミ箱に捨てた。
English: Discarded the Bellboy's Affidavit.

Japanese: （‥‥この写真の男こそ、ぼくが探すべき人物だ！）
English: (The man in this photo is the one I want!)

Japanese: （梅世が口を閉ざしたとなると、この男を知っているのは‥‥？）
English: (Why did April May not want to talk about it... she must know him!)

Japanese: ねぇ、知ってるぅ？
English: Hey, guess what.

Japanese: あたし、あなたのコト、あんまり好きくない？　みたいな。
English: Actually, I, um, really hate your guts.

Japanese: ‥‥協力するワケ、ないじゃなぁい。
English: So get lost, because, well, I'm not cooperating.

Japanese: （‥‥それは、ごもっとも）
English: (Thanks, I noticed.)

Japanese: ‥‥松竹　梅世は取り調べを受けているそうだ。
English: Apparently, Miss May is in questioning.

Japanese: 今日はもう、話は聞けそうにないな。
English: I doubt they'll let me talk to her today.

Japanese: 面会のようすを監視する看守だ。
English: This guard monitors the visitor's room.

Japanese: さっきから、まったく動かない。さすが、プロだな。
English: He hasn't moved an inch. A real pro, this guy.

Japanese: ごていねいに、カメラで見張っているようだ。
English: Smile for the camera...

Japanese: 手がかりなるものはない
English: No clues here.

Japanese: ９月　８日　午後　３時３７分留置所　面会室
English: September 8, 3:37 PM Detention Center Visitor's Room

Japanese: あれから、１日が過ぎた。
English: I can't believe it's only been a day since the first trial.

Japanese: ‥‥いよいよ、明日はぼくの裁判だ。
English: My trial begins tomorrow.

Japanese: 小中は、ぼくをワナにかける気でいる。
English: White's going to set a trap for me.

Japanese: 当然、検察側もグルだ！おそらく、あの御剣も‥‥。
English: And the prosecution will be in on it, of course. Edgeworth, included.

Japanese: 昨日、ぼくの担当だと名乗る国選弁護士が現れた。
English: An attorney was assigned to me by the state, yesterday.

Japanese: しかし、ぼくはことわった。‥‥１つ、考えがあったからだ。
English: I refused. I had an idea.
Wright!

Japanese: 弁護士さん！
English: Mr. Wright!

Japanese: あ、真宵ちゃん。
English: Oh, Maya!

Japanese: よかったね。出してもらえたんだ。留置所。
English: Great, they let you out of detention.

Japanese: はい、さっき。弁護士さんのおかげです！
English: Just now, yes. It's all thanks to you!

Japanese: はは。なんだか、立場がギャクになっちゃったね。
English: Hah. Now I'm afraid we've switched places.

Japanese: どうしてですか！なんで、弁護士さんが！
English: What? You mean, you...?

Japanese: ぼくは、これまでのことを真宵ちゃんに話した。
English: I explained what had happened to Maya.
I don't believe it!

Japanese: その男‥‥お母さんを‥‥
English: How many people does that man need to destroy before he's satisfied!? My mother...

Japanese: お姉ちゃんを‥‥
English: My sister...

Japanese: そして、弁護士さんまで‥‥
English: And now you!

Japanese: ‥‥ゆるせない！
English: This has gone too far!

Japanese: 弁護士さん！　あたしに‥‥何かできること、ないですか！
English: Mr. Wright, please tell me, is there anything I can do?

Japanese: え。‥‥あるかな‥‥？
English: Um... well...

Japanese: じゃあさ。弁護をたのむよ。あした。
English: Alright, you can be my defense lawyer tomorrow!

Japanese: わかりました！
English: Alright!

Japanese: え。
English: Huh?

Japanese: まかせてください！　これでも、綾里家の血がながれてますから！
English: Leave it to me! I am Mia's sister, after all! Lawyership runs in our blood!

Japanese: （綾里家の血は、霊力がウリなんじゃなかったっけ？）
English: (Wasn't it E.S.P. that ran in your blood...?)

Japanese: じゃあ、『べんごしにゅうもん』を買いに、さっそく本屋さんへ‥‥
English: I'd better run to the bookstore and pick up a copy of "Law for Rookies."

Japanese: じゃあ、おうえんをたのむよ。法廷で。
English: Well, you could cheer for me in court.

Japanese: おうえん、ですか？あの、高校野球、みたいな？
English: Cheer for you? You mean... like a cheerleader?

Japanese: ん？　‥‥そうだね。うん。それ。
English: Huh? Um, yeah, like that.

Japanese: わかりました！まかせてください！
English: Alright! Leave it to me!

Japanese: え。
English: Huh?

Japanese: じゃあ、さっそく学生服とタイコを買いに‥‥
English: I'd better go get a uniform and some pom-poms...!

Japanese: わかった。じゃあ、脱獄をてつだってよ。
English: Right. Okay, listen up. I want you to help me break out of here!

Japanese: だ、ダツゴク‥‥ですか？
English: You mean... a jail break?

Japanese: うん。チャンスはもう、今夜しかないからさ。
English: Yeah. Tonight's our only chance!

Japanese: わかりました！
English: Alright!

Japanese: え。
English: Huh?

Japanese: そうですね。じゃあさっそく、“てつヤスリ”を買わないと‥‥！
English: Oh, I'd better go get a hacksaw while the stores are still open.

Japanese: あ、それから“なわばしご”とクルマの手配も‥‥！
English: Oh, oh! And a rope ladder, and a getaway car! Can you drive?

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥。...い、いやいや、ちょっと待った！
English: W-wait. Wait wait wait.

Japanese: な、なんですか！
English: What what what?

Japanese: 冗談に決まってるじゃないか！
English: I'm kidding! It was a joke!

Japanese: ‥‥そ、そんなぁ！
English: No way!

Japanese: いいよ、ありがとう。
English: No really, I was kidding. But thanks. It's good to know you're on my side.

Japanese: 気持ちだけで、じゅうぶんだから。（やってもらえることも、ないし）
English: (And there really isn't anything you can do for me anyway...)

Japanese: だ、ダメですそれじゃあ！
English: But... but I can't just sit here and do nothing!

Japanese: ‥‥じっとしてられないんです！その男に、キャンと言わせたいの！
English: I've got to give that man a piece of my mind!

Japanese: （キャンと、ねえ‥‥）
English: (Just a piece...?)

Japanese: わかった。
English: Okay.

Japanese: じゃあ明日、法廷に来てくれるかな。
English: Then, come to the court tomorrow.

Japanese: は‥‥はい！ありがとうございます！
English: O-okay! I'll be there!

Japanese: が、がんばります、あたし！
English: I'll show them a thing or two!

Japanese: 世紀が変わっても、あいかわらず犯罪は増加の一途をたどり‥‥、
English: It's the beginning of a new century, yet with crime it's the same old story.

Japanese: 時間のかかる法廷のシステムでは処理しきれなくなってしまった。
English: In fact, it's gotten worse. Lengthy court proceedings are no longer realistic.

Japanese: そのため、数年前から設置された序審法廷の期間は、最長でも３日。
English: Beginning a few years ago, a limit of three days was put on initial court trials.

Japanese: たいていは１日で終了してしまう。もちろん、有罪判決で、だ。
English: Almost all finish in a day. Most with a guilty verdict.

Japanese: まさか、ぼく自身が被告席に立つことになるとは思わなかった。
English: I never thought I would end up in the defendant's chair myself.

Japanese: 明日は、真犯人が目撃証人として法廷に出てくる。
English: Tomorrow the true culprit will appear as a witness.

Japanese: ‥‥ぼくか、あいつか‥‥最後の勝負だ！
English: This is it! It's me or him!

Japanese: つづく
English: To be continued.

Japanese: ９月　７日　某時刻星影法律事務所
English: September 7 Grossberg Law Offices

Japanese: （なんだ‥‥大センセイ、今日もいないのか）
English: (Huh. Looks like Grossberg is out today... again.)

Japanese: （もしかして、ぼくを、さけているのかな‥‥）
English: (Maybe he's avoiding me for some reason?)

Japanese: あ、あれ？
English: Wait a second...

Japanese: 昨日はここに、巨大な絵がかざってあったような。
English: Wasn't there a giant painting hanging on that wall...?

Japanese: たしかあれは‥‥
English: Yeah... yeah, it was a painting of...

Japanese: ‥‥そんな絵だったっけ。よくおぼえてないや。
English: Wasn't it? It wasn't a very memorable painting, anyhow.

Japanese: マホガニー製のどっしりしたデスクだ。
English: A solid mahogany desk.

Japanese: 深いツヤが出るまでみがき込まれている。
English: The wood's been polished to a deep luster.

Japanese: 来客用のテーブルだ。
English: A table for clients.

Japanese: 上品な黒檀のシガレットケースと、純金製のライター、か？
English: Hmm... an elegant ebony case, and if I'm not mistaken, that lighter's made of solid gold.

Japanese: なんだかわからないけど、高そうなことに間違いはない。
English: Even I can tell someone here's got money to burn.

Japanese: 最高級の観葉植物だ。
English: An expensive potted plant.

Japanese: 名前はわからないが、どうせ高いんだろう。
English: No idea what kind of plant it is, but it's probably the most expensive one available.

Japanese: マホガニー製の高そうな本棚に、高そうな本がズラリ。
English: Expensive-looking mahogany bookshelves, filled with expensive-looking books.

Japanese: ‥‥でも、あまり読まれたようなあとがないのが気になるな‥‥。
English: Hmm... funny... they don't look like they've ever been read.

Japanese: これは‥‥古い写真、か。２枚かさねて、おいてあるぞ。
English: What's this? Old photos? There are two lying here.

Japanese: 写真のウラに、エンピツ書きでメモが書いてある。
English: Something's been written in pencil on the backs.

Japanese: 《ＤＬ６号事件?資料１》《ＤＬ６号事件?資料２》か。
English: "DL-6 Incident - Exhibit A," "DL-6 Incident - Exhibit B"...

Japanese: どっちの写真を見よう‥‥？
English: Let's take a look at these...

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥...どこかで見たようなフンイキが‥‥。
English: I'm sure I've seen this person somewhere...

Japanese: ‥‥この写真、ちょっと借りるか。
English: Perhaps I'll borrow this photo.

Japanese: １枚ぐらい持っていっても、まあバレないだろう。
English: I'm sure no one will miss just one little photo.

Japanese: 何かの手がかりになるかもしれない‥‥借りよう。
English: And it might be a valuable clue... I'll take it for now.

Japanese: 証拠品《写真》を法廷記録にそっと挟んだ。
English: Photograph quietly added to the Court Record.

Japanese: なぜ、大先生はこんな男の写真を‥‥？
English: Why would Grossberg have a picture of this man...?

Japanese: 何かの手がかりになるかもしれない‥‥借りよう。
English: This might be a valuable clue... I'll take it for now.

Japanese: ‥‥借りるだけだよ。あとで返しておくさ。
English: I'm just borrowing it. I'll return it later.

Japanese: 証拠品《写真》を法廷記録にそっと挟んだ。
English: Photograph quietly added to the Court Record.

Japanese: ‥‥まあ、今はまだいらないな。
English: I don't need it now, whatever it is.

Japanese: 他に手がかりがなくなったら、また来ればいいや。
English: If I ever run out of clues, I'll know where to find these.

Japanese: （そのときまで、この写真があるかどうか、わからないけど）
English: (Although I can't be sure the photos will still be here then...)

Japanese: デスクの上の写真だ。
English: A photo lies on the desk.

Japanese: 今、持っている写真ととりかえようかな‥‥？
English: Maybe I should switch it with the one I took?

Japanese: よし。とりかえよう‥‥。
English: I think I'll swap them...

Japanese: 証拠品《写真》を法廷記録に挟んだ。
English: Photograph added to the Court Record.

Japanese: ‥‥いや、やっぱりやめておこう。
English: No, on second thought, I'd better not.

Japanese: デスクの上の写真だ。
English: A photo lies on the desk.

Japanese: 今、持っている写真ととりかえようかな‥‥？
English: Maybe I should switch it with the one I took?

Japanese: よし。とりかえよう‥‥。
English: I think I'll swap them...

Japanese: 証拠品《写真》を法廷記録に挟んだ。
English: Photograph added to the Court Record.

Japanese: ‥‥いや、やっぱりやめておこう。
English: No, on second thought, I'd better not.

Japanese: 手がかりなるものはない
English: No clues here.

Japanese: （やれやれ。あいかわらず、ルスか‥‥）
English: (*sigh* He's out again?)

Japanese: （あれ。机の上の写真がなくなってるぞ）
English: (Huh? No more photos on the desk...)

Japanese: あ、あれ？
English: Wait a second...

Japanese: 昨日はここに、巨大な絵がかざってあったような。
English: Wasn't there a giant painting hanging on that wall...?

Japanese: たしかあれは‥‥
English: Yeah... yeah, it was a painting of...

Japanese: ‥‥そんな絵だったっけ。よくおぼえてないや。
English: Wasn't it? It wasn't a very memorable painting, anyhow.

Japanese: マホガニー製のどっしりしたデスクだ。
English: A solid mahogany desk.

Japanese: 深いツヤが出るまでみがき込まれている。

English: A table for clients.

Japanese: 上品な黒檀のシガレットケースと、純金製のライター、か？
English: Hmm... an elegant ebony case, and if I'm not mistaken, that lighter's made of solid gold.

Japanese: なんだかわからないけど、高そうなことに間違いはない。
English: Even I can tell someone here's got money to burn.

Japanese: 最高級の観葉植物だ。
English: An expensive potted plant.

Japanese: 名前はわからないが、どうせ高いんだろう。
English: No idea what kind of plant it is, but it's probably the most expensive one available.

Japanese: マホガニー製の高そうな本棚に、高そうな本がズラリ。
English: Expensive-looking mahogany bookshelves, filled with expensive-looking books.

Japanese: ‥‥でも、あまり読まれたようなあとがないのが気になるな‥‥。
English: Hmm... funny... they don't look like they've ever been read.

Japanese: さっきまであった写真がなくなっているな。
English: Those photos that were lying there are gone now.

Japanese: センセイ、一度は帰ってきたのかな‥‥。
English: I guess Grossberg must have stopped in.

Japanese: 手がかりなるものはない
English: No clues here.

Japanese: （まったく。どこをほっつき歩いているんだか‥‥）
English: (Alright, this is getting ridiculous. Where the heck is that loafer?)

Japanese: ウォッホォン！
English: *Ah-HHHHEM!*

Japanese: （お‥‥この、ワンパターンなセキ払いは‥‥）
English: (Ah, that old familiar clearing of the throat...)

Japanese: あー、またチミかね。
English: Ah hah! You again.

Japanese: どうも、センセイ。
English: Um, hello, Mr. Grossberg.
Well, well.

Japanese: なかなかミゴトぢゃったな、今日は。
English: You were quite the thing, my boy.
Excuse me...?

Japanese: 法廷ぢゃよ、法廷。
English: The trial! The trial!

Japanese: （なんだ、見にきてたのか）
English: (He was there...?)

Japanese: 若いコロのワシを見ておるようぢゃったワイ。
English: Reminded me of myself when I was a youth!

Japanese: （まあ、千尋さん仕込み‥‥だからな）
English: (I guess something got passed down through Mia... maybe?)

Japanese: 思い出すのォ、あのコロを。
English: It brings back memories, it does!

Japanese: “青春の日々はぁ‥‥青いレモンのカオリぃ‥‥”かァ。
English: "Ah... the days of my youth... like the scent of fresh lemon..." you see.

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥‥‥...もうしわけない。
English: I apologize.

Japanese: ？?ワシも‥‥ダメになってしまったもんぢゃ。
English: I... It was all a bit too much for me, my boy.

Japanese: 今日のチミを見て、ワシは‥‥。
English: Seeing you today, I... well, I...

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥...（反省するより情報をくれよ！）
English: (I appreciate the reminiscing, but I'd much rather you gave me some information!)

Japanese: たしか昨日は、ここに巨大な絵があったはずだけど。
English: Wasn't there a giant painting hanging on that wall...?

Japanese: たしかあれは‥‥
English: Yeah... yeah, it was a painting of...

Japanese: ‥‥そんな絵だったっけ。よくおぼえてないや。
English: Wasn't it? It wasn't a very memorable painting, anyhow.

Japanese: マホガニー製のどっしりしたデスクだ。
English: A solid mahogany desk.

Japanese: 深いツヤが出るまでみがき込まれている。
English: The wood's been polished to a deep luster.

Japanese: 来客用のテーブルだ。
English: A table for clients.

Japanese: 上品な黒檀のシガレットケースと、純金製のライター、か？
English: Hmm... an elegant ebony case, and if I'm not mistaken, that lighter's made of solid gold.

Japanese: なんだかわからないけど、高そうなことに間違いはない。
English: Even I can tell someone here's got money to burn.

Japanese: 最高級の観葉植物だ。
English: An expensive potted plant.

Japanese: 名前はわからないが、どうせ高いんだろう。
English: No idea what kind of plant it is, but it's probably the most expensive one available.

Japanese: マホガニー製の高そうな本棚に、高そうな本がズラリ。
English: Expensive-looking mahogany bookshelves, filled with expensive-looking books.

Japanese: ‥‥でも、あまり読まれたようなあとがないのが気になるな‥‥。
English: Hmm... funny... they don't look like they've ever been read.

Japanese: さっきまであった写真がなくなっている。
English: That photo that was lying there is gone now.

Japanese: １枚、借りていること、やっぱりバレてるのかな？
English: Hmm... I wonder if someone noticed that one was missing?

Japanese: 手がかりなるものはない
English: No clues here.

Japanese: 見にきていたんですね、法廷。
English: So you came to see the trial?

Japanese: ‥‥ま、まあ、の。
English: Yes, yes I did.

Japanese: ゆんべは、気になってねむれなかった‥‥。
English: Something was bothering me all last night, you see. I couldn't get a wink of sleep.

Japanese: え？　どうしてですか？
English: Really? What was that?

Japanese: そりゃあチミ。千尋クンの妹さんのコトぢゃからの。
English: Well, you see, it's just... Mia's sister, that poor girl.

Japanese: ‥‥チミには、ほんとうにカンシャしとる。
English: My boy, I owe you my thanks, truly.

Japanese: もし、負けそうになっていたら、ワシは‥‥どうしていたぢゃろ。
English: I don't know what I would have done if things had gone poorly for the girl.

Japanese: （そんなに心配なら、なぜ引き受けてくれなかったんだ？）
English: (If you were that worried about it, why didn't you offer to defend her!?)

Japanese: 依頼を断った理由。昨日も聞きましたけど‥‥
English: I asked before, but, why did you refuse her request for defense?

Japanese: やっぱり、教えてもらえませんか？
English: I think I have a right to know.
A right, Mr. Wright?

Japanese: すまん‥‥す、少し考えさせてくれんか‥‥。
English: No, no, I'm sorry. It's just, I need more time to think about it, my boy.

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥。...（かなり、なやんでいるみたいだけど‥‥、）
English: (He does seem troubled about something...)

Japanese: （口を開かせるためには、もっと材料が必要なのかもしれない）
English: (Maybe I can find something that will inspire him to talk.)

Japanese: たしか昨日は、ここに大きな絵がエラそうに‥‥。
English: The other day I'm sure there was a big, pretentious-looking painting on that wall...

Japanese: “エラそうに”‥‥？
English: "Pretentious-looking"...?

Japanese: ‥‥ああ、いえ。
English: Well... you know the one I mean.

Japanese: でも、どこ行っちゃったんですか？‥‥あれ。
English: Anyway, where did it go?

Japanese: う、うん‥‥その。
English: Ah, yes, well...

Japanese: あ、あきちゃったんでな。
English: I... got rid of it. Got quite tired of the thing, really.

Japanese: ‥‥う、その。う、売っちゃったんぢゃ
English: I, er, sold it, you see. Yes, that's right.

Japanese: （売った？そんな、バカな‥‥！）
English: (Sold it!? I'm not sure I buy that...)

Japanese: でも、あの絵、大事なものだったんじゃないんですか？
English: Wasn't that painting rather important to you, sir?

Japanese: ‥‥何があったんですか？
English: Did something happen?

Japanese: うるさいんぢゃぁっ！
English: I don't see how it's any of your business!

Japanese: もう、絵のことは言わんでくれ！
English: Please, speak no more of that accursed painting.

Japanese: ‥‥ワシだって‥‥ワシだってなぁ‥‥。
English: I didn't... I...

Japanese: ‥‥ぶつぶつ。
English: *mumble* *mumble*

Japanese: ‥‥星影先生？
English: Mr. Grossberg...?

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥...す、すまん。今は１人で考えたいのぢゃ‥‥。
English: Ah, my apologies. I need to think about things. Alone.

Japanese: （ぼくのことが目に入っていないみたいだな‥‥）
English: (He barely even glanced in my direction...)

Japanese: すみません。この写真なんですけど‥‥。
English: Excuse me, I was wondering about this photograph...
Where did you get that!?

Japanese: お借りしていました。
English: I borrowed it.

Japanese: か、返しなさい！
English: Well, give it back at once!

Japanese: 星影先生。この男のことを教えてください。
English: Mr. Grossberg. Tell me about the man in this photo.

Japanese: こいつは、事件の夜、梅世の部屋にいたはずなんです。
English: He may have been in that hotel room with April May on the night of the murder.

Japanese: ‥‥何か、ごぞんじなんでしょう？
English: Do you know something about him... anything?
Hmm. Ahem!

Japanese: ‥‥わかった‥‥教える。
English: Very well. I'll tell you what I know.

Japanese: ただし‥‥。
English: However...

Japanese: ワシからの情報だということは、もらさんでくれ‥‥。
English: You didn't hear it from me, if you follow.

Japanese: わかりました。
English: I follow.

Japanese: そいつは小中　大（こなかまさる）。
English: That man is Mr. Redd White.

Japanese: 情報処理会社《コナカルチャー》の社長ぢゃ。
English: He's the President of Bluecorp.

Japanese: （コナカ‥‥マサル‥‥か）
English: (Redd White... Hmm.)

Japanese: “情報処理会社”‥‥探偵のようなものですか？
English: Bluecorp... they're something like a detective agency, right?

Japanese: ‥‥ヤツは、他人の情報で商売する“プロ”ぢゃ。
English: They're information pros. They sell information, you see. Sensitive information.

Japanese: この国で、もっともタチの悪い男ぢゃよ‥‥。
English: Despite his name, Mr. White has the blackest reputation of any man in this country.

Japanese: もし会うつもりなら、妙なネタを握られんよう、
English: Should you run across him, watch what you say.

Japanese: くれぐれも注意するんぢゃよ。
English: You wouldn't want him digging up any dirt on you.

Japanese: （‥‥情報で商売する“プロ”か）
English: (Hmm... selling information. Sounds like blackmail.)

Japanese: とりあえず、コナカルチャーの場所を教えてください。
English: Can you tell me where Bluecorp is located?

Japanese: う、うむ‥‥。
English: Hmm... I suppose.

Japanese: （小中　大、か！）
English: (Mr. Redd White, at last!)

Japanese: （ついに、男の手がかりをつかんだぞ！）
English: (Finally, a lead on this guy!)

Japanese: （梅世に犯行がムリだった以上、こいつこそ、犯人のはず‥‥！）
English: (Even if April May couldn't have done it, he could have!)

Japanese: （さっそく、行動だ！）
English: (Time to take action!)

Japanese: 《写真》を星影先生に返した。
English: Returned photograph to Mr. Grossberg.

Japanese: すまんが‥‥１人にしてくれんかの。
English: Now, I must ask you to leave.

Japanese: 考えたいことがあるんぢゃ‥‥。
English: I need to think about things. Alone.

Japanese: すみません。この写真なんですけど‥‥。
English: Excuse me, I was wondering about this photograph...

Japanese: ‥‥そ、それは‥‥！か、返しなさい！
English: Where did you get that!? Give it back at once!

Japanese: 勝手に持ち出してすみません。
English: I'm sorry to have taken it without your knowledge.

Japanese: でも‥‥誰なんですか？この男は。
English: But... I have to know. Who is this man?

Japanese: 事件の夜、梅世の部屋にいたのは、たぶん、こいつなんです。
English: He was most likely in April May's room the night of the murder.

Japanese: ‥‥！な、なんだって！!
English: What's that you say!?

Japanese: ほ、ほ、ほんとうかね！‥‥それは！
English: A-are you QUITE sure? My boy?

Japanese: （‥‥な、なんだこのあわてぶりは‥‥？）
English: (What has him so flustered I wonder...?)

Japanese: す、す、すまんが、帰ってくれんか！
English: I... I beg your pardon, but I must ask you to leave.

Japanese: １人にしてほしいんぢゃ‥‥！
English: I need to be alone.

Japanese: （‥‥！）(...!)（センセイ、なんだかだまりこんでしまったぞ‥‥）
English: (He's fallen silent as a stone. A rather large stone.)

Japanese: （とりあえず、この写真は返そう）
English: (I guess I should return that photo.)

Japanese: 《写真》を星影先生に返した。
English: Returned photograph to Mr. Grossberg.

Japanese: すまんが、この件については、何も言うことはできんのぢゃ。
English: I beg your pardon, but I am not at liberty to speak on the matter.

Japanese: ‥‥...‥‥‥‥...‥‥‥‥‥‥...（あれ。センセイ、ぼくに気づいてないみたいだぞ‥‥）
English: (Huh? I don't think he's noticed me standing here.)

Japanese: （セキ払いでも、してみるか）
English: (Maybe I should... clear my throat?)

Japanese: ウォッホォン！
English: Aaa-HHHEM!

Japanese: ‥‥わっ！
English: Jumping Jehosephats!

Japanese: な、なんぢゃ、チ、チミか！
English: Oh! You!

Japanese: どうしたんですか？　“人生のタソガレ”みたいな顔をして。
English: What's wrong? You looked so pensive... like an old man at the end of his days.

Japanese: むむ、ワシゃまだボケとらんぞ！
English: Hmm? I'm not senile yet!

Japanese: ‥‥この事件のことを考えておったんぢゃ。
English: I was just thinking about this whole mess...

Japanese: （星影先生‥‥そうとうツラいことがあるみたいだ）
English: (Something's really bothering him, that much is clear.)

Japanese: たしか昨日は、ここに巨大な絵があったはずだけど。
English: Wasn't there a giant painting hanging on that wall...?

Japanese: たしかあれは‥‥
English: Yeah... yeah, it was a painting of...

Japanese: ‥‥そんな絵だったっけ。よくおぼえてないや。
English: Wasn't it? It wasn't a very memorable painting, anyhow.

Japanese: マホガニー製のどっしりしたデスクだ。
English: A solid mahogany desk.

Japanese: 深いツヤが出るまでみがき込まれている。
English: The wood's been polished to a deep luster.

Japanese: 来客用のテーブルだ。
English: A table for clients.

Japanese: 上品な黒檀のシガレットケースと、純金製のライター、か？
English: Hmm... an elegant ebony case, and if I'm not mistaken, that lighter's made of solid gold.

Japanese: なんだかわからないけど、高そうなことに間違いはない。
English: Even I can tell someone here's got money to burn.

Japanese: 最高級の観葉植物だ。
English: An expensive potted plant.

Japanese: 名前はわからないが、どうせ高いんだろう。
English: No idea what kind of plant it is, but it's probably the most expensive one available.

Japanese: マホガニー製の高そうな本棚に、高そうな本がズラリ。
English: Expensive-looking mahogany bookshelves, filled with expensive-looking books.

Japanese: ‥‥でも、あまり読まれたようなあとがないのが気になるな‥‥。
English: Hmm... funny... they don't look like they've ever been read.

Japanese: 手がかりなるものはない
English: No clues here.

Japanese: 見にきていたんですね、法廷。
English: So you came to see the trial?

Japanese: ‥‥ま、まあ、の。
English: Yes, yes I did.

Japanese: ゆんべは、気になってねむれなかった‥‥。
English: Something was bothering me all last night, you see, couldn't get a wink of sleep.

Japanese: え？　どうしてですか？
English: Really? What was that?

Japanese: そりゃあチミ。千尋クンの妹さんのコトぢゃからの。
English: Well, you see, it's just... Mia's sister, that poor girl.

Japanese: ‥‥チミには、ほんとうにカンシャしとる。
English: My boy, I owe you my thanks, truly.

Japanese: もし、負けそうになっていたら、ワシは‥‥どうしていたぢゃろ。
English: I don't know what I would have done if things had gone poorly for the girl.

Japanese: 依頼を断った理由。
English: I asked before, but, why did you refuse her request for defense?

Japanese: やっぱり、まだ教えてもらえませんか？
English: I think I have a right to know.
A right, Mr. Wright?

Japanese: すまん‥‥す、少し考えさせてくれんか‥‥。
English: No, no, I'm sorry. It's just, I need more time to think about it, my boy.

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥。...（かなり、なやんでいるみたいだけど‥‥、）
English: (He does seem troubled about something...)

Japanese: （ぼくにも、なんとなく想像はついてきたんだよな‥‥）
English: (I'm starting to have a feeling I know what it is.)

Japanese: コナカルチャーに行ってきました。
English: So, I paid Bluecorp a visit.

Japanese: ‥‥ほ、ほう、そうかね。
English: Oh? Oh, I see.

Japanese: 星影先生。
English: Mr. Grossberg...

Japanese: 実はさっきから、気になっていることがあるんですよ。
English: I have to admit, something has been bothering me.

Japanese: は、はて‥‥。なんぢゃろうな。
English: Oh? What is it? Well, out with it, my boy!

Japanese: ええ。それは‥‥
English: You see, it's just...

Japanese: 小中の写真です。
English: That photo of Mr. White...

Japanese: なんで星影先生が、彼の写真を持っているんですか？
English: Why would you have his photo, Mr. Grossberg?

Japanese: う、うむむ。‥‥昔、ちょっとな‥‥。
English: Umm... yes, well. It's been a long time since then...

Japanese: 昔‥‥ですか？
English: A long time ago?

Japanese: ぼくの考えは違います。
English: I'm sorry... but I think not.

Japanese: あなたと小中は、今でもつながっている。
English: I think you still have ties to Mr. White even today.

Japanese: な、なんぢゃと！‥‥バ、バカな！
English: R-really! Whatever gave you that idea!

Japanese: い、いったい、ヤツとどんな関係があると言うんぢゃ！
English: W-what connection could I possibly have to an individual such as he?

Japanese: ‥‥カンタンなことです。それは、
English: It's pretty straightforward, actually...

Japanese: ズバリ、飲み友だちです！
English: You go drinking together!

Japanese: どうです？トモダチなんでしょ、２人は？
English: You're friends, aren't you?

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥...バカ言っちゃイカン。
English: Now you're being foolish, my boy.

Japanese: なんでワシが、あんなヤツと飲まにゃならんのぢゃ！
English: Why in the world would I ever drink with the likes of him!?

Japanese: （あれ、違ったか‥‥？）
English: (Huh? Whoops... maybe that wasn't it.)

Japanese: あなたは、小中に弱みを握られていますね？
English: Mr. White has something on you doesn't he? Blackmail?

Japanese: ‥‥！...!し、知らん‥‥！なんのことか‥‥。
English: I don't... I don't know what you're talking about!

Japanese: 星影先生。
English: Mr. Grossberg, sir...

Japanese: 昨日は、そこの壁に大きな絵がかかっていましたよね。
English: There was a giant painting hanging right there the other day, was there not?

Japanese: “ゼッタイに手ばなさない”と言っていた、あの絵です。
English: The one you said you had "no intention of parting with"?

Japanese: 見かけたんですよ、今日。
English: Well, I saw it. Today.

Japanese: コナカルチャーの社長室で。
English: It was in the CEO's office at Bluecorp. Redd White's office.

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥。...そうか‥‥。気がついたか。
English: So... you noticed.

Japanese: ま、あれだけデカい絵ぢゃ。‥‥気づくわな、そりゃ。
English: I suppose I should have guessed you would. It is a large painting...

Japanese: 話してください。星影先生。
English: Mr. Grossberg... tell me what happened.

Japanese: ズバリ、小中はあなたの情報提供者なんじゃないですか？
English: Redd White is supplying you with information, obviously!

Japanese: な、なんぢゃと？
English: What's that now?

Japanese: あなたはなぜか、彼のことをかばおうとするフシがあります。
English: You've been protecting him all along.

Japanese: それは、あなたが彼の会社を利用しているからでしょう！
English: You protect him because you need his services!

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥...バカ言っちゃイカン。
English: Now you're being foolish, my boy.

Japanese: 誰が、あんなヤツにたのむもんか‥‥！
English: What possible use could I have for the services of the likes of him!?

Japanese: （あれ、違ったか‥‥？）
English: (Huh? Whoops... maybe that wasn't it.)

Japanese: 最初から、不自然でした。
English: I thought it was strange.

Japanese: かわいい弟子だった千尋さんの妹が巻き込まれた事件なのに、
English: Here was Maya, your own apprentice's little sister, in desperate need...

Japanese: それを引き受けないなんて。
English: Accused of killing her own sister no less, and you wouldn't take the case!

Japanese: ‥‥その理由が、わかりました。
English: I finally realized why.

Japanese: あなたと小中には、ある関係があるんです。それは‥‥
English: You and Redd White are connected. He's...

Japanese: あなたと小中は、先輩と後輩の関係にあるんじゃないですか？
English: I'll bet you and White go way back! He's your boss now, and was before, I bet!

Japanese: な、なんぢゃ？‥‥せ、先輩？
English: My what? Boss? Are you serious?

Japanese: なんか、お２人は同じニオイがただよっていますからね。
English: Yeah! You both have that sort of "we're in the same club together" feel to you.

Japanese: ‥‥やめてくれ。
English: Please, stop.

Japanese: 冗談は、それぐらいでいいぢゃろ。
English: You have a very peculiar sense of humor, Mr. Wright.

Japanese: ‥‥わかったよ。話そう。
English: Very well, I'll talk.

Japanese: （な、なんだよ冗談って‥‥）
English: (What does he mean... "sense of humor"? I was serious!)

Japanese: ぼくには理解できませんが‥‥、
English: It's not something I can claim to understand...

Japanese: あなたと小中は、愛しあっているんですね？
English: But you and Mr. White are lovers, aren't you!

Japanese: な、な、な、なんぢゃ！
English: W-w-what! My boy!

Japanese: 星影先生。
English: Mr. Grossberg.

Japanese: 昨日は、そこの壁に大きな絵がかかっていましたよね。
English: The other day, there was a giant painting hanging on that wall.

Japanese: あの絵、今日、見かけたんですよ。
English: I saw that painting again, today.

Japanese: コナカルチャーの社長室で。
English: It was in the CEO's office of Bluecorp.

Japanese: プレゼント、だったんでしょう？
English: It was a present, wasn't it?

Japanese: い、いったいどこからそんな発想が出てくるんぢゃ！
English: Well, my boy, you have a splendid imagination!

Japanese: ‥‥ぼくには理解できません‥‥。
English: I... I don't understand how you could...

Japanese: 理解できん発想をするな！
English: That's because I'm not, we're not... Don't be ridiculous!

Japanese: （おかしいな‥‥ぼくの見込み違いだったか‥‥）
English: (Hmm... I guess I was wrong. But it just seemed so right!)

Japanese: 星影先生。
English: Mr. Grossberg, sir...

Japanese: 昨日は、そこの壁に大きな絵がかかっていましたよね。
English: There was a giant painting hanging right there the other day, was there not?

Japanese: “ゼッタイに手ばなさない”と言っていた、あの絵です。
English: The one you said you had "no intention of parting with"?

Japanese: 見かけたんですよ、今日。
English: Well, I saw it. Today.

Japanese: コナカルチャーの社長室で。
English: It was in the CEO's office at Bluecorp. Redd White's office.

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥。...そうか‥‥。気がついたか。
English: So... you noticed.

Japanese: ま、あれだけデカい絵ぢゃ。‥‥気づくわな、そりゃ。
English: I suppose I should have guessed you would. It is a large painting...

Japanese: あなたと小中の間には、何か“関係”があるハズです。
English: Mr. Grossberg, I know you and Mr. White are connected somehow!

Japanese: か、関係だと‥‥？
English: C-connected, you say?

Japanese: そうです！　それは‥‥、
English: Yes! And I know what it is...

Japanese: ズバリ、小中はあなたの情報提供者なんじゃないですか？
English: You're his client, aren't you? He's giving you information!

Japanese: な、なんぢゃと？
English: What's that now?

Japanese: あなたはなぜか、彼のことをかばおうとするフシがあります。
English: You've been protecting him all along.

Japanese: それは、あなたが彼の会社を利用しているからでしょう！
English: You protect him because you need his services!

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥...あの絵は、何かの情報に対するお礼として、わたしたのでは‥‥？
English: You gave him that painting in exchange for some valuable information, didn't you?

Japanese: バカ言っちゃイカン。
English: Utterly ridiculous.

Japanese: たとえ何があろうと、あいつにだけは、たのまん！
English: Me, his client? I would never willingly deal with that man. Never!

Japanese: （あれ、違ったか‥‥？）
English: (Huh? Did I get it wrong?)

Japanese: あなたは小中に、何か弱みを握られているんじゃないですか？
English: Mr. White has something on you doesn't he? Blackmail?

Japanese: ‥‥！...!あの絵が、それを証明していると思いますけどね。
English: I think that painting is fairly gaudy proof.

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥...わかった‥‥。ちょうどいいチャンスかもしれん。
English: Very well. This may be the chance I've been waiting for.

Japanese: すべて話して、そろそろ‥‥ラクになるかのお。
English: Maybe it's time to get this off my chest, so I can finally rest easy again.

Japanese: チミが千尋クンの部下なのも、何かの運命なのぢゃろ‥‥。
English: After all, you were Mia's understudy. Perhaps it was fate?

Japanese: （‥‥どういうことだ？）
English: (What's he talking about...?)

Japanese: ぼくには理解できませんが‥‥、
English: It's not something I can claim to understand...

Japanese: あなたと小中は、愛しあっているんですね？
English: But you and Mr. White are lovers, aren't you!

Japanese: な、な、な、な、な、な、な、なんぢゃ！
English: W-w-what! My boy!

Japanese: あの絵は、あなたから彼に贈られた、
English: You sent that painting to him!

Japanese: とろけるようなアイの‥‥
English: As a sign! A sign of undying love!

Japanese: ちょ、ちょ、ちょ、ちょ、ちょ、ちょっと待ちなさい！
English: M-m-my boy, please! You're letting your fancies run away with you!

Japanese: い、いったいどこからそんな発想が出てくるんぢゃ！
English: Where do you get these bizarre ideas?

Japanese: ‥‥ぼくには理解できません‥‥。
English: I... I don't understand how you could...

Japanese: 理解できん発想をするな！
English: That's because I'm not, we're not... Don't be ridiculous!
Enough.

Japanese: 話すとしよう。‥‥ハジをしのんで、な。
English: I'll swallow my pride and tell you all.

Japanese: （やっぱり、２人は‥‥）
English: (I knew it! They are lovers!)

Japanese: だから、ちがぁう！
English: N-no! We are NOT lovers!

Japanese: 小中　大は、人を脅迫することで生きておる。
English: Redd White is a man who makes his living through intimidation.

Japanese: 《コナカルチャー》は、人の弱みを探り出すための調査会社ぢゃ。
English: Bluecorp is a company that excels in finding people's weaknesses, I'm afraid.

Japanese: ‥‥そしてワシは、この１５年間ヤツにカネを払いつづけておる。
English: I've been paying them for 15 years now...

Japanese: 《ＤＬ６号事件》のネタでな。
English: All because of the "DL-6 Incident," as you may have guessed.

Japanese: （あの写真のウラに書いてあった事件名だ‥‥）
English: (The name on the back of those photographs!)

Japanese: ワシが真宵さんの弁護を引き受けなかったのは、
English: As you suspected, I could not stand in defense of Maya because of this.

Japanese: チミの考えているとおり、小中に脅迫されておるからぢゃ。
English: White would have destroyed me if I did.

Japanese: （やっぱり、そうか‥‥）
English: (So that's the connection!)

Japanese: チミには言いにくいことぢゃが‥‥
English: It is hard for me to tell you this, my boy.

Japanese: 小中を逮捕させることは、ゼッタイに不可能ぢゃ。
English: But arresting Redd White will be nigh on impossible.

Japanese: ど、どうしてですか！
English: Impossible? Why!?

Japanese: やつは裁判官?弁護士?検事局長?警察‥‥そして、政治家。
English: He has information on everyone. It gives him an iron grip!

Japanese: 司法の要人のネタをにぎって、自由にあやつっておるのぢゃ。
English: He owns judges, attorneys, prosecutors, police... and politicians.

Japanese: ‥‥な、なんですって‥‥！
English: What!?

Japanese: みんな、‥‥自分がキズつくことはできんのぢゃよ。
English: They are bound, unable to do harm to themselves, and therefore, to him.

Japanese: そんな目でワシを見るな‥‥。それが‥‥老いというものぢゃ。
English: Don't look at me like that. What you see is nothing more than the weight of many years.

Japanese: 《ＤＬ６号事件》って‥‥なんですか？
English: What is the "DL-6 Incident"?

Japanese: ＤＬ６号というのは、警察の事件分類ナンバーぢゃ。
English: "DL-6" is nothing more than the sorting code the police gave the case.
It was 15 years ago now...

Japanese: ワシは、ある霊媒師の依頼を受けた。
English: I received a request from a medium. A spirit medium.

Japanese: （れ、霊媒師‥‥？）
English: (A medium...?)

Japanese: 名前は、綾里　舞子（あやさとまいこ）といった。
English: Her name was Misty Fey.

Japanese: （アヤサト‥‥！）
English: (Fey!)

Japanese: そのとおり。‥‥千尋クンの、母親ぢゃよ。
English: Indeed. She was Mia's mother.

Japanese: 彼女は、警察の依頼で、ある殺人事件の被害者の霊媒を‥‥。
English: She had been investigating a murder at the bequest of the police.

Japanese: そして‥‥失敗したんぢゃ。
English: And... she failed.

Japanese: そのため、彼女は警察からサギで訴えられかけておった。
English: As a result, the police called her a fraud.

Japanese: （‥‥昨日、真宵ちゃんが話していた事件だ！）
English: (This is what Maya was talking about the other day!)

Japanese: ワシは彼女のためにはたらき、助けてやった。
English: I did all I could for her, and in the end, cleared her of wrongdoing.

Japanese: 結局、その殺人事件はいまだ未解決、なんぢゃよ‥‥。
English: That murder case, however remains unsolved to this day.

Japanese: それが、ＤＬ６号事件、ぢゃ。
English: That case, is the DL-6 Incident.

Japanese: それで、星影先生はなぜ、小中に脅迫を‥‥？
English: But why were you blackmailed over this, Mr. Grossberg?

Japanese: ＤＬ６号事件は当時、トップシークレットぢゃった。
English: The DL-6 Incident was top- secret at the time.

Japanese: 当然ぢゃな。警察が霊媒にたよったなど、
English: It made sense. The police didn't want people to know they were using a medium!

Japanese: ゼッタイ、知られてはならん。
