English: Yeah, I saw 'em! I saw everything!

Japanese: ええええええっ！
English: What!?

Japanese: でもなっ！　お前らなんかに、教えてやるもんか！
English: But... but no way am I telling you losers!

Japanese: あっ！　待って‥‥‥‥。
English: Wha--!? Wait...

Japanese: はなせよ！
English: Lemme go!
He's gone.

Japanese: あれ？
English: Huh?

Japanese: 逃げるときにぶつかったテーブルから、何か転がり落ちたよ。
English: Something fell off the table when he bumped into it on his way out.

Japanese: ビン、か‥‥。
English: A... bottle?

Japanese: なんでこんなものが、テーブルに‥‥？
English: Why was this sitting there, I wonder?

Japanese: 《カラッポの小ビン》をポケットにしのばせた。
English: Empty Bottle slipped into pocket.

Japanese: ところであの子、何か言ってたよな。
English: Anyway, what was that kid saying?

Japanese: “ゼンブ見てたんだ”って‥‥。
English: He "saw everything"...

Japanese: ‥‥フン！
English: Evildoers!

Japanese: １０月１８日　某時刻撮影所?スタッフエリア
English: October 18 Global Studios Employee Area

Japanese: ‥‥あっ！
English: Hey!

Japanese: きみ！‥‥ちょ、ちょっと待てよ！
English: Hey, you! W-wait a sec!

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥...なるほどくん。‥‥今の子‥‥？
English: Phoenix... Was that the boy?

Japanese: ええ。‥‥あれが大滝　九太です。
English: Yeah. His name's "Cody Hackins."

Japanese: 楽屋に逃げ込んだみたいね。
English: I think he ran into the dressing room.

Japanese: “お茶”“みそ汁”“おしるこ”“くず湯”“おでん”‥‥
English: Let's see... cola, candy, chips, gum...

Japanese: ラインナップが和風に統一されている。
English: Huh. It's so... normal.

Japanese: “ウーロン茶”すらないぞ。
English: I was expecting some cool theme snacks or something. Popcorn, at least!

Japanese: 私、この“流しそうめん”をいただいてみたいわ。
English: Look at this! "Spaghetti in a Soda Can"...?

Japanese: そ、“そうめん”ですか？
English: S-spaghetti?

Japanese: つゆを飲んじゃって、メンだけがカンの底に残るんじゃあ‥‥。
English: Wouldn't just the sauce come out and the noodles all stay stuck inside?

Japanese: “一気にガッと行ってください”って書いてあるわね、カンに。
English: "Best when slammed." I guess they want you to drink it in one gulp...?

Japanese: “ガッ”とねえ‥‥。
English: Slamming spaghetti. Right...

Japanese: 楽屋に入るドアだ。カギはかかっていないみたいだ。
English: The door to the dressing room. It's not locked.

Japanese: さっき、九太くんが入っていったわね。
English: This is where Cody ran...

Japanese: お話、聞きに行きましょうか。
English: Let's go in and see if we can get him to talk.

Japanese: 巨大な倉庫だ。
English: That must be the storage room.

Japanese: 撮影用の大道具がしまってあるようだ。
English: That's where they keep all the film equipment.

Japanese: モーターを乗せた、電動自転車だ。
English: Looks like one of those electric-powered bicycles.

Japanese: なんともいえない中途ハンパな感じが、ぼくはキライだ。
English: I dunno about these. I mean, what's the point?

Japanese: あら。けっこうベンリなのに。
English: It makes it a lot easier to go up hills!

Japanese: 姉妹そろって、もっと運動しましょうよ！
English: Where have I heard that line before...?

Japanese: 九太くん、ここから入ってきたんでしょう？
English: This is the drain where Cody came in, right?

Japanese: そうですよ。
English: Right.

Japanese: いいわね、男の子は。やんちゃで。
English: My, what a rambunctious lad.

Japanese: （“やんちゃ”って、ひさしぶりに聞いたな‥‥）
English: ("Rambunctious lad"? Do the dead all speak like that?")

Japanese: １０月１８日　某時刻撮影所?楽屋
English: October 18 Global Studios Dressing Room

Japanese: 事件があった日、トノサマンの着ぐるみ、ここにあったんだよね。
English: This is where the Steel Samurai costume was, on the day of the murder.

Japanese: 荷星さん、そう言ってたっけね。
English: That's what Mr. Powers says, at least.

Japanese: 荷星さんがヒルネしてるのを見て、盗んだんじゃないかな。
English: I guess they snuck in and took it while he was sleeping.

Japanese: でもさ。もし荷星さんがヒルネしなかったら、
English: But Nick, what if Mr. Powers hadn't been sleeping?

Japanese: ハンニンはどうするつもりだったのかなあ‥‥？

English: I got here that day around 2:00...

Japanese: オバチャンに見られないように道のそとの林の中を歩いていたら、
English: I had to come in through the woods out back so that old lady wouldn't see me.

Japanese: すっかり迷っちゃったんだ。３０分ぐらいかな‥‥？
English: I got kinda lost, though. I was in there for maybe a half hour or so.

Japanese: ‥‥で、やっとスタジオの前に出たんだよ。
English: Then I finally got to the studio.

Japanese: そうしたら‥‥、
English: After that...

Japanese: どうしたの、九太くん？
English: What is it, Cody?

Japanese: トノサマンが、悪いヤツをやっつけたんだよ！
English: Th-the Steel Samurai killed the bad guy!

Japanese: トノサマン?スピアーでさ！いつものように、イチゲキだよ！
English: He used his Samurai Spear! Just like always! One shot, one kill!

Japanese: すごい迫力だった‥‥。オレ、コワくなっちゃって。
English: It happened so fast... I got scared.

Japanese: あの日は、それで帰ったんだ。
English: I went home after that.

Japanese: ‥‥そ、そうなんだ。それはタイヘンだったねえ。
English: I... I see. It must have been hard for you.

Japanese: あんた、コトバにココロがこもってないなあ。
English: Man, you say those nice things but it's so obvious you aren't speaking from the heart.

Japanese: ‥‥千尋さん。
English: (Umm... right.) Mia?

Japanese: はい。
English: Yes?

Japanese: これってつまり、トノサマンが殺人犯ということですよね。
English: I think we can say for sure this means the Steel Samurai did it.

Japanese: そうね。
English: True enough.

Japanese: そして、カントクたちはアリバイがあるから、トノサマンじゃない。
English: And the director has an alibi, so it wasn't him.

Japanese: そのとおりよ。
English: Indeed.

Japanese: ‥‥すると、犯人は、荷星さんしかいないことになる！
English: So... the only person left who it could have been is Will Powers!

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥...予想外のことだけど、
English: I have to admit, it's kind of a shock.

Japanese: この子を法廷で証言させたら、依頼人はカクジツに有罪ね。
English: But if you put this boy on the witness stand, your client will be "guilty" for sure.

Japanese: ‥‥法廷に呼ぶの、やめましょう。
English: Let's not call him then...

Japanese: そうね。そっとしておきましょう。
English: I think that would be wise. Best to leave this one alone.

Japanese: そうは行かないッス！
English: Not so fast, pal!

Japanese: 今の話、聞かせてもらったッス！
English: I heard everything!

Japanese: この少年は、重要な証人ッス。
English: This boy here's a key witness!

Japanese: ワレワレが、手あつくホゴするッス！
English: And he's under police protection, starting now!

Japanese: さあ少年！
English: C'mon, son.

Japanese: 刑事さんと、警察署までランデブーッス！
English: You've got a rendezvous with me down at the precinct!

Japanese: や、やだよ！‥‥うわっ！
English: No! I ain't going! Waah!

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥...こ、こまったわね。
English: That didn't go so well.

Japanese: 手がかりは、これでゼロになっちゃったわ。
English: We're back to zero leads.

Japanese: ‥‥サイアクな形で。
English: And now we have a serious handicap in court.

Japanese: ど、どうするんですか！千尋さん！
English: W-what're we going to do, Mia!

Japanese: ‥‥え、えーと。
English: Well, hmm...

Japanese: 私、そろそろ帰らないと。
English: I... really need to be going.

Japanese: だいじょうぶ。なんとかなりますから。
English: Don't worry, Phoenix. I'm sure you'll be fine!

Japanese: いやいやいや！
English: Whoa whoa whoa!

Japanese: 明日、法廷、来てくださいよ！‥‥自信、ないから‥‥。
English: C-come to the trial tomorrow, please! I need you there!

Japanese: なるほどくん。‥‥１つ聞かせて。
English: Phoenix, can I ask you something?

Japanese: あなたは、荷星さんの無実を信じているの？
English: Do you really believe that Mr. Powers is innocent?

Japanese: あたりまえじゃないですか！
English: Of course he is!

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥...そのヒトコトが聞きたかったの。
English: That's all I wanted to hear.

Japanese: いいわ。‥‥あした、法廷でね。
English: Excellent. I'll see you in court, then.

Japanese: （ここまで荷星さんに不利な状況になると‥‥）
English: (I never expected things to go this badly for Powers's case...)

Japanese: （自信がなくなってくるよなあ‥‥）
English: (I'm not sure we can win this one.)

Japanese: なるほどくん。
English: Phoenix?

Japanese: あなたが信じなくて、誰が信じられるの！
English: If you don't believe him, who will!?

Japanese: （ホンキでおこられてしまった）
English: (Uh oh, she's mad.)

Japanese: まあ、いいわ。明日、法廷で会いましょう。
English: Oh well... I'll see you in court tomorrow then.

Japanese: はい！‥‥よろしくおねがいします。
English: Great! Thanks, Mia!

Japanese: よろしくね。
English: See you soon.

Japanese: つづく
English: To be continued.

Japanese: 九太くん。‥‥これ。
English: Cody... This look familiar?

Japanese: あっ！　オレのトレカ！
English: Hey! My UR!

Japanese: と‥‥“とれか”？
English: "You are"... huh?

Japanese: “トレーディングカード”のことだよ！
English: "U.R." You know, "ultra rare"! That card's really hard to get!

Japanese: オトナのクセに、なんにも知らねえのな！
English: Man, for a grown-up, you sure are dumb!

Japanese: これ、あげるからさ。話、聞かせてよ。
English: I'll give you this... IF you help me.

Japanese: それ、もともとオレのじゃねえか！
English: But that's my card! By offering me something I already own, you're in effect

Japanese: シホン主義社会のキホンは等価交換だぜ！
English: eschewing the very basis of our consumer society, namely the principle of fair trade!

Japanese: オトナのクセに、なんにも知らねえのな！
English: Man, for a grown-up, you sure are dumb!

Japanese: （うぐ。‥‥こムズカシイこと言いやがって‥‥）
English: (W-what do they teach these kids in school these days!? Quantum physics!?)

Japanese: だいいちさぁ、‥‥いらねえよ、そんなカード。
English: Whatever, I don't need that card.

Japanese: え。
English: Huh?

Japanese: そのカードさ、ダブって持ってるんだ。
English: I got a double.

Japanese: あんたにくれてやるよ。
English: Just keep it. It's yours.

Japanese: （おいおい、話が違うぜ‥‥）
English: (What!? After I went through all that trouble...)

Japanese: オレと取り引きしたけりゃ、もっとレアなヤツを持って来な！
English: If you want to trade, find me a REALLY rare card!

Japanese: れあ？‥‥焼きカゲンのコトか‥‥？
English: "Really rare"!? You mean there's something rarer than "Ultra Rare"!?

Japanese: オトナのクセに、なんにも知らねえのな！
English: Ultra Rare Premium cards are way rarer than plain Ultra Rare cards! Come ON!

Japanese: レアカード。めずらしいカードのコトだよ！
English: Man, for a grown-up, you sure are dumb!

Japanese: レアカード、ねえ‥‥。
English: Premium... rare... why do I feel like we're talking about steaks?

Japanese: なあ、このカード‥‥。
English: See this card?

Japanese: あ、ああッ！　そのカード！
English: Yeah, so... Wait! That's it!!!

Japanese: オレの持ってない、最後のレアカードだ！
English: That's the last Ultra Rare Premium card I need!

Japanese: な、なあ、おい。クレ！クレよぉぉ！
English: C'mon give it to me, please! You gotta give it to me!

Japanese: （な、なんだ？このクイツキのよさは‥‥）
English: (Wow, I've never seen him so... eager.)

Japanese: よ、よおし。おニイさんとトリヒキをしないか？
English: R-right! Okay! How about a trade?

Japanese: いいぜ！
English: You're on!

Japanese: “トノサマン?スピアー”に“アクダイカーン”もつけるよ！
English: I'll give you a "Samurai Spear" and throw in an "Evil Magistrate" to boot!!

Japanese: い、いやいやいやいや。
English: No no no no... not that kind of trade.

Japanese: ‥‥カードはいいんだ。質問に答えてくれよ。
English: I don't want cards. I want information.

Japanese: なーんだ、そんなことか。いいよ。何でも聞いてよ。
English: Huh? You sure? Fine by me!

Japanese: 《レアカード》を九太くんにあげた。
English: Gave URP Card to Cody.

Japanese: さあ。なるほどくん。お話を聞きましょう。
English: Okay, Phoenix. Let's hear what he has to say.

Japanese: こちらに有利なことがあったら、明日の法廷に来てもらうんです。
English: If he knows something that could help us, we can have him take the stand tomorrow.

Japanese: あっ。トノサマン。
English: Hey, it's the Steel Samurai.

Japanese: なんか、足を引きずってるみたいだケド‥‥。
English: Huh? Why's he dragging his foot like that?

Japanese: これがどうかしたかい？
English: Where'd you get this, anyway?

Japanese: カッコイイよな。トノサマン?スピアー。
English: The Samurai Spear! That's totally cool.

Japanese: オレもほしいなー。
English: I wish I had one, too...

Japanese: （真宵ちゃんと話が合いそうだな‥‥）
English: (This kid and Maya would get along great.)

Japanese: ありがとな。コレ。
English: Thanks for this.

Japanese: それ、おねーちゃんにあげたんだぞ！
English: Hey, I gave that to the nice lady, not you, loser!

Japanese: あんたがカッテに見るなよな！
English: No looking at that, okay!

Japanese: （かわいくねえよお‥‥）
English: (Believe me, I wasn't going to...)

Japanese: なんだよそれ。
English: What's that? Bo-ring!

Japanese: つまんないモノ、見せるなよな！
English: Man, how can one person be so lame!? If you were a superhero you'd be Lame-o-man!

Japanese: １０月１８日　某時刻第１スタジオ前
English: October 18 Studio One Entrance

Japanese: あれっ。
English: Hey, Nick.

Japanese: 今日は、イトノコ刑事さん、いないね。
English: It looks like Detective Gumshoe isn't here today.

Japanese: そうだね。
English: You're right.

Japanese: 今日の裁判、大騒ぎだったから、きっと忙しいんだろう。
English: He's probably up to his neck in paperwork after the commotion at today's trial.

Japanese: ね。ね。なるほどくん。
English: So, Nick...

Japanese: 今日の裁判で言ってた“第２スタジオ”って、
English: Remember that "Studio Two" we heard about at the trial today?

Japanese: あの、木が倒れてる道の方に行くとあるんだよね。
English: It was down that path with the fallen tree, right?

Japanese: ああ。そんなこと言ってたっけね。
English: Yeah, I think that's what they said.

Japanese: カントクさんたち、今日もいるかもしれないよ。
English: Maybe the director and the rest of them are there today?

Japanese: 今がチャンス！‥‥行ってみようよ！
English: Now's our chance, Nick! Let's check it out!

Japanese: 第１スタジオへ通じる入口。
English: The entrance to Studio One.

Japanese: スタジオは、衣袋　武志が死体で見つかった現場だ。
English: This is the studio where they found Jack Hammer's body.

Japanese: 裁判では、この奥には“第２スタジオ”があるという。
English: In the trial, they said Studio Two was down this path.

Japanese: サルのクビがジャマだけど、道はなんとか、とおれそうだ。
English: The monkey head is in the way, but we should be able to get by it without a problem.

Japanese: うわあ。このサルのクビ、ものすごく重いよ。
English: This monkey head is real heavy!

Japanese: そりゃ、そこの木を折っちゃうぐらいだからなあ。
English: Well, it did knock that tree over when it fell.

Japanese: 事件があった日に折れたんだよね。おサルさんのクビと、この木‥‥。
English: Both of these, the monkey head and the tree, fell the day of the murder, didn't they.

Japanese: ああ。イトノコ刑事がそんなこと言ってたね。
English: Yeah, that's what Detective Gumshoe said.

Japanese: この撮影スタジオのマスコット?キャラクターだ。
English: The studio mascot.

Japanese: なんでも、“テングザル”をモチーフにしてるとか。
English: Apparently it's a monkey of some kind.

Japanese: でも、テングって、こんなダンゴっ鼻なのかなあ？
English: I've never seen a monkey with a nose like that before!

Japanese: 早くクビ、なおせばいいのに。
English: Why don't they put his head back on?

Japanese: あのスタッフの子にたのんでみようか。
English: Maybe we should ask that assistant?

Japanese: ‥‥ムリじゃないか、あの子じゃ。いろんな意味で。
English: I'm not sure we really want her fixing anything.

Japanese: あのクビ、クレーンでも使わなきゃビクともしそうにないし。
English: And you'd probably need a crane to move that thing.

Japanese: たしかにねー。
English: Probably so.

Japanese: このゲートをとおった人間を自動的に撮影するカメラだ。
English: That's the security camera that automatically takes pictures of people here.

Japanese: 事件当日は、被害者が殺害されるまでの時間に、２人撮影した。
English: It took two pictures on the day of the murder, before the actual time of death.

Japanese: １人は、トノサマン。もう１人は、小学生の少年。
English: One of them was the Steel Samurai. The other was a grade school boy.

Japanese: どうやら今日は作動してないみたいだ。
English: It doesn't seem to be switched on today.

Japanese: ちょっとつかれたな。‥‥座って休もうか。
English: I'm bushed. Maybe I'll sit down here for a bit.

Japanese: あーあ。これだからトシはとりたくないよね。
English: This is exactly why I don't want to get old, ever.

Japanese: ぼ、ぼくはまだワカいぞ！
English: H-hey! I'm still young! I'm just tired!

Japanese: なるほどくん。“若さ”を主張しだしたら、オシマイだよ。
English: Nick, when you start having to say "I'm still young," you know it's all over.

Japanese: （いやいや、ほんとうに若いんだってば！）
English: (But... but I AM still young!)

Japanese: 『おいでませ』だって。
English: It says "Welcome."

Japanese: 休みの日は、一般の人たちが遊びにくるからね。
English: They let people in here when they're not filming, right?

Japanese: なるほどくん。今度、遊びにこようか。
English: Nick! Let's come here some day! Whaddya say?

Japanese: あのオバチャンが入れてくれたら、ね。
English: Sure, if that security lady lets us in.

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥。...あのサルマゲどん。
English: That's "Mr. Monkey."

Japanese: 元気なときは、時間をアナウンスするの。
English: When it wasn't broken, it announced the time... in "ooks."

Japanese: “４時でごザル”とか。
English: One "ook" per hour. Ook ook ook ook. Always with the ooking.

Japanese: （サルだからなあ‥‥）
English: (It IS a monkey, after all.)

Japanese: あのクビを調べてみなさい。
English: Check its head.

Japanese: クビがこわれた時間で、時計が止まっているわ。
English: The clock inside stopped when it broke.

Japanese: ‥‥！...!なるほどくん。時計、２時１５分で止まってるよ。
English: Nick, it's stopped at 2:15.
That's right.

Japanese: この道は、２時１５分から４時すぎまでふさがっていたの。
English: This path was blocked from 2:15 till after 4:00.

Japanese: だから、アタクシたちは、無実。
English: Therefore, we're innocent.

Japanese: イブクロさんが第１スタジオで死んだのは、２時３０分‥‥か。
English: Mr. Hammer died in Studio One at 2:30...

Japanese: じゃ、そういうことだから。
English: See? Good bye.

Japanese: 《サルマゲどんのクビ》のデータを法廷記録にファイルした。
English: Mr. Monkey data added to the Court Record.

Japanese: ‥‥どうなってるの‥‥？
English: What do we do, Nick?

Japanese: ニボサブさん以外、イブクロさんを殺せた人、いないじゃない‥‥。
English: There isn't anyone besides WP who could have killed Hammer!

Japanese: もう‥‥だめだあ‥‥。
English: It's over... We're finished!

Japanese: （まいったな‥‥）
English: (It sure doesn't look good.)

Japanese: （一度、事務所へもどって考えてみるか‥‥）
English: (Guess we should head back to the office and plan our strategy...)

Japanese: この道は“第２スタジオ”に通じている。
English: This is the path that goes to Studio Two.

Japanese: 事件当日は、午後２時すぎから４時まで、
English: The mascot's head fell right on the path on the day of the murder.

Japanese: このサルのクビが道をしゃ断していたらしい。
English: The path was completely blocked between 2:00 and 4:00.

Japanese: たしかに、アタマの中には時計とメガホンがセットされてる。
English: I see. There's a clock and a speaker in the monkey's head.

Japanese: いつもなら《３時でごザル》とか言ってるのだろう。
English: It would usually tell the time by "ook"ing.

Japanese: 今、時計は２時１５分で止まっている。
English: The clock is stopped at 2:15 now.

Japanese: ひとやすみしたいところだが、そんな場合でもないだろう。
English: I really want to take a break, but we have work to do!

Japanese: しかし、誰もいないベンチがならんでいる図っていうのは、
English: Still, it's kind of sad to see so many empty benches...

Japanese: なんかこう、サビしいな‥‥。
English: Kind of like if you threw a party, and nobody came.

Japanese: “おいでませ”‥‥か。
English: "Welcome"...

Japanese: この事件が解決したら、ここも人があふれるのかな。
English: I wonder if the murder will make this place famous, too.

Japanese: １０月１８日　某時刻第１スタジオ内
English: October 18 Studio One

Japanese: ここ、あんまり来たくないな。
English: I... don't really want to be in here.

Japanese: まあ‥‥。そりゃ、殺人現場だし。
English: Yeah... It is a murder scene.

Japanese: あたし、他のところに行きたい。
English: I want to go someplace else. Nick?

Japanese: ‥‥やれやれ。
“書き割り”っていうやつだ。
English: Looks like a backdrop for a stage.

Japanese: これ、トノサマンで使う背景かな。
English: This must be used in the Steel Samurai show.

Japanese: そうだよ。“ネオ?フジヤマ”っていう山だね。
English: Of course! That's the mountain, Neo Fujiyama!

Japanese: トノサマンって、江戸の話だろ。なんで富士山が見えるんだ？
English: Doesn't the Steel Samurai take place in Olde Tokyo? Can you even see Mt. Fuji from there?

Japanese: 江戸じゃなくて、“ネオ?エドシティ”だからね。
English: Not Olde Tokyo, Nick! "Neo" Olde Tokyo!

Japanese: うわー。すごいなあ。高そうなカメラ。
English: Wow, look at that camera! That must cost a ton!

Japanese: お、おい。さわるなって。
English: Yeah! So don't touch it!

Japanese: なるほどくん、撮影してあげようか。
English: Hey, smile, Nick, you're a star!

Japanese: たのむからやめてくれ！
English: S-stop that!

Japanese: 衣袋　武志の死体の形が、ロープでマーキングされている。
English: They outlined where Jack Hammer's body lay with tape.

Japanese: 死体は怪人アクダイカーンの着ぐるみの中から発見されたため、
English: His body was found still inside the Evil Magistrate costume.

Japanese: その形どおりにマーキングされている。
English: I guess that's why the head part looks like a helmet.

Japanese: ヤリで刺されたのに血のあとが見られないのも、
English: Funny, I expected a spear stabbing would leave at least a few bloodstains.

Japanese: 着ぐるみの中で殺害されたためだろう。
English: I guess the costume must have absorbed most of it...

Japanese: あ、これ！　カントクさんが座るヤツだよね！
English: Hey! Look! That's the chair the director sits in!

Japanese: 一度、座ってみたかったんだよねー。
English: I've always wanted to sit in one of these.

Japanese: なるほどくん、あれは？
English: Hey Nick, take a look at that.

Japanese: カメラの前で、カチンて鳴らす、白黒のヤツ。
English: One of those black-and-white boards they clap at the start of filming!

Japanese: あと、メガホンメガホン。
English: And look! A megaphone! A real live megaphone!

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥...はい。なるほどくん。‥‥アクション！
English: Ready, Nick? Action!

Japanese: （‥‥ここまでヘンな子だとは思わなかったな‥‥）
English: (This girl is more troubled than I thought.)

Japanese: 撮影のとき、光を反射させるために使う。レフ板とかいったかな。
English: That's for reflecting light. They call it a reflecting screen or something like that.

Japanese: ふうん。
English: Huh.

Japanese: なんだよ。その気のなさそうなヘンジは。
English: What? Where's your enthusiasm for all things entertainment?

Japanese: だってあたし、やるならカントクかトノサマンがいいもん。
English: Well, lighting is fine, but I'd want to be the director. Or maybe the Steel Samurai!

Japanese: （何をやる気なんだこの子は‥‥）
English: (I think she's being serious...!)

Japanese: あ、ハシゴだ。
English: Look, a ladder!

Japanese: それはキャタツだよ。
English: That's a "step"-ladder.

Japanese: どう違うの？同じようなものじゃない。
English: So? What's the difference?

Japanese: もっとホンシツを見ようよ、なるほどくん。
English: You need to stop judging things based on narrow-minded cultural assumptions, Nick!

Japanese: ははは。
English: R-right... sorry. (This girl is OUT there!)

Japanese: また来ちゃったね‥‥殺人現場。
English: We're back at the scene of the crime.

Japanese: 調べることをすませたら、すぐ他のところ、行こうね。
English: Let's find what we need and get out of here quick, Nick.

Japanese: あ、これ！　カントクさんが座るヤツだよね！
English: Hey! Look! That's the chair the director sits in!

Japanese: 一度、座ってみたかったんだよねー。
English: I've always wanted to sit in one of these.

Japanese: （“カントクが座る”‥‥？）
English: (The director...!)

Japanese: 真宵ちゃん。
English: Maya...

Japanese: ちょっと、そのイスのあたり調べてみてよ。
English: Take a look around that chair for me, would you?

Japanese: さっき、カントクが言ってたろ。
English: Remember that script the director was talking about?

Japanese: “どこかに台本、置き忘れてきた”って‥‥。
English: Didn't he say he'd left it somewhere?

Japanese: ‥‥あっ！
English: Ahah!

Japanese: あったよ！　なるほどくん。‥‥台本。
English: Found it, Nick! The script!

Japanese: ‥‥やっぱりね。
English: Good work!

Japanese: 《台本》をポケットにつっこんだ。
English: Script placed in pocket.

Japanese: “書き割り”っていうやつだ。
English: A stage backdrop.

Japanese: 真宵ちゃんによると、“ネオ?フジヤマ”というらしい。
English: This is "Neo Fujiyama," according to Maya.

Japanese: どう見ても“絵”なんだけど、子供たちにはバレないのだろうか。
English: It looks incredibly fake, but I guess the kids don't mind.

Japanese: 撮影用の機材がならんでいる。
English: A collection of expensive film equipment.

Japanese: この中のどれか１つでもコワしたら、
English: I can't even begin to imagine how much just one of these costs.

Japanese: その日のうちに成歩堂法律事務所はツブれてしまうだろう。
English: If we broke one, we'd go bankrupt for sure.

Japanese: 衣袋　武志の死体の形が、ロープでマーキングされている。
English: They outlined where Jack Hammer's body lay with tape.

Japanese: 死体は怪人アクダイカーンの着ぐるみの中から発見されたため、
English: His body was found still inside the Evil Magistrate costume.

Japanese: その形どおりにマーキングされている。
English: I guess that's why the head part looks like a helmet.

Japanese: ヤリで刺されたのに血のあとが見られないのも、
English: Funny, I expected a spear stabbing would leave at least a few bloodstains.

Japanese: 着ぐるみの中で殺害されたためだろう。
English: I guess the costume must have absorbed most of it...

Japanese: ディレクターズ?チェア。カントクさんが座るイスだ。
English: A classic director's chair.

Japanese: あの宇在カントクのシリが、こんなイスにおさまるのだろうか？
English: I'm surprised they don't have an extra large one for Mr. Manella.

Japanese: 撮影のとき、光を反射させるために使うもの。
English: A panel for reflecting light.

Japanese: たしか、レフ板とかいったかな。
English: They call it a "reflecting board" or something.

Japanese: なんのヘンテツもないキャタツだ。
English: A perfectly normal stepladder.

Japanese: ぼくは高いところが大キライなので、のぼったことがない。
English: I've never actually used one. I don't like heights.

Japanese: ニンゲンは、地に足をつけて生活すべきだと思うな。
English: I'm a firm believer that humankind was made to keep both feet on the ground.

Japanese: １０月１８日　某時刻第２スタジオ前
English: October 18 Studio Two Entrance

Japanese: ここ、第２スタジオ、だよね？
English: This... is Studio Two?

Japanese: そのはずだけど。
English: I thought so...

Japanese: あんまり“スタジオ”って感じじゃないよねー。
English: It doesn't really look much like a "studio," does it?

Japanese: たしかになあ。
English: No, that it does not.

Japanese: あ‥‥。コテージがあるよ。
English: Hey... There's a trailer over there.

Japanese: 撮影用のセット‥‥にしてはシッカリ建てられているなあ。
English: It looks pretty sturdy for a movie set trailer.

Japanese: なるほどくん。ほんとにここ、スタジオなのかなあ‥‥？
English: Are we sure this is a studio?

Japanese: スタッフ移動用の、大型のライトバンだ。
English: A light van for moving staff and equipment around.

Japanese: よく手入れされていて、免許さえあれば、すぐ動かせる。
English: They've kept it in pretty good shape. Looks like it'd run fine.

Japanese: え！　じゃあ、じゃあ、ドライブしようよ、撮影所１周。
English: Well, let's go for a drive!

Japanese: 免許があれば、の話だよ。
English: Sure... if I had a license.

Japanese: うわあ。なるほどくん。免許、持ってないんだ。
English: Nick! You don't have a driver's license!?

Japanese: そのかわりに、ホラ。
English: But, look, I have this!

Japanese: こんなところで弁護士バッジつきつけたって、ダメだよ！
English: Why are you showing me your attorney's badge, Nick?

Japanese: ２人で食事をしたあとらしい。
English: Looks like two people ate here.

Japanese: 皿の上には、何も残っていない。
English: There's nothing left on the plates.

Japanese: あ。この人たちも食べたんだね、ホネつきステーキ。
English: Hey, so they ate t-bone steaks too.

Japanese: こだわるなあ、ステーキに‥‥。
English: What is it with steaks around here?

Japanese: そりゃ、ステーキは別バラだから。
English: Hey! There's always room for steak!

Japanese: ‥‥でも。なんかちょっと、ヘンだなあ‥‥。
English: But... something does seem out of place...

Japanese: 何が？
English: What?

Japanese: イワ感、ていうの？なんだろ、この感じ‥‥。
English: I... don't know. It just feels "odd."

Japanese: 大きく“２”と書いてある。
English: There's a big "2" painted here.

Japanese: ここが“第２スタジオ”だからじゃないかな。
English: Probably because this is Studio Two?

Japanese: ‥‥たぶんそうだ。スタジオには見えないけどね。
English: Probably... Though it doesn't look anything like a studio.

Japanese: 最近、取りつけられたらしい小さな焼却炉だ。
English: An incinerator. Apparently they just put this one in.

Japanese: コゲたススがこびりついていて、あまり手入れされていない。
English: It's already covered in soot. They must not clean it much.

Japanese: 撮影セットを組むための資材みたいだけど‥‥。
English: These look like materials for making a movie set.

Japanese: ずいぶん長いこと、置きっぱなしになっているみたい。
English: They've been sitting here for quite some time.

Japanese: まるで、資材置き場だね。
English: This might as well be a junkyard.

Japanese: よく見ると、建てられてから数年はたっているようだ。
English: On closer inspection, this trailer looks like it's been here for a while.

Japanese: これはもう、“セット”なんてもんじゃなさそうだ。
English: I don't think this is part of some temporary film set.

Japanese: 何やら花が咲いている。
English: There're some flowers here.

Japanese: わあ、キレイだねー。ちゃんと手入れされてるよ。
English: Wow, so pretty! They're taking good care of these.

Japanese: あんまり近づくなよ。その鉄柵、あぶないから。
English: Don't get to close. That fence looks dangerous.

Japanese: ねえねえ、なるほどくん！入ってみようよ！
English: Nick! Let's go inside!

Japanese: 怒られないかなあ‥‥。
English: You think it's okay...?

Japanese: ‥‥あれ。カギがかかってるよ。
English: Oh well. It's locked.

Japanese: うう‥‥つまんない。
English: Aww... shucks.

Japanese: 名もなき花が咲いている。
English: Nameless flowers bloom in solitude.

Japanese: もしかしたら名前はあるのかもしれないが、
English: They probably have a proper name...

Japanese: ぼくは一生、知ることなく終わるのだなあ。
English: But it is not for me to know. Not now, nor ever, till the end of my days.

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥。...イミもなくしみじみしてしまった。
English: I need a vacation.

Japanese: １０月１８日　某時刻第２スタジオ前
English: October 18 Studio Two Entrance

Japanese: （誰もいない、か‥‥）
English: (This place is deserted...)
*crash!*

Japanese: きゃっ！　ななな、なるほどくん。いいい、今の音、なに？
English: Eek! N-N-Nick! W-w-what was that noise!?

Japanese: コテージの中から聞こえてきたみたいだけど‥‥。
English: It sounded like it came from inside the trailer...

Japanese: ‥‥中に誰か、いるみたいだね。
English: Someone must be inside...

Japanese: す‥‥すみませーん。
English: H-Hello?

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥...返事がないぞ。
English: No answer.

Japanese: アヤしいよ、なるほどくん！
English: Pretty suspicious, if you ask me, Nick!

Japanese: よし‥‥入ってみよう！
English: Let's go in!

Japanese: 怒られないかなあ‥‥。
English: I'm not sure we should be barging in...

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥‥‥あれ。‥‥カギがかかってるよ。
English: ...Huh. It's locked.

Japanese: えー。カギは？
English: What? Don't we have a key?

Japanese: そりゃまあ、正門前の警備員の詰所にあるんじゃないか。
English: No. But there's probably one in the guard station at the main gate.

Japanese: 調査のためだよ。借りてこよう！
English: Then what are we waiting for? Let's borrow it!

Japanese: （貸してもらえるかなあ‥‥）
English: (If they'll let us...)

Japanese: さっき詰所から借りてきたカギ、使えるかな‥‥。
English: I wonder if the key we borrowed from the guard station will work?
It opened.

Japanese: よし！　行くよなるほどくん！
English: Great! Let's go, Nick!

Japanese: （お。やる気マンマンだな）
English: (She seems eager all of a sudden...)

Japanese: さ。お先にどうぞ。なるほどくん。
English: Well, you first, Nick!

Japanese: （コシは引けているみたいだ）
English: (Ah, not THAT eager.)

Japanese: コテージの入口だ。カギがかかっている。
English: The trailer door. It's locked.

Japanese: たぶん、カギは警備員の詰所にあると思うんだけど‥‥。
English: The key is probably in the security guard station.

Japanese: 借りられるかどうか、自信はない。
English: I doubt they'd let us borrow it.

Japanese: コテージへ入るドアだ。カギはかかっていない。
English: The trailer door. It's unlocked.

Japanese: スタッフ移動用の、大型のライトバンだ。
English: A light van for moving staff and equipment around.

Japanese: よく手入れされていて、免許さえあれば、すぐ動かせる。
English: They've kept it in pretty good shape. Looks like it'd run fine.

Japanese: ２人で食事をしたあとらしい。
English: Looks like two people ate here.

Japanese: 皿の上には、何も残っていない。
English: There's nothing left on the plates.

Japanese: 大きく“２”と書いてある。
English: There's a big "2" painted here.

Japanese: おそらくここが“第２スタジオ”だからなのだろう。
English: Probably because this is Studio Two?

Japanese: ‥‥あまり、“スタジオ”というフンイキでもないのだが。
English: Probably... Though it doesn't look anything like a studio.

Japanese: 最近、取りつけられたらしい小さな焼却炉だ。
English: An incinerator. Apparently they just put this one in.

Japanese: コゲたススがこびりついていて、あまり手入れされていない。
English: It's already covered in soot. They must not clean it much.

Japanese: 撮影セットを組むための資材みたいだけど‥‥。
English: These look like materials for making a movie set.

Japanese: ずいぶん長いこと、ほったらかしになっている。
English: They've been sitting here for quite some time.

Japanese: スタジオというよりは、資材置き場という感じだ。
English: This might as well be a junkyard.

Japanese: よく見ると、建てられてから数年はたっているようだ。
English: On closer inspection, this trailer looks like it's been here for a while.

Japanese: これはもう、“セット”なんてもんじゃなさそうだ。
English: I don't think this is part of some temporary film set.

Japanese: 名もなき花が咲いている。
English: Nameless flowers bloom in solitude.

Japanese: もしかしたら名前はあるのかもしれないが、
English: They probably have a proper name...

Japanese: ぼくは一生、知ることなく終わるのだなあ。
English: But it is not for me to know. Not now, nor ever till the end of my days.

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥。...イミもなくしみじみしてしまった。
English: I need a vacation.

Japanese: ドアにはカギがかかっていない。
English: The door is unlocked.

Japanese: 今なら、自由に入ることができそうだ。
English: We could probably just walk right in.

Japanese: １０月１８日　某時刻第２スタジオ?コテージ内
English: October 18 Studio Two Trailer
Eek!

Japanese: びっくりしたぁ！
English: S-someone's in here!

Japanese: 誰。
English: Names.

Japanese: え。あ、あたしたち、二、ニボサブさんの弁護士ですけど。
English: O-our n-names? Um, w-we're WP's lawyers, and, um...

Japanese: そう。
English: I see.
A-and who might you...?

Japanese: 姫神。‥‥プロデューサーの。
English: Dee Vasquez. The producer.

Japanese: （スラリとした‥‥キレイなヒトだな‥‥）
English: (Dee Vasquez... She's quite beautiful.)

Japanese: なるほどくん、もう行こう！他のところ、調べようよ。
English: Nick, let's get out of here. Isn't there someplace else we have to check?

Japanese: そうだな。
English: Y-yeah.

Japanese: ちょっと。
English: Wait.

Japanese: な、なんですか？
English: Y-yes?

Japanese: ウザイに会ったら、これ、わたしておいて。
English: If you see Manella, give him this.

Japanese: な、なんであたしたちがそんなコト‥‥！
English: W-why do we have to do your errands...!
I... I don't think I like her. *sniff*

Japanese: 泣くなよ。
English: Don't cry. She'll take it as a sign of weakness.

Japanese: わかりました。見かけたら、わたしておきます。
English: Alright. We'll give it to him if we see him.

Japanese: 《姫神のメモ》を法廷記録に挟んだ。
English: Vasquez's Memo added to the Court Record.

Japanese: 古びたポスターだ。
English: An old movie poster.

Japanese: “ダイナマイト侍主演、衣袋　武志”か。
English: "'Dynamite Samurai' starring Jack Hammer."

Japanese: 被害者のイブクロさん、昔はすごかったんだなあ。
English: I guess Mr. Hammer used to be quite the star.

Japanese: “武者ぶるいの夏主演、衣袋　武志”。
English: "'Samurai Summer' starring Jack Hammer."

Japanese: これまたずいぶん古いポスターだな。
English: This poster's quite old.

Japanese: あー、これ！なつかしいなあ。
English: Wow! It's been years since I saw that show!

Japanese: 誰にでもある“ひと夏のロマンス”がテーマなんだよね。
English: It was one of those typical summer romance stories... but it was great!

Japanese: テーマはともかく、それがなんでサムライにウマなんだよ。
English: A typical summer romance... with a samurai on a horse?

Japanese: 昔の映画のポスターだな。“武士節　主演、衣袋　武志”
English: Another old movie poster. "'The Singing Samurai' starring Jack Hammer."

Japanese: “武士節”って、やっぱり“ぶしぶし”って読むのかな。
English: What the hell do samurai sing about? Chopping off heads?

Japanese: あー、それ！
English: Oh. My. God.

Japanese: ジマンのノドで大スターにのし上がる、武士の話だよ。
English: That movie was the best! It's about a samurai who sings his way to fame and fortune!

Japanese: イブクロさん、歌もうまかったからね。
English: Mr. Hammer was a very accomplished singer, you know.

Japanese: スターにのし上がる話なのに、なんで時代が室町なんだよ‥‥。
English: Singing samurai stars... What's next? Ninja ballet?

Japanese: ‥‥特に手がかりになるものは入っていないな。
English: Nothing in here that could be a clue.

Japanese: 紙コップとか、丸めた資料なんかが捨ててあるだけだ。
English: Just some paper cups and crumpled documents.

Japanese: ここで会議をしていたらしい。
English: This is where they had that meeting.

Japanese: 机の上には、資料にお茶、紙コップなどが並んでいる。
English: There are documents and paper cups scattered around.

Japanese: くもりガラスで外は見えない。
English: The glass is frosted. I can't see outside.

Japanese: １０月も半ばをすぎて、陽ざしもすっかりやわらいできた。
English: This far into October, the dimming daylight makes it feel like winter's coming.

Japanese: トノサマンで使うセットのデザインを決めていたようだ。
English: It looks like they were designing a set to use for the Steel Samurai show.

Japanese: セットを組む予算なんかもメモされている。
English: They've got some figures for how much the set costs...

Japanese: ‥‥なんでこんなにお金がかかるんだ‥‥？
English: I can't believe they spend that much money on those sets!

Japanese: 事件当日のことなんですけど‥‥。
English: I was wondering if you could tell me about the day of the murder...
M-Ms. Vasquez?

Japanese: 台本。
English: Script.

Japanese: は、はい？
English: E-excuse me?

Japanese: 台本、探してるの。
English: Script. I'm looking for a script.

Japanese: だ、台本、ですか？
English: A s-script?

Japanese: トノサマンの第１３話。
English: "The Steel Samurai, Episode 13."

Japanese: 今、読みたいの。
English: I need it.

Japanese: あのお、事件があった日のこと、聞きたいんですけど。
English: Umm... could we ask you a bit about the day of the murder?

Japanese: アタクシは、台本を読みたいの。
English: I need to read it.
(We're getting nowhere fast.)

Japanese: あの、あの、トノサマンについて話が聞きたいんですけど！
English: Um, w-we'd really like to ask you about the Steel Samurai!
Ms. Vasquez?

Japanese: テレビ、やってるわ。毎週。
English: It's on TV. Every week.

Japanese: 言うべきことは全部、そこで言ってるから。
English: That's all I have to say about that.

Japanese: なるほどくん！テレビ見てろって！
English: N-Nick! She's telling us to go watch TV!? The nerve of her!

Japanese: ‥‥ぼくに怒るなよ。
English: Hey, don't get mad at me.

Japanese: カントク‥‥えーと、宇在さん、でしたっけ。
English: About the director... Sal Manella, was it?

Japanese: 彼は、どういう‥‥？
English: What, er, exactly is his role here...?

Japanese: 言わなかったかしら。
English: Perhaps I didn't make myself clear?

Japanese: アタクシ、今、台本を探しているの。
English: I'm looking for a script.

Japanese: 他のことは、興味がないわ。
English: I can't be bothered with anything else.
Nick?

Japanese: 撮影所って、こんなヘンなのしかいないの？
English: Are all people in the entertainment business this... weird?

Japanese: ‥‥ううん‥‥たしかに。
English: It's starting to look like it.

Japanese: 事件当日、道がふさがっていたんですか？
English: On the day of the murder, the path that leads here was blocked?

Japanese: ここへ来る途中、サルマゲどん、見たでしょ？
English: You saw Mr. Monkey on the way here, correct?

Japanese: さ‥‥“サルマゲどん”？
English: M-"Mr. Monkey"?

Japanese: クビの折れたサルよ。
English: The monkey with the broken head.

Japanese: ああ‥‥あれですか‥‥。（すごいネーミングだな‥‥）
English: Oh, right, that... (What an original name...)

Japanese: あのクビ、事件があった日に風でこわれてね。
English: Its head fell over in the wind on the day of the murder.

Japanese: クビをどかす作業が始まったのが３時すぎ。
English: They didn't start moving the head out of the way until after 3:00.

Japanese: 道を通れるようになったのが、４時すぎだったの。
English: It was after 4:00 by the time the path was unblocked.

Japanese: いい。
English: Capice?

Japanese: イブクロが死んだのは、２時半。
English: Everyone in this trailer was stuck here until the path was cleared.

Japanese: でも、ここにいた人間は、午後４時すぎまで、
English: Stuck in this trailer. Stuck until after 4:00.

Japanese: このコテージに閉じこめられていたの。
English: Hammer died at 2:30.

Japanese: 第１スタジオになんか、行けたはずがないわ。
English: Thus, none of us could have gone to Studio One.

Japanese: な、なんですって！
English: W-what!?

Japanese: これはホント。
English: It's true.

Japanese: ３時すぎに、クレーンを使ってクビをどけてもらったの。
English: A crane came just after 3:00 to move the head.

Japanese: そとの会社の人にたのんでね。作業した人たちは覚えてるはず。
English: We called some people in to clear the way. I'm sure they'd corroborate my story.

Japanese: で、でも！
English: B-but wait!

Japanese: クビがこわれたのが、２時３０分より後だったら、
English: What if the head fell over after 2:30?

Japanese: 第１スタジオへ行くことはできたはずです！
English: Then you could have gone to Studio One!

Japanese: ２時３０分‥‥イブクロが死んだ時間ね。
English: 2:30... the time of death.
Very well.

Japanese: 来なさい。
English: Come.

Japanese: 持ってきましたよ。はい！　台本です！
English: Here, we found it! Your script!

Japanese: そう。
English: Ah.

Japanese: 《台本》を姫神さんに渡した。
English: Script handed to Dee Vasquez.
Umm... uh...

Japanese: おしまいですか、それで。
English: You're not going to talk to us?

Japanese: 静かに。
English: Quiet.

Japanese: 今、台本を読んでるから。
English: I'm reading.

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥！...!ちょっと！イイカゲンにしてください！
English: Just you hold on! What's the big idea!?

Japanese: ナニサマのつもりですか！あた、あたしたちは‥‥！
English: Who do you think you are anyway!? And, and do you even know who we are!?

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥。...荷星の弁護士？
English: Powers's lawyers?

Japanese: ‥‥は、はい。
English: Umm... right.

Japanese: うたがってるの？　アタクシを。
English: Am I a suspect?

Japanese: あ、いえ、その。‥‥そういうワケじゃあ‥‥。
English: N-no, it's just, well, no, but...

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥。...事件のあった日のこと、だったかしら？
English: You wanted to know about the day of the murder?

Japanese: は、はい。聞かせてください。
English: Y-yes. Anything you could tell us would be a big help.

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥。...正午から、ここで会議があったのは知ってるわね？
English: You know there was a meeting here at noon?

Japanese: はい。カントクとか、テレビ局の人たちといっしょに‥‥。
English: Yes. With the director and the people from the network...

Japanese: そう。
English: Correct.

Japanese: じゃあ、よく聞きなさい。
English: Now, listen closely.

Japanese: あの日の午後、このコテージにいた人間は、
English: None of the people in this trailer that afternoon went to Studio One.

Japanese: 誰も、第１スタジオに行くことは不可能だったの。
English: It was impossible for us to leave.

Japanese: な、なぜですか？
English: Impossible? Why?

Japanese: 道がふさがってたから。
English: The path was blocked.

Japanese: ‥‥道が？
English: The... path?

Japanese: ‥‥興味ないわ。
English: I'm not interested.

Japanese: １０月１８日　某時刻第２スタジオ?コテージ内
English: October 18 Studio Two Trailer

Japanese: あら、こんにちは。
English: Oh, hi.

Japanese: あ、どうも。こんなところで、何を‥‥？
English: Hello. What brings you here?

Japanese: あ、ええ。ちょっと、資料の整理を‥‥。
English: Oh, well, I had to arrange some stuff...
Umm...

Japanese: はい？
English: Yes?

Japanese: そちらのカノジョ‥‥なんか、フンイキ、変わりましたねー。
English: The, um, girl with you. Doesn't she seem a little different?

Japanese: まるで別人みたい。
English: Like, is she even the same person as before?

Japanese: 気のせいですよ。
English: Um, nope! She's the same as always.

Japanese: そうですよ。
English: Same as always.

Japanese: ‥‥違うと思うけど‥‥。
English: Okay... whatever you say...

Japanese: “武者ぶるいの夏主演、衣袋　武志”。
English: "'Samurai Summer' starring Jack Hammer."

Japanese: これまたずいぶん古いポスターだな。
English: This poster looks like it's been hanging here for years.

Japanese: それ、真宵が好きな映画よ。
English: This is one of Maya's favorite movies.

Japanese: うちの事務所のポスター、これにしましょうか。
English: Maybe we should replace the poster in the office with this one?

Japanese: それは‥‥ちょっとカナシイかな。
English: That... would be kind of sad.

Japanese: 昔の映画のポスターだな。“武士節　主演、衣袋　武志”
English: Another old movie poster. "'The Singing Samurai' starring Jack Hammer."

Japanese: “武士節”って、やっぱり“ぶしぶし”って読むのかな。
English: What the hell do samurai sing about? Chopping off heads?

Japanese: 室町時代をブタイにしたミュージカル映画よ。
English: It's a samurai musical set in ancient Japan.

Japanese: ミュ、ミュージカルですか。
English: A s-samurai musical? Is that even a genre!?

Japanese: ‥‥千尋さん、けっこうくわしいんですね。
English: Well, I'm impressed. You know your stuff, Mia.

Japanese: 真宵が大好きだから、そのおかげでちょっと、ね。
English: Oh, Maya's the real fan. I just picked a little up.

Japanese: あれから、あの子は見かけた‥‥？
English: Have you seen that boy since then?

Japanese: いいえ。
English: Nope.

Japanese: さっきまで、タイヘンだったんですよ。
English: You missed it, though.

Japanese: 警備員のオバチャン、あの子を追いかけ回してたんですけど、
English: The security lady was chasing around after the boy so fast I thought she'd collapse.

Japanese: 結局、逃がしちゃって。‥‥すごく怒ってました。
English: Of course, he got away in the end. Boy, was she mad!

Japanese: うん。ぼくたちも見たよ。
English: Actually, we saw them. How's the old windbag doing?

Japanese: さっき、詰所でようかんをニギりつぶしてました。
English: She was squeezing donuts through her clenched fists back at the guard station.

Japanese: （オバチャンも、そこまでムキになるなよ‥‥）
English: (Some people take their jobs a little too seriously.)

Japanese: カベのポスター、みんなイブクロさんの‥‥？
English: Are all the posters on the wall here of Hammer?

Japanese: そうですよ。‥‥ホント、こんなことになってザンネンです。
English: Yes. It's really a terrible loss for the studios.

Japanese: でも、最近は人気が落ちていた、とか‥‥。
English: But... his popularity had been waning recently, right?

Japanese: そうですね‥‥。
English: That's true.

Japanese: ‥‥あのことがあってから、ですね‥‥。
English: Then again, after what happened...

Japanese: “あのこと”‥‥？
English: After WHAT happened?

Japanese: 弁護士さん、知らないんですか？イブクロさんのこと‥‥。
English: Y-you mean, you don't know about Hammer...?

Japanese: ‥‥知らないけど。
English: No, what?

Japanese: ゴ、ゴメンなさい。
English: I-I'm sorry.

Japanese: 私、よけいなこと、言っちゃったみたい‥‥。
English: I shouldn't have said anything.

Japanese: 気になるなあ。‥‥何？　“あのこと”って。
English: Wait, now you have to tell me. What happened with Hammer?

Japanese: す、すみません。
English: I-I'm sorry.

Japanese: わ、私の口からは‥‥。
English: I'm probably not the one who should tell you.

Japanese: （やれやれ。また“口どめ”ってヤツか‥‥）
English: (Uh oh. Why do I get the feeling something's being covered up here?)

Japanese: ‥‥さあ。
English: Umm...

Japanese: すみません。私には、なんのことか‥‥。
English: Sorry. I'm really not sure what that is.

Japanese: あの、これ、どう思う？
English: Do you know anything about this?

Japanese: あ。トノサマンのトレーディングカード。
English: Hey, that's a Steel Samurai trading card.

Japanese: 実は、私も集めてるんですよ。
English: I collect those, actually.

Japanese: あと１枚でコンプリートするんですよねー。
English: I'm one card away from a complete set!

Japanese: こんぷりーと？
English: A complete set?

Japanese: カードを全種類、そろえることです。
English: Yeah, a set of one of each card.

Japanese: ‥‥へえ。
English: Huh...

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥‥‥...あ、ああああああああああああッ！
English: Wait...Waitwaitwaitwait!!!

Japanese: これ！　これ！　このカード！ずっと探していた、最後の１枚！
English: That's it!!! That's the card I need! You don't know how long I've been looking for it!

Japanese: あ、あの‥‥。
English: Uh...

Japanese: は、はしたない女だと思われるかもしれません。でも！
English: I don't care what kind of girl you might think I am, I need that card!

Japanese: おねがいします！　できましたら、私と、その、トレードを‥‥。
English: P-please! I beg you! Please trade with me!

Japanese: ト、トレード？
English: T-trade with you?

Japanese: 交換です！
English: Yes, trade cards!

Japanese: あの、私、レアカードがあまっているんです。
English: Look, I'll even trade you an Ultra Rare Premium card!

Japanese: レ、レアカード？
English: Trade me a... huh?

Japanese: 珍しいカードのことです。なにとぞ、おねがいします！
English: Yes, it's a good deal for your Ultra Rare!

Japanese: なにとぞォッ！
English: Please!!!

Japanese: なるほどくん！　オトメにハジをかかせてはダメよ！
English: Phoenix! Can't you see she's desperate?

Japanese: （‥‥なんなんだ、この意味不明なモリアガリは！）
English: (W-what's everybody getting so excited about...?)

Japanese: いいですよ。
English: Okay.

Japanese: ホントですかッ！うれしいッ！
English: Really!? Th-thanks!!!

Japanese: じゃ、これ。はい。
English: Then this is for you.

Japanese: 《レアカード》を法廷記録にはさんだ。
English: URP Card added to the Court Record.

Japanese: きゃっほう！
English: Yaa-hoo!

Japanese: （行っちまった‥‥）
English: (And there she goes...)

Japanese: ‥‥いいことをすると、すがすがしい気持ちになりますね。
English: Doesn't doing good things like that make you feel great?

Japanese: ほんとにそうですね。
English: Yeah. Yeah, it does.

Japanese: ‥‥いや、これ、ぼくのものじゃないから‥‥。
English: No... no, I can't. See, this card isn't really even mine.
I... I see.

Japanese: そうですよね‥‥
English: O-of course.

Japanese: そんなもんですよね‥‥
English: Of course it wouldn't be yours.

Japanese: そんなもんなんですよね‥‥
English: Of course you couldn't trade it with me.

Japanese: どうせそんなもんなんですよね‥
English: Of course I won't ever get that card, ever.

Japanese: 私なんてどうせそんなもんなんです
English: Of course I don't even really deserve that card.

Japanese: なるほどくん、どうするんですか！心神喪失状態になってますよ！
English: Phoenix, do something! She's blacking out!

Japanese: わ、わ、わかりましたよ。交換しましょう。
English: O-o-okay, okay! I'll trade!

Japanese: １０月１９日　午前９時４２分地方裁判所　被告人第１控え室
English: October 19, 9:42 AM District Court Defendant Lobby No. 1
Umm... Mr. Wright?

Japanese: はい？
English: Yes?

Japanese: さっきから、なんでそんなケワしいカオを‥‥？
English: Why do you look so... unhappy?

Japanese: あ‥‥いえ。ははは。
English: O-oh! Oh, nothing! Eh heh heh.

Japanese: なんでもないですよ‥‥
English: Really, it's nothing.

Japanese: ね、千尋さん‥‥。
English: Right, Mia?

Japanese: （‥‥うわ、コワいカオ‥‥）
English: (Uh oh. She looks even unhappier...)

Japanese: なるほどくん。
English: Phoenix.

Japanese: あなたの依頼人は今、ほとんど死にかけているわ。
English: Your client is now practically a dead man walking...

Japanese: そういう意味で、彼にはとても親近感をおぼえるわね。
English: Perhaps that's why I feel particularly close to him.

Japanese: 悪い冗談はやめてくださいよ‥‥。
English: This is no time for dead- people jokes, please.

Japanese: 今回は、こちらに武器がまったくないわ。
English: You know we're going into this trial utterly defenseless.

Japanese: でも、荷星さんが本当に無実ならば、
English: Yet, if Mr. Powers is truly innocent...

Japanese: 私たちは、どこかで見落としをしているはず‥‥。
English: We should be able to find something overlooked in the evidence to prove it.

Japanese: “見落とし”‥‥。
English: Something "overlooked"...?

Japanese: この法廷で、それを見つけ出せなければ、
English: We have to find something, Phoenix, today.

Japanese: 私たちは負けるわ。‥‥今日中に、かならず。
English: It's that, or lose the trial.

Japanese: （キビシイ戦いになるな‥‥）
English: (This isn't going to be easy.)

Japanese: １０月１９日　午前１０時地方裁判所　第４法廷
English: October 19, 10:00 AM District Court Courtroom No. 4

Japanese: これより、荷星　三郎の法廷を開廷します。
English: The court is now in session for the trial of Mr. Will Powers.

Japanese: 検察側、準備完了しております。
English: The prosecution is ready, Your Honor.

Japanese: 弁護側も、準備完了しています。
English: The defense is ready, Your Honor.

Japanese: では、御剣検事。冒頭弁論をおねがいします。
English: Very well. Mr. Edgeworth, your opening statement, please.

Japanese: 昨日の法廷で、意外な事実があきらかになった。
English: An unexpected fact has come to the attention of the court.

Japanese: 事件当日、英都撮影所には、他にも関係者がいた、という。
English: Yesterday, we learned that there were other people present at the studios!

Japanese: 今日は、彼らが、事件には関係なかったことを証明したい。
English: Today, I would like to show evidence proving they had nothing to do with the murder.

Japanese: ‥‥わかりました。
English: Very well.

Japanese: では、さっそく証人を呼んでいただきましょう。
English: You may call your first witness, Mr. Edgeworth.

Japanese: （あのプロデューサーが出てくるのかな‥‥）
English: (I wonder if that producer is going to come out...)

Japanese: 最初の証人、宇在　拓也を入廷させていただきたい。
English: The prosecution calls Mr. Sal Manella to the stand.

Japanese: （‥‥カントクが来たか‥‥）
English: (... or that director.)

Japanese: 証人。名前と職業は？
English: Will the witness state his name and profession?

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥。...なんだよぅ。シッケイだなぁ！（怒）
English: How r00d can j00 get!

Japanese: ボクを知らないのかよぅ！
English: J00 don't know ME!?

Japanese: カントクだぞぅ！　トノサマンは、ボクが作ってるんだぞぅ！（爆）
English: I'm the director! I make the Steel Samurai, n00b! ROFL!

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥‥‥‥...う、宇在　拓也。カ、カントクしてます。テレビの。
English: S... Sal Manella. I'm a director. Television.

Japanese: 証人は事件当日、英都撮影所にいたんですね？
English: Were you at Global Studios on the day of the murder?

Japanese: は、はい。
English: Y-yes, Your Honor.

Japanese: ‥‥ふむう。
English: Hmm...

Japanese: では、その日のことを《証言》してもらいましょうか。
English: Very well. Please testify to the court about the events of that day.

Japanese: なるほどくん。
English: Phoenix.

Japanese: まずは、この証人の証言をくずしましょう。
English: Let's start by picking this testimony apart.

Japanese: 荷星さんが犯人でないとしたら、
English: If Powers is innocent, you know what that means.

Japanese: あやしいのは、あの日コテージにいた人たちですから。
English: Someone in the trailer on that day did it!

Japanese: は、はい。（いきなりプレッシャーだ‥‥）
English: R-right. (She doesn't waste any time putting on the pressure!)

Japanese: ～事件があった日のこと～
English: -- The Day of the Murder --

Japanese: あの日は、朝の９時ごろ、英都スタジオに入ったけど。
English: I was at the studios from around 9:00 that morning.

Japanese: 午前中は、んーと、アクションの打ち合わせ、かな。（考）
English: During the morning, I was doing... umm... an action scene run-through.

Japanese: なんか、思ったより、時間がかかっちゃったんだよなあ。
English: It took a lot more time than I thought it would.

Japanese: おヒル、みんなはスタッフエリアでゴハン食べたみたいだけど。
English: I hear that everyone else ate lunch in the Employee Area...

Japanese: コテージでカイギがあったから、ボク、食べられなくて。
English: But I had a meeting in the Studio Two trailer, so I ended up skipping lunch.

Japanese: カイギは、ケッキョク４時までやってたのかな。
English: We were in the meeting until around 4:00...

Japanese: カイギ中は、‥‥えーと、誰も席を、その、立たなかったな。
English: During the meeting, well, I'm pretty sure no one left their chairs.

Japanese: ‥‥そんなところかな。
English: That's about it.

Japanese: ふむう‥‥。
English: Hmm...

Japanese: 被害者のイブクロさんが殺害されたのが、２時３０分ごろ。
English: The time of Mr. Hammer's death was 2:30 PM.

Japanese: そのころは、会議中だったわけですか‥‥。
English: And according to your testimony, you were in a meeting at that time.

Japanese: では、弁護人。‥‥《尋問》を。
English: Mr. Wright, you may begin your cross-examination.

Japanese: はい。
English: Yes, Your Honor.

Japanese: ～事件があった日のこと～
English: -- The Day of the Murder --

Japanese: あの日は、朝の９時ごろ、英都スタジオに入ったけど。
English: I was at the studios from around 9:00 that morning.

Japanese: 午前中は、んーと、アクションの打ち合わせ、かな。（考）
English: During the morning, I was doing... umm... an action scene run-through.

Japanese: なんか、思ったより、時間がかかっちゃったんだよなあ。
English: It took a lot more time than I thought it would.

Japanese: おヒル、みんなはスタッフエリアでゴハン食べたみたいだけど。
English: I hear that everyone else ate lunch in the Employee Area...

Japanese: コテージでカイギがあったから、ボク、食べられなくて。
English: But I had a meeting in the Studio Two trailer, so I ended up skipping lunch.

Japanese: カイギは、ケッキョク４時までやってたのかな。
English: We were in the meeting until around 4:00...

Japanese: カイギ中は、‥‥えーと、誰も席を、その、立たなかったな。
English: During the meeting, well, I'm pretty sure no one left their chairs.

Japanese: なるほどくん。
English: Phoenix.

Japanese: このアリバイが成立しちゃったら、荷星さんはオシマイよ。
English: If his alibi holds, Powers is finished.

Japanese: なんとかゆさぶって、‥‥情報を引き出すの。
English: Press him as hard as you can and find out where he's lying!

Japanese: は、はい！
English: R-right!

Japanese: いつも、その時間に？
English: Is that when you always come in to work?

Japanese: いやあ。‥‥時間はバラバラかな。それが“ギョーカイ人”だよ。
English: Oh, oh no. I come in all sort of hours. It's the "industry" way.

Japanese: （“ギョーカイ人”って、そういう意味だったのか‥‥）
English: (Yeah, I know all about the freaks in your "industry"...)

Japanese: でも、あの日は１日、忙しかったなあ‥‥。（泣）
English: Still, FWIW, I was pretty busy all that day. :(

Japanese: それを聞かせていただきたい。
English: Tell us more about that.

Japanese: スタッフエリアで？
English: In the Employee Area?

Japanese: そうだよ。
English: That's right.

Japanese: トノサマンとアクダイカーンの決闘シーン。
English: It was a fight scene between the Steel Samurai and the Evil Magistrate.

Japanese: その打ち合わせ、他には、誰がいましたか？
English: Was anyone else at the run-through?

Japanese: そういえば、警備員のオバチャンが、じーっと見ていたなあ。（笑）
English: Oh, yeah. That security lady was sitting there watching the whole thing. LOL.

Japanese: イブクロちゃんに茶色い声援を送っていたよ。
English: She was cheering on Hammer, if you can believe it.

Japanese: （“茶色い声援”ねえ‥‥）
English: (She certainly has the lungs for it...)

Japanese: 他には、誰も‥‥？
English: Anyone else?

Japanese: うん。‥‥それだけ。
English: Nope, just us four.

Japanese: それは、どうしてですか？
English: Why did it go on so long?

Japanese: えーと、知ってると思うけど。
English: Um, I'm pretty sure j00've already heard about this...

Japanese: ニボサブちゃんが、足をヒネっちゃって。
English: But WP twisted his ankle during the run-through, see...

Japanese: （‥‥ああ。そんな話があったな‥‥）
English: (Oh, right, thus the limp.)

Japanese: おかげで、おヒル、食べそこなっちゃった。（恨）
English: Which of course led to me missing my lunch. ROFS!

Japanese: “おヒル”‥‥ですか。
English: (ROFS? Rolling on the Floor... Starving?)

Japanese: “ゴハン”ですか。メニューは、何を‥‥？
English: What exactly did they have for lunch?

Japanese: ホネつきステーキだよ。‥‥スタッフに焼かせてさぁ。
English: T-bone steak! The assistant cooked some up.

Japanese: （そういえば、スタッフエリアにホネの乗った皿があったっけ）
English: (Come to think of it, there was a plate with some bones on it in the Employee Area.)

Japanese: “みんなは食べた”ということは、証人は食べなかったというわけだ。
English: "Everyone else" meaning that the witness did not eat with them.

Japanese: じゃあケッキョク、ホネつきステーキは食べずじまいですか？
English: So, in the end, you didn't get to eat?

Japanese: そうなんだよ。ステーキはね。‥‥ヒドい話だろ？
English: Yeah. No steak, at least! Can j00 believe it?

Japanese: ‥‥はあ。
English: That must have been tough.

Japanese: なるほどくん。
English: Phoenix.

Japanese: はい？
English: Yeah?

Japanese: 今の話、どう？オカシイと思わない？
English: Doesn't something about that seem odd to you?

Japanese: そうですね。‥‥別に、オカシイとは思いませんけど。
English: Well, no, not really. Seems pretty straightforward.

Japanese: そう。‥‥私の思い違いかしらね。
English: Oh. Perhaps I was wrong, then.

Japanese: ええ。実は、ちょっと気になることがあるんですよ。
English: Actually, there is something bothering me.

Japanese: カントク。今、“ステーキは”食べなかったと言いましたが、
English: Mr. Manella, just now you said "steak, at least."

Japanese: それ以外のものは食べたわけですか？
English: So you ate something other than steak?

Japanese: お！　よくわかったねぇ！
English: Yeah, good call!

Japanese: ポケットにチョコがあったからさ。それで、飢えをしのいだんだ。
English: I had some chocolate in my pocket, which I used to stave off starvation!

Japanese: ‥‥な、なるほど。
English: I see...

Japanese: （たいしたことじゃなかったな‥‥もっと何かあると思うんだけど）
English: (Is that all? I was sure I was on to something there.)

Japanese: そうですね。たしかにヘンです。
English: Yeah, it does seem odd, now that you mention it.

Japanese: カントク！
English: Mr. Manella!

Japanese: え、えっ！な、なんだよぅ‥‥（汗）
English: H-hmm? W-what do j00 mean? *sweats*

Japanese: ぼくがコテージに行ったとき、テーブルの上には、
English: When I went to that trailer, I saw something on the table.

Japanese: スタッフエリアと同じ皿が２人前、ありましたよ。
English: There were two plates on the table--the same kind of plates as in the Employee Area.

Japanese: あれは、誰が食べたんですか？
English: Who ate lunch there?

Japanese: う、うがッ！
English: M-mmpf!

Japanese: いや、あの、その、どの、この‥‥よ、よくわかったねぇ。（汗）
English: No, er, ah, um, eh heh. Good call! *sweats*

Japanese: その‥‥はずかしいから言わなかったんだケド‥‥。
English: I, er, I was embarrassed so I didn't mention it...

Japanese: 食べたんだよね、ケッキョク。
English: But I did eat, after all.

Japanese: ホネつきステーキを、ですか。
English: A t-bone steak, you mean?

Japanese: まァね。せっかくスタッフに焼かせたんだし。
English: Yeah, well, I mean the assistant went through all that trouble...

Japanese: あとで食べようと思って、持って行ったから、コテージに。
English: I brought it to the trailer, thinking I could eat it later.

Japanese: （食いイジが張ってるなあ‥‥）
English: (Clearly a man who likes to eat. I'd suspected as much...)

Japanese: で？　いつ食べたんですか？
English: So, when exactly did you eat it?

Japanese: カイギ中に、１回だけ休憩があったからさ。
English: We took one break during that meeting.

Japanese: そのときに、ペロリと。（汁）
English: I, er, wolfed it down then. *drool*

Japanese: （キタナイなあ‥‥）
English: (A mental image I will carry with me to my grave...)

Japanese: （でも‥‥待てよ）
English: (Wait a second...!)

Japanese: （休憩をとったとなると、証言におかしいところが出てくるぞ）
English: (If they took a break in that meeting, that contradicts his testimony!)

Japanese: （‥‥もう一度、そのへんをゆさぶってみるか‥‥）
English: (I'll press on that one a bit more...)

Japanese: なんの話をしていたんですか？
English: What were you discussing?

Japanese: トノサマンの、これからのストーリーとか、予算のこと。
English: The Steel Samurai story, and our budget.

Japanese: スポンサーの連中、このボクに向かってさぁ、
English: Get this! The sponsors turn to me and say, "Mr. Manella."

Japanese: “キミの書くシナリオは、コドモ向けじゃないねえ”だって。
English: "Those scripts of yours are not appropriate for children." Can j00 believe it!?

Japanese: （なんか、わかる気がする‥‥）
English: (Actually, I kind of can.)

Japanese: あの。誰か途中で席をはずしたりしなかったんですか？
English: So, nobody left the table during the whole meeting?

Japanese: 一度も休憩をとらなかったんですか？
English: You didn't take a single break?

Japanese: えーとぉ‥‥。
English: Er... well...

Japanese: そ、そうさぁ！とらなかったぞぉ！（汗）
English: Y-yeah! Not a one! *sweats*

Japanese: （‥‥“（汗）”がちょっと気になるけど‥‥）
English: (Hmm... what's he sweating so much about, I wonder?)

Japanese: （キメ手がないからなあ‥‥）
English: (If only I had an idea...)

Japanese: （何かつかんでから、あとでもう一度、ゆさぶってみるか）
English: (I'll wait for one to come up and hit him with it later.)

Japanese: 一度も休憩をとらなかったんですか？
English: You didn't take a single break?

Japanese: えーとぉ‥‥。
English: Er... well...

Japanese: そ、そうさぁ！とらなかったぞぉ！（汗）
English: Y-yeah! Not a one! *sweats*

Japanese: （‥‥“（汗）”がちょっと気になるけど‥‥）
English: (Hmm... what's he sweating so much about, I wonder?)

Japanese: （キメ手がないからなあ‥‥）
English: (If only I had an idea...)

Japanese: （‥‥って、待てよ！）
English: (Wait a second...)

Japanese: （なんか、引っかかるような‥‥）
English: (Maybe I do have an idea!)

Japanese: （‥‥いや。気のせいだろ）
English: (No, on second thought, I'm drawing a blank.)

Japanese: （ヘタにつっかかって、気を悪くさせるのもなんだし‥‥）
English: (If I press too hard in the wrong place, I'll just end up scaring him into silence.)

Japanese: （うん。ここは、ようすを見よう）
English: (Maybe I'll just sit back and see what happens.)

Japanese: ちょっと待ってください！
English: Wait a second!

Japanese: カントク！　‥‥さっきあなたは、こう言いましたね？
English: Mr. Manella, you've just contradicted yourself!

Japanese: “休憩中にステーキを食べた”と！
English: Didn't you just tell the court that you ate that t-bone steak during a break?

Japanese: あッ！
English: Oops! ROFL!

Japanese: どうなんですか！
English: Well?

Japanese: ‥‥う、うう。
English: Umm...

Japanese: 証人。‥‥どうなんですか？
English: Mr. Manella, what's this all about?

Japanese: ま、まあ‥‥。とったかな‥‥休憩。
English: W-well, yeah, I guess we did take a little break.

Japanese: なるほどくん！よくやったわ！
English: Phoenix! Great job!

Japanese: 休憩をとったなら、そのあいだに、スタジオに行けたかもしれない！
English: If they took a break, one of them could have gone to the studio during that time!

Japanese: 裁判長！
English: Your Honor!

Japanese: 休憩時間について、《証言》をおねがいします！
English: I call on the witness to testify to the court about this break!

Japanese: わかりました。
English: Very well.

Japanese: では、証人。《証言》をおねがいします。
English: Mr. Manella, your testimony, please.

Japanese: ひいぃ‥‥。
English: Urk!

Japanese: ‥‥クックックッ‥‥。
English: Heh heh heh...

Japanese: （笑ってるよ‥‥）
English: (Uh oh. Edgeworth is laughing...)

Japanese: ～“休憩”について～
English: -- The Break --

Japanese: たしかに、休憩はとったけどさあ‥‥。（爆）
English: Yeah, FWIW, we took a break... ROFL!

Japanese: たった１５分だよ、１５分。
English: But it was only 15 minutes! 15! That's only 13 in Base 12!

Japanese: 第１スタジオで殺人なんて、できるわけないだろお。（笑）
English: Not enough time for someone to, say, commit murder in Studio One! LOL!

Japanese: ホネつきステーキ食べてるだけで終わっちゃうって！（怒）
English: That's only just enough time to eat a t-bone steak, if you ask me! *steams*

Japanese: ふむう‥‥。
English: Hmm...

Japanese: 私なら、ステーキも食べ終わらないでしょうな。
English: I don't think it would even be enough time for that, but that's just me.

Japanese: ‥‥では、《尋問》を。
English: Very well. You may begin the cross-examination.

Japanese: ～“休憩”について～
English: -- The Break --

Japanese: たしかに、休憩はとったけどさあ‥‥。（爆）
English: Yeah, FWIW, we took a break... ROFL!

Japanese: たった１５分だよ、１５分。
English: But it was only 15 minutes! 15! That's only 13 in Base 12!

Japanese: 第１スタジオで殺人なんて、できるわけないだろお。（笑）
English: Not enough time for someone to, say, commit murder in Studio One! LOL!

Japanese: ホネつきステーキ食べてるだけで終わっちゃうって！（怒）
English: That's only just enough time to eat a t-bone steak, if you ask me! *steams*

Japanese: どう？　なるほどくん。
English: Well, Phoenix?

Japanese: こんな短い証言じゃあ、ムジュンしようがないですよ！
English: That testimony was too short to wring any kind of useful information out of!

Japanese: とにかく、第１スタジオに行けたことを証明しましょう！
English: All you have to do is prove that he was able to go to Studio One in that time!

Japanese: （‥‥できるかなあ‥‥）
English: (Oh, great. Is that all...)

Japanese: その休憩は、何時にとったんですか！
English: What time exactly did you take this break?

Japanese: えーと‥‥。
English: Hmm...

Japanese: たしか、２時３０分から１５分間だったと思うけど。
English: I'd say it was from around 2:30 or so until 2:45.

Japanese: （２時３０分！）(2:30!!!)（事件が起こった時間だ！）
English: (That's the time of death!)

Japanese: （第１スタジオで、衣袋さんを殺害して、もどってくる‥‥）
English: (So he could have gone to Studio One, killed Hammer, and come back...)

Japanese: （時間的には、なんとか可能だな‥‥）
English: (I guess it's possible, time-wise...)

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥‥‥。...その１５分間は、何をしていたんですか？
English: What were you doing for those 15 minutes?

Japanese: ホネつきステーキを食べていたに決まってるだろ？
English: Eating my t-bone steak! What else!?

Japanese: たしか、コテージのテーブルには、皿は２枚、ありましたけど‥‥？
English: There were two plates on the trailer table...?

Japanese: ああ、もう１皿は、姫神さんが食べたんだ。
English: Oh, right. The other one was Diva's... sorry, Dee Vasquez's plate.

Japanese: （姫神‥‥あの、プロデューサーか‥‥）
English: (Dee Vasquez... the producer.)

Japanese: ホネつきステーキを１５分で食べるとは、なかなか早い‥‥。
English: To eat a t-bone steak in 15 minutes... that's quite a feat.

Japanese: どうしてですか？
English: Why is that?

Japanese: 食べるのは早そうに見えますけどね。
English: I have to say, you look like a guy who gets through his meals pretty fast.

Japanese: 相手は、さめてコチコチになったホネつきステーキだよ。
English: Well, I was facing a cold, slightly chewy t-bone steak...

Japanese: さすがのボクも、ちょっと手こずったね。（笑）
English: Even I have to take my time eating that kind of food. LOL

Japanese: ‥‥もう、イミのない尋問は、たくさんだ。
English: Haven't we had enough of this pointless line of questioning?

Japanese: 裁判長！
English: Your Honor!

Japanese: これまでの証言でアキラカなとおり、
English: The testimony to this point has made one certain fact painfully clear:

Japanese: コテージにいた人間は、今回の事件には関係ない！
English: The people in the trailer had nothing to do with this murder!

Japanese: なぜなら、第１スタジオに行くのはムリだったからだ！
English: It was impossible for any of them to go to Studio One!

Japanese: ‥‥何ッ！
English: What!?

Japanese: それとも弁護人は、
English: Something wrong, Mr. Wright?

Japanese: コテージにいた人間が、殺人現場に行ったと言うつもりか！
English: Surely you aren't suggesting one of the people in the trailer went to Studio One!

Japanese: ふむう‥‥。
English: Hmm...

Japanese: どうですか？　弁護人。
English: Well, Mr. Wright?

Japanese: なるほどくん。‥‥ここは、大事なポイントよ。
English: Phoenix, this is critical!

Japanese: よく考えてね！
English: Think about it before you give your answer!

Japanese: コテージの人間が、第１スタジオに行ったと主張しますか？
English: Do you claim someone from the trailer went to Studio One?

Japanese: （‥‥ここでコテージの人間が無関係と認めてしまっては）
English: (If I agree to let everyone in that trailer off the hook then the trial is over...)

Japanese: （犯人は荷星さんしかいなくなってしまう！）
English: (Mr. Powers will be found guilty for sure!)

Japanese: 被害者が殺害された２時３０分。コテージでは、休憩時間でした。
English: The victim was murdered at 2:30, the exact time of the break in the meeting.

Japanese: １５分も時間があれば、犯行をすませることは可能です！
English: With 15 minutes, murder is certainly a possibility!

Japanese: ふむう‥‥。
English: Hmm...

Japanese: そういう考え方もできますね‥‥。
English: I suppose you might say that...

Japanese: （よしよし‥‥。裁判長が考え込んでるぞ‥‥）
English: (Alright, I've got the judge thinking now!)

Japanese: ‥‥クックックッ‥‥。
English: Tsk, tsk, tsk...

Japanese: 毎度まいど、笑わせてくれるな、キミは‥‥。
English: Never a dull moment with you, Mr. Wright!

Japanese: ‥‥なんだと！
English: What's your point, Edgeworth!

Japanese: キミも、それなりに調査はしているんだろう？
English: You've been doing your homework, haven't you?

Japanese: 法廷記録をよく見てみたまえ。
English: Look at the Court Record.

Japanese: 法廷記録‥‥？
English: The Court Record...?

Japanese: ２時１５分から４時まで、あの道は通れなかったのだよ？
English: Surely, you knew that from 2:15 to 4:00 the path between the studios was blocked?

Japanese: “サルマゲくん”のクビが道をふさいでいたのだから！
English: Mrs. Monkey's head had fallen across the path!

Japanese: あっ！　そういえば！
English: Whoa! You're right!

Japanese: たしかに被害者が死んだ時間、コテージは休憩に入っていた。
English: True, the break in the trailer meeting came right at the time of death...

Japanese: しかし！
English: However!

Japanese: スタジオへ通じる道は、ふさがっていたのだ！
English: The path from there to the scene of the crime was blocked!

Japanese: “サルマゲくん”のクビによって！
English: The fallen Mrs. Monkey head barred the way!

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥‥。...（ホントは“サルマゲどん”なんだけど‥‥）
English: (It's actually "Mr. Monkey," but Edgeworth has a point...)

Japanese: （それにツッコミを入れる気にもなれないな‥‥）
English: (And somehow, I feel no desire to correct him.)

Japanese: 返すコトバも見つからないようですね。
English: No counter-argument, Mr. Wright?

Japanese: あなたの負けですな、弁護人。
English: Then I'm afraid you lose this round.

Japanese: （‥‥ここで認めたくはないけど‥‥）
English: (I don't want to write off so many possible suspects...)

Japanese: （ムリだとわかっていることを主張するワケにも行かない‥‥）
English: (But I can't keep claiming the impossible, either...)

Japanese: ‥‥たしかに、コテージの人間がスタジオに行くのは、不可能です。
English: I agree that it was impossible for anyone in the trailer to go to the studio in that time.

Japanese: ハッ！
English: Ha hah!

Japanese: いつものように、考えもなくつっかかってくるかと思えば、
English: I thought you might be thoughtlessly treading on thin ice again!

Japanese: なかなかモノわかりがいいな‥‥。
English: But I see you had at least an inkling of the truth.

Japanese: どういうことですか？‥‥御剣検事。
English: What do you mean, Mr. Edgeworth?

Japanese: カンタンなこと‥‥。
English: It's quite simple...

Japanese: たしかに被害者が死んだ時間、コテージは休憩に入っていた。
English: True, the break in the trailer meeting came right at the time of death...

Japanese: しかし！
English: However!

Japanese: スタジオへ通じる道は、ふさがっていたのだ！
English: The path from there to the scene of the crime was blocked!

Japanese: “サルマゲくん”のクビによって！
English: The fallen Mrs. Monkey head barred the way!

Japanese: 強風によってクビが折れたのは、２時１５分だった。
English: At around 2:15, a strong gust of wind knocked off the studio mascot's head.

Japanese: つまり。
English: Ergo!

Japanese: ２時３０分に休憩をとっても、あの道は通れなかったわけだ。
English: When the people in the trailer took a break at 2:30, the way to the studio was blocked.

Japanese: “サルマゲくん”のクビがあったのだから。
English: Blocked... by Mrs. Monkey's severed head!

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥‥。...（ホントは“サルマゲどん”なんだけど‥‥）
English: (It's actually "Mr. Monkey," but Edgeworth has a point...)

Japanese: （それにツッコミを入れる気にもなれないな‥‥）
English: (And somehow, I feel no desire to correct him.)

Japanese: 以上をもって‥‥。
English: I believe we have seen enough evidence.

Japanese: この証人に対する質問は、終了する。
English: I would like to relieve Mr. Manella from the stand.

Japanese: （お、終わっちまったよ‥‥）
English: (Wh-what? It's over?)

Japanese: では、私の意見を申し上げましょう。
English: Very well. The court's opinion on this case is as follows:

Japanese: 事件当日、第２スタジオのコテージに、数名の人間がいた。
English: We have found that there were several other people in Studio 2 on the day of the murder.

Japanese: しかし、彼らは、第１スタジオに行けなかったわけだから、
English: However, it is also clear that none of these people could have gone to Studio One.

Japanese: この事件に関係ないことはあきらかです。
English: They therefore have no relation to this case.

Japanese: また、このトノサマンの写真ですが、
English: Furthermore, with regards to the photo of the Steel Samurai...

Japanese: 体格から考えて、被告人以外の人間は、着ることはできない。
English: Given the size of the costume, no one other than Mr. Powers could have worn it that day.

Japanese: あとは、このトノサマンが犯人であるという、
English: All that is lacking is decisive evidence that he is the one who did it.

Japanese: その決定的な証拠があれば、それで決まるのですが‥‥。
English: If we had that, I'm afraid I would have to find Mr. Powers guilty.

Japanese: 裁判長。
English: Your Honor.

Japanese: 当然、検察側は用意している。‥‥決定的な証人を。
English: The prosecution is pleased to announce that we indeed have decisive evidence: a witness.

Japanese: 御剣検事！　“決定的な証人”とは、どういう‥‥？
English: Who is this witness, Mr. Edgeworth?

Japanese: 彼は、トノサマンが被害者を殺害した瞬間を目撃しているッ！
English: My witness saw the very moment when the Steel Samurai skewered the victim!

Japanese: 静粛に！　静粛に！
English: Order! I will have order!

Japanese: わかりました。
English: I see.

Japanese: １０分間の休憩を挟んで、その証人に話を聞きましょう。
English: The court will take a 10 minute recess, after which we will hear your witness.

Japanese: それでは、休憩に入ります！
English: Court is adjourned for recess!

Japanese: １０月１９日　午前１１時４分地方裁判所　被告人第１控え室
English: October 19, 11:04 AM District Court Defendant Lobby No. 1

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥‥‥。...ち、千尋さん。どうしましょう‥‥。
English: What do we do now, Mia?

Japanese: コテージの人間は、事件には無関係、ってことに‥‥。
English: If everyone in that trailer has an alibi...

Japanese: ‥‥...‥‥‥‥ゴメンね、なるほどくん。
English: I'm sorry, Phoenix.

Japanese: どうやら、私の見込み違いだったみたいね。
English: I guess I was wrong.

Japanese: そ、そんな！
English: M-Mia!

Japanese: 千尋さんがあきらめちゃったら、ぼくは、もう‥‥！
English: Don't tell me you're giving up! If you give up, what hope do I have...?

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥...カン違いしないで。
English: Don't get me wrong.

Japanese: 私は今まで、あきらめたことなんて、一度もないわ。
English: I've never given up on a trial before, not while there was a chance.

Japanese: 今の尋問であきらかになったのは、ただ１つよ。
English: Only one thing became clear in your cross-examination.

Japanese: “コテージにいた人間は、第１スタジオには行けなかった”
English: The people in the trailer could not have gone to Studio One.

Japanese: 私、そこに何か秘密があると思っていたんだけど‥‥。
English: I thought there was more to it than that...

Japanese: そうじゃなかった。‥‥それだけのことよ。
English: But I was wrong. That's all there is.

Japanese: あの‥‥。
English: Umm...

Japanese: ボク‥‥、どうなっちゃうんでしょう？
English: Er... What's going to happen to me?

Japanese: なんだか、もう、法廷中の人たちが“お前がやったんだ！”って、
English: It kinda seems like everyone in that courtroom thinks I did it...

Japanese: そう言ってるような気がして‥‥。
English: They think I'm a murderer!

Japanese: だいじょうぶです。
English: Don't worry, Mr. Powers.

Japanese: あなたが無実ならば、私たちはかならず、それを証明します。
English: If you are innocent, we will prove it. I guarantee it.

Japanese: 私たちにまかせて、堂々としていてください。
English: Leave it to us, and... be yourself. Be strong.

Japanese: ‥‥トノサマンなんですから。あなたは。
English: You are the Steel Samurai, hero to children everywhere, after all.

Japanese: きょ‥‥
English: You...

Japanese: 恐縮です。
English: Thanks. *sniff*

Japanese: さあ。なるほどくん。
English: Okay, Phoenix.

Japanese: がんばらないとね。
English: This one's for the kids!

Japanese: トノサマンが大好きな、子供たちのためにも‥‥！
English: Let's do it!

Japanese: １０月１９日　午前１１時１５分地方裁判所　第４法廷
English: October 19, 11:15 AM District Court Courtroom No. 4

Japanese: では、審理を再開しましょう。
English: Court is back in session for the trial of Mr. Will Powers.

Japanese: ‥‥検察側としては、
English: The prosecution has a concern.

Japanese: まだおさなく、イタイケな小学生を殺人事件の証人として呼んだうえ、
English: As our witness is a grade schooler of tender years, and this is a murder case...

Japanese: そこの弁護人に、イジの悪い質問をさせたくはない‥‥。
English: We worry that the defense might cause unnecessary trauma with his cruel questioning!

Japanese: （好きなコト言いやがって‥‥）
English: (Nice to see Edgeworth taking the moral high ground...)

Japanese: しかし、こうなってはいたしかたない。
English: However, we have no choice.

Japanese: 証人として、大滝　九太少年を入廷させていただこう！
English: The prosecution calls Cody Hackins to the stand.

Japanese: 裁判長。‥‥とりあえず証人に、何か台を。
English: Your Honor. Perhaps you could arrange a box for him to stand on?

Japanese: そ、そうですね。
English: Oh, r-right.

Japanese: 係官。‥‥みかん箱を。
English: Guard, please bring him a box. One of those donut crates should do.

Japanese: では証人。名前と学年を。
English: Will the witness state his name and grade in school.
Witness!

Japanese: なんだよ！オトナだからって、イバるなよな！
English: What!? Just 'cause you're all grown-up don't mean you can push me around!

Japanese: むぐぐ‥‥。
English: Mrph...

Japanese: だめよ、九太くん。
English: Cody?

Japanese: ちゃんと、質問には答えるの。
English: Answer his question, okay?

Japanese: あっ、お姉ちゃん！
English: H-hey, it's you! The nice lady!

Japanese: ボク、大滝　九太。小学校２年生です！
English: I'm Cody Hackins. I'm in 2nd grade!

Japanese: （先が思いやられるな‥‥）
English: (I get the feeling this is going to be a long, long day...)

Japanese: 御剣検事。
English: Mr. Edgeworth.

Japanese: 相手は子供なんですから、もっとこう、ヤサシクするように。
English: Please remember that you're speaking to a child. Try to be gentle.

Japanese: む、むむむむむ。
English: M-mrrrrph!

Japanese: ‥‥証人。いや、‥‥九太くん。
English: Witness! Er, I mean, Cody.

Japanese: （なんか、イタイタしいな‥‥）
English: (He's having trouble with this "gentle" thing.)

Japanese: キミは、その、事件のあった日、英都撮影所に行ったね？
English: You were present, er, you were at Global Studios on the day of the, er, incident?

Japanese: 行っちゃ悪ィかよ！
English: You got a problem with that!?

Japanese: ‥‥そのとき見たことを、その。ワレワレに聞かせてほしいなあ。
English: Please tell us what you saw that day.

Japanese: え？　オジさんと、そこのエラそうなヒゲのおジイさんに？
English: What, pops? You want me to tell you and gramps with the beard over there?

Japanese: “おニイさん”だ。
English: Just... Mr. Edgeworth will be fine.

Japanese: “ヒゲのおじさん”です。
English: I prefer "bearded gentleman" myself.

Japanese: （やれやれ‥‥）
English: (A very long day...)

Japanese: ところで。
English: Incidentally,

Japanese: 法廷内は、カメラの持ち込みはキンシされているはずですが。
English: photographic equipment is strictly forbidden in this courtroom.

Japanese: う、うむむ。
English: M-mpht!

Japanese: “カメラがダメなら証言しない”と言われてしまったもので‥‥
English: M-my apologies, Your Honor. He said he wouldn't testify if he couldn't bring it...

Japanese: トクベツに許可していただきたい。
English: I'd like special permission, if that's possible.

Japanese: コドモとトリヒキして、しかも負けるなよ！
English: Wait, so you're saying you had to bargain terms with a kid... and you LOST?

Japanese: このカメラ、こないだ買ってもらったばっかりなんだぜ！
English: Hey! I just got this new camera!

Japanese: まだ、ちょっと使い方、よくわかんないんだけどさ！
English: Don't really know how to use it all that good yet!

Japanese: 最近は、どこかへ行くときは、ゼッタイ持っていくんだぜ！
English: But I bring it with me wherever I go!

Japanese: ‥‥なるほどくん。
English: Phoenix.

Japanese: あのカメラ、ビミョーに気になるわ‥‥。
English: I wonder if he had that camera on the day of the murder...

Japanese: ちょっと、法廷記録にファイルを作った方がいいわね。
English: You'd better make a note of it in the Court Record.

Japanese: 証拠品《九太のデジタルカメラ》のデータを法廷記録にファイルした。
English: Cody's Camera added to the Court Record.

Japanese: では、九太くん。
English: Very well, Cody.

Japanese: 事件のあった日、見たことを《証言》してもらいましょう。
English: Please testify to the court about what you saw the day of the incident.

Japanese: ～事件があった日のこと～
English: -- Witness's Account --

Japanese: オレ、一度トノサマンのリハーサルが見たくてさあ。
English: I wanted to see a Steel Samurai rehearsal, just once.

Japanese: インターネットで地図を見て、あの日、撮影所に行ったんだ。
English: I found a map on the Internet, and went to the studios that day.

Japanese: 警備員のオバチャンに見つからないように、道のそとの林を通って、
English: I went through the woods, off the path, so that old lady wouldn't catch me.

Japanese: オレ、とにかくスタジオに行こうとしたんだ。
English: I was going for the studio.

Japanese: とちゅう、３０分ぐらい道にまよっちゃったんだけど。
English: I got kinda lost on the way, though. For about 30 minutes.

Japanese: スタジオのところに出たら、トノサマンがいたんだ！
English: When I came out by the studio, there was the Steel Samurai!

Japanese: オレ、うれしくて。見てたら、悪人が出てきてさあ。
English: It totally rocked! Right before my eyes, out came the bad guy!

Japanese: トノサマンは‥‥モチロン、その悪人をたおしたよ！
English: Of course, the Steel Samurai took him down! Pow!

Japanese: もしカメラを持っていれば、シャッターチャンスだったよ。
English: If I had my camera with me, that woulda been the time for a shot, I tell you.

Japanese: ケッキョク、スタジオには入れなくて、その日は帰ったんだ。
English: Anyway, I couldn't get into the studio, so I went home.

Japanese: ふむう‥‥。
English: Hmm...

Japanese: では弁護人、《尋問》をおねがいしますが、
English: Very well. The defense may begin its cross-examination.

Japanese: 相手は少年です。‥‥ヤサシクするように。
English: And, be gentle. Remember you're talking to a child.

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥。...（そんな必要、ないような気がするけど‥‥）
English: (This kid is tougher than most adults we see in here, honestly...)

Japanese: ～事件があった日のこと～
English: -- Witness's Account --

Japanese: オレ、一度トノサマンのリハーサルが見たくてさあ。
English: I wanted to see a Steel Samurai rehearsal, just once.

Japanese: インターネットで地図を見て、あの日、撮影所に行ったんだ。
English: I found a map on the Internet, and went to the studios that day.

Japanese: 警備員のオバチャンに見つからないように、道のそとの林をとおって、
English: I went through the woods, off the path, so that old lady wouldn't catch me.

Japanese: オレ、とにかくスタジオに行こうとしたんだ。
English: I was going for the studio.

Japanese: とちゅう、３０分ぐらい道にまよっちゃったんだけど。
English: I got kinda lost on the way, though. For about 30 minutes.

Japanese: スタジオのところに出たら、トノサマンがいたんだ！
English: When I came out by the studio, there was the Steel Samurai!

Japanese: オレ、うれしくて。見てたら、悪人が出てきてさあ。
English: It totally rocked! Right before my eyes, out came the bad guy!

Japanese: トノサマンは‥‥モチロン、その悪人をたおしたよ！
English: Of course, the Steel Samurai took him down! Pow!

Japanese: もしカメラを持っていれば、シャッターチャンスだったよ。
English: If I had my camera with me, that woulda been the time for a shot, I tell you.

Japanese: （それを見て、コワくなって家に帰ったわけか‥‥）
English: (I guess seeing the killing freaked him out and he went home...)

Japanese: なるほどくん。
English: Phoenix.

Japanese: 相手は少年よ。
English: Remember, he's a child.

Japanese: あまりキツく言わずに、でもヨウシャしちゃダメよ。
English: Use gentle words... but be firm.

Japanese: （‥‥そんなムチャな‥‥）
English: (Easy for you to say...)

Japanese: そんなにトノサマンが好きなんだ。
English: You really like the Steel Samurai, huh?

Japanese: アッタリマエだろ！
English: Course I do!

Japanese: オレ、テレビはゼンブ、ビデオに録画してるし、
English: I've taped every show!

Japanese: デパートのショーも、ゼッタイに行くんだ！
English: And I go to all the live performances!

Japanese: たいしたもんだね。
English: That's impressive.

Japanese: 遊園地とかにも行くの？
English: Have you been to the amusement park?

Japanese: アッタリマエだろ！
English: Course! Duh!

Japanese: オレのお父さん、つきあいがいいんだから！
English: My Dad always takes me!

Japanese: （“オトウサン”か。タイヘンな職業だよな‥‥）
English: (Poor, poor Dad...)

Japanese: スタジオに行ったのは、あの日が初めてだったの？
English: Was that day the first time you'd been to the studio?

Japanese: 実は、何回か行ったことがあるんだけどさ。
English: Uh, actually I've been a couple of times.

Japanese: まだ、リハーサルの日に当たったことがないんだよ！
English: I never manage to get there on a rehearsal day!

Japanese: 警備員のオバサンはうるさいしさあ‥‥。
English: And that security lady's always yelling...

Japanese: ほほお。‥‥では、つづきを。
English: Right, right. Please continue.

Japanese: 言われなくても話してやるよ。
English: Chill, pops! I'm getting to it!

Japanese: 人の話のコシを折るなよな！
English: How am I supposed to talk with you cuttin' me off all the time? Geez!
"Off the path"...?

Japanese: うん。あの撮影所、山を切り開いて作っただろ？
English: Yeah, you know how the studios are in a valley there?

Japanese: だから、ちょっと道をはずれると、もう林になってるんだよ。
English: If you go off the path a little, there's woods. That's where I was!

Japanese: ‥‥なるほどね。
English: I see.

Japanese: それでさあ。
English: Anyhow...

Japanese: スタジオに行くとちゅう、何か変わったことはなかった？
English: Did nothing unusual happen on your way to the studio?

Japanese: “変わったこと”？
English: "Unusual"?

Japanese: たとえば、こんなサルは見た？
English: Did you see this monkey by any chance?

Japanese: ああ。“サルマゲどん”ね。
English: Oh, you mean Mr. Monkey?

Japanese: そいつは見たけど。クビは折れてなかったよ！
English: Yeah I saw him, but his head wasn't busted!

Japanese: スタジオには、すぐ行けたのか‥‥な？
English: So you went straight to the studio?

Japanese: 林の中でまよったの？
English: You got lost in the woods?

Japanese: そうだよ。もう、どこに向かって歩いてるのか、わかんなくて。
English: Yeah. I couldn't figure out which way I was walking.

Japanese: スタジオの青いトビラが見えたときは、ホント、うれしかったよ。
English: Man, was I relieved when I saw the blue studio doors.

Japanese: それでさぁ。
English: But guess what happened next!

Japanese: トノサマンが？
English: The Steel Samurai?

Japanese: うん。スタジオの門のところにいたんだ。
English: Yeah, he was standing by the studio gate.

Japanese: ちょっと門のところで何か考えていたみたいだけど、
English: It looked like he was thinking about something before going in.

Japanese: そのうち、門を開けて、中に入っていったんだよ。
English: Then he opened up the gate and went inside.

Japanese: ふうん。‥‥それから？
English: I see. What then?

Japanese: “悪人”って、どんなヤツだった？
English: What sort of "bad guy" was it?

Japanese: ‥‥人間だったよ。背が高くて、ヤセたヤツ。
English: A guy. A person. Kinda tall. Skinny.

Japanese: 男？　女？
English: Are you sure it was a guy?

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥。...わかんない。‥‥遠かったし。
English: I dunno. He was kinda far away.

Japanese: （なんだよ。‥‥ハッキリしないな‥‥）
English: (Huh, he's being awfully vague...)

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥。...その悪人は、どうなった？
English: What happened to the bad guy?

Japanese: え？
English: Huh?

Japanese: ええと‥‥。
English: Well...

Japanese: うん‥‥。動かなくなっちゃった。死んだみたいに、ピクリとも。
English: Well, he kinda didn't move. Not a bit. Like he was dead.

Japanese: さすが、プロのアクションスターだよね！
English: I-I guess he's a real pro! What a great actor!

Japanese: そ、そうだね。
English: Y-yes, I suppose he was.

Japanese: “もし”カメラを持っていれば？
English: "If" you had your camera?

Japanese: なんだ、カメラ、なかったの？
English: You mean, you weren't carrying your camera then?

Japanese: そ、そうだよ。
English: N-no, I wasn't.

Japanese: オ、オレだって、いつもカメラ持ってるわけじゃないさ！
English: I-it's not like I have it all the time!
(Really...?)

Japanese: 九太くん。‥‥今の話、ちょっとおかしいな。
English: Cody, what you just said seems, well, a little strange.

Japanese: きみ。さっきは、いつでもデジカメは持ち歩く、って、
English: Didn't you say before that you always bring your digital camera wherever you go?

Japanese: ハッキリ、そう言ってたじゃない。
English: You were quite clear about that.

Japanese: え！
English: Huh!?

Japanese: ダメだよ。ウソついちゃあ！
English: Cody, you shouldn't lie here. You understand that, right?

Japanese: 弁護人！‥‥ちょっといいですかな？
English: Mr. Wright! A word with you...

Japanese: （しまった。‥‥キツく言いすぎたか？）
English: (Uh oh... was I putting the pressure on too much?)

Japanese: なんですか？“でじかめ”って？
English: What is this "digital" camera contraption you're talking about?

Japanese: えーと。‥‥“デジタル?カメラ”のことです。
English: It's umm... a digital camera, Your Honor.

Japanese: 最近はやっている、カメラの一種です。
English: It's kind of a new sort of camera.

Japanese: （セツメイのしようがない‥‥）
English: (How do I explain that!?)

Japanese: なるほど。
English: I see.

Japanese: とにかく。九太くん。
English: Anyway, Cody...

Japanese: きみが撮影所に行くとしたら、カメラをわすれるはずがない！
English: I can't believe you wouldn't bring your camera on a trip to the studios!

Japanese: ‥‥持っていたんだろう？
English: You did bring it, didn't you?

Japanese: うう‥‥。
English: Umm...

Japanese: 弁護人ッ！
English: Mr. Wright!

Japanese: 少年をオドすとは、見下げはてた男だな！
English: How cruel you are, to terrorize a poor child so!

Japanese: 少年だろうが検事だろうが、ウソはよくない！
English: I don't care if he's a child or a prosecuting attorney! No one should lie in court!

Japanese: なんでそこに“検事”が出てくるのだ‥‥。
English: What do you mean "or a prosecuting attorney"!?

Japanese: どうなのかな、九太くん。
English: Well, Cody?

Japanese: な、なんだよ！
English: Wh-what!

Japanese: ああ。たしかにカメラは持ってたよ！
English: Yeah, so I had my camera. So what!?

Japanese: それがなんだってんだよぉ！
