English: She said she was more worried about me than anything.

Japanese: うるさいな。‥‥ちょっとぐらい、いい気分にさせてくれよ。
English: Aww, just go on and take the compliment, will you?

Japanese: こ、この男は‥‥
English: Hmm? Strange...

Japanese: はて。見たことがあるような、ないような‥‥。
English: I feel as though I've seen this man somewhere before!

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥‥‥。...ああああああああッ！
English: Aaaaaaaaaaaaah!

Japanese: 思い出しましたか？
English: Did you remember!?

Japanese: この事務所にいた弁護士ぢゃ！
English: He was a lawyer! Here, in my office!

Japanese: “生倉　雪夫（なまくらゆきお）”ぢゃよ！
English: That's Hammond! Robert Hammond!

Japanese: ナマクラさん‥‥？
English: Mr. Hammond...?

Japanese: 御剣　怜侍が、この男を射殺したというのか‥‥。
English: And you say this is the man Miles Edgeworth shot?

Japanese: ‥‥綾里　舞子さん。
English: Misty Fey.

Japanese: 千尋クンと、真宵さんのおかあさんぢゃ。
English: Mother of Mia and Maya Fey.

Japanese: 御剣　怜侍に見せてやるといい。
English: Show this photograph to Miles Edgeworth.

Japanese: 何か、しゃべる気になるぢゃろう。
English: He'll have something to say to you then.

Japanese: これが‥‥犯行の瞬間、かね。
English: So this is the moment the crime took place, eh?

Japanese: はい。
English: Yes.

Japanese: ハッキリ御剣とは、わからんな。
English: You can't really say for sure that's Edgeworth.

Japanese: それが、あの男にとって幸運なのか、不運なのか‥‥。
English: I'm not sure whether that's a good thing or a bad thing.

Japanese: ワシには、なんとも言えん‥‥。
English: Not sure at all...

Japanese: ハレツ音に反応してシャッターを切るカメラ‥‥？
English: So, this camera on the lake takes a photo whenever it hears an explosion sound?

Japanese: そうなんですよ。
English: That's what she said.

Japanese: そんなモンが、ツゴウよくセットされていたと‥‥？
English: And it just happened to be set up on the beach, pointing out on the lake?

Japanese: ええ。‥‥“流星を撮る”って言ってましたけど。
English: Yes... she said it was to photograph "shooting stars."

Japanese: “流星”と“ハレツ音”‥‥。なんのカンケイもないのォ。
English: Hmm. Shooting stars... explosions... what's the connection?

Japanese: ちょっとクサいの。‥‥その婦女子。
English: I'd be suspicious of this witness of yours.

Japanese: すまんの。
English: Er... my apologies.

Japanese: ワシにわかることはないようぢゃ。
English: I'm not sure I can help you with that.

Japanese: “ひょうたん湖　自然公園”と書いてある。
English: The sign says "Gourd Lake Nature Park."

Japanese: 休日は、家族づれでにぎわう、都会のオアシスといったところだ。
English: This place is full of families picnicking on the weekend.

Japanese: でも、滝はないんだよね。
English: But... no waterfall.

Japanese: 都会の人間は、修行はしないからね。
English: Not many picnickers come here for spiritual training, Maya.

Japanese: 葉を落とした木々に、冬の訪れを感じる今日このごろ。
English: I feel winter's chill from the bare leaf trees today...

Japanese: うーん。冬は人を詩人にさせるなあ。
English: *sigh* What is it about winter that turns people into poets?

Japanese: ねー、サムいよなるほどくん。早くどこか行こうよ。
English: I don't know, but my toes are starting to feel numb.

Japanese: （サムいって、ぼくの詩か？）
English: (Yes... my poetry has that effect on some people.)

Japanese: プラスチック製のベンチが並んでいる。
English: A lineup of plastic benches.

Japanese: “マンジュウを買って、食って行け”というつもりだろうが、
English: I guess the idea is you buy a dog and eat it here.

Japanese: こう寒くては、座るヤツもいないだろう。
English: I doubt anyone would sit here and eat on a day like this.

Japanese: ‥‥いや、マンジュウがあれば、真宵ちゃんなら座るかも。
English: Except maybe Maya... if she had a Samurai Dog.

Japanese: 妙に目立たない道しるべが立っている。
English: Huh... I almost didn't see that signpost.

Japanese: “左‥‥ボートのりば”“右‥‥公園出口”
English: "Left - Boat Docks" "Right - Exit"

Japanese: マンジュウ屋の屋台らしい。‥‥今は、やっていない。
English: A hotdog stand. It's closed...

Japanese: 和風のたたずまいに、悪趣味なクリスマスのかざりつけ。
English: The Christmas fringe looks a little half-baked.

Japanese: そして、ノボリには“とのさまんじう”の文字。
English: The banner reads "Samurai Dogs"...

Japanese: ‥‥この節操のなさはすごいな‥‥。
English: Somebody needs to redecorate.

Japanese: ゴミは入っていない。
English: The trashcan is empty.

Japanese: この公園は、管理のよさがウリだからな‥‥。
English: At least the place is well maintained.

Japanese: あ。ベンチだよなるほどくん。
English: Hey, Nick, benches!

Japanese: おマンジュウ食べながら、ちょっと休もうか。
English: Let's take a break... maybe have a dog?

Japanese: い、いやいやいや。
English: I think not.

Japanese: ‥‥このサムいのに、ベンチでマンジュウもないよ。
English: It's too cold to sit and eat hotdogs out here!

Japanese: ダメだねー都会っ子は。
English: Wimpy city boy!

Japanese: 滝にでも打たれて、もっとキタえないと！
English: You should try standing under a freezing waterfall some time!

Japanese: ふーん。‥‥広いね。ひょうたん湖。
English: Wow. Gourd Lake is really big.

Japanese: そうだね。
English: Yeah.

Japanese: ね、なるほどくん。どうして“ひょうたん湖”なの？
English: Say, Nick. Why is it called "Gourd Lake"?

Japanese: ああ。
English: Oh.

Japanese: ムカシこの湖は、ひょうたんの名産地だったんだ。
English: Well, a long time ago, they used to grow gourds here.

Japanese: えっ！　それは意外！
English: Whoa! No way!

Japanese: あたしはまた、上から見たらひょうたんの形、とか、
English: I was sure it was because the lake looked like a gourd when viewed from above!

Japanese: そんなありふれた理由かと思ってたよ‥‥。
English: You know, like an hourglass shape?

Japanese: ‥‥ま。そういう説もあるみたいだけど。
English: Well, it is shaped like a gourd, actually. But that's just a coincidence.

Japanese: へー、なるほどねえ‥‥。
English: Oh. Okay.

Japanese: それにしても“トノサマンジュウ”ねえ‥‥。
English: I can't get over the Samurai Dogs...

Japanese: しかも、“元祖”だよ。
English: The "Original" Samurai Dogs, no less.

Japanese: いや、ホレ。“元祖”って、言ったモン勝ちだからサ。
English: Hey, man, whoever calls their product the "original" first wins!

Japanese: ついでに“本舗”とかつけといたらどうだ？
English: Why don't you add "world- famous" to the sign?

Japanese: おっ！　それ、いただき！
English: Hey, good idea!

Japanese: （‥‥やれやれ）
English: (What have I done...?)

Japanese: 高そうなカメラが湖面に向けてセットされている。
English: An expensive-looking camera faces the lake.

Japanese: その横には、大きなマイクと、青いシートをかぶった‥‥
English: Next to it is a large microphone and a blue plastic sheet.

Japanese: これは、コンピュータみたいだな。カメラにつながっている。
English: Hmm. Looks like a computer is attached to the camera.

Japanese: カンバンにはヒトコト、“キャンプ禁止”と書かれている。
English: The sign says "No Camping."

Japanese: ‥‥わざわざ、その前にキャンプを張るとは‥‥。
English: Funny place to pick to pitch your tent.

Japanese: じゃあ！　もしこのカンバンが“放火禁止”だったら‥‥！
English: Wait, what if the sign said "No Setting Tents on Fire"?

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥‥‥...そんなわけないだろ。
English: I don't think they have signs like that.

Japanese: そうかなあ‥‥。
English: Oh...

Japanese: ずいぶんランボーにあつかわれているらしい。
English: This SUV has seen better days.

Japanese: ボディのそこら中がデコボコしている。
English: It's dented all over.

Japanese: こんなところまでクルマで入ってくるとは‥‥。
English: I can't believe anyone would drive their car down here.

Japanese: ビニールシートの上には、食料に、情報雑誌。
English: There's food and some magazines on the sheet.

Japanese: この寒空の下でキャンプとは。‥‥そうとうのツワモノだな。
English: It takes a pretty tough skin to camp in this cold.

Japanese: ねえねえ、なるほどくん。
English: Hey, Nick!

Japanese: なんだ、また食いたいとか言うんじゃないだろうな。
English: What, don't tell me you're hungry again?

Japanese: ちがうよ。
English: No, no.

Japanese: なんで飯ごうって、ペコッてヘコんでるの？
English: I was just wondering, why are camping pots and pans made of aluminum?

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥‥‥...六法全書には書いてなかったよ。
English: They didn't talk about that in any of the law books.

Japanese: じゃあ、法律で決まってるわけじゃないんだ‥‥。
English: So, there's no law saying they have to be made out of aluminum, then!

Japanese: （勝手にナットクしてくれているみたいだ‥‥）
English: (I'm not having this conversation...)

Japanese: さまざまな木がビッシリ立ちならんでいる。
English: The trees grow quite thick here.

Japanese: 奥の方は、闇に溶けている。陽の光がとどいていないのだろう。
English: Further back, the trees fade into the shadow where the sunlight can't reach them.

Japanese: 貸しボート屋の管理小屋だ。
English: A small boat rental shop.

Japanese: 人がいるようなケハイはない。
English: Doesn't look like anyone is around.

Japanese: 事件があったせいだろう。今日は営業していないようだ。
English: They're probably closed because it's Christmas.

Japanese: この奥も森になっているみたいだ。
English: There's more forest off that way.

Japanese: こっちの方に行っても、調査のヤクには立たないだろう。
English: I doubt I'd find any helpful clues in there.

Japanese: 湖の上に、ボートが浮かんでいる。
English: There are some boats floating at the dock.

Japanese: 殺人事件は、このボートの上で起こったわけか‥‥。
English: Was one of these boats used in the murder, I wonder?

Japanese: なるほどくん。
English: Nick?
Huh?

Japanese: やっぱり、乗りに来るの、やめよう。
English: I changed my mind. I don't really want to go for a boat ride.

Japanese: 警察のマスコットキャラクター‥‥って、こんなんだったっけ？
English: Hey, is that the police department's mascot...?

Japanese: “タイホくん”だよ。
English: That's the "Blue Badger"!

Japanese: ワシが考えて、ワシが作った。ワシだけのマスコットさ。
English: It was my idea! I made it! It's my mascot!

Japanese: そ、そうなんですか。
English: I... see. How nice.

Japanese: コイツを刑事課のマスコットにするのが、ワシの最後の仕事だよ。
English: I'll get him assigned mascot of the Criminal Affairs Dept. if it's the last thing I do!

Japanese: ‥‥がんばってください。
English: Um, good luck!

Japanese: 刑事さんだろうか。
English: That must be one of the detectives.

Japanese: ‥‥何やら、ひとりでぶつぶつつぶやいている。
English: He's mumbling something to himself.

Japanese: “クニのおっかさん、泣いてるぞ‥‥”
English: "...your mama could see you know she'd be cryin'..."

Japanese: “吐いちまえよ。ラクになるぞ‥‥”
English: "...a break and spit it out, you lowlife scumbag..."

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥‥‥...取り調べのイメージ?トレーニングをしているようだ。
English: He must be doing image training for interrogations.

Japanese: 刑事課の課長さんだろう。
English: This must be the chief of the detectives here.

Japanese: ‥‥パソコンの画面を、食いいるように見つめている。
English: He's glued to his computer screen.

Japanese: なにぃ‥‥！“ひょうたん湖で‥‥”
English: Wha...!? "Gourd Lake...!"

Japanese: “ヒョッシーを目撃”だと‥‥！
English: "Gourdy sighted"!!! I don't believe it!

Japanese: （‥‥もっとダイジな事件があるだろう！）
English: (Shouldn't you be reading something more important...?)

Japanese: 刑事たちの机がならんでいる。
English: These are the detectives' desks.

Japanese: パソコンに、資料。‥‥思ったより片づいているな。
English: There are computers and files on each one. Funny, they're a lot tidier than I'd expect.

Japanese: たぶん、あまり席にいることがないんだろう。
English: I guess the detectives don't spend a lot of time at their desks.

Japanese: 婦人警官がビシッと敬礼している。
English: A poster of a female police officer...

Japanese: ‥‥と思ったら、制服を着たアイドルのグラビアだった。
English: Wait, no. That's the latest "Babes in Uniform" calendar. My bad.

Japanese: 刑事さんだろうか。
English: That must be one of the detectives.

Japanese: ‥‥何やら、ひとりでぶつぶつつぶやいている。
English: He's mumbling something to himself.

Japanese: “よーし、全員カベに向かって手をつくんだ‥‥”
English: "All right, hands against the wall, all o' you..."

Japanese: “逃げようったってムダだぜ。オレは背中に目があるんだ‥‥”
English: "Don't even think about escaping! I got eyes in the back of my head!"

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥‥‥...現場に踏み込むときのイメージ?トレーニングをしているようだ。
English: He must be doing image training for arrests.

Japanese: 刑事さんだろうか。
English: That must be one of the detectives.

Japanese: ‥‥何やら、ひとりでぶつぶつつぶやいている。
English: He's mumbling something to himself.

Japanese: “なるべく目立たないカッコウで、まわりにとけ込む‥‥”
English: "...move in the crowd. Wear drab clothes..."

Japanese: “ターゲットの視界にはゼッタイ入ってはイケナイ‥‥”
English: "Never enter the target's field of vision..."

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥‥‥...尾行するときのイメージ?トレーニングをしているようだ。
English: He must be doing image training for trailing.

Japanese: 刑事さんだろうか。
English: That must be one of the detectives.

Japanese: ‥‥何やら、ひとりでぶつぶつつぶやいている。
English: He's mumbling something to himself.

Japanese: “近所の人との接触は、ゼッタイに避けるべし‥‥”
English: "Never talk to people in the neighborhood..."

Japanese: “部屋のデンキは消して、カーテンは閉じておく‥‥”
English: "Turn all the lights in the room off, and close the curtains..."

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥‥‥...張り込みするときのイメージ?トレーニングをしているようだ。
English: He must be doing image training for a stake-out.

Japanese: １２月２６日　午前９時４４分地方裁判所　被告人第２控え室
English: December 26, 9:44 AM District Court Defendant Lobby No. 2

Japanese: ‥‥カルマ？
English: Karma...?

Japanese: そう。狩魔　豪（かるまごう）検事。
English: That's right, Manfred von Karma. He's the best prosecutor there is.

Japanese: ‥‥この４０年間、まだ一度も負けたことがない。
English: He hasn't lost a case in his 40-year career.

Japanese: まさに、奇跡を生きる男だ。
English: He is a god of prosecution, Wright! A god!

Japanese: い‥‥一度も‥‥？
English: Not a single case?

Japanese: 彼は、有罪判決のためならばそれこそ、なんでもする。
English: He'll do anything to get a guilty verdict, anything.

Japanese: なんか、お前みたいだな、御剣。
English: Hmm. Sounds like someone else I know, Edgeworth.

Japanese: ‥‥フッ。
English: Hmph.

Japanese: わかっていない。
English: You don't understand.

Japanese: 彼はまさに“なんでも”する。‥‥恐ろしい人なんだ。
English: I mean he'll really do anything. Manfred von Karma is a man to be feared.

Japanese: （証拠をデッチ上げるような男がナニを言ってるんだよ‥‥）
English: (That's quite a claim coming from someone who forges evidence...)

Japanese: 私は、彼の下で“検事”というものをテッテー的に、たたきこまれた。
English: He taught me what it really means to "prosecute."

Japanese: な、なんだって！
English: Wh-what!?

Japanese: 私の１０倍タチの悪い男、と考えてもらいたい。
English: Just picture a prosecutor as vicious as me... multiplied by a factor of ten.

Japanese: うえー‥‥。
English: Ugh...

Japanese: じゃあ、じゃあ、御剣さんのお師匠さんなんですか？　その人。
English: So... so was he your teacher, then, Mr. Edgeworth?

Japanese: うム、‥‥まあ、そんなものだな。
English: Something like that...

Japanese: ヒドい人だね！　カワイイ弟子を有罪にしようなんて‥‥
English: And now he's trying to get you found guilty!? What a creep!

Japanese: あ、それとも。
English: Oh, wait...

Japanese: ワザと手を抜いて、御剣さんをムザイに‥‥
English: Maybe he's planning on losing on purpose to help you out!

Japanese: ‥‥言ったはずだ。
English: Not a chance...

Japanese: 彼は、この４０年間、負けたことがない。
English: He hasn't lost once in 40 years. 40 years!

Japanese: そして、私の２０倍、タチが悪いのだ。
English: He's as ruthless as me, times twenty!

Japanese: ‥‥サイアクだぁ。
English: That's pretty ruthless...

Japanese: 私にとっては、神のような存在だ。
English: Like I said. He's a god among prosecutors.

Japanese: （ぼくにとっての千尋さんみたいなもんか‥‥）
English: (I guess that's something like Mia was to me.)

Japanese: （千尋さんといえば‥‥）
English: (Speaking of Mia...)

Japanese: あのさ。
English: Um, Maya?

Japanese: なあに？
English: Uh huh?

Japanese: こんなときこそ、千尋さんのチカラを借りたいと思うんだけど。
English: We could really be using Mia's help right now... don't you think?
Oh...

Japanese: ？?ダメなの。
English: I can't.

Japanese: ‥‥がんばってみたんだけど、お姉ちゃんに、とどかないの。
English: Sorry. I tried, I really tried, but I couldn't reach.

Japanese: “とどかない”？
English: You couldn't "reach"?

Japanese: 最近、修行の方もお休みしてるから‥‥
English: I think it's because I haven't been training.

Japanese: 霊力が、また弱くなっちゃったみたい‥‥。
English: My powers are weak again...

Japanese: ‥‥こまったな‥‥このカンジンなときに。
English: Oh man, what bad timing!

Japanese: ‥‥ゴメンね。
English: I'm really sorry.

Japanese: なんとか、がんばってみるから。
English: I'll try my best!

Japanese: たのむよ。
English: I hope so!

Japanese: ‥‥何をコソコソやっている？
English: What are you whispering about?

Japanese: い、いや‥‥なんでもない。
English: O-oh, it's nothing.

Japanese: そろそろ時間だな。‥‥行こうか。
English: Well, it's time. Let's head in...

Japanese: １２月２６日　午前１０時地方裁判所　第３法廷
English: December 26, 10:00 AM District Court Courtroom No. 3

Japanese: これより、御剣　怜侍の法廷を開廷します。
English: Court is now in session for the trial of Mr. Miles Edgeworth.

Japanese: 弁護側、準備完了しています。
English: The defense is ready, Your Honor.

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥‥‥。...あの‥‥狩魔検事。準備の方は‥‥？
English: Erm, Mr. von Karma? Is the prosecution ready?
Fool...

Japanese: 準備もなく、このワガハイがここに立つわけがなかろう。
English: You seriously think that I would stand here were I not completely prepared?

Japanese: は、はい。‥‥スミマセン。
English: R-right, my apologies!

Japanese: （弱ェ！）
English: (He's even got the Judge scared!)

Japanese: あ、では、冒頭弁論をおねがいします。
English: Very well, your opening statement, please.

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥...決定的な証拠。
English: Decisive evidence...

Japanese: 決定的な証人。
English: A decisive witness...

Japanese: ‥‥他に何か、必要なものは？
English: What else could possibly be required?

Japanese: あ‥‥いえ。それでじゅうぶんだと思います。
English: Ah... er, nothing of course. That should be fine.

Japanese: では、さっそく証人を呼んでいただきましょう。
English: The prosecution may call its first witness.

Japanese: な、なんか“カク”が違うよ、あのヒト‥‥。
English: What's with this guy? Is he royalty or something?

Japanese: ヤダなあ、あんなのとやり合うのかよ‥‥。
English: How am I supposed to fight against this!?

Japanese: 捜査にあたったイトノコギリ刑事を。
English: I call the detective in charge of this case, Detective Dick Gumshoe.

Japanese: （‥‥イトノコ刑事か‥‥）
English: (Okay, Gumshoe's first... Let's see how this goes.)

Japanese: まず、事件の説明をしたまえ。
English: Describe the incident. Now!

Japanese: は‥‥はッ！
English: Y-yessir!

Japanese: （イトノコさん‥‥キンチョーしてるみたいだな）
English: (Detective Gumshoe looks nervous...)

Japanese: では、その、上面図を見ていただくッス。
English: Er, please take a look at the map.

Japanese: 事件は、クリスマスイブの深夜、零時ごろ起こったッス。
English: The murder happened late Christmas Eve, around midnight.

Japanese: 湖の中央に、ボートが１つ。
English: There was one boat in the very middle of the lake.

Japanese: ボートの上には、男が２人。
English: There were two men on the boat.

Japanese: 午前零時１０分ごろ、ここでキャンプをしていた女性が、
English: Now, there happened to be a woman camping here on the edge of the lake.

Japanese: ２発の銃声を聞いているッス。
English: At 12:10 AM, she heard two pistol shots.

Japanese: ボートは、貸しボート小屋の方へ逃げて行ったッス。
English: Then the boat started to move. It went towards the boat rental shop.

Japanese: ‥‥ウム。
English: Hmm.

Japanese: 証拠品《上面図》を法廷記録にファイルした。
English: Overhead Map added to the Court Record.

Japanese: では、逮捕したときの状況を《証言》したまえ。
English: Testify to the court about your arrest. Now!

Japanese: あ、ちょ、ちょっと！狩魔検事。
English: W-w-wait! Mr. von Karma...

Japanese: 何かね。
English: Yes?

Japanese: いちおう、この法廷は私が進行役ですので‥‥。
English: Actually, I'm the one that's supposed to be handling these proceedings...

Japanese: キサマのすべきことは、１つ。
English: Wrong. There is only one thing you need to do here.

Japanese: その木づちをたたいて“有罪”と言えばよい。
English: You will slam down your gavel and say the word guilty. That is your role!

Japanese: は、はあ。‥‥了解しました。
English: Y-yes, of course. You're quite right.

Japanese: （了解するなよ！）
English: (No he's not!!!)

Japanese: ～御剣を逮捕するまでのこと～
English: -- The Arrest of Edgeworth --

Japanese: あれは零時３０分すぎだったッス。署に、男から通報が入ったッス。
English: A man called into the station around 30 minutes after midnight.

Japanese: ワレワレはパトカーで現場に急行したッス。
English: We headed to the scene of the crime as fast as we could.

Japanese: そこでワレワレは、御剣検事と会ったッス。
English: That's where we found Mr. Edgeworth.

Japanese: まさか、御剣検事がアヤシイとは思わなかったッス。
English: Now, I didn't suspect him of anything at all.

Japanese: しかし‥‥翌朝、湖から男の死体が見つかって‥‥、
English: But... the next morning, a body was found in the lake.

Japanese: ‥‥ケッキョク、御剣検事を逮捕することになったッス‥‥。
English: So we had to arrest Mr. Edgeworth.

Japanese: ふむう‥‥。では、
English: Hmm... I see. Very well...

Japanese: 《尋問》をしてくれたまえ。‥‥弁護士。
English: Begin your cross-examination, attorney. Now!

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥‥‥。...～御剣を逮捕するまでのこと～
English: -- The Arrest of Edgeworth --

Japanese: あれは零時３０分すぎだったッス。署に、男から通報が入ったッス。
English: A man called into the station around 30 minutes after midnight.

Japanese: ワレワレはパトカーで現場に急行したッス。
English: We headed to the scene of the crime as fast as we could.

Japanese: そこでワレワレは、御剣検事と会ったッス。
English: That's where we found Mr. Edgeworth.

Japanese: まさか、御剣検事がアヤシイとは思わなかったッス。
English: Now, I didn't suspect him of anything at all.

Japanese: しかし‥‥翌朝、湖から男の死体が見つかって‥‥、
English: But... the next morning, a body was found in the lake.

Japanese: ‥‥ケッキョク、御剣検事を逮捕することになったッス‥‥。
English: So we had to arrest Mr. Edgeworth.

Japanese: ボートから見つかった凶器が決定的な証拠になったッス。
English: The murder weapon we found in the boat was decisive evidence.

Japanese: なるほどくん。
English: Nick.

Japanese: なんか、イトノコさん、少しアイマイだね‥‥。
English: Doesn't it seem like Detective Gumshoe is being a little vague?

Japanese: たぶん、あの検事が指示しているんだよ。
English: I bet von Karma is feeding him lines!

Japanese: ヘタにゆさぶると、地雷がバクハツするんだろ。
English: His testimony's probably filled with landmines just waiting for me to press.

Japanese: どうしよう‥‥？
English: What do we do?

Japanese: バクハツをカクゴでゆさぶってみるしかない、かな。
English: Grit our teeth and press, I guess. What else can we do?

Japanese: オトコだねえ、なるほどくん！
English: Well said, Nick! You're the man!

Japanese: “男”から通報が入ったんですか？
English: You received a call from a "man"?

Japanese: そ‥‥そッス。
English: Er... yup.

Japanese: しかしさっき、あなたはこう言った。
English: But you said there was a woman camping there?

Japanese: “キャンプをしていた女性が”２発の銃声を聞いた、と。
English: She was the one who heard the two gunshots, right?

Japanese: その女性と、通報した“男”は別人だ。
English: That woman and the "man" who called in the report are two different people, obviously!

Japanese: ど、どういうことですか？
English: Different people?

Japanese: 目撃者は、２人いたのだ。
English: There were two witnesses! (Urk!)

Japanese: ２人の話は、ほぼ同じ内容だったのでね。
English: Their testimonies were quite similar however.

Japanese: 今日は、キャンプをしていた女性の方に来てもらったのだ。
English: Today I've summoned the woman who was camping.

Japanese: （キャンプをしていた女性って、ナツミさんのことだよな‥‥）
English: (The woman who was camping... Lotta Hart.)

Japanese: 刑事。‥‥それからどうなった？
English: What happened next, Detective?

Japanese: 通報を受けてから湖につくまで、どれぐらいかかりましたか？
English: How long was it between receiving the report and your arrival at the lake?

Japanese: そうッスね‥‥たぶん、３分ぐらいだったッス。
English: Er, well... I'd say it was about three minutes.

Japanese: また、ずいぶん早いですね。
English: That's pretty fast!

Japanese: ウチの署の今月の標語は、“急がば回らずまっすぐに”ッス。
English: Our motto for the month is "get there quick."

Japanese: 刑事！　署の機密事項をカンタンにもらすな！
English: Detective! You will refrain from casually revealing department secrets!

Japanese: は‥‥はッ！モウシワケないッス！
English: Y-yessir! Sorry, sir!

Japanese: 来年の給与査定、楽しみにしておくことだ。
English: Do that, and you'll be able to look forward to your next salary review.

Japanese: ‥‥また楽しみが増えたッス‥‥。
English: So much to look forward to, these days...

Japanese: 何をしている！　つづけたまえ！
English: This is no time for daydreaming! Continue!

Japanese: は‥‥はッ！
English: Y-yessir!

Japanese: そのとき、御剣検事はどんなようすでしたか？
English: What was Mr. Edgeworth like when you saw him then?

Japanese: そッスね‥‥。
English: Well...

Japanese: 自分の見たところ、落ちついていてとても殺人など‥‥
English: From what I saw, he looked pretty relaxed. Not like a murderer at all, really.

Japanese: 刑事！　主観的な意見を言うのはやめたまえ！
English: Detective! The court requires the facts, NOT your opinion!

Japanese: 何年、刑事をやっているッ！
English: How many years have you been on the force!?

Japanese: 意味があるのは、客観的なデータだけだ！
English: Facts only, Detective! Hard, cold, objective facts!
Y-y-yessir!

Japanese: （‥‥くそ。いちいち異議の多い検事だな‥‥）
English: (Man, he's got his share of objections...)

Japanese: なぜ、アヤシイと思わなかったんですか？
English: Why didn't you think he was suspicious?

Japanese: それは！
English: You should know!

Japanese: ワレワレと検事には、カタい信頼関係が‥‥
English: We have a deep, trusting relationship with the prosecutors.

Japanese: 刑事！　いいかげんな証言はするな！
English: Detective! The court isn't interested in your musings!

Japanese: “信頼関係”だと！‥‥アマッたれるな！
English: Deep? "Trusting"? Poppycock!

Japanese: そんなタワ言を並べる前に、誤認逮捕をへらすことだ！
English: I've never heard so many flippant comments from an active detective on the force!

Japanese: ‥‥ムギュウ‥‥
English: Mmph!

Japanese: （イトノコ刑事、究極までヘコんだな‥‥）
English: (Detective Gumshoe doesn't look so good...)

Japanese: さっさと、つづけたまえ！
English: Continue! Now!

Japanese: 死体には、何か手がかりがありましたか？
English: Did you find any clues on the body?

Japanese: 死体から、弾丸が１発見つかったッス。
English: A single bullet was recovered from the body.

Japanese: 心臓を撃ち抜いていたッス。‥‥それが致命傷ッス。
English: He was shot through the heart... fatally.

Japanese: 裁判長。その“弾丸”を提出しよう。
English: Judge! Here's the bullet...

Japanese: うまくホネをはずれていたので、しっかり原型をとどめている。
English: It didn't strike bone, so its shape is well preserved.

Japanese: わかりました。‥‥受理します。
English: Very well. The court accepts this bullet into evidence.

Japanese: 証拠品《ピストルの弾丸》のデータを法廷記録にファイルした。
English: Pistol Bullet added to the Court Record.

Japanese: ど、どうしてですか？
English: W-why is that?

Japanese: ボートから、凶器が発見されたッス。
English: Well, we found the murder weapon in the boat.

Japanese: 凶器‥‥？
English: The murder weapon...?

Japanese: ピストル‥‥ッス。
English: A pistol.

Japanese: 証人。
English: Detective Gumshoe...

Japanese: それは重要なことです。証言しなおしてください。
English: That is a vital piece of information. Please revise your testimony.

Japanese: わ‥‥わかったッス。
English: Right! S-sorry, Your Honor.

Japanese: なぜ、それが“決定的”なんですか？
English: What about the pistol made it "decisive evidence"?

Japanese: クックックッ‥‥。
English: Tsk tsk tsk...

Japanese: （‥‥うわあ‥‥御剣ソックリな、イヤな笑い‥‥）
English: (Ack! He has the same evil laugh as Edgeworth...)

Japanese: ボートから見つかったピストルには指紋があったッス‥‥。
English: There were fingerprints on the pistol found in the boat.

Japanese: 御剣検事の右手の指紋が、ハッキリと‥‥。
English: They were clear prints from Mr. Edgeworth's right hand.

Japanese: な‥‥なんですって！
English: Wh-what!?

Japanese: せ、静粛に！　静粛に！
English: Order! Order!

Japanese: 凶器に、御剣検事の指紋がついていたんですか！
English: So Mr. Edgeworth's finger- prints were found on the murder weapon!?

Japanese: は‥‥はッ。
English: Y-yes, Your Honor.

Japanese: 裁判長。‥‥これが問題の凶器だ。
English: Judge! This is the weapon in question.

Japanese: じゅ‥‥受理します。
English: A-accepted into evidence.

Japanese: 証拠品《ピストル》のデータを法廷記録にファイルした。
English: Pistol added to the Court Record.

Japanese: よろしいか。
English: Members of the court...

Japanese: ‥‥これで、凶器の《ピストル》と《弾丸》がそろったわけだ。
English: We now have the pistol used in the murder, and the bullet found in the body!

Japanese: 刑事。
English: Detective!

Japanese: はは、は、はッ！
English: Y-y-yessir!

Japanese: 死体から発見された弾丸は、そのピストルから撃たれたものか？
English: Was the bullet found in the body fired from this pistol?

Japanese: はッ。弾丸の線条痕が、そのピストルと一致したッス。
English: Yes. The ballistic markings on the bullet match the pistol.
Hmm...

Japanese: ね、ね。なるほどくん。
English: Hey, Nick!

Japanese: なあに？“せんじょうこん”って。
English: What does he mean, ballistic markings?

Japanese: おどろいたな‥‥
English: Shocking!

Japanese: この場に、線条痕も知らないドシロウトがいようとは‥‥。
English: To imagine someone here does not know something as basic as ballistic markings!

Japanese: うわぁ‥‥。こっち、ニラんでるよなるほどくん‥‥。
English: N-Nick! He's glaring at me!

Japanese: ふぅ‥‥いたしかたない。説明してやろう。
English: Tsk... very well, I'll explain.

Japanese: ‥‥裁判長。やりたまえ。
English: Actually, Judge! You do it.

Japanese: えッ！　わ、私が‥‥？
English: Eh? M-me?
Erm, ahem.

Japanese: 線条痕というのは、ピストルの“指紋”みたいなものです。
English: Ballistic markings are like the "fingerprints" of a gun.

Japanese: ピストルで撃たれた弾丸には必ず、銃身によるキズがつきます。
English: The barrel leaves distinctive marks on each bullet it fires.

Japanese: そのキズを調べれば、弾丸がどのピストルから撃たれたかが、
English: You can examine these ballistic fingerprints to see which gun fired the shot.

Japanese: ハッキリ特定できるのです。
English: It's quite accurate.

Japanese: そう。
English: Indeed.

Japanese: そして、この被害者の心臓から見つかった弾丸は、
English: This leads to one, inevitable conclusion: the bullet found in the victim's heart...

Japanese: 間違いなく、このピストルから発射されているのだ。
English: was, without a doubt, fired from this pistol.

Japanese: この、被告人の指紋がベッタリついているピストルから、な。
English: This pistol which, as you may recall, was covered with the defendant's own fingerprints!

Japanese: せ‥‥静粛に！　静粛に！
English: O-order! Order!

Japanese: （まずいな‥‥）
English: (This is bad...)

Japanese: （これじゃあ、御剣が撃ったとしか考えられないぞ‥‥！）
English: (This makes it look like Edgeworth did it!)

Japanese: いかがか？　裁判長。
English: Well, Judge?

Japanese: ほぼ、決定的といっていいでしょう。
English: I'd say it's almost decisive, yes.

Japanese: 正直なところ、この時点で、判決を下すことも可能です。
English: Honestly, I could declare a verdict at this point.

Japanese: しかし‥‥
English: However...

Japanese: 目撃証人の証言も気になる、と。‥‥了解した。
English: You wish to hear the witness speak, no doubt.

Japanese: では。
English: Very well.

Japanese: ワガハイ、少しつかれたので、ここで休憩をとろう。
English: I am somewhat fatigued, and so I will take a brief break.

Japanese: 証人を呼ぶのは、それからでよかろう。
English: I will call my witness after the recess.

Japanese: 休憩は１０分でいいかな。‥‥裁判長！
English: Which will last ten minutes. Judge!

Japanese: は、はい？
English: Y-yes?

Japanese: 何をしている。１０分間、休憩だ。
English: What are you doing? A ten minute recess! Now!

Japanese: い、いや、それは‥‥。
English: B-but, wait, I...

Japanese: 早く、そのウスぎたない木づちを鳴らさんかッ！
English: Just bang your flimsy gavel and get on with it, man!

Japanese: はいィッ！
English: Y-yes!

Japanese: では、ここで１０分間の休憩をとります。
English: Ahem. This court will take a ten minute recess.

Japanese: （‥‥イイのか？　コレで！）
English: (Who's running this court, anyway!?)

Japanese: １２月２６日　午前１１時９分地方裁判所　被告人第２控え室
English: December 26, 11:09 AM District Court Defendant Lobby No. 2

Japanese: 御剣！　どういうことだよ！
English: Edgeworth! What's going on here!?

Japanese: 凶器のピストルには、お前の指紋がついてたんだぞ！
English: Your fingerprints were on the murder weapon!

Japanese: う‥‥ううむ‥‥。
English: Uh... hmm...

Japanese: しかも、この写真を見ればわかるとおり、
English: And that foggy photo makes one thing clear...

Japanese: 被害者を撃つことができたのは、この写真の男しかいない！
English: The only one who could have shot that man was the person in the photo!

Japanese: ‥‥たしかに‥‥。
English: True...

Japanese: このボートに乗っているのは、お前なのか？
English: Was that you in the boat?

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥...そうだ。‥‥それは、私だ。
English: Yes. It was me.

Japanese: なんてこった‥‥。
English: What!?

Japanese: しかし‥‥信じてくれ。私は撃っていない。
English: But... you must believe me. I didn't shoot him.

Japanese: じゃあ、いったい誰が！
English: Th-then who did!?

Japanese: それが‥‥よくわからない。
English: I... don't know.

Japanese: ‥‥わからない？目の前で見ていたんだろう！
English: You don't know!? Weren't you right there!?

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥...いきなり、至近距離で銃声が聞こえたんだ。
English: I heard a gunshot from very close by.

Japanese: そして、あの男はボートから落ちた。
English: Then... the other man fell from the boat.

Japanese: ‥‥なぜかは知らんが‥‥、
English: I can't say why, but...

Japanese: あの男は、自分で自分を撃ったのではないだろうか‥‥。
English: I thought, at the time, that he had shot himself!

Japanese: じ‥‥自殺だ、っていうのか？
English: Y-you mean it was a suicide!?

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥...それしか考えられない。
English: That's the only explanation I can come up with.

Japanese: ‥‥ううん。（そんなことが、あるのか？）
English: Huh... (How am I going to convince anyone of that!?)

Japanese: ‥‥ところで、真宵ちゃん？
English: Say, Maya?

Japanese: え‥‥な、なに？
English: Huh? Wh-what?

Japanese: 千尋さんは？
English: Any progress with Mia?

Japanese: ううう‥‥。
English: Oh...

Japanese: ごめんなさい。‥‥ダメ、みたい‥‥。
English: Sorry. It's no good.

Japanese: ‥‥ううん。
English: Ugh...

Japanese: なるほどくん。
English: I know...

Japanese: ‥‥やっぱり、あたしじゃなんにもできないのかな？
English: I'm no good for anything, am I, Nick?

Japanese: お姉ちゃんのいないあたしなんて、意味ないのかな？
English: If I can't call my sister, I might as well not be here, right?

Japanese: ええと、それはその‥‥。
English: Um, well, actually...

Japanese: （法律の知識もない、法廷のテクニックも知らない‥‥）
English: (You don't know law, you don't have any trial experience or techniques...)

Japanese: （助けにはならないケド‥‥そんなこと、言えないよな）
English: (Yeah, you're pretty much useless... but I can't say that!)

Japanese: ‥‥なるほどくん。カオに出てるよ。
English: It's okay Nick. You don't have to say anything. Your face said it for you.

Japanese: （し‥‥しまった‥‥！）い‥‥いや、その‥‥ゴメン。
English: (Whoops!) N-no, o-of course we... I'm sorry.

Japanese: いいんだよ、なるほどくん。
English: It's okay, Nick.

Japanese: そ、そんなことはないよ。
English: No, of course not, I need you here!

Japanese: 一生けんめい助けようとしてくれているのはわかるから、
English: I can see you're always trying to help out.

Japanese: そのキモチだけでも‥‥
English: Even if you don't actually help... it's the thought that counts, right?

Japanese: ムリしなくていいよ、なるほどくん。
English: It's okay Nick, you don't have to make me feel better.

Japanese: あたし、弁護のことなんてなんにも知らないもんね。
English: I don't know anything about trials, or defense...

Japanese: 霊媒師なのに、霊も呼び出せないんだもん‥‥。
English: What's more, I'm a spirit medium who can't even contact spirits...

Japanese: ‥‥誰だって、調子がワルいときはあるよ。
English: Aww, everyone has their off days!

Japanese: ぼくだって、今はたまたまうまいコトやってるけど、
English: I mean, I've just been getting lucky lately...

Japanese: こんなの、いつまでつづくかわからないんだからさ！
English: But you never know when my luck is going to run out!

Japanese: ‥‥ホント？
English: Really...?

Japanese: ななな、成歩堂！
English: W-w-whoa! Wright!

Japanese: フキツなコトを言うな！‥‥心臓にワルい。
English: Don't jinx this case any worse than it already is! It's bad for my heart...

Japanese: ははは。（マイったな‥‥）
English: Oh? Oh! S-sorry... (Whoops!)

Japanese: では、審理を再開しましょう。
English: Court is back in session.

Japanese: 狩魔検事。‥‥証人をおねがいします。
English: Mr. von Karma, call your witness.

Japanese: ウム。
English: Yes.

Japanese: では、大沢木　ナツミを入廷させてほしい。
English: Will Ms. Lotta Hart take the stand?

Japanese: 大沢木　ナツミ。大学の研究生をしている？
English: Lotta Hart, you are a research student at a university?

Japanese: はい。
English: That I am.

Japanese: さっそくだが、事件の夜、目撃したことを話してもらおう。
English: Good. Begin by telling us what you saw the night of the incident.

Japanese: よけいなことは、いっさいハブいてもらいたい。
English: And don't add anything trivial or subjective.

Japanese: いいな。
English: Understand?

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥...なんやアンタ、そのタイドは‥‥
English: Y'all need to learn some manners.

Japanese: ‥‥いいな。
English: UNDERSTAND?

Japanese: ‥‥え、ええわ。わかったワ。
English: Y-yeah, I understand, I understand!

Japanese: えー、では。‥‥《証言》をおねがいします。
English: Er... very well. Your testimony, please.

Japanese: ～事件の夜、目撃したこと～
English: -- Witness's Account --

Japanese: あれは、クリスマス?イブの夜の、零時をすぎたころやったわ。
English: It was Christmas Eve, just after midnight, I reckon.

Japanese: ウチ、クルマの中におったんや。
English: I was in my car.

Japanese: 湖の方で、“パアン”て音がしてなあ。
English: I heard this "bang" come up from the lake.

Japanese: 窓から見ると、ボートの上に男が２人、おったんや。
English: When I looked out the window, I saw two gents in a boat.

Japanese: そのとき、もう１回、音がしたんや。
English: Then there was another bang...

Japanese: 湖の上には、間違いなくそのボートしか、なかったワ。
English: There wasn't nary a thing on the lake but that boat.

Japanese: それで、けっこう。
English: Enough.

Japanese: ‥‥うう。
English: Huh?

Japanese: 裁判長。
English: Judge!

Japanese: 彼女はたまたま、このときのようすを撮影していた。
English: She happened to take a photo of the incident!

Japanese: そのときの写真だ。‥‥受理していただきたい。
English: This is that photo. Accept it as evidence.

Japanese: こ‥‥これは！
English: W-well! This is a surprise!

Japanese: まさに、発砲のシュンカンではありませんか！
English: This looks like... the very moment of the murder!

Japanese: し‥‥静かにしなさい！退廷を命じますぞ！
English: O-Order! I will remove you from the court if I do not have order immediately!

Japanese: 証言にあったとおり、発砲があったとき、
English: As the witness testified, she looked at the lake when she heard the shot.

Japanese: 湖の上には、このボートしか存在しなかった。
English: There were no other boats on that lake!

Japanese: つまり、撃ったのは、いっしょに乗っていた男‥‥
English: So, the man in the boat with the victim must have been the one who shot him...

Japanese: すなわち、御剣　怜侍しかいない！
English: Yes. It was the defendant, Miles Edgeworth!!!

Japanese: 静粛に！　静粛に！　静粛に！‥‥静まりなさい！
English: Order! Order! Order...! I will have order!

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥...いかがか？　裁判長。
English: Well, Judge?

Japanese: これで、決定的ですね。
English: The evidence is... decisive.

Japanese: ‥‥私の迷いも、完全になくなりました。
English: I have very little doubt about this case.

Japanese: では、この場で御剣　怜侍に対する判決を‥‥
English: Very well, this court finds the defendant...

Japanese: ちょっと待ってください！
English: W-wait! Your Honor!

Japanese: ぼくの《尋問》がまだです！
English: I haven't cross-examined the witness yet!
A cross-examination?

Japanese: 尋問も何も、証拠写真がある。
English: We have photographic proof! What question can there possibly be!?

Japanese: この写真は、すべてを物語っている！
English: This photo is worth a thousand words... and they all read guilty!

Japanese: ‥‥ムダだ。
English: You lose.

Japanese: それともキサマは‥‥
English: Or...

Japanese: 今の証言に、何かムジュンした部分を見つけたのかね？
English: Do you claim to have found a contradiction in her testimony!?

Japanese: ‥‥！...!それならば、《尋問》をやってもらおうじゃないか。
English: Very well. If you have to, you may cross-examine the witness.

Japanese: しかし、もし当てずっぽうに探りを入れたあげく、
English: You will only flounder and ask meaningless questions!

Japanese: 何も出なかったら！
English: You will fail to find anything!

Japanese: そのときは、法廷侮辱罪を適用してもらうぞ。
English: And then, I will have you held in contempt of court!

Japanese: あの‥‥なるほどくん？“ほうてい”‥‥何？
English: Uh, Nick... contempt...?

Japanese: “ブジョク罪”。‥‥意味はわかるだろ？
English: Contempt of court, you know?

Japanese: うん。‥‥なんとなく。
English: I... guess I understand.

Japanese: それで、どう？
English: Well... what are you going to do?

Japanese: さっきの証言、ムジュンした部分はあった？
English: Do you really think there was a contradiction with the facts in her testimony?

Japanese: ‥‥い、１カ所“これかな”という部分が‥‥。
English: I... think I noticed one little thing...

Japanese: すごいね、なるほどくん！‥‥あたし、気づかなかったよ。
English: Wow, I'm impressed, Nick! I didn't notice anything!

Japanese: じゃあ、アイツの挑戦、受けてやろうよ！
English: Right! Let's take him on!

Japanese: う‥‥うん。（自信、ないんだけどなあ‥‥）
English: Y-yeah... (I got a bad feeling about this...)

Japanese: ‥‥正直なところ、見つからなかったんだよな‥‥。
English: Honestly, I found nothing.

Japanese: ナツミさんの証言、ちょっとおかしかったよね。
English: I dunno, I thought her testimony was strange.

Japanese: ハッキリしたこと、なんにも言わないんだもん。
English: She was so vague about everything!

Japanese: 狩魔検事が、言わせなかったんじゃないかな。
English: I bet von Karma probably fed her her lines.

Japanese: ‥‥でも。
English: But...

Japanese: ここで尋問しなかったら、あの裁判長‥‥。
English: But if you don't cross- examine her, the judge will...

Japanese: 間違いなく木づちをたたいて“有罪”だな‥‥。
English: He'll bang down that "flimsy gavel" of his and declare Edgeworth guilty.

Japanese: ここは、ハッタリで押しとおすしかないね。
English: I guess we'll just have to bluff it.

Japanese: でも、シッパイしたら‥‥法廷侮辱罪だぞ。
English: But if we mess up... contempt of court.

Japanese: 負けちゃったら‥‥いっしょだよ！
English: Hey, if we lose we'll be held in public contempt!

Japanese: そ‥‥そうだな。
English: Er... right.

Japanese: わかりました。‥‥尋問をします！
English: I understand... I will cross-examine the witness!

Japanese: クックックッ‥‥‥‥よかろう。
English: Tsk tsk tsk... Very well!

Japanese: 時間のムダにならないことを祈ってますよ。
English: I pray for your sake this isn't a waste of time.

Japanese: ～事件の夜、目撃したこと～
English: -- Witness's Account --

Japanese: あれは、クリスマス?イブの夜の、零時をすぎたころやったわ。
English: It was Christmas Eve, just after midnight, I reckon.

Japanese: ウチ、クルマの中におったんや。
English: I was in my car.

Japanese: 湖の方で、“パアン”て音がしてなあ。
English: I heard this "bang" come up from the lake.

Japanese: 窓から見ると、ボートの上に男が２人、おったんや。
English: When I looked out the window, I saw two gents in a boat.

Japanese: そのとき、もう１回、音がしたんや。
English: Then there was another bang...

Japanese: 湖の上には、間違いなくそのボートしか、なかったワ。
English: There wasn't nary a thing on the lake but that boat.

Japanese: （む‥‥ムジュンなんて、見つからないぞ‥‥）
English: (Th-there weren't any contradictions in there!)

Japanese: ごめんね、なるほどくん。お姉ちゃんがいれば‥‥。
English: Sorry, Nick. If only my sister were here...

Japanese: （真宵ちゃん、気にしてるみたいだな‥‥）
English: (Maya's really taking this hard.)

Japanese: “零時をすぎたころ”‥‥ということは、
English: Just after midnight, you say?

Japanese: 正確にはクリスマスイブではなく、もうクリスマスになっていた？
English: In other words, it was no longer Christmas Eve... but Christmas Day!

Japanese: え？　ま、まあ、そうやなあ。
English: Huh? Uh, yeah, well, yes.

Japanese: ‥‥たしか、ムジュンを指摘するという約束では‥‥？
English: I know you want to find contradictions, but really!

Japanese: うむむ‥‥。
English: Mmmph.

Japanese: そのとおりですね。
English: I hope your next contradiction is a little more relevant to the trial.

Japanese: 証人は、証言をつづけてください。
English: Witness, continue your testimony.

Japanese: あの場所でキャンプをしていたのは、なぜですか？
English: Why were you camping there, anyway?

Japanese: ウチ、大学の研究員やねん。
English: I'm a research student at my university.

Japanese: 研究に使う写真を撮ろうと思ってなァ。
English: I was taking pictures to use in my research.

Japanese: （‥‥さっきから、どうもハッキリしないんだよな‥‥）
English: (What research? This all sounds suspicious...)

Japanese: ナツミさん。
English: Ms. Hart.

Japanese: 研究というのは、具体的にはどういう‥‥？
English: Could you be more specific about your "research"?

Japanese: “証人がなぜ、あの場でキャンプをしていたのか？”
English: What does the witness's motive in camping by the lake have to do with this case!?

Japanese: そんなことは、事件にはなんのカンケイもない！
English: The answer is "nothing"! I object to this line of questioning!

Japanese: 質問は却下する。
English: Objection sustained!

Japanese: い、いやいやいや。‥‥決めるのは私です！
English: W-w-wait, now, I'm the one who says that!

Japanese: じゃあ、サッサと却下したまえ！
English: Well, then say it already!

Japanese: 質問を却下します。
English: Objection sustained.

Japanese: （ダメだ、コイツは‥‥）
English: (Thanks for nothing, Your Honor...)

Japanese: （‥‥まあ、今のところは見逃しておくか‥‥）
English: (I think I'll let that one slide for now.)

Japanese: そのときは、湖は見ていなかったんですか？
English: So, you weren't looking at the lake at that time?

Japanese: そうやなあ。
English: Nope.

Japanese: ウチが湖を見たのは、その音を聞いてから‥‥
English: I looked after I heard that noise.

Japanese: 何度も言わせるな。
English: She's said that already!

Japanese: ワガハイは“ムジュンを指摘しろ”と言ったはずだ。
English: I asked you to find contradictions!

Japanese: ちまちまと、情報収集などしてもらっては困る！
English: Not leisurely chat with the witness!

Japanese: （くそぉ‥‥）
ハッキリ見たんですか？２人の男を‥‥。
English: Could you clearly see the two men?

Japanese: 写真、見てみィ。ちゃんと写っとるやん？
English: Just look at the picture! Clear enough for you?

Japanese: （‥‥どうしよう‥‥？）
English: (Uh oh...)

Japanese: ちょっと待ってください！
English: Wait a second!

Japanese: ぼくは、写真のことを聞いたんじゃない！
English: I wasn't asking you about the photo!

Japanese: “あなたが”見たのかどうかを聞いているんです！
English: I was asking if YOU saw the two men!

Japanese: そ‥‥そりゃあ、モチロン‥‥‥
English: Uh, yeah, well, of course...

Japanese: 証人は見たと証言している！それに、写真もある！
English: The witness has testified that she saw them! There's also a photo!

Japanese: いいかげん、指摘したまえ！‥‥おトクイの“ムジュン”を！
English: You'd best look elsewhere for your precious contradictions!

Japanese: （何か、かくしてることがありそうなんだけど‥‥）
English: (He jumped in quick... He's hiding something!)

Japanese: （‥‥まあ、今のところは見逃しておくか‥‥）
English: (I think I'd best let that one slide...)

Japanese: そのときは、見たんですか？撃ったシュンカンを‥‥。
English: Were you watching the very moment the shot rang out?

Japanese: ええ、まあ、そりゃあ‥‥。
English: Well, yeah, sure...

Japanese: 弁護人は、どうでもいいことばかり聞いておる！
English: You're asking meaningless questions! Meaningless!

Japanese: “ムジュン”を指摘したまえ！‥‥さあ！
English: Contradictions, Mr. Wright. Not meaningless babble!

Japanese: （狩魔のやつ‥‥）
English: (von Karma, I think I hate you.)

Japanese: （ぼくに、証人と話をさせないつもりらしいな‥‥）
English: (He's trying to keep me from talking to the witness...)

Japanese: （いったい、なぜ‥‥？）
English: (To what end...?)

Japanese: それは、たしかですか？
English: Are you sure about that?

Japanese: ああ、間違いないで！
English: Yeah, sure as a country gal can be!

Japanese: （どうやら、ずいぶん、自信があるみたいだな‥‥）
English: (That sounds pretty sure.)

Japanese: どうしてそんなに自信があるんですか？
English: How come you're so sure?

Japanese: そりゃあアンタ、湖をスミズミまで見たからなァ。
English: Well, heck, I scanned the whole lake.

Japanese: （“湖をスミズミまで見た”？）
English: ("Scanned the whole lake"?)

Japanese: （まるで、ボートよりも、湖の方に興味があったみたいだな‥‥）
English: (It almost sounds like she was more interested in the lake than the boat...)

Japanese: ナツミさん。‥‥あなたは、
English: Ms. Hart... you--

Japanese: ‥‥弁護士。
English: Mr. Wright!

Japanese: 証人はすでに答えておる。‥‥じゅうぶんな答えを、な。
English: The witness has answered the question in full. Mmph!

Japanese: これ以上の質問に、意味はない。却下！
English: No need for further questions! Objection sustained!

Japanese: あ、あの‥‥それは私が‥‥。
English: U-uh, that's what I'm...

Japanese: 却下でいいな！
English: Sustained!

Japanese: ‥‥はい。
English: Y-yes, of course.

Japanese: （‥‥まいったな‥‥）
English: (Oh great...)

Japanese: （‥‥どうすればいい！）
English: (What am I supposed to do now?)

Japanese: （ナツミさんも自信マンマンだ‥‥）
English: (Lotta seems pretty sure of herself.)

Japanese: （ヘタにつっこまない方がいいかもしれないな‥‥）
English: (I'd better not forge blindly ahead here.)

Japanese: （それにしても‥‥）
English: (Still... this whole thing stinks.)

Japanese: そこまで！
English: Enough!

Japanese: もうけっこうです、成歩堂くん。
English: I think we've heard all we need to hear, Mr. Wright.

Japanese: どうやらあなたは、“ムジュン”を指摘することはできないようだ。
English: It seems you are unable to find a contradiction in the testimony worth noting.

Japanese: いや、しかし‥‥！
English: B-but, Your Honor!

Japanese: ‥‥約束は守ってもらう。
English: You keep your promise!

Japanese: 弁護人。
English: Mr. Wright.

Japanese: これ以上の発言には、法廷侮辱罪を適用します。
English: I am afraid that I will have to penalize any further outbursts... By holding you in contempt of court!

Japanese: ‥‥そうなったら、即座に退廷しなければならない。
English: And if that happens, you'll have to leave the courtroom immediately!

Japanese: いいのかな‥‥？
English: Understood...?
Uh... uh huh.

Japanese: なるほどくん‥‥。
English: Nick...!

Japanese: やっぱり、ナツミさんの証言は少しおかしいよ‥‥。
English: Lotta's testimony is fishy, Nick! Real fishy!

Japanese: た‥‥たしかになんかスッキリしないよなあ。
English: I... know what you mean.

Japanese: でも‥‥。
English: But...

Japanese: 発言を封じられたら、もう、どうすることも‥‥。
English: If I can't say anything, what can I do?

Japanese: この時点で、審理はじゅうぶんだと考えます。
English: I believe we've covered the evidence sufficiently to make a decision.

Japanese: では、被告人に判決を言い渡していただこう。
English: Then, pass your judgment!

Japanese: わかりました。
English: Very well.

Japanese: では、御剣　怜侍。証言台についてください。
English: Mr. Miles Edgeworth, please take the stand.

Japanese: い‥‥いまのは、‥‥どなたが‥‥？
English: Wh-who was that!?

Japanese: あ、あた‥‥あたしですっ！
English: It... was me.

Japanese: （真宵ちゃん！）
English: (Maya!)

Japanese: ど‥‥どうしましたか？トイレなら‥‥
English: I-is something wrong? D-do you need to use the facilities?

Japanese: そうじゃありません！
English: No, I do not!

Japanese: ナツミさん！あなたの証言はオカシイです！
English: Lotta Hart! Your testimony stinks!

Japanese: 湖を見ていたのかどうかはアイマイだし、
English: It's unclear whether you were actually looking at the lake!

Japanese: 御剣検事さんを見たかどうかもハッキリしないし‥‥。
English: It's highly doubtful that you actually saw Mr. Edgeworth!

Japanese: ホントのコト、話してください！
English: Tell us the truth!

Japanese: 人のいのちが、‥‥かかってるんですよ！
English: This is a matter of life or death!
Lotta!

Japanese: あなたは、ホントに御剣検事をハッキリ見たんですか！
English: Did you really clearly see Mr. Edgeworth that night!?

Japanese: 御剣検事が、ピストルを撃つところを‥‥！
English: Did you see him fire that pistol!?

Japanese: やめなさい！
English: You will stand down!

Japanese: ‥‥弁護側の発言は認めません！
English: The court does not acknowledge the defense's outburst!

Japanese: 答えて！　ナツミさん！
English: Answer me, Lotta!

Japanese: なんや！　ヒトをワルモノあつかいしよって！
English: What's the big idea, treating me like some kind of criminal!?

Japanese: 見たでウチは！‥‥御剣を、ハッキリと‥‥
English: I saw him! I swear it! I saw Edgeworth...

Japanese: そこまでだ！
English: Enough!

Japanese: ‥‥裁判長。
English: Judge...

Japanese: ‥‥法廷侮辱罪で、弁護士を退廷させたまえ。
English: Declare the defense in contempt of court!

Japanese: ‥‥わ‥‥わかりました。
English: Y-yes... yes, of course.

Japanese: 約束ですから、しかたありませんね。
English: I'm sorry, but you WERE warned.

Japanese: 係官！　成歩堂　龍一の身柄を拘束しなさい！
English: Guard! Escort Mr. Wright out of the courtroom!

Japanese: 法廷侮辱罪により、退廷していただきます‥‥
English: He is in contempt of court, and must leave.

Japanese: （ここまでか‥‥！）
待ってください！
English: Wait!

Japanese: は、発言をしたのは、あた、あたしです！
English: I-I was the one who made the outburst, Your Honor!

Japanese: なるほどくんは、カンケイありません！
English: Nick is innocent!

Japanese: ハッ！
English: Hah!

Japanese: たいした違いはあるまい。
English: What's the difference?

Japanese: どっちにしろ、残っているのはもう、有罪宣告のみだ。
English: All that remains is for the guilty verdict to be declared!

Japanese: ‥‥そうだな？弁護士?成歩堂　龍一！
English: Isn't that right, Mr. Phoenix Wright!?

Japanese: それは違います！
English: Wrong!

Japanese: ‥‥なんだと？
English: What!?

Japanese: （くやしいけど‥‥そのとおりだな‥‥）
English: (Dammit, he's right...)

Japanese: な‥‥なるほどくん！どうにかならないの！
English: N-Nick! There must be something we can do!

Japanese: ‥‥ム‥‥ムリだよ。
English: I... don't think there is.

Japanese: だって、もう尋問は終わっちゃったんだから‥‥。
English: The cross-examination is over already!

Japanese: で‥‥でも！　でも！今、ナツミさん、言ったよ！
English: B-but... but didn't Lotta just say she saw him?

Japanese: “御剣検事を見た”って。
English: She did, Nick! She just said that she saw Mr. Edgeworth!

Japanese: さっきは、そんなこと‥‥言ってなかったよ！
English: She didn't say that in her testimony, did she?
Y-you're right!

Japanese: 裁判長！
English: Your Honor!

Japanese: たった今、大沢木　ナツミさんはこう言いました！
English: Did you hear what Ms. Hart just said?

Japanese: “御剣検事を、ハッキリ見た”と！
English: She said she clearly saw Mr. Edgeworth!

Japanese: これは、今までにない《証言》です！
English: That was not in the testimony!

Japanese: 弁護側は、この証言に対して、《尋問》をする権利があります！
English: That changes her testimony, and I have a right to cross-examine her again!

Japanese: 静粛に！　静粛に！　静粛に！
English: Order! Order! Order! Order! Order! Order!

Japanese: そんな勝手な言いぶんがとおるか！
English: You're in contempt of court! It's too late for wild claims!

Japanese: 裁判長！‥‥そんな意見は、却下だ！
English: Judge! Sustain my objection!
I'm sorry, Mr. von Karma, but I cannot.

Japanese: なにっ！
English: What!?

Japanese: 大沢木　ナツミさんは、新しい《証言》をしました。
English: Ms. Lotta Hart has made a new testimony.

Japanese: 当然、弁護人は《尋問》をする権利があります！
English: The defense does have a right to cross-examine her again.

Japanese: しかし！　弁護人は法廷侮辱罪で‥‥！
English: B-but he is in contempt of court!

Japanese: ブジョクしたのは、あたしです！
English: No, I am!

Japanese: 逮捕するなら、あたしだけにしてください！
English: If you're going to arrest someone, arrest me!

Japanese: ‥‥ふむう。
English: Hmm...

Japanese: わかりました。
English: Very well.

Japanese: 綾里　真宵！
English: Maya Fey!

Japanese: あなたには、退廷してもらいましょう。
English: You will leave the courtroom immediately.

Japanese: なるほどくん！
English: Nick!

Japanese: ‥‥あたし、こんなことしかできないから。
English: I did what I could...

Japanese: あとは、たのんだよ。‥‥おねがい！
English: You have to do the rest! Good luck!

Japanese: 真宵ちゃん‥‥！
English: M-Maya...!

Japanese: フン！　くだらん‥‥。
English: Peh! I care not for this melodrama!

Japanese: わかっているだろうな、弁護士！
English: Listen well, Mr. Wright!

Japanese: ワガハイの証人に、ゆさぶりなど許さんぞ！
English: I do not tolerate badgering of my witnesses!

Japanese: （なんとしても‥‥）
English: (I'm running out of time...)

Japanese: （ムジュンをつきつけなくてはならないワケか‥‥）
English: (I'd better find a contradiction in here or else...)

Japanese: では！　《尋問》をしていただきましょう。
English: Mr. Wright! Begin your cross-examination!

Japanese: ～最後の発言について～
English: -- That Last Statement --

Japanese: ウチ、ハッキリ見たんや。ボートの男は、たしかに御剣やった。
English: I saw it clear as day. The man on the boat was Mr. Edgeworth!

Japanese: （‥‥証言は、たったこれだけ）
English: (That's it...?)

Japanese: （ここにムジュンを見つけられなかったら‥‥）
English: (Uh oh. I don't know if I can find anything in that...)

Japanese: （真宵ちゃんに合わせるカオがないな‥‥）
English: (What will Maya say...?)

Japanese: では、もう１人の男は‥‥
English: Well, what about the other man?

Japanese: さきほどからの約束を、何度ムシするつもりか！
English: You cannot expect to be allowed to blithely ignore your promise, Mr. Wright!

Japanese: 証人の証言に“ムジュンがある”とキサマは言ったはずだ。
English: I believe you claimed there was a contradiction in the witness's testimony!

Japanese: さっさと、それを示したまえ！できるものならな！
English: Well, find it! If you can!

Japanese: 弁護人。‥‥ペナルティを受けてもらいましょう。
English: Mr. Wright, I have to assign you a penalty.
(Damn...!)

Japanese: ‥‥やっと。
English: Got you.

Japanese: やっとつかまえましたよ。‥‥ナツミさん。
English: Got you, Ms. Hart! Finally!

Japanese: な‥‥なんや。
English: Wh-what!? You got what?

Japanese: この写真を見てください。
English: Look at this photograph.

Japanese: ウチが撮った写真‥‥？
English: The photo I took...?

Japanese: いいですか。写真はハッキリ写しています。
English: The very same. There's something I want you to see in this photo... it's quite clearly visible.

Japanese: 湖に立ちこめる、キリをね。
English: The fog, Ms. Hart.

Japanese: ど‥‥どういうことや‥‥？
English: So... so?

Japanese: あなたのごジマンの高価なフィルムでも、
English: This picture was taken with professional, high quality film, correct?

Japanese: ボートに乗っている男のカオは、まったく写っていません。
English: Yet even it could not capture the faces of the men on the boat!

Japanese: なのに、なぜ！
English: Yet you claim you saw Mr. Edgeworth!

Japanese: あなたには、御剣が見えたのですかッ！
English: How!?

Japanese: あッ！
English: Whut!? Wha--!

Japanese: ‥‥たしかに、そのとおりです！
English: Mr. Wright has a point!

Japanese: だからワガハイは、そのことは証言させなかったのだ！
English: That's why I told her not to say that in her testimony! Please!

Japanese: しかし、証人は今、こうして証言をしてしまった！
English: Yet, now she has said it, Mr. von Karma.

Japanese: なぜ御剣を見ることができたか！説明をしていただきたい！
English: How could you possibly see Mr. Edgeworth!? Explain yourself!

Japanese: 証人。
English: Ms. Hart.

Japanese: な‥‥なんや！
English: What!

Japanese: あなたには、被告人が見えたのですか？
English: Could you see the defendant that night?

Japanese: ト‥‥トーゼンや！
English: O-of course! I said I could and I meant I could!

Japanese: では、そのことを《証言》していただきましょう。
English: Then, please testify as to the circumstances of your sighting.

Japanese: （‥‥やったぞ！）
English: (I did it!)

Japanese: （ついにスキを見つけた！‥‥今度こそ‥‥！）
English: (I finally found a hole in von Karma's carefully vague testimony!)

Japanese: ～どうして御剣を目撃できたか？～
English: -- How Edgeworth Was Seen --

Japanese: たしかに、あの晩は寒くて、ぎょうさんキリが出ててん。
English: Yer right. It was a cold night, and the fog was thick as grits.

Japanese: せやからウチ、カメラをセットしてクルマの中に戻ったワケや。
English: So, once I was finished setting up my camera, I got back in the car.

Japanese: でもな。ウチ、ちゃんと双眼鏡を持ってきてたからな。
English: Still, I brought my binoculars with me.

Japanese: 湖で音がしたときは、双眼鏡を使ォて見たわけや。
English: When I heard that noise out on the lake, I looked with my binoculars.

Japanese: どや。これなら問題ないやろ？
English: See? No problem!

Japanese: ふむう‥‥。双眼鏡を使った‥‥。
English: Hmm...You used binoculars?

Japanese: わかりました。では、弁護人。‥‥尋問を。
English: Very well. You may begin your cross- examination, Mr. Wright.

Japanese: （なんとしても、ムジュンを見つけなければ‥‥）
English: (This one'd better be good!)

Japanese: ～どうして御剣を目撃できたか？～
English: -- How Edgeworth Was Seen --

Japanese: たしかに、あの晩は寒くて、ぎょうさんキリが出ててん。
English: Yer right. It was a cold night, and the mist was thick as grits.

Japanese: せやからウチ、カメラをセットしてクルマの中に戻ったワケや。
English: So, once I was finished setting up my camera, I got back in the car.

Japanese: でもな。ウチ、ちゃんと双眼鏡を持ってきてたからな。
English: Still, I brought my binoculars with me.

Japanese: そのカメラは、流星を撮影するためにセットしたものや。
English: The camera was set up to take pictures of a meteor shower.

Japanese: 湖で音がしたときは、双眼鏡を使ォて見たわけや。
English: When I heard that noise out on the lake, I looked with my binoculars.

Japanese: （‥‥狩魔のジャマが入ろうと‥‥）
English: (I don't care how many von Karmic objections I get...)

Japanese: （なんとしても、突破口を見つけてやるぞ‥‥）
English: (I'm going to find a hole in this testimony if it's the last thing I do!)

Japanese: じゃあなぜ、御剣を目撃できたんですか！
English: So? How could you see Edgeworth!?

Japanese: まあまあ、待ちィな。今、話すワ。
English: Now, just hold your horses for a second!

Japanese: セッカチな男はモテへんで。
English: You hasty Yankee types'd never find a gal where I'm from.

Japanese: モテませんよ弁護人。
English: Defense attorneys have trouble with that as it is.

Japanese: （ダメ押しされてしまった‥‥）
English: (Nobody loves me...)

Japanese: “カメラ”‥‥ですか？
English: Your "camera"...?

Japanese: そうや。自動シャッターをセットして‥‥、
English: Yeah. It's got an automatic...

Japanese: 今、問題になっているのは、“御剣検事を見たこと”だ。
English: The issue we are concerned with here is Ms. Hart seeing Mr. Edgeworth!

Japanese: カメラのことなど、カンケイない！
English: The camera has nothing to do with this at all!

Japanese: ‥‥異議を認めます。
English: Objection sustained.

Japanese: （なぜ狩魔は、質問に答えさせないんだろう‥‥？）
English: (Argh! He's not letting her answer any of my questions!)

Japanese: “双眼鏡”ですか？
English: Binoculars?

Japanese: せや。双眼鏡や。
English: Yeah, binoculars.

Japanese: 昨日のお話では、たしか、あなたは流星を撮影していたんですよね？
English: Yesterday, you mentioned that you were out looking for shooting stars, correct?

Japanese: そ‥‥そうやけど。
English: Well... yeah.

Japanese: 星を見るなら、双眼鏡ではなく、望遠鏡じゃないんですか‥‥？
English: Wouldn't you need a telescope, not binoculars, for that?

Japanese: ‥‥！...!そもそも、あなたがセットしたカメラは、
English: I've got doubts about your camera, too!

Japanese: ほんとうに、流星を撮影するためのものだったのですか？
English: Was that really to take pictures of meteor showers?

Japanese: カメラのことなど、事件にはなんのカンケイもない！
English: The camera is irrelevant to this case!

Japanese: そんなことは言い切れない！
English: You can't say that for certain!

Japanese: ふむう‥‥成歩堂くん。
English: Hmm... Mr. Wright.

Japanese: カメラのことは、それほど重要なことなのですか？
English: Is the camera really relevant to this case?

Japanese: もし重要なことならば、つづけて尋問をゆるしましょう。
English: If you believe it is, you may continue with this line of questioning.

Japanese: だが！
English: But know this!

Japanese: 何も出なかった場合は、カクゴしてもらうぞ！
English: If you find nothing with this, there will be consequences!

Japanese: 弁護人、どうしますか？
English: Well, Mr. Wright?

Japanese: カメラの問題を、さらに追求しますか？
English: Do you wish to press further about the camera?

Japanese: ‥‥わかりました。質問を取り下げます。
English: Okay, Your Honor... I withdraw my question.

Japanese: （狩魔検事のことだ‥‥）
English: (I have to remember who I'm dealing with!)

Japanese: （ぼくに異議をとなえさせて、発言を封じる作戦かもしれない）
English: (I bet von Karma wants to trap me so he can put me in contempt!)

Japanese: （ここは、やめておいたほうがよさそうだ‥‥）
English: (I think I'll hang back for now and see what happens.)

Japanese: ‥‥フン！
English: Hah!

Japanese: （ここは、勝負どころだ！強気にいこう！）
English: (This is make-it-or-break-it time!)

Japanese: カメラの問題は、とんでもなく重要です。
English: The camera is of utmost importance, Your Honor.

Japanese: この事件のすべてと言ってもいいでしょう。
English: It is, perhaps, the key to this entire case!

Japanese: ‥‥だから、証言をしていただきたい！
English: Therefore, I will continue my line of questioning!

Japanese: （‥‥なんて、出まかせを言ってしまった‥‥）
English: (Wow! Maybe I went a little overboard there...)

Japanese: わかりました。
English: Very well!

Japanese: 証人！　カメラについて、キチンと証言してください。
English: Ms. Hart! You will testify to the court about the camera!

Japanese: はいはい。
English: Yeah, yeah, I hear ya.

Japanese: ‥‥キリがかかっていたら、双眼鏡で見ても同じなのでは‥‥？
English: If there was a heavy fog, how would binoculars change that at all?

Japanese: どういうこと？
English: What do you mean?

Japanese: 双眼鏡で見たからと言って、キリが晴れるわけではありません。
English: Even binoculars can't see through fog!

Japanese: それで、ハッキリ見えたんですか？
English: But... you say you clearly saw him?

Japanese: ううん‥‥。
English: Er...

Japanese: 見えたと‥‥思うで‥‥。
English: I did... yeah.

Japanese: そこまで！
English: Enough!

Japanese: ギモンの余地はないな‥‥。
English: There is no room for doubt in her testimony!

Japanese: （ナツミさん、最後は口ごもっていたぞ‥‥）
English: (Hmm. She sounded pretty doubtful to me!)

Japanese: （でも、ムジュンをつきつけないことには、先へ進めないか‥‥）
English: (But... I have to find a clear contradiction first.)

Japanese: ナツミさん。
English: Ms. Hart.

Japanese: 流星を撮影するのに、なぜあの湖を選んだんですか？
English: What made you choose that lake to photograph meteors?

Japanese: あの湖は、キリが濃くて、星の撮影には向かないような‥‥。
English: You know the fog gets thick on that lake. It's not very suited to stargazing.

Japanese: え、あ‥‥。そやね。その、なんだ。
English: Yeah, well... Y'see, I...

Japanese: ウチ、そこまでアタマが回らへんかったんやァ。
English: I guess I wasn't thinking too straight! Har!

Japanese: 弁護士！
English: Mr. Wright!

Japanese: 証人のアタマがワルいことを攻撃するのは、やめてもらおう。
English: I will not have you badgering my witness because of her challenged intellect!

Japanese: な‥‥なんや！
English: Now wait a minute!

Japanese: 証言をつづけたまえ。‥‥御剣を見た方法について、だ。
English: Continue your testimony. You were saying how it was that you saw Edgeworth?

Japanese: うう‥‥。
English: Grr...

Japanese: よけいなことは言わんでよろしい。
English: No unnecessary comments, please.

Japanese: “流星を撮影していた”‥‥その証言は、ウソですね！
English: You were photographing shooting stars? That's a lie!

Japanese: な、なんやてェ！
English: S-says who!?

Japanese: この写真を見てください。
English: Take a look at this photograph.

Japanese: これは、自動シャッターで撮影した写真ですね？
English: This was taken on an automatic setting, was it not?

Japanese: そ‥‥そうや。
English: Y-yeah, that's right.

Japanese: 流星を撮影するなら、カメラは空を向いていなければならない！
English: You have to point a camera upwards to take photos of the stars, Ms. Hart!

Japanese: しかし、この写真では、
English: But, as this photograph clearly shows...

Japanese: あきらかにカメラは、湖面を向いている！
English: The camera was pointed directly at the lake!

Japanese: “流星を撮影していた”‥‥その証言は、ウソですね！
English: You were photographing shooting stars? That's a lie!

Japanese: な、なんやてェ！
English: S-says who!?

Japanese: ぼくは昨日、実際にセットされたカメラを見ました。
English: I saw the camera you set up yesterday.

Japanese: あきらかにカメラは、湖面に向けてセットしてありました！
English: It was pointed directly at the lake!

Japanese: 流星を撮影するなら、カメラは空を向いているはずです！
English: You have to point a camera upwards to take photos of the stars, Ms. Hart!

Japanese: ううッ！
English: Ooof!

Japanese: 成歩堂くん！どういうことですか？
English: Mr. Wright! What are you driving at?

Japanese: 証人は、流星を撮影していたのではなかった！
English: The witness was not at the lake to photograph shooting stars, Your Honor!

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥！...!じゃ、じゃあ‥‥何を撮影していたんですか？
English: W-well then, what exactly was she photographing?

Japanese: いや‥‥そこまではわかりませんが‥‥。
English: Well... that I don't know.

Japanese: 証人が何を撮影しようとしていたか‥‥
English: What the witness takes photos of is entirely her business!

Japanese: そんなことは問題ではない！その上‥‥、
English: This has nothing to do with the case!

Japanese: その答えすら提示することができないとは笑止！
English: Not to mention that you can't even show us another reason why she might have been there.

Japanese: ‥‥弁護士。
English: Mr. Wright...

Japanese: これ以上、材料がないなら、尋問は終了してもらうぞ！
English: If you are out of material, I suggest we end this mockery of a cross-examination.

Japanese: （まずい‥‥このまま終わるワケにはいかない！）
English: (Uh oh... I can't let the trial end like this!)

Japanese: （ここは、とにかく何か見せておかないと！）
English: (I have to show them something!)

Japanese: 裁判長！‥‥これを見てください。
English: Your Honor! Take a look at this...

Japanese: ナツミさんが何を撮影しようとしていたか‥‥！
English: What was Ms. Hart trying to photograph at the lake?

Japanese: いかがです？
English: Well, Your Honor? What do you think?

Japanese: 正直、サッパリわかりません。
English: I... think I'm confused.

Japanese: しょせん、弁護士などそんなものだろう。
English: Wrong again, Mr. Wright. So very wrong.

Japanese: ここでは、ハッタリは通用しないのだ。
English: You can't get through this one with your weak bluffs!

Japanese: さ、裁判長！
English: Y-Your Honor!

Japanese: お遊びはここまでにして、今度こそ証拠品を見せましょう。
English: Enough playing around! Here's the real evidence!

Japanese: お遊びをやめるのは、あなたの方です！
English: Enough playing around is my line, Mr. Wright!

Japanese: は、はあ。
English: Uh...

Japanese: ナツミさん。
English: Ms. Hart.

Japanese: あなたが撮影しようとしていたのは、これです！
English: This is what you were trying to photograph!

Japanese: なんですか？‥‥雑誌の記事‥‥？
English: What's this? A newspaper article?

Japanese: “ひょっしー”‥‥？
English: Gourdy...?

Japanese: ああ‥‥最近、ひょうたん湖で目撃された‥‥。
English: Ah, the sighting at Gourd Lake...

Japanese: ナツミさん！‥‥どうなんですか？
English: Well, Ms. Hart?

Japanese: ‥‥し、知らんで！なんや、証拠でもあるん？
English: I... I never heard of no lake monster! You got proof or something?

Japanese: ウチが、その“ひょっしー”の写真を撮ろうとしてた証拠や！
English: Let's see you prove that I was down at the lake trying to photograph this "Gourdy."

Japanese: ‥‥証拠は、ありませんけど‥‥。
English: Well, I don't have proof...

Japanese: ‥‥実にくだらない男だ！
English: Bah! Your ineptitude is entertaining, Mr. Wright, but enough is enough!

Japanese: 証拠もないのに、あらぬ言いがかりを‥‥
English: I've had enough of baseless claims made without a hair of evidence to support them!

Japanese: そこまで言うなら、証拠を見せましょう！
English: Well all right, if that's how you want to play... I'll show you evidence!

Japanese: 弁護人！だいじょうぶなんですか？
English: Mr. Wright! Are you sure about this?

Japanese: （どうも、あの検事にバカにされると、ガマンできない！）
English: (I'm sure sick of that smarmy prosecutor putting me down!)

Japanese: （これって、挑発に乗っているだけなのかな‥‥？）
English: (Or... did he taunt me so I'd get mad and make a mistake?)

Japanese: 証拠！　もちろんありますよ！
English: I have it! Proof!

Japanese: ふむう。‥‥それはおもしろい。
English: Hmm... Intriguing!

Japanese: では、見せていただきましょう。‥‥慎重におねがいしますよ。
English: Very well, let's see it. And... no joking around this time, please.

Japanese: 証人が、ヒョッシーの写真を撮ろうとしていたことを示す、証拠品を！
English: Here is proof that the witness was trying to photograph Gourdy, the lake monster!

Japanese: 証拠は、これです！‥‥カンタンなことですよ。
English: Here's my proof! It's simple!

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥。...カンタンなことなら、間違えないでいただきたい。
English: If it's simple... then why have you obviously made an error, Mr. Wright?
Urk...?

Japanese: なんやそれ？
English: What's that!?

Japanese: 帰れ。
English: Go home. Consider a career change.

Japanese: （しまった！）
English: (Gah!)

Japanese: （ぼくもふくめて、みんなリカイ不能か！）
English: (How can they so casually toss aside this eviden... Oh. Whoops. Wrong evidence.)

Japanese: ‥‥やれやれ。もう一度、おねがいしましょう。
English: Luckily for you, I'm in a patient mood, Mr. Wright. One more time please.

Japanese: 証拠は、ナツミさん！あなたのカメラですよ！
English: The proof, Ms. Hart, is your own camera!

Japanese: ‥‥！...!あなたのカメラは、“破裂音”に反応するようセットされていた。
English: Your camera was set to take photos in response to loud noises, correct?

Japanese: この写真が撮られたのも、銃声にカメラが反応したからです。
English: Thus this photograph here, taken when a gun fired on the lake.

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥。...そして、ここに“ヒョッシー”の記事があります。
English: And here, this article about "Gourdy."

Japanese: この記事によると、
English: According to this article...

Japanese: ヒョッシーがあらわれるときには、“破裂音”がするらしいですね。
English: Gourdy made a loud noise when it emerged!

Japanese: どうですか！
English: Well?

Japanese: あなたはヒョッシーを撮影しようとしていた！
English: You were trying to photograph Gourdy, weren't you!

Japanese: だから、カメラの自動シャッターを“破裂音”にセットしていたんだ！
English: That's why you had set your camera to respond to loud noises!

Japanese: 証拠は、この“ヒョッシー”の記事です！
English: The proof is this article about Gourdy the lake monster!

Japanese: ‥‥！...!この記事によると、
English: According to this article...

Japanese: ヒョッシーは“破裂音”に反応してあらわれるらしいですね。
English: When Gourdy breaks through the water, it makes a loud noise!

Japanese: そして、あなたのカメラも、
English: Which is why you set up your camera with that microphone!

Japanese: “破裂音”に反応してシャッターが切られるよう、セットされていた！
English: You had it set to respond to loud noises!

Japanese: この写真が撮られたのも、カメラが銃声に反応したからです！
English: And that's how you got this picture when the pistol fired!

Japanese: 静粛に！　静粛に！
English: Order! Order!

Japanese: ‥‥なるほど‥‥。
English: I see...

Japanese: 私もじつは、オカシイと思っていたんですよ‥‥。
English: I, too, thought it was a little strange.

Japanese: （ホントかよ‥‥）
English: (Yeah, sure!)

Japanese: ‥‥どうです？　ナツミさん。
English: Well, Ms. Hart?

Japanese: あなたは、ヒョッシーを撮ろうと、キャンプをしていたんですね？
English: You were camping there to try and take a photo of Gourdy, weren't you?
Yeah.

Japanese: ‥‥オミゴトやね。さすがは弁護士さんや。
English: Not bad. Are all you lawyers that smart?

Japanese: でも。
English: So, smart boy, I was down there trying to photograph Gourdy, you got me.

Japanese: だから、何なん？
English: So what?
Huh...?

Japanese: ウチの見たものに、変わりはないで。
English: That don't change what I saw, does it?

Japanese: ‥‥そのとおり。
English: Exactly!

Japanese: 長い時間をかけて、キサマが証明したことは、
English: What you just used several precious minutes of our time to prove...

Japanese: この証人が、カイジュウの撮影をユメ見る、バカな女ということだ。
English: is nothing more than that the witness is an idiot who thinks monsters exist!

Japanese: な、なんやソレ！
English: H-hey!

Japanese: しかし、だからどうだというのだ？
English: But, as she so succinctly said, so what!?

Japanese: 何も変わらないではないか？
English: It changes nothing!

Japanese: （そんなことはない）
English: (Not true!)

Japanese: （お前たちは、ヒョッシーのことをかくそうとしていた）
English: (You were hiding the whole thing about Gourdy for some reason, I know it!)

Japanese: （かくさなければならないワケがあったからだ！）
English: (But what could it have been...?)

Japanese: （今度は、それを暴いてやるぞ‥‥）
English: (Whatever it is, I'm getting to the bottom of this!)

Japanese: 証人。
English: Ms. Hart.

Japanese: なぜ、ヒョッシーのことをかくそうとしたのですか？
English: Why did you hide the fact that you were searching for Gourdy from the court?

Japanese: 最後に、すべてを《証言》してください。
English: Please revise your testimony.

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥。...わかったワ。‥‥証言、する。‥‥なんも変わらんと思うけど。
English: Right. Fine, I'll testify. It won't change nothing, though.

Japanese: （ゼッタイ、何かが変わっているはずだ）
English: (Something will change... it has to!)

Japanese: （それを‥‥見抜く！）
English: (And I'm going to spot it!)

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥。...～ナツミのこと?事件当夜のこと～
English: -- Lotta's New Testimony --

Japanese: 実はウチ、大学の研究員ちゃうんや。
English: Actually, I'm not a research student at a university.

Japanese: ホンマはな‥‥、フリーカメラマンやってんねん。
English: I'm an investigative photographer.

Japanese: 今、話題のヒョッシーのハッキリした写真を撮れば、
English: Imagine what a scoop it'd be if I got a picture of that monster!

Japanese: スクープになる思うて、キャンプ張っとったんや。
English: That's why I was camping out by the lake.

Japanese: でも、かくしてたんは、それだけやで。
English: But, that's all I was hiding.

Japanese: “パアン”て音がして、見たら湖にボートが浮かんでたんや。
English: When I heard the "bang" I looked right straight out at that lake.

Japanese: 他に見るモンもないねんから、ずっとボート見てるしかないやん。
English: There wasn't much else to look at, so I just watched that boat the whole time.

Japanese: で、男の手もとが光って、もう１回、音が聞こえたんや。
English: Then I saw a flash, near one of the men's hands, and I heard another gunshot.

Japanese: ウチはホンマ、ボートをジッと見てたんや。‥‥ホンマやで。
English: I was looking right at that boat, the whole time, cross my heart and hope to fry.

Japanese: ふむう‥‥。では成歩堂くん、《尋問》を‥‥。
English: Hmm... Well, Mr. Wright. You may cross-examine the witness.

Japanese: 証人の証言は、さっきと変わっておらぬ。
English: The witness's testimony is unchanged from before!

Japanese: 証人が研究員だろうとカメラマンだろうと、同じこと！
English: Whether she is a research student or a photographer has no bearing on this case!

Japanese: 《尋問》など、いらぬッ！
English: There is no need to waste more of our time with another pointless cross-examination!

Japanese: は‥‥はあ。
English: Er... hmm.

Japanese: ぼくはあくまで、弁護側の権利を主張します！
English: I claim the defense's right to cross-examine the witness, Your Honor!

Japanese: （狩魔のコトバには、かならず意味がある‥‥）
English: (von Karma's up to something, I know it!)

Japanese: （尋問をさせたくないのは、今の証言にムジュンが‥‥）
English: (He doesn't want me to cross- examine her because... why? Was there a contradiction?)

Japanese: ‥‥わかりました。《尋問》を認めます。
English: Very well. You may begin the cross-examination.

Japanese: そこまで言うなら、何か考えがあるのでしょう。
English: You seem sure of yourself, you must have something in mind.

Japanese: あるか、そんなもの！
English: Hah! That would be a first!

Japanese: （ははは‥‥）
English: (Hah hah, very funny.)

Japanese: わかっていると思いますが、これは最後の尋問です。
English: You understand that this is your last chance at a cross-examination, Mr. Wright?

Japanese: 問題がなかったら、証人は退廷してもらいます。
English: If there is no problem with the testimony this time, we will let the witness leave.

Japanese: その時点で、判決を言い渡したいと思います。
English: I will announce my verdict at that time, Mr. Wright.

Japanese: ‥‥よろしいですね。
English: Understood?
Yes, Your Honor.

Japanese: ～ナツミのこと?事件当夜のこと～
English: -- Lotta's New Testimony --

Japanese: 実はウチ、大学の研究員ちゃうんや。
English: Actually, I'm not a research student at a university.

Japanese: ホンマはな‥‥、フリーカメラマンやってんねん。
English: I'm an investigative photographer.

Japanese: 今、話題のヒョッシーのハッキリした写真を撮れば、
English: Imagine what a scoop it'd be if I got a picture of that monster!

Japanese: スクープになる思うて、キャンプ張っとったんや。
English: That's why I was camping out by the lake.

Japanese: でも、かくしてたんは、それだけやで。
English: But, that's all I was hiding.

Japanese: “パアン”て音がして、見たら湖にボートが浮かんでたんや。
English: When I heard the "bang" I looked right straight out at that lake.

Japanese: 他に見るモンもないねんから、ずっとボート見てるしかないやん。
English: There wasn't much else to look at, so I just watched that boat the whole time.

Japanese: で、男の手もとが光って、もう１回、音が聞こえたんや。
English: Then I saw a flash, near one of the men's hands, and I heard another gunshot.

Japanese: （‥‥この証言の中にムジュンを見つけなければ）
English: (I'd better find some sort of contradiction in this testimony...)

Japanese: （あの検事に勝つことはできない‥‥）
English: (I won't be able to beat von Karma any other way...)

Japanese: （何かあるはずだ‥‥！）
English: (There has to be something!)

Japanese: じゃあ、あなたは何者なんですか！
English: Then what manner of person are you!?

Japanese: “なにもの”ゆうほど大したモンやあらへん。
English: I'm not sure I'm high- falutin' enough to be called a "manner" of anything.

Japanese: “フリーカメラマン”‥‥ですか。
English: An "investigative photographer"?

Japanese: そうや。
English: Yep.

Japanese: スクープ写真撮って、マスコミに買うてもらうんや。
English: You get your photo, and sell it to the press. It's that kind of business.

Japanese: あのな。去年、妹の成人式で写真を撮ってやったんや。
English: Hey, I was taking pictures at my sister's graduation last year...

Japanese: ほしたら！
English: N' guess what!

Japanese: ほしたら？
English: Umm... what?

Japanese: 空に、ＵＦＯが写ってたんや！
English: There was a yoofoh just a' hanging in the sky!

Japanese: “ゆーほー”‥‥？
English: A "yoofoh"...?

Japanese: 空とぶ円盤や。
English: You know, an "Unidentified Flying Object"? A yoofoh!

Japanese: このときウチは、さとったんや。
English: That's when I had sort of a revelation.

Japanese: “自分は、スクープカメラマンになるべき女や”ってな。
English: I knew I should become an investigative photographer!

Japanese: そ‥‥それはよかったですね。
English: I... I see.

Japanese: （“スクープ”のイミを、完全にとり違えているな‥‥）
English: (Kind of a shaky basis for a career...)

Japanese: ‥‥話題なんですか？“ヒョッシー”って。
English: Is "Gourdy" really all that newsworthy?

Japanese: あた、あたりまえやんか！‥‥テレビでもやってたで！
English: Heck yeah! They even had him up on the TV!

Japanese: （ローカル局の“まちのうわさ”のコーナーじゃないか‥‥）
English: (I'm not sure that appearing on the local news "rumor of the month" show qualifies.)

Japanese: （先月はたしか“どんぐり山にヤマンバあらわる！”だった）
English: (Last month's show was "Bigfoot Sighted on Acorn Hill, I believe.)"

Japanese: なんや！　今回は雑誌に写真も出たんや！　ホンモノやで！
English: Hey! They also had a picture of him in the newspaper! For real!

Japanese: 弁護人。
English: Mr. Wright.

Japanese: この証人、ツッコミ出したらキリがありません。
English: This is one fight I do not believe you can win.

Japanese: 先に進みましょう。
English: Let's keep moving, shall we?

Japanese: ‥‥はあ。
English: Yes, Your Honor.

Japanese: それで、カメラに自動シャッターをとりつけて‥‥？
English: That's why you put the automatic sensor on your camera?

Japanese: そうや。大学のセンセから借りてきたマシーンや。
English: Yep. Borrowed it from a friend at a university.

Japanese: まわりの音の波形をブンセキして、破裂音がしたら‥‥
English: It analyzes every sound it picks up, and when it gets a "bang!"...

Japanese: シャッターが下りる、と。
English: It snaps a shot.

Japanese: そうや。
English: Yep.

Japanese: ‥‥で？　今まで、何枚ぐらい撮影したんですか？
English: So, how many pictures has it taken so far?

Japanese: カメラのシャッターが作動したんは、あの晩だけや。
English: The only time the camera triggered was that night.

Japanese: そもそも、なんでかくしてたんですか？
English: I think it's time you told us why you felt you had to hide your true purpose at the lake.

Japanese: 正体バラしたら、ライバルが増えるやん。
English: Heck, if word got out what I was up to, the lake'd be swarmin' with competitors!

Japanese: ライバル？
English: Competitors...?

Japanese: ヒョッシーを狙うカメラマンどもや！
English: Yeah! Second-rate shutterbugs trying to steal my scoop!

Japanese: ‥‥はあ。
English: Ah...

Japanese: かくしていた理由は、それだけですか？
English: Is that the only reason you were hiding the truth?

Japanese: いや、実はナ‥‥
English: W-well, actually...

Japanese: 事件にカンケイないことは聞かないでもらおう！
English: Mr. Wright! I'll not have you asking questions with no relevance to this case!!!

Japanese: （やっぱり、狩魔が口止めしていたみたいだな‥‥）
English: (Whatever you say, von Karma. I know you told her to keep quiet.)

Japanese: どんな音でしたか？
English: Exactly what sort of sound was it?

Japanese: 聞いたことないけど、やっぱり銃声だったんちゃうか？
English: Well, I never heard one before so I can't say for sure... but it sounded like a gunshot.

Japanese: 意外にアッサリした音やったで。
English: It was a lot sharper a sound than I would have expected.

Japanese: ‥‥“他に見るモノがなかった”？
English: There wasn't "much else to look at"?

Japanese: そうや。
English: Yep.

Japanese: （‥‥そうだろうか‥‥。　もし、彼女が破裂音を聞いたら‥‥）
English: (I dunno. If she heard a bang... and she thought Gourdy was out there...)

Japanese: （ボートなんか、見ているハズがないぞ‥‥）
English: (I kind of doubt she'd waste any time looking at a boat.)

Japanese: なんや？　ヒトのカオ、そんなに見つめちゃって！
English: What? What did I do now? What're you giving me that look for?

Japanese: （‥‥どう考えてもオカシイ。あれをつきつけてみるか‥‥）
English: (Definitely suspicious... Maybe it's time for some evidence...)

Japanese: 証人。‥‥先を。
English: Witness! Continue.

Japanese: せかすなや、オッサン。
English: Hold your hushpuppies, pops, I'm getting there.

Japanese: そのとき、湖にはボートしかなかったんですか？
English: Was there nothing on the lake but the boat at that time?

Japanese: ははあ。どこかベツの場所から撃たれた、とか考えてるんやな？
English: Huh? Wait, so you're thinking maybe he was shot from some other place?

Japanese: それはないと思うで。
English: I don't think so, nope.

Japanese: 湖は、さざ波ひとつなかったし、岸にも人影はなかったワ。
English: The lake was smooth as glass, and nobody was on the shore, neither.

Japanese: （ううん‥‥）
ナツミさん！
English: Ms. Hart!

Japanese: あなたは、本当にボートを見ていたんですか？
English: Were you REALLY looking at that boat!?

Japanese: な‥‥何を言うんや！アタリマエやん！
English: W-what's with you!? Course I was looking at it!

Japanese: 湖には、ボートしかなかったんや！‥‥フツー、それを見るやん！
English: It was the only thing out there! Any normal person'd be looking at it!

Japanese: フツーはそうかもしれません。
English: I agree, any normal person would.

Japanese: しかし、あなたはフツーではなかった！
English: But you are far from normal!

Japanese: な‥‥、なんやて！
English: Wh-what!? Y'all wanna step over here and say that!?

Japanese: あなたは、ヒョッシーの写真を撮るためにキャンプをしていた！
English: You were camping at the lake to take a picture of Gourdy!

Japanese: それならば！
English: Think about it--

Japanese: 破裂音を聞いたあなたは、何はともあれ、
English: What would you do if you heard a loud noise?

Japanese: 湖の上に、ヒョッシーのすがたを探したはずです！
English: You'd be scanning the lake for any sign of Gourdy, that's what!

Japanese: だから、ボートなんかに注意をはらうハズがない！
English: You wouldn't give the boat a second thought!

Japanese: あッ！
English: Aah!

Japanese: 静粛に！　つづけてください！成歩堂くん！
English: Order! Continue, Mr. Wright!

Japanese: あなたは、双眼鏡でボートを見ていたと証言した！
English: You testified that you were watching the boat through binoculars!

Japanese: しかし、ただボートを見るのに、双眼鏡はいらない！
English: However, you wouldn't need binoculars to watch that boat!

Japanese: あなたは、必死にヒョッシーを探していたんです！
English: You needed them to search for Gourdy... and that's what you were doing!
Well!?
Hmph...

Japanese: そういえば、ウチ‥‥。
English: Well, now that y'all mention it...

Japanese: たしかに‥‥双眼鏡を取って、湖をスミズミまで、何度も‥‥
English: I did sort of take my binoculars and kind of scan the lake a bit...

Japanese: ヒョッシーがいないかと思って‥‥。
English: I mean, Gourdy might be out there, n' all...

Japanese: しょ‥‥証人！
English: M-Ms. Hart!

Japanese: では、証人は、ボートは見ていなかったと‥‥？
English: A-are you saying that you were NOT watching the boat, then?
S-sorry, y'all.

Japanese: ウソついてたワケじゃないねん‥‥。
English: I wasn't fibbing, really.

Japanese: ウチ‥‥殺人事件の目撃者になれる思うて、その‥‥。
English: I was, just... I thought y'know, I could be witness to a murder n' all!

Japanese: ちょっとウレシなってもうてな。
English: I kinda got excited.

Japanese: ボートを見た、て‥‥思い込んでもうてん‥‥。
English: I was sure I was watching that boat... till now.
This... this is totally uncalled for--

Japanese: で、でも‥‥、でもな！
English: B-but hey!

Japanese: この写真は、バッチリ証拠になるやろ！
English: You got the photograph! You got proof!

Japanese: ふむう‥‥。
English: Hmm...

Japanese: しかし、誰が撃ったかまでは、見えないようですが‥‥
English: Still, we can't see who is shooting who in this.

Japanese: そうやろ！　そうやろ！
English: Right! Right!

Japanese: ‥‥だからなァ、ウチ、この写真を‥‥‥
English: That's why I took this photo n'...

Japanese: ‥‥証人。もうケッコウだ。
English: Witness... That's enough.

Japanese: ごくろうだった。‥‥もう、キサマは口を開く必要はない。
English: You've had a long day. Shut your pie-hole.

Japanese: キ‥‥“キサマ”？
English: Sh-shut my what!?

Japanese: （ナツミさん、今、何を言いかけたんだろう‥‥？）
English: (What was she going to say?)

Japanese: （“だからなァ、ウチ、この写真を‥‥”か）
English: (She took the photo... and what?)

Japanese: （そういえば、昨日‥‥）
English: (Wait a second...)

Japanese: ‥‥しかも彼女、証拠写真まで撮っていたッス。
English: She even had a photograph to prove it.

Japanese: この写真じゃあ、誰が撃ったか見えませんね‥‥。
English: But you really can't tell from the photo who is shooting.

Japanese: 彼女、これからその写真を拡大するって言ってたッス。
English: That's why she said she's going to enlarge the photo.

Japanese: 画像はアラくなるけど、もう少しハッキリするはずや！　‥‥とか。
English: She said "it'll drop the quality a mite, but should let us see who's who!"

Japanese: （彼女はあの写真を拡大したはずだ）
English: (She enlarged that photo!)

Japanese: （なぜ狩魔は、その写真を提出させないんだ‥‥？）
English: (Why won't von Karma let her show it?)

Japanese: （もしかして‥‥）
English: (I've got a hunch!)

Japanese: （もしかしたら、その拡大写真に、何か狩魔に不利なことが‥‥？）
English: (I bet that enlarged photo shows something bad for von Karma!)

Japanese: （‥‥ここは、勝負どころだ！）
English: (This is my chance!)

Japanese: （ぼくのカン違いだったら、御剣の有罪は決定的になる！）
English: (If I'm wrong, though, it'll mean prison for Edgeworth... or worse.)

Japanese: （さて‥‥どうする‥‥！）
English: (What should I do!?)

Japanese: （これが狩魔のワナだったら‥‥）
English: (If this a trap...)

Japanese: （とりあえず、ここはようすを見るべきか‥‥）
English: (I'd better hold back and see how things go.)

Japanese: （しかし‥‥このまま見すごしたらもう尋問は終わってしまう‥‥）
English: (But... if I wait now, the cross-examination will be over!)

Japanese: （ホントに、このまま何もしないでいいのか‥‥！）
English: (Can I really risk that!?)

Japanese: では、これで大沢木　ナツミさんの尋問を終了します。
English: This hereby ends the cross- examination of Ms. Lotta Hart.

Japanese: ‥‥やれやれ。ずいぶん回り道をしたものだ‥‥。
English: And none too soon. That was a flagrant waste of my time.

Japanese: 狩魔検事。‥‥他に何か、ありますか？
English: Mr. von Karma, do you have anything to add?

Japanese: 最初に言ったはずだ。
English: I stated everything I needed to when this trial began.

Japanese: 決定的な証拠。
English: Decisive evidence.

Japanese: 決定的な証人。
English: A decisive witness.

Japanese: ‥‥他に何か、必要なものは？
English: What else could possibly be required?

Japanese: そうですね。ありません。
English: Nothing, of course.

Japanese: （しまった！　やっぱり、食いさがるべきだったか！）
English: (Oh no! I should have pressed further!)

Japanese: （ここは、勝負に出るしかない！一気に行くぞ‥‥！）
English: (I have to fight now or I could lose everything!)

Japanese: ナツミさん！この写真を見てください。
English: Ms. Hart! Look at this photograph.

Japanese: あなたは、この写真を拡大したそうですね！
English: You enlarged this photograph, did you not?

Japanese: そ、そうや！　そうなんや！
English: Y-yeah! I did!

Japanese: その写真は、なぜ提出されないんですか！
English: Why has that enlargement not been presented to the court!?

Japanese: そ‥‥そんな写真は存在しない！
English: B-because it does not exist!

Japanese: ナニ言ってるんや！
English: What're y'all talking about!?

Japanese: “それは提出してはならん”ってドナりつけたのは、
English: You were the one who told me not to show it in court in the first place!

Japanese: アンタやないか！
English: You old fool!

Japanese: どういうことですか！狩魔検事！
English: What's the meaning of this, Mr. von Karma!

Japanese: ウ‥‥ウム‥‥
English: Er... erm...

Japanese: ナツミさん！
English: Ms. Hart!

Japanese: 提出してください！‥‥拡大写真を！
English: Show the photo to the court! Show us the enlargement!

Japanese: 検察側は、提出に反対する！
English: The prosecution objects to the submission of this evidence!

Japanese: 異議は却下します。
English: Objection... denied.

Japanese: 証人。‥‥写真を見せてください。
English: The witness will show the enlargement to the court.

Japanese: これが、そうや！
English: Here it is.

Japanese: ‥‥やはり、誰が撃ったかは見わけられませんね‥‥。
English: Hmm. We still cannot see who is firing in this.

Japanese: 被告人であるような、そうではないような‥‥。
