Japanese: （やはり‥‥、弾丸が‥‥！）
English: (The bullet!)
Mr. von Karma...?
You!

Japanese: あなただったんですか‥‥！
English: It was you!

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥...‥‥こうなることを恐れたゆえ、
English: I was afraid this would happen.

Japanese: ワガハイはあえて、ダマっておったのだ‥‥。
English: And so, I remained silent.

Japanese: ‥‥？...?たしかに、ワガハイの肩には弾丸が残っておる。
English: Indeed, there is a bullet in my shoulder.

Japanese: しかし、それは事件とはマッタク関係がない。
English: However... it has nothing to do with this incident!

Japanese: な‥‥なんだと！
English: What!?

Japanese: 肩の弾丸は、ＤＬ６号事件より以前に受けたものだ。
English: I was shot in the shoulder long before the DL-6 Incident!

Japanese: ワガハイは“事件とは関係ない”と主張する！
English: I claim that the bullet in my shoulder has no relation to DL-6!

Japanese: し‥‥しかし、狩魔検事！それを‥‥証明できるのですか？
English: B-but, Mr. von Karma! Can you prove that?

Japanese: ‥‥“証明”だと？
English: "Prove"?

Japanese: ワガハイにそんなギムはない。
English: I have no obligation to prove anything!

Japanese: ‥‥証明しなければならないのは、キサマの方だ、弁護士。
English: It is you who must prove something here, Mr. Wright!

Japanese: ‥‥ワガハイではない。
English: Not I.

Japanese: な‥‥成歩堂くん。
English: M-Mr. Wright?

Japanese: どうですか？証明できるのですか？
English: Well? Can you prove it?

Japanese: 狩魔検事の肩の弾丸は、ＤＬ６号事件によるものだ、と？
English: Can you prove that the bullet in von Karma's shoulder was from DL-6?

Japanese: ‥‥できるわけがない。
English: Of course he can't!

Japanese: キサマらには、ＤＬ６号事件の資料など、ないのだからな！
English: You don't have any of the DL-6 evidence!

Japanese: （‥‥昨日、あわてて資料室から持ち出したクセに‥‥）
English: (That's because you took it out of the Records Room yesterday!)

Japanese: ‥‥証拠がない以上、ワガハイを罪に問うことは不可能だ。
English: With no proof, you cannot convict me of any crime!

Japanese: ザンネンながら、な。
English: So sorry, Mr. Wright.

Japanese: ザンネンなのはアンタの方だ、‥‥狩魔検事。
English: No... I'm the one who's sorry, Mr. von Karma.

Japanese: な‥‥なんだと？
English: Wh-what!?

Japanese: 惜しかったね。‥‥あと、もう１日だったのに。
English: You were close... one day away from freedom.

Japanese: あるんだよ‥‥証拠ならな！
English: You see... I have proof!

Japanese: な‥‥なにッ！
English: Wh-what!?

Japanese: 御剣を有罪にするために、自分で墓穴を掘るとはね‥‥。
English: Who would have thought you would have dug your own grave trying to convict Edgeworth!

Japanese: あんたの肩の弾丸と、ＤＬ６号事件を結びつける‥‥
English: I can link that bullet in your shoulder to the DL-6 Incident...

Japanese: これが、最後の証拠だ！
English: And here's my final proof!

Japanese: な‥‥なるほどくん‥‥。
English: N-Nick...?

Japanese: 最後ぐらい、カッコよくキメてよお‥‥。
English: Umm... If this is your final proof, don't you think you should choose a little better?

Japanese: 違ったかな？　今の証拠？
English: Huh? You mean, that was the wrong evidence?

Japanese: みんな、妙にケワしいカオしてるけど‥‥。
English: Everyone looks kinda puzzled...

Japanese: 弁護人。
English: Mr. Wright.
(Uh oh, he's really mad.)

Japanese: はやくやりなおした方がよさそうだよ‥‥！
English: I think you'd better dig out the right evidence, and quick!

Japanese: わわ、わかった。
English: R-right!

Japanese: ‥‥ラッキーだったな、狩魔検事。しかし、次はハズさない！
English: You got lucky, von Karma, but this next one is the real evidence, okay!?

Japanese: あんたの肩の弾丸と、ＤＬ６号事件と結びつける‥‥
English: I can link that bullet in your shoulder to the DL-6 Incident...

Japanese: そ‥‥それは‥‥
English: Th-that's...

Japanese: 弾丸‥‥ですか？
English: A bullet?

Japanese: キ‥‥キサマッ！どうしてそれを‥‥ッ！
English: Where did you get that!?

Japanese: これは、ＤＬ６号事件で使用された弾丸です。
English: This is the bullet used in the DL-6 Incident.

Japanese: 被害者の御剣　信の心臓から摘出されました。
English: This was taken from the heart of the victim, Mr. Gregory Edgeworth.

Japanese: 保存状態は良好で、線条痕もハッキリ残っています。
English: The bullet is preserved quite nicely, with all the ballistic markings intact.
Ballistic markings...

Japanese: ‥‥今から２日前。
English: You may recall the term.

Japanese: 最初の法廷でも、線条痕の話が出ましたね‥‥。
English: It came up in the first trial, two days ago.

Japanese: 線条痕というのは、ピストルの“指紋”みたいなものです。
English: Ballistic markings are the fingerprints of a weapon.

Japanese: ピストルで撃たれた弾丸には必ず、銃身によるキズがつきます。
English: All bullets fired from a gun are marked with that weapon's unique pattern.

Japanese: そのキズを調べれば、弾丸がどのピストルから撃たれたかが、
English: By examining the markings, you can tell which weapon fired the bullet.

Japanese: ハッキリ特定できるのです。
English: It's quite accurate.

Japanese: ここに、２つの弾丸があります。
English: We have two bullets in our possession.

Japanese: 御剣　信の心臓から摘出された、この弾丸。
English: One, the bullet removed from Gregory Edgeworth's heart.

Japanese: そして、狩魔検事。あなたの肩に埋まっている弾丸。
English: The other, Mr. von Karma, is the bullet buried in your shoulder.

Japanese: ２つの弾丸の線条痕を調べた結果‥‥、
English: We could analyze both bullets...

Japanese: もし、その線条痕が一致したら、
English: Then, if the markings matched...

Japanese: ２つの弾丸は、同じピストルから発射されたことが証明されます！
English: We would know that both bullets had been fired from the same gun!

Japanese: 同じピストル‥‥すなわち、御剣　信を撃った“凶器”です！
English: The very same pistol... in other words, the murder weapon that killed Gregory Edgeworth!

Japanese: う‥‥ぐぐ‥‥‥‥むむむ‥‥
English: Mmmph... mmmph!

Japanese: ‥‥狩魔検事。
English: Mr. von Karma?

Japanese: その肩に埋まっている弾丸を摘出してください。
English: You will let us remove the bullet from your shoulder.

Japanese: そして‥‥この弾丸の線条痕と比べてみましょう。
English: Then we'll compare the ballistic markings to those on this bullet...

Japanese: それで‥‥この事件は解決です！
English: And solve this case once and for all!

Japanese: いかがですか！　狩魔検事！
English: Well, Mr. von Karma!?
Mmmrrrrrrgggh!

Japanese: うぐおおおおおおおおおおおおおをををををををををををををッ！
English: Uuaaaaaaaaaaaaa aaaaaaaaaaaaargh!

Japanese: おおおおお‥‥おお‥‥おおおおおヲヲお‥‥‥‥おおヲヲヲヲヲ‥‥
English: Uaaaaarrrr... aaa...oooh. Worrrrrrrggg... aaa...oooooh...
That scream...

Japanese: ‥‥どこかで聞いたことのある‥‥
English: I've heard that scream before...

Japanese: ‥‥‥そうだ‥‥あのときだ‥‥‥
English: Wait... I know!

Japanese: 「助けてくれッ！　い、息が‥‥」
English: "H-help! I can't breathe!"

Japanese: 「うるさい！　ダマるんだ！‥‥こっちまでおかしくなる！」
English: "Quiet! I said quiet! You're not making this any easier!"

Japanese: 「オレの空気を吸うな！い‥‥息の根を止めてやる！」
English: "Stop breathing my air! I'll... I'll stop you!"

Japanese: 「オレの空気を吸うなァァァァ！」
English: "Stop breathing my aaaaair!"

Japanese: （ぼくのおとうさんから‥‥）
English: (Get away...!)

Japanese: （おとうさんから、はなれろぉっ‥‥！）
English: (Get away from my father!)
*BANG*

Japanese: うぐおおおおおおおおおおおおおをををををををををををををッ！
English: Uuaaaaaaaaaaaaa aaaaaaaaaaaaargh!

Japanese: あのときの悲鳴だ‥‥。
English: It's that scream I heard in the elevator!

Japanese: ‥‥１５年前。私がエレベータで聞いたのは‥‥
English: Fifteen years ago...

Japanese: 狩魔検事！あなたの悲鳴だったのか！
English: von Karma! It was you who screamed!

Japanese: ‥‥狩魔検事‥‥？
English: Mr. von Karma?

Japanese: おおおヲヲおおおおおおヲヲヲおおおヲヲヲおおおヲヲヲヲヲおおおお
English: Wooorrrrrrroooorrgggh

Japanese: ‥ミツルギィィィィぃぃぃ‥‥‥‥‥‥‥‥ミツルギィィィィぃぃぃ‥
English: Eh...Edgewoooooorrth......... ...Ehh...Edgewoooooorrth......

Japanese: マタシテモ‥‥ワガハイニ‥‥‥‥タテツクトハ‥‥‥‥
English: Only...you would... dare...defy me...!

Japanese: やはり‥‥あなたが‥‥！
English: So... it was you!

Japanese: ‥‥親子そろって‥‥目ざわりなヤツらだ‥‥
English: You and your father are my curse!

Japanese: ワガハイの経歴に“処罰”のドロをぬったオヤジに‥‥
English: Your father shamed me with a penalty on my record!

Japanese: ワガハイの肩に、生涯消えぬ傷痕を刻んだムスコ‥‥
English: And you... you left a scar on my shoulder that would never fade!

Japanese: ‥‥この手で葬ってやるうぅぅ‥‥死刑台に送ってやるぞおぉぉ‥‥
English: I... I'll bury you! I'll bury you with my bare hands! Death! Death!
Fifteen years earlier...

Japanese: 検事局長。‥‥もうしわけない。
English: Mr. Chief Prosecutor... I am sorry.

Japanese: ‥‥狩魔くん。キミらしくもないヘマをしたものだ‥‥。
English: von Karma! It's not like you to make this kind of error...

Japanese: あのミツルギ弁護士に、シッポをつかまれるとはね‥‥。
English: I never would have thought that Edgeworth would be the one to catch you.

Japanese: ‥‥油断‥‥したようだ‥‥。
English: I... was careless.

Japanese: ザンネンだが、キミには処罰を受けてもらうよ。
English: I'm sorry, but you will have to be penalized.

Japanese: さすがに、今回ばかりはかばうわけにもいかないのでね。
English: I've covered for you in the past, but not this time.
Edgeworth!!!

Japanese: ‥‥味わったことのないショックだった。
English: It was a shock like none I had ever known.

Japanese: このワガハイが、処罰されるなど‥‥
English: Me? Penalized!?

Japanese: 精神の落ちつきを取りもどすのに、数時間かかった‥‥。
English: It took hours for me to regain my composure.

Japanese: ‥‥気がつくとワガハイは、闇の中にいた。
English: Suddenly, I found myself in the darkness...

Japanese: そこは、裁判所の資料室だった。
English: I was in the court records room.

Japanese: いつの間にか、そんなところに来ていたらしい。
English: I must have wandered in there without thinking where I was going.

Japanese: 部屋の中は真っ暗だった。‥‥どうやら停電しているようだ。
English: The room was pitch black. The lights must have gone out.

Japanese: 廊下に出て、手さぐりでエレベータの前に出た。
English: I went out in the hall and felt my way to the elevator.

Japanese: エレベータのボタンを押す。‥‥しかし、反応がない。
English: I pressed the button, and nothing happened.

Japanese: 次の瞬間‥‥。
English: Then... there was a noise!

Japanese: 何が起こった‥‥！‥‥肩に焼きつく、この痛み‥‥
English: I was in pain! A horrible, burning pain in my shoulder!

Japanese: そのとき‥‥電力が回復した。目の前で、ドアが開く。
English: Just then, the lights came back on. The elevator door opened before my eyes.

Japanese: ３人の人間が見えた。‥‥彼らは、酸欠で気絶していた。
English: I saw three people inside, all lying unconscious from oxygen deprivation.

Japanese: フシギなことに、足下にピストルが転がっていた。
English: Much to my surprise, a pistol lay at my feet.

Japanese: ‥‥これは運命なのだ‥‥ワガハイはそう知った。
English: I knew then... it was destiny.

Japanese: 最後の瞬間、御剣　信は気絶していた。
English: In his last moments, Gregory Edgeworth was still unconscious.

Japanese: ヤツは、誰に撃たれたかも知らぬまま、死んだのだ。
English: He died, never knowing who had shot him.

Japanese: 後日、霊媒師の口を介して灰根を告発したようだが‥‥。
English: Later, he spoke through a medium, blaming Mr. Yogi.

Japanese: ワガハイは、被害者自身をもカンペキにあざむいてやったのだ！
English: He was fooled! It was the perfect crime!

Japanese: ‥‥クックックッ‥‥
English: Tsk tsk tsk...

Japanese: あのエレベータのトビラを、ふたたび開ける男が現れるとはな。
English: Who would have thought another man would have come to open that elevator door?

Japanese: 裁判長！
English: Judge!

Japanese: は‥‥はい！
English: Wh-what?

Japanese: 何をしておる！
English: What are you doing!?

Japanese: このささやかなゲームに幕を引くのは、キサマの役目だろう！
English: Do your job! Bring an end to this miserable charade!

Japanese: 今がそのときだ！さっさと、幕を引くのだ！
English: Now! End it!

Japanese: わ‥‥わかりました‥‥。
English: V-very well.

Japanese: 私たちはどうやら、迷宮の出口にたどりついたようです。
English: It appears that we have come a very long way to the end of this maze.

Japanese: １５年の歳月の涯てに‥‥。
English: Fifteen years later...

Japanese: ‥‥御剣　怜侍。
English: Mr. Miles Edgeworth?

Japanese: はい。
English: Yes, Your Honor.

Japanese: あなたは、無実だった。
English: You were innocent. You are innocent.

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥...すべては、“悪夢”にすぎなかったのです。
English: As you said, it was all a "nightmare."
Yes, Your Honor.

Japanese: 御剣　怜侍に対する判決を言い渡します。
English: This court finds the defendant, Mr. Miles Edgeworth...

Japanese: では、本日はこれにて閉廷！
English: That is all. The court is adjourned!

Japanese: １２月２８日　午後５時３８分地方裁判所　被告人第２控え室
English: December 28, 5:38 PM District Court Defendant Lobby No. 2

Japanese: なるほどくん！　なるほどくん！ついにやったね！
English: Nick! Nick! We did it!

Japanese: 見た？　見た？　カルマ検事のしみったれたカオ。
English: Did you see his face!? von Karma looked even paler than usual!

Japanese: あれ、ヘーキなフリしてるけど、心の中はドシャ降りだよ！
English: He's pretending to be all cool, but inside you crushed him, Nick! Crushed!

Japanese: いいキミだねー。いやまったく。
English: I gotta say, I'm impressed.

Japanese: ううう‥‥それにしてもあぶなかった。
English: Heh... It was pretty close, though.

Japanese: ‥‥もう、ゼッタイダメだと思ったよ‥‥。
English: I was sure we'd had it.

Japanese: そうだね。あたしも、ずーっと半泣きだったよ。
English: I know, I was on the verge of tears the whole time, myself.

Japanese: ま。今となってはイイ思い出だけどね！
English: But now it's all just a good memory!
So, it's finally over, Edgeworth.
Wright.

Japanese: ん？
English: Yeah?

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥‥‥...‥‥その‥‥、なんと言えばいいのか‥‥。
English: I... I'm not sure how to say this.

Japanese: そういうときはね。“ありがとう”って言うんだよ！
English: I know! I know! Try "thank you."

Japanese: そ、そうなのか。
English: I... I see.

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥‥‥...あ、ありがとう、成歩堂。
English: Th-thank you, Wright.

Japanese: ど、どういたしまして。
English: Y-you're welcome.

Japanese: うーん‥‥イマイチ、ハリアイがないなあ‥‥。
English: I think you could have done better than that!

Japanese: ム‥‥スマン。こういうの、ニガテで‥‥。
English: Oof! S-sorry... I'm not good at this sort of thing.

Japanese: もう！　ダメだなあ、御剣検事は。
English: You got a lot to learn, Edgeworth!

Japanese: （‥‥やれやれ‥‥）
English: (Dear, dear...)

Japanese: うおおおおおおおおおおおおおっ！
English: Whoooooooooooooop!

Japanese: さすがッス！　自分が見込んだだけのコトはあったッス！
English: Amazing, pal! You pulled through just like I thought you would!

Japanese: 糸鋸　圭介。この恩は一生、わすれないッス！
English: I'll never forget this! I owe you one, pal.

Japanese: 今夜は、パーッとやるッス！自分がゴチソウするッス！
English: And tonight, let's party! Dinner's on me!

Japanese: 今月から給料がちょっぴり下がったッスが、気にしねッス！
English: Yeah, my salary went down a bit this month... But who cares!

Japanese: ほらほらほら、御剣検事。イトノコ刑事を見ならうの！
English: See, Mr. Edgeworth? You should take a lesson from Detective Gumshoe!

Japanese: あんな感じにやると、カンシャのキモチが伝わるんだよ！
English: That's how you say "thank you"!

Japanese: ‥‥ム。な‥‥なるほど。
English: Mmm. I... I see.

Japanese: コホン。
English: *ahem*

Japanese: う、うおおおおおおおおおっ！
English: Whooooooooooooop!

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥‥‥...ダメだ。‥‥ヤッパリ、私なんか‥‥。
English: I... I feel foolish.

Japanese: まあまあ、少しずつ慣れていきましょうよ。
English: Don't worry. Take it a little at a time. You'll get used to it.

Japanese: （こんなスナオな御剣を見るの、１５年ぶりだな‥‥）
English: (It's been fifteen years since I've seen Edgeworth this... unguarded.)

Japanese: おっ、いたいた！
English: Hey, y'all!

Japanese: あ、ナツミさん！
English: Lotta!

Japanese: いやー、アンタら。カッコよかったでー。
English: Y'all were great in there!

Japanese: ありがとうございます。
English: Thank you!

Japanese: おッ。ニイちゃん。無罪、おめでと！
English: Yo, Edgeworth! Congrats!

Japanese: ム‥‥お、おおきに。
English: Er... thank y'all very much.

Japanese: ウチには、最初からわかってたんやで！
English: I knew you were innocent from the start, of course.

Japanese: あんたが無罪だ、ってなあ！
English: Just look at you! You wouldn't stick your hand in the cookie jar even if no one was there!

Japanese: ‥‥たしか、初日の検察側の証人だったような‥‥。
English: You... were the witness on the first day of the trial, weren't you.

Japanese: まま。まあまあ。細かいこと、気にしなさんな！
English: Yeah, well, let bygones be bygones, eh?

Japanese: そういえば、ナツミさん‥‥、今は、何を‥‥？
English: Speaking of which, what are you doing now, Lotta?

Japanese: おー、今か？大学にもどったで。
English: Who, me? Aww, I went back to college.

Japanese: スクープカメラマンになる夢は、もうオシマイや。
English: I gave up trying to be an investigative photographer pretty quick.

Japanese: そうなんですか‥‥。
English: Really? That's too bad.

Japanese: あれ？
English: Huh?

Japanese: ？?あそこにいるの、公園のマンジュウ屋、ちゃうか？
English: Isn't that the hotdog guy from the park?
Huh?

Japanese: ‥‥死ぬんだぁ‥‥。
English: It's over, Nick! My life is over!

Japanese: な、なんでおまえがそんなカオしてるんだよ！
English: Wh-why the sad face, Larry!? What happened now!?

Japanese: 成歩堂ぉ‥‥。オレ、そろそろ死ぬからさぁ。
English: Oh, Nick... I'm not long for this world.

Japanese: い、いやいやいや。
English: Uh... you don't look sick...

Japanese: カズミがよお‥‥パリで暮らすって言い出して‥‥、
English: It's Kiyance! Sh-she's goin' to live in Paris! Paris, Nick!

Japanese: 置いていかれちまったんだよぉぉ！
English: She's leaving me behind!!!

Japanese: （‥‥やれやれ‥‥）
English: (Larry, Larry...)

Japanese: お。御剣。いたのか。
English: Yo, Edgey! There you are!

Japanese: ム。そりゃまあ、いる。
English: Um, yes, here I am.

Japanese: おめでとうな、御剣。‥‥コレ、オレからのお祝い！
English: Congrats, Edgey! Here... a little gift from me in celebration!

Japanese: “お祝い”？　‥‥めずらしいな。
English: Celebration? That's unusual for you.

Japanese: お。アンタも後で来るッス！ゴチソウするッス！
English: Harry Butz! You come along tonight too! My treat, pal!

Japanese: は、はあ。楽しみにしてます。
English: Huh? Uh... thanks! Looking forward to it!

Japanese: （オイ、成歩堂）
English: (Yo, yo, Nick!)

Japanese: （アイツ、オレを取り調べした刑事だぜ‥‥）
English: (That's the suit that questioned me!)

Japanese: （ゴチソウって、まさかカツ丼じゃねえだろうな‥‥）
English: (When he says treat... that's not police-talk for prison food, right? Right?)

Japanese: そ、それはないと思うぞ。
English: Uh, I think you'll be fine, Larry.

Japanese: ‥‥成歩堂。
English: Wright...

Japanese: ん？　どうした？
English: Yeah? What's up?

Japanese: ‥‥金が入っているぞ。矢張がくれた封筒。
English: That envelope that Larry gave me. It's got money in it.

Japanese: そりゃそうだろ。“お祝い”だからな。
English: Well, yeah. That's not that strange. People give money away to celebrate sometimes.
It's $38.00, Wright.

Japanese: ‥‥また、ずいぶんハンパな金額だな‥‥。
English: Huh. What a weird amount. I mean, it's not a little, but it's not a lot either.
$38.00 exactly?

Japanese: ‥‥ななな、なるほどくん！
English: N-N-Nick!

Japanese: 小学校のころ、御剣検事が盗まれた給食費って、たしか‥‥！
English: Wasn't that exactly the amount of lunch money that was stolen from Mr. Edgeworth in school!?
No...

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥まさかああああッ！矢張、お前ェェェェッ！
English: No!!! Larry, it was you!!!

Japanese: 何をイロめきたっている？成歩堂。
English: What are you so surprised about, Wright?

Japanese: え？
English: Huh?

Japanese: たしかに矢張は、あの日カゼで学校を休んでいた。
English: Larry was absent that day from school, right?

Japanese: だが、だからといって犯人でないという理由にはならん。
English: But that doesn't automatically rule him out as a suspect.

Japanese: え！
English: What?

Japanese: １５年前の、あの日。
English: Think back to that day, fifteen years ago.

Japanese: 休んだはいいが、タイクツだった矢張は、学校に来てみたのだろう。
English: Larry took the day off, but he was bored, he came in to school anyway.

Japanese: そして、まあ‥‥、ちょっと手が伸びてしまったんだろうな。
English: Then he saw the money lying there... and the rest is history.

Japanese: 伸びちまったんだろうねえ、イヤハヤ、これが‥‥。
English: I never was good at History! Heh...

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥‥‥...御剣‥‥まさかお前、‥‥知ってたのか？
English: Edgeworth... you didn't know, did you?

Japanese: ‥‥アヤシイとは思っていた。
English: I suspected.

Japanese: いつもの矢張なら、お前をかばうのはオカシイ。
English: I just couldn't picture Larry protecting you like he did that day.

Japanese: みんなといっしょになって、ここぞとばかり責めたてただろう。
English: Everyone else was saying you did it. The whole class was against you, remember?

Japanese: ‥‥うむむむむ。
English: Yeah... too well.

Japanese: 成歩堂。もしかしたらお前は知らないかもしれないが、
English: Wright, you may not know this, but we used to have a saying back in school.

Japanese: この男は、こう言われていたんだ。“事件のカゲにヤッパリ矢張”と。
English: "When something smells, it's usually the Butz."

Japanese: そのコトバなら、イヤというほど思い知らされてるよ！
English: I know, I know.

Japanese: ‥‥いやあ、成歩堂。まさか、キミが気づいていなかったとは。
English: Really, Wright. I'm surprised you didn't figure it out!

Japanese: 意外だねこりゃあ。
English: Well, this is sure an unexpected turn of events, eh?

Japanese: ‥‥御剣。
English: Edgeworth...

Japanese: ん？
English: Hmm?

Japanese: 言えよ！
English: You should have told me!

Japanese: まあまあ、なるほどくん。もう１５年前のコトでしょ？
English: Now, now, Nick. It was fifteen years ago!

Japanese: これって“じこう”ってヤツだよね？　御剣検事。
English: Don't you think the "statute of limitations" has run out, Mr. Edgeworth?

Japanese: そういうこと、だな。
English: I'd say so, yes.

Japanese: そういうことだよ。
English: There you have it!

Japanese: ‥‥まったく‥‥。
English: Grr...

Japanese: お前らにカンシャして弁護士になったぼくの立場はどうなるんだ？
English: Where does that leave me!? I became a defense attorney because of what you two did!

Japanese: 感動的なまでのお人よし、といったところだろうか。
English: Well, I'd call you a goody-two-shoes to the extreme.

Japanese: とんだお調子モノ、とも言えるよナ！
English: Yeah! And you get worked up too easily, too!

Japanese: し、死刑だ！コイツを死刑にしてくれェッ！
English: D-death! The death sentence for both of you!

Japanese: ‥‥こんなことなら、検事になりゃよかったあ！
English: Man, if I only had known, I'd have become a prosecutor!

Japanese: ‥‥それは私も同じだ。
English: The same goes for me, only the other way around...

Japanese: “もしかしたら、自分は父親を撃ってしまったのかもしれない”
English: For the longest time, I thought that I might have killed my own father.

Japanese: “自分は罪人かもしれない”
English: I thought I might be a criminal.

Japanese: ‥‥私は、そういう自分を罰する意味もあって検事になった。
English: I became a prosecutor in part to punish myself.

Japanese: ‥‥こんなことなら、弁護士になりたかったよ。
English: If I had known the truth, I might have become a defense attorney after all.

Japanese: ‥‥御剣。
English: Edgeworth...

Japanese: かわるか、成歩堂。
English: Want to switch, Wright?

Japanese: おーい。みんなあ。‥‥写真、撮るでー。
English: Hey, y'all! Line up, I'll take a photo!

Japanese: ほらほら、写真だって。行こうよ！
English: Hey, photo time! Let's go!

Japanese: そのあとは、ゴチソウッス！
English: And after that, dinner on me!

Japanese: その夜は、イトノコ刑事の決死のサービスで‥‥、
English: Detective Gumshoe took us out on the town that night.

Japanese: ぼくたちは、すべてから解放された御剣を祝った。
English: We celebrated Edgeworth's new found freedom.

Japanese: ‥‥もちろん、御剣本人はまだ留置所の中だったのだが‥‥。
English: Although, Edgeworth was still in detention.

Japanese: １２月２９日　午前５時２分成歩堂法律事務所
English: December 29, 5:02 AM Wright & Co. Law Offices

Japanese: うーん‥‥昨日はさわぎすぎたなあ‥‥
English: Whoa... I went a little overboard yesterday.

Japanese: まだ、アタマがガンガンするぞ‥‥。
English: My head hurts...

Japanese: まだ５時か‥‥。もうひと寝入りするかな。
English: Huh? It's still only 5:00. Maybe I should go back to sleep...

Japanese: あれ。
English: Hmm?

Japanese: （これは‥‥手紙？）
English: (What's this? A letter?)

Japanese: おはよう、なるほどくん。
English: Good morning, Nick.

Japanese: 昨日のなるほどくんは、ホント、カッコよかったです。
English: You were really impressive yesterday.

Japanese: そんななるほどくんを見ていて、こう思いました。
English: Seeing you...

Japanese: “こんなことでいいのかな”って。
English: It made me think about what I'm doing here.

Japanese: あたしは、霊媒師です。‥‥まだ、１人前じゃないけど。
English: I'm a spirit medium... in training, of course.

Japanese: あたしも、御剣検事のチカラになりたかった。
English: I wanted to help Mr. Edgeworth too.

Japanese: なるほどくんを助けたかったです。‥‥でも、
English: I wanted to help you.

Japanese: できませんでした。‥‥とても、くやしかったです。
English: But I couldn't. I was useless.

Japanese: だから、あたしは一度、修行に出ようと思います。
English: So, I've decided to go back to my training.

Japanese: そして、まず、霊媒師として１人前になろうと思います。
English: I'll become a full-fledged spirit medium, for starters.

Japanese: ‥‥カオを見ると、言えなくなりそうなので、手紙にしました。
English: I couldn't say it to your face, so I left this letter.

Japanese: さようなら、なるほどくん。
English: Goodbye, Nick.

Japanese: （さ‥‥“さようなら”って‥‥）今、何時だ‥‥！
English: (G-goodbye!?) What time is it?

Japanese: 始発の発車時間、すぎている‥‥。と、とにかく、駅へ‥‥！
English: Gah! The first trains for the mountains have already left! To the station!

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥‥‥...やっぱり‥‥いないか。
English: I guess I'm too late...
Hey!

Japanese: な、なるほどくん！
English: N-Nick!

Japanese: 真宵ちゃん！
English: Maya!
So... you're leaving?

Japanese: ‥‥うん。
English: Yeah.

Japanese: くやしいから。霊を呼べない霊媒師なんて。
English: It's hard being a spirit medium who can't talk to spirits.
And...

Japanese: あたしがいなくても、もう平気だもんね、なるほどくん。
English: I think you'll do fine without me, Nick.

Japanese: じゃあ‥‥元気でね。
English: Be good... okay!

Japanese: ‥‥ちょっと待って！
English: W-wait!

Japanese: な、なに？
English: What?

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥‥‥‥‥...御剣を助けられたのは、真宵ちゃんたちのおかげだよ。
English: I never could have saved Edgeworth without your help.
Huh?

Japanese: 最後の法廷で‥‥聞いたんだよ。千尋さんの声を。
English: On the last day of the trial... I heard her. I heard Mia's voice!

Japanese: お姉ちゃんの‥‥？
English: You heard my sister...?

Japanese: 声だけ、だけど‥‥。
English: Yes... only her voice, but still...

Japanese: 最後の最後、もうダメだ、ってときに‥‥。
English: It was at the very end when I thought we'd lost everything.

Japanese: ‥‥そうなんだ。さすが、お姉ちゃんだな‥‥。
English: Well, that's my sister for you.

Japanese: イトノコ刑事さんも、星影センセイも、ヤッパリさんも。
English: Detective Gumshoe helped, and Mr. Grossberg, and even Larry...

Japanese: ‥‥やっぱり、あたしだけだ。なんのヤクにも立たなかった‥‥。
English: I'm the only one who couldn't help. I was useless, Nick.

Japanese: 狩魔にトドメを刺したのは、真宵ちゃんだよ。
English: But you were the one who stopped von Karma, Maya! Huh?

Japanese: ‥‥あ、あたしは、なんにもしてないよ！
English: I-I didn't do anything!

Japanese: ただ、おろおろしていただけ‥‥。
English: All I did was wander around in a daze...

Japanese: ‥‥悪いけど、こっちには証拠があるからね。
English: Sorry, but I have evidence that you helped!

Japanese: ‥‥しょうこ？
English: E-evidence?

Japanese: （スネている真宵ちゃんに、証拠を見せてあげよう）
English: (Show Maya some evidence to cheer her up!)
Huh?

Japanese: ごめんね。‥‥あたしにはよくわからないや。
English: S-sorry, Nick. I guess I don't understand.

Japanese: （し、しまった！　実は、ぼくもよくわかってないんだよな‥‥）
English: (Uh oh! Now that I think about it I don't understand this evidence either!)

Japanese: ‥‥いいんだよ。ムリしてなぐさめてくれなくて。
English: It's okay. You don't have to try and cheer me up.

Japanese: ‥‥でも。
English: But...

Japanese: いつか、たよれる真宵ちゃんになってみせるからね！
English: One day, I'll come back and be useful, I promise!

Japanese: ‥‥弾丸？
English: A bullet...?

Japanese: 狩魔は、ＤＬ６号事件の資料をすべてかくすつもりだった。
English: von Karma was convinced he had taken all of the evidence pertaining to DL-6!

Japanese: でも、真宵ちゃんが守ったんだよ。最後の証拠品を。
English: But you were the one who rescued the last piece of evidence we needed!

Japanese: ‥‥この弾丸が、狩魔をたおしたんだ。
English: This was the bullet that put an end to von Karma!

Japanese: この弾丸をぼくにくれたのは、真宵ちゃんだよ。
English: And you were the one who gave it to me!

Japanese: ‥‥なるほどくん‥‥。
English: Nick...

Japanese: ありがとう。‥‥真宵ちゃん。
English: Thanks, Maya. I couldn't have done it without you.
I'll be back soon.

Japanese: え？
English: Huh?

Japanese: １人前になったら、帰ってくる。
English: I'm going to complete my training, and come back!
Okay. I'll be waiting.

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥‥‥...やっぱり、あたしがいなくちゃダメだからね、なるほどくんは！
English: Of course you will. You can't run that office by yourself! You're hopeless!

Japanese: そ‥‥そんなことはないけどさ。
English: Uh, I don't know about that...

Japanese: じゃ‥‥行くね。
English: So... bye.

Japanese: うん。
English: Bye.

Japanese: ありがとう‥‥なるほどくん。
English: Thanks... Nick.

Japanese: ‥‥これで、ぼくの物語はおしまいだ。
English: And so my story ends. Time to turn a new page...

Japanese: 新米弁護士だった自分に別れを告げて、
English: and say goodbye to the novice defense attorney that I once was...

Japanese: ぼくは今、新しいページをめくり、新しい物語を生きている。
English: Now a new story begins...

Japanese: ‥‥あいかわらずのカオぶれで‥‥
English: With the same old crazy cast of characters...

Japanese: まだまだ甘いな！このシロウト弁護士が！
English: Hah! Don't think you've graduated yet, amateur!

Japanese: 成歩堂くん。‥‥もう一度、考えなおしなさい。
English: Mr. Wright. Perhaps you'd like to rethink that claim?

Japanese: は、はあ‥‥。では、おコトバに甘えて‥‥
English: Er... yes, Your Honor. (Uh oh... I got a bad feeling about this...)

Japanese: ■企画?脚本?監督巧　　舟
English: ■Concept-Script-Direction Shu Takumi

Japanese: 今年の正月は、なんと御剣検事が年始のアイサツに来てくれたッス！
English: Hey, pal! Mr. Edgeworth came down to the precinct to wish me a happy new year!

Japanese: こんなコト、初めてッス！うれしかったッスねー。
English: Talk about a pleasant surprise!

Japanese: うおおおおおおおおおおおおおっ！イトノコギリ刑事ィィィィィィィ！
English: Whoooooooooooooop! Detective Gumshooooooooooe!

Japanese: ‥‥と叫んだあと、クビをひねりながら帰って行ったッスが、
English: Then he hung his head low and went right back outside.

Japanese: ありゃあいったい、なんだったッスかねえ‥‥。
English: Kind of like he was embarrassed or something. Strange, huh?

Japanese: ■グラフィック末包　久美子
English: ■Graphics Kumiko Suekane

Japanese: え？　成歩堂？　‥‥さあねえ。しばらく会ってねえなあ。
English: Huh? Nick? Nah, haven't seen him lately.

Japanese: あ、オレ？　最近は、とうふ屋でバイトしてるよ。
English: Who, me? I've been working at a cheese shop!

Japanese: ミユキのヤツ、いい女なんだけど、ちょっと金がかかるからさあ。
English: That Missy's a nice lady, but she not exactly what you'd call a "cheap date."

Japanese: ま、今、彼女ハワイに行ってて会えないんだけどね‥‥。
English: Huh? Oh, she's in Hawaii right now, yeah...

Japanese: ■グラフィック岩元　辰郎
English: ■Graphics Tatsurou Iwamoto

Japanese: 成歩堂くんですか。‥‥モチロンおぼえてますよ。
English: Who? Wright? Yeah, I remember him.

Japanese: なんでも、最近はずいぶんカツヤクしてるとか。
English: I hear he's been busy lately.

Japanese: こんなこと言いたかないけど、この私が育てたようなモンですよ。
English: You know, not to ring my own bell, but I sort of taught him everything he knows.

Japanese: 彼も、きっと私にカンシャしてると思いますね。
English: I'm sure he's grateful.

Japanese: ■メインプログラム大谷　規之
English: ■Main Programming Noriyuki Otani

Japanese: 成歩堂　龍一‥‥？はて‥‥。
English: Phoenix Wright...? Hmm...

Japanese: ああ、このワタクシが宣誓書を書いてやった、弁護士。
English: Ah! The defense attorney for whom I wrote that affidavit for, yes!

Japanese: あ、ワタクシ。今度、板東ホテルの支配人に決まりまして。
English: Oh, you should know, I've taken over management of the Gatewater Hotel recently.

Japanese: 近くへお越しの際は、ぜひ当ホテルへ‥‥。
English: Should you be in the area, please, stop by...

Japanese: ■プログラム遠藤　正勝
English: ■Programming Masakatsu Endo

Japanese: ウォッホォン！‥‥なんぢゃ、チミは。
English: *Ah-HHHHEM!* Hmm? Oh, it's you.

Japanese: なるほどう　りゅういち？‥‥ああ、千尋クンの‥‥。
English: Phoenix Wright? Ah yes, Mia's understudy, was he not?

Japanese: どうしておるかのお。最近、カオを見せんが‥‥。
English: I wonder how he's doing. Haven't seen him of late...

Japanese: “青春の日々はぁ‥‥青いレモンのカオリぃ‥‥”かァ。
English: "Ahh...the days of my youth... like the scent of fresh lemon..." you see.

Japanese: ■音　　楽杉森　雅和
English: ■Music Masakazu Sugimori

Japanese: ‥‥成歩堂　龍一？そんなスターいたっけ？
English: Phoenix Wright...? He an actor?

Japanese: オバチャン、認めないよ！そんなヘンな名前の俳優なんてサ！
English: Well, I'm not buying it! You can't be a star with a name like "Phoenix"!

Japanese: それよりサ今度イブクロちゃんのムカシの映画がねェまとめて発売
English: Did you know that they're finally putting some of Hammer's old movies out

Japanese: されんのよ何でもＤＶＤぼっくすとかいうシロモノらしいんだけど
English: on DVD in one of them box set thingies that you can buy now-a-days and all

Japanese: なんだろうねＤＶＤってだいたいアレだね最近はなんでも横文字で
English: not that I know what a "DVD" is or why they use three- letter words for just about

Japanese: ワケがわかんないョいくらナウいオバチャンでもお手上げだねェ
English: everything these days how am I supposed to keep it all straight anyhow, hmm?

Japanese: ■効果音森　敦史
English: ■Sound Effects Atsushi Mori

Japanese: おかげさまで、ヒメサマンが大人気でして‥‥。
English: I'm pleased to announce the Pink Princess is a hit!

Japanese: 成歩堂さんには、ホント、足を向けて寝られませんよ。
English: I sure owe that Mr. Wright a great deal. *sniff*

Japanese: 撮影が終わるまで、前以上にカオを出さないようにしてますよ。
English: Oh, and I'm keeping my face out of the public eye till the show's over.

Japanese: コドモたちのユメをこわしちゃいけませんからね‥‥
English: I wouldn't want to ruin any kids' dreams, y'know.

Japanese: ■宣　　伝中谷　洋　　寺田　歩未
English: ■Publicity Hiroshi Nakaya Ayumi Terada

Japanese: あ。こないだ真宵ちゃんからお手紙もらったんですよ。
English: Oh, I got a letter from Maya the other day.

Japanese: なんでも、滝に打たれすぎてカゼひいちゃったんですって。
English: It sounds like she caught a cold standing under a waterfall.

Japanese: お見舞いがわりに、ヒメサマンのトレカ、送ってあげました。
English: I wanted to visit, but didn't have time, so I sent her some Pink Princess trading cards.

Japanese: なかなか買えないんですって。どんな所に住んでるんでしょうね？
English: She says she can't buy them where she is. What kind of place is she living at anyway?

Japanese: ■取扱説明書内田　裕美子
English: ■Manual Yumiko Uchida

Japanese: なるほどー？　‥‥誰それ？それより、今はヒメサマンだよ！
English: Wright? Who's that? You wanna talk? Let's talk Pink Princess! Alright!

Japanese: ‥‥でもさ。この前、スタジオに忍び込んだとき、
English: But... you know. I snuck into the studio the other day.

Japanese: オレ、見ちゃったんだ。ヒメサマンの中に入ってるヤツ。
English: And I saw her... the one inside the Pink Princess suit!

Japanese: もう、ショックだったよ！あんなムサいのがヒメサマンとは！
English: Ugh! What a dog! It was kind of a shock for a boy of my tender age.

Japanese: ■プロデュース稲葉　敦志
English: ■Producer Atsushi Inaba

Japanese: モチロンおぼえとるで。成歩堂やろ？　弁護士の。
English: Yeah, I remember Wright! That lawyer guy!

Japanese: え、ウチ？　スクープカメラマン目指して、今は修行中や！
English: Huh, me? I'm in training to become an paranormal photographer!

Japanese: あのな。あの日、撮った写真にな。写っとったんや‥‥心霊が！
English: You know that picture I took of everyone? Well, just behind them there's a ghost!

Japanese: やっぱりウチには、才能がある！って思いなおしたワケや‥‥。
English: For real! Now that's talent! I'm gonna be famous!

Japanese: ■製作総指揮三上　真司
English: ■Executive Producer Shinji Mikami

Japanese: 本法廷は、これ以上の審議の必要性を認めません。
English: This court sees no reason to further prolong the trial.

Japanese: さらに、被告人に対する判決を決める時間も、必要としません。
English: Nor is there any need for more time to decide the case against the defendant.

Japanese: これは、きわめて明白な事件です。疑問の余地はない！
English: This case is extremely clear. I see no room for mis- interpretation of the facts.

Japanese: 被告人、御剣　怜侍に判決を言い渡します！
English: This court finds the defendant, Mr. Miles Edgeworth...

Japanese: 被告人はこれより身柄を司直に預けるものとし、
English: The accused will surrender to the court immediately,

Japanese: １ヶ月以内に、高等裁判所において通常裁判を受けることとします。
English: to be held pending trial at a higher court within a month from today's date.

Japanese: では、本日はこれにて閉廷！
English: That is all. The court is adjourned!

Japanese: 真宵ちゃんが事務所を去ってから、２ヶ月‥‥
English: It's been two months since Maya left the office...

Japanese: ぼくは１度も法廷に立っていない。
English: Two months without a single trial.

Japanese: 依頼はあったけど、なんとなく‥‥その気になれなかった。
English: I've had offers... But none I took.

Japanese: ‥‥そう。あの日、彼女が訪ねてくるまでは。
English: That is... until the day that girl showed up.

Japanese: ２月２２日　午前１０時　２分成歩堂法律事務所
English: February 22, 10:02 AM Wright & Co. Law Offices

Japanese: （やれやれ。依頼を引き受ける気はないのに‥‥）
English: (Why do I come here to the office every day?)

Japanese: （どうして毎日、事務所を開けてるのかな、ぼく‥‥）
English: (It's not like I want to work...)

Japanese: あー。やっと帰ってきた！
English: There you are! Finally!

Japanese: もう！　どこへ行ってたんですか！
English: Where have you been!?

Japanese: お姉ちゃんの裁判、もう明日なんですよ！
English: My sister's trial is tomorrow!
Um... who are you?

Japanese: そんなコトより今、ダイジなのは！
English: It doesn't matter who I am! It only matters who YOU are!

Japanese: あなたが、弁護士の綾里　千尋さんだというジジツですっ！
English: You're the famous defense attorney, Mia Fey!
Oh, uh... You're not Mia Fey, are you?

Japanese: すみませんが‥‥綾里　千尋はその。もう、ここには‥‥
English: I'm sorry but Ms. Mia Fey no longer... works here.

Japanese: じゃあ、あなたは‥‥お茶くみのヒト、ですか？
English: So you are...? The coffee boy?

Japanese: ‥‥成歩堂　龍一です。いちおう、弁護士の。
English: I'm Phoenix Wright... A defense attorney.

Japanese: ‥‥なるほど‥‥なるほど‥‥あッ！
English: Wright... Wright... Wait! You're THE Phoenix Wright!?

Japanese: もしかして、あの！御剣検事殺人事件の！
English: The Phoenix Wright from the Edgeworth murder case!?

Japanese: ま、まあ、そんなトコロです。（御剣は死んでないけど）
English: Um, yes, that's correct. (It wasn't Edgeworth who was murdered, though...)

Japanese: あー、よかった！　この際、ゼータクは言ってられないし‥‥
English: That's a relief then! You're better than nobody!

Japanese: 悪いけど‥‥今はちょっと、依頼は受けていないんですよ。
English: I'm sorry... I'm afraid I'm not taking cases right now.

Japanese: え！　でも、成歩堂さんですよね？弁護士で。無敵の。
English: But, you are Phoenix Wright, right? The undefeated defense attorney?

Japanese: ‥‥とにかく、今は事件を引き受けていないんですよ。
English: Look, I'm not accepting any new cases.

Japanese: 申しわけありませんけど。
English: I'm sorry, but you'll have to try elsewhere.

Japanese: おねがいです！‥‥もう、時間がないの。
English: Please! I'm out of time!

Japanese: そう言われましても‥‥
English: But...

Japanese: お、お姉ちゃんが‥‥
English: Please, you have to help! I-it's my sister!
(Maya...? Could it be...?)

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥‥‥‥‥‥...わかりました。お話をうかがいましょう。
English: Okay. I'll hear you out.

Japanese: ほ、ホントですかっ！ありがとうございます！
English: R-really!? Thank you so much!

Japanese: あたし‥‥カガク捜査官の宝月　茜（ほうづきあかね）です！
English: My name's Ema, Ema Skye. I'm a scientific investigator.

Japanese: （か。かがくそうさかん‥‥？）
English: (Scientific investigator?)

Japanese: 千尋さんが“チャーリー”と名づけてかわいがっていた植物。
English: Mia's plant, "Charley."

Japanese: 真宵ちゃんがいなくなってからも、こいつの世話だけは忘れない。
English: I've been taking care of him in Maya's absence.

Japanese: ムズカシイ法律の本があざ笑うように並んでいる。
English: Difficult-looking legal books stand in a formidable row. They mock me.

Japanese: ちょっと読んでみたら、アタマが痛くなった。
English: I tried reading one, and it made my head hurt.

Japanese: しまうとき、落っことして足まで痛くなった。
English: When I closed it, it slipped out of my hand. Then my foot hurt too.

Japanese: 今日も向かいのホテルは大はんじょうだ。
English: Looks like it's cleaning day again at the hotel across the way.

Japanese: なんでも今度、支店を出すそうだ。
English: I hear they're planning a second branch outside the city.

Japanese: ‥‥ふと目をやったら、あのボーイと目が合ってしまった。
English: Egads! The bellboy was staring right at me.

Japanese: 千尋さんが使っていたデスクだ。
English: Mia's desk.

Japanese: 依頼を引き受けなくなってから、ますます座る機会がへった。
English: I sit here even less now that I've stopped taking cases.

Japanese: ‥‥たまにはそうじしないとなあ。
English: I ought to at least dust it off once in a while.

Japanese: トノサマンのポスターが張ってある。
English: There's a poster of the Steel Samurai on the wall.

Japanese: 真宵ちゃんが、帰りぎわに張りかえていったみたいだ。
English: Maya stuck it up here on the day that she left.

Japanese: せっかくの置きみやげだから、そのままにしてあるんだけど‥‥
English: I didn't have the heart to take it down.

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥‥‥...たまに、お客さんの視線が妙にイタいときがある。
English: I do sometimes get strange looks from the clients, though.

Japanese: あかねさん、でしたっけ。たしか、《科学捜査官》とか‥‥
English: Ema, was it? So you're a "scientific investigator"?

Japanese: ええ！　そうなんです。それが何か？
English: Yes! That's right! Is... something wrong?

Japanese: それにしてはずいぶん、落ちつきが‥‥いや、若いなあ、って。
English: No, it's just, you seem kind of, er, jumpy. Or maybe just... young?

Japanese: あ、申しおくれました！今年で１６才になります！
English: Young? I'll be sixteen years old this year!

Japanese: ああ、そうなんですか‥‥って、ちょっと待って！
English: Oh, I see... wait! Only sixteen!?

Japanese: ‥‥１６才？あと３年もすれば、正式に鑑識課への配属が決まりますから。
English: I'm set to be formally assigned to Forensics in three more years.

Japanese: あたし、ちょっとしたモンなんです‥‥すでにこのトシにして、もう。
English: My work is becoming quite well known... At my age, no less!

Japanese: ‥‥あの。現在の職業をセイカクに言うと‥‥？
English: Um, so what exactly is your current position, then?

Japanese: まあ、カガク的にあえてゲンミツに言うのであれば‥‥
English: Well, legally speaking...

Japanese: “こうこうにねんせい”？になるのかもしれませんケド。
English: I guess you'd call me an "Eleventh Grader."

Japanese: でも、あたしは！　身もココロも！すでにこのトシにして、もう！
English: But I'm ready to do my job! At my age, no less!

Japanese: （やれやれ‥‥タマゴか。この子も）
English: (Great, another future professional in training...)

Japanese: それで‥‥事件、っていうのは？たしか、裁判は明日だとか。
English: So what's this about a case? You said the trial's tomorrow?

Japanese: あの。お姉ちゃんじゃありません！ヒトをナイフで刺し殺すなんて‥‥
English: My sister didn't do it! She wouldn't stab someone with a knife! She wouldn't!

Japanese: ‥‥殺人事件、なんだ。
English: So... it's a murder case.

Japanese: お姉ちゃんが刺したトコを見た人がいても、カンケーありません！
English: I don't care if there's a witness who saw her do it! She didn't do it!

Japanese: だってあたし、カガク的に知ってるから！　お姉ちゃんは‥‥
English: I know she didn't do it! It's a scientific fact!

Japanese: ‥‥目撃者がいるんだ。
English: And... there's a witness.

Japanese: と、とにかく！　お姉ちゃんとお話ししてみてください！
English: J-just talk to her! You have to talk to her!

Japanese: あ、ああ‥‥そうさせてもらうよ。
English: Right... I suppose I will.

Japanese: ホントは、綾里　千尋さんを連れていく、って約束したんですけど。
English: I promised her I'd bring Mia Fey, but...

Japanese: （そういえば‥‥）
English: (That's interesting...)

Japanese: （どうして知っていたんだろう？千尋さんのコト‥‥）
English: (How would she know Mia?)

Japanese: 大きくなったら科学捜査官になりたいんだ。
English: So, you want to be a scientific investigator when you grow up, then?

Japanese: な、なんですか！あたし、コドモじゃありません！
English: E-excuse me? I'm not a child, I'll have you know!

Japanese: それにしても、ずいぶんマニアック‥‥具体的なユメだね。
English: Still, it's good to have a goal. Albeit a very unusual one.

Japanese: これからの捜査は、もっと科学的にハンニンを追いつめるべきです！
English: I believe investigations should be done scientifically!

Japanese: ね。そう思いませんか？
English: Don't you?

Japanese: は、はあ。（目をキラキラさせているぞ‥‥）
English: Uh, yeah. (Sure can't fault her for a lack of enthusiasm.)

Japanese: そうすれば、お姉ちゃんだってきっと‥‥
English: If this case is handled scientifically, I'm sure my sister's name will be cleared!

Japanese: お姉ちゃん‥‥？
English: Your sister...?

Japanese: あたし、いろいろ勉強してますから‥‥そのうち、見せてあげますね！
English: I've been doing research, you know!

Japanese: 最新のカガク捜査のなんたるかを！
English: I'm developing a new scientific method of case investigation! I'll show you when I'm done!

Japanese: ‥‥楽しみにしておくよ。
English: I'm looking forward to it.

Japanese: （とにかく、留置所へ行ってお姉さんの話を聞いてみるか）
English: (Guess I should get down to the Detention Center and talk to her sister.)

Japanese: お姉ちゃんが言ってたんです。
English: My sister asked for her specifically.

Japanese: アヤサト　チヒロさん‥‥学生時代の後輩なんだ、って。
English: Mia Fey... was a few years below her in school.

Japanese: （千尋さんのセンパイ、か）
English: (So she went to the same school as Mia.)

Japanese: いつか、弁護士が必要になったらチヒロさんに‥‥って。
English: She always told me to go to Mia if I ever needed a defense attorney...

Japanese: ムカシ、よく言ってたから。だから、あたし‥‥
English: And, well... I need one.

Japanese: ちなみに千尋さん、女性だけどね。
English: Um, incidentally, Mia is a woman.

Japanese: チヒロ‥‥あたしもウスウス“ヘンだな”と思ったんですけど。
English: Mia... Yeah, I thought it was a little strange when I saw you, too.

Japanese: なんだか、お姉さんと仲がいいみたいだね。
English: Well, it's nice of you to help your sister out like this. You must be close.
Well...

Japanese: 今のお姉ちゃん‥‥アタシ、好きじゃありません。
English: Actually, when she gets like she is now, I kind of hate her.
(Huh?)

Japanese: でも‥‥あたしには、たったひとりの家族だから。
English: But... But she's my only family.

Japanese: ‥‥“たったひとり”？あの、ご両親は‥‥
English: Your only family? What about your parents?

Japanese: 小さいころに、交通事故で亡くなっちゃいました。
English: They died in a car accident when I was little.

Japanese: そう、なんだ‥‥
English: Oh... I'm sorry.

Japanese: ホラ。これが弁護士バッジだよ。
English: See this? It's my Attorney's Badge.

Japanese: ふむふむ。ほおほお。これがウワサの‥‥
English: Ahh! Well! I've never seen a real one before.

Japanese: （こんなに食いついてくる子は初めてだな‥‥）
English: (You're the first one who's actually been interested in mine, believe me.)

Japanese: 材質は銀。金メッキが少しはがれてきてますね。
English: Its composition is mostly silver. The gold plating is flaking a bit.

Japanese: （カガク的に分析されてしまった）
English: (She analyzed it. Scientifically...)

Japanese: 硫化物による腐食は、まだみたい。５０００円ぐらい、でしょうか。
English: There doesn't appear to be any corrosion due to sulfides. I'd give you $50.00 for it.

Japanese: 悪いけど、売る気はないよ。‥‥まだ。
English: Sorry, but it's not for sale. Yet...

Japanese: あの。これ、なんだけど。
English: Here, see this?

Japanese: ふむふむ。やはり弁護士さんは見せたがり屋である、と。
English: Ahh! I've noticed that defense attorneys have a tendency to want to show people things.

Japanese: （弁護士の生態をカガク的にカンサツされてしまった‥‥）
English: (What is this? A behavioral study of lawyers?)

Japanese: ‥‥すみません、成歩堂さん。
English: I'm sorry, Mr. Wright.

Japanese: ん？　なんのコトかな。
English: Huh? About what?

Japanese: お姉ちゃんが‥‥その。あんな感じで。
English: My sister... She's not always like that you know.

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥‥...まさか、また検事さんを弁護するハメになるとは思わなかったよ。
English: I just never expected to be defending another prosecutor again.

Japanese: お姉ちゃん‥‥ムカシはあんなじゃなかったんです。
English: She's changed a lot.

Japanese: やさしくて、いつも笑っていて‥‥みんなにも、好かれていて。
English: She used to be so gentle, always smiling. Everybody liked her.

Japanese: ‥‥ふうん。（想像がつかないぞ‥‥悪いけど）
English: I see... (Sorry, but I'm having trouble imagining that.)

Japanese: 何かあったのかな？　お姉さん。
English: What happened to her?

Japanese: ‥‥あたしにも、ハッキリした理由はわからないんですけど‥‥
English: I don't know for certain myself...

Japanese: ココロ当たりがあるような、ないような‥‥あるような。
English: I think maybe she... Well, maybe not.

Japanese: （カガク的に割り切れない事情がありそうだな）
English: (Sounds like there's something there that defies a simple scientific explanation.)

Japanese: とにかく、現場に行ってみよう。検事局の地下駐車場、だっけ？
English: Let's go check out this underground parking at the Prosecutor's Office, shall we?

Japanese: は‥‥はいッ！
English: O-Okay!

Japanese: ２月２２日　某時刻留置所　面会室
English: February 22 Detention Center Visitor's Room
(Hmm, I wonder what's wrong with Ema?)

Japanese: （あかねちゃん、ここへ来てから急におとなしくなっちゃったな）
English: (She got quiet all of a sudden as soon as we arrived.)

Japanese: ‥‥看守。面会は断ったハズですよ。
English: Guard... I thought I told you I didn't want visitors.

Japanese: すすす、す、すみません！しし、しかし、妹さんがですね‥‥
English: S-s-s-sorry, ma'am! It's j-j-just, your sister...

Japanese: イイワケは聞きません。
English: No excuses!

Japanese: 来月の給与査定‥‥楽しみにしておくことです。
English: Or did you not want a raise this year, hmm?

Japanese: ははは、は、はッ！
English: U-u-u-understood, ma'am!

Japanese: （ななな、な、なんだなんだ！）
English: (Wh-wh-wh-what was that all about?)

Japanese: お。お姉ちゃん‥‥
English: H-hi, Lana.

Japanese: ‥‥私のキオクちがい、かしら。
English: Funny.

Japanese: ここへは来ないようにと言ったつもりでいました。
English: I seem to remember specifically telling you NOT to come here.

Japanese: ‥‥二度と。
English: Perhaps my memory is failing? L-look...

Japanese: あ、あたしだって。来たくなかったよ！　‥‥だけど。
English: I didn't want to come here either, okay?

Japanese: 裁判はもう明日だし、弁護士さんだって、まだ‥‥
English: But your trial's tomorrow and you still don't have a defense attorney!

Japanese: 被告席に立つのは、私。あなたには、カンケイありません。
English: I'll be the one in court tomorrow. This has nothing to do with you, Ema.

Japanese: ‥‥そうですね？成歩堂　龍一弁護士。
English: Isn't that right, Mr. Wright?

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥‥‥！ぼくのコト‥‥知ってるんですか。
English: Hey! How do you know me?

Japanese: 千尋さんからうかがっています。あなたのことは、いろいろ。
English: Mia mentioned you. I've heard... quite a bit.

Japanese: ‥‥あの。あなたは、どういう‥‥？
English: Er, I'm sorry. What exactly is it that you do...?

Japanese: 宝月　巴（ほうづきともえ）と申します。
English: My name is Lana, Lana Skye.

Japanese: ‥‥地方検事局?主席検事です。
English: I'm Chief Prosecutor for this district.

Japanese: け、検事‥‥！検事さん、なんですか‥‥！
English: Y-you're a prosecutor!?

Japanese: （‥‥こ、この姉妹‥‥グーゼン、なのか？）
English: (Two sisters... one a lawyer. Could this be a coincidence?)

Japanese: （あかねちゃんに、ともえさん‥‥どこからどう見ても‥‥）
English: (Ema... Lana... I mean, they're just like...)

Japanese: どうしたんですか？　成歩堂さん。
English: Is something wrong, Mr. Wright?

Japanese: 面会のようすを監視する看守だ。
English: This guard monitors the visitor's room.

Japanese: ともえさんの冷気を全身にあびて、震え上がっている。
English: He's frozen in fear of the frigid Miss Lana.

Japanese: ‥‥ココロなしか、ぼくもサムくなってきた。
English: I'm feeling a bit chilly myself.

Japanese: ごていねいに、カメラで見張っているようだ。
English: Smile for the camera...

Japanese: 最初に申しあげておきます。ご存じかもしれませんが‥‥
English: There's something you should know from the start.

Japanese: な、なんでしょう。
English: W-which is?

Japanese: 今回の案件では、被疑者は全面的に容疑を認めています。
English: The suspect in this case has confessed to the crime.
Huh?

Japanese: ちょ、ちょっと待ってよ！“被疑者”って‥‥
English: W-wait! But the suspect... The suspect is...!

Japanese: つまり、真犯人は‥‥この私。いかがかしら？　成歩堂弁護士‥‥
English: Me. I did it. Well, Mr. Wright?

Japanese: とにかく‥‥何があったのか、話していただけませんか？
English: Well... why don't you begin by telling me exactly what happened.

Japanese: 事件が発生したのは、きのうです。２月２１日、午後５時１５分。
English: The crime took place yesterday, February 21, at 5:15 PM.

Japanese: ずいぶんハッキリしてますね。
English: That's quite specific.

Japanese: 目撃者の証言です。
English: It was in the witness's deposition.

Japanese: ‥‥私、犯行の瞬間をハッキリ見られましたので。
English: A witness clearly saw me committing the crime.

Japanese: そ‥‥それはどうも、その。運が悪かったですね。
English: Uh... My, that was a bit of bad luck, wasn't it?

Japanese: 現場は、地方検事局の地下駐車場。
English: The crime took place in the underground parking lot at the Prosecutor's Office.

Japanese: 遺体は、私の部下のクルマの後部トランクから発見されました。
English: The body was found in the trunk of my subordinate's car.

Japanese: 検事局‥‥ですか。（ブカのクルマ、ねえ‥‥）
English: The Prosecutor's Office, huh? (In your subordinate's car trunk? Classy...)

Japanese: この私が、現行犯でタイホされた場所でもあります。
English: I was arrested on the spot. Caught red-handed, as it were.

Japanese: （やれやれ‥‥）
English: (My, my...)

Japanese: それで、亡くなった被害者というのは‥‥？
English: So, who was the victim?

Japanese: 警察局の捜査官。いわゆる“刑事”です。
English: An investigator with the Police Department. I suppose the correct term is "Detective."

Japanese: けいじ‥‥さん。
English: A detective...?

Japanese: 死因は、失血死です。‥‥胸部をナイフでひと突きしましたので。
English: Death was due to a loss of blood. He was stabbed once in the stomach.

Japanese: ‥‥あなたが、ですか。
English: By... you?

Japanese: 即死にはいたりませんでしたが、それが致命傷だったようです。
English: Death wasn't immediate, but the wound was fatal.

Japanese: はあ‥‥
English: I see...

Japanese: 成歩堂弁護士。もう一度言います。被害者は、刑事だった‥‥
English: Allow me to repeat myself, Mr. Wright. The victim was a detective.

Japanese: これが何をイミするか？モチロン、おわかりでしょう？
English: You know what that means, don't you?

Japanese: え！
English: Uh oh!

Japanese: ナニをイミするんですか？成歩堂さん！
English: What? Mr. Wright? What does it mean?

Japanese: そ、それは、モチロン、その‥‥
English: Well, it means...

Japanese: 警察は、威信にかけて、有罪判決を“取り”に来る‥‥そういうこと。
English: The police department will consider it a matter of pride to have me found guilty.

Japanese: いかなる手を使っても、私を追いつめようとするでしょう。
English: They will use any means at their disposal to do so.

Japanese: （やれやれ‥‥トンでもないコトになっちまったな‥‥今回も）
English: (This case gets worse and worse with everything I learn.)

Japanese: それで、あなたは‥‥たしか、《主席検事》とか。
English: So, you're the Chief Prosecutor?

Japanese: ‥‥そうです。
English: That is correct.

Japanese: 地方検事局が抱えている全公判をマクロなカタチで把握して‥‥
English: I'm responsible for overseeing every trial handled by prosecutors in this district.

Japanese: 適切な検事のアテンドと、ケア。彼らのタスクをつねに管理する‥‥
English: I make sure the prosecutors have what they need to do the job, and manage every aspect.

Japanese: ‥‥カンタンに言えば、そんな職務でしょうか。
English: Those are my responsibilities, in a nutshell.

Japanese: （ゼッタイ、もっとカンタンに言えると思うけど‥‥）
English: (That's an awfully large nutshell.)

Japanese: ‥‥それよりも。
English: Still, I'm a little surprised.

Japanese: あなたは、地方検事局の主席検事のカオも知らなかったのですか。
English: I would think you'd recognize the district's Chief Prosecutor, Mr. Wright.

Japanese: え。
English: Huh?

Japanese: ‥‥あり得ないコト。そう申し上げるほかありません。
English: In fact, it seems impossible you wouldn't...

Japanese: あの‥‥お姉ちゃん。その、手の包帯‥‥どうしたの？
English: Um... Lana? What happened to your hand?

Japanese: ああ。これかしら？
English: Oh, this?

Japanese: ちょっと、自分の手を切ってしまいましたの‥‥刺すときに。
English: I cut myself by accident. When I stabbed him, that is.

Japanese: え。
English: Huh?

Japanese: 私も、さすがに殺人には不慣れなものですから‥‥
English: I'm not very good at being a criminal, I suppose.

Japanese: （‥‥やれやれ。こんなのを弁護しろってのか‥‥？）
English: (How am I supposed to defend this!?)

Japanese: （そういえば‥‥）
English: (Time to change the subject...)

Japanese: （たしか“センパイ”なんだよな。‥‥千尋さんの）
English: (Wait, she was in the class ahead of Mia, wasn't she...?)

Japanese: あの。シツレイですけど‥‥センパイ、なんですよね？
English: Um, you were in school with Mia, correct?

Japanese: ‥‥綾里　千尋さんの。
English: A few years above her?

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥‥‥あかねが、そんなことまで？
English: ... Ema told you that, too, did she?

Japanese: い、いいじゃない！　こうして、事務所から連れてきたんだから！
English: W-well, why not? I did drag him all the way here from his office!

Japanese: あらゆるイミで、彼女と共通点があるようには見えませんけど。
English: Although it seems he has very little in common with Mia...

Japanese: （ヨケイなお世話だよ！）
English: (Hey!)

Japanese: ‥‥法科大学時代のことです。私が３回生、彼女は特別聴講生‥‥
English: It was in law school. I was in my third year, and she was auditing the class.

Japanese: まわりの学生とは、アキラカにフンイキがちがいました。
English: She was different than the other students.

Japanese: フンイキが‥‥
English: Different?

Japanese: 弁護士になるためならば、なんでもやる‥‥そんなハクリョクが。
English: She was strong... She'd do anything to become a defense attorney. Anything.

Japanese: きっと‥‥だから、この私に近づいてきたのでしょう。
English: That... was probably why she was attracted to me.

Japanese: どういうことですか？
English: E-excuse me!?

Japanese: お姉ちゃん、主席だったんです。法科大学で、イチバン‥‥
English: Intellectually attracted! Lana was top of her class in school.

Japanese: 優秀なのです。‥‥ワタクシは。
English: I was the best there was.

Japanese: ‥‥はあ。
English: Oh...

Japanese: あ。ちなみに、あたしも優秀ですからっ！
English: I'm doing pretty good in school too, by the way!

Japanese: （姉妹で同じコトを言っても、ゼンゼンちがって聞こえるな）
English: (It sounds a bit different when Ema says it...)

Japanese: ‥‥まだ、キレイですね。このバッジ。
English: Your badge looks new.

Japanese: 毎日、みがいてますからね。
English: I polish it daily.

Japanese: 数年もすれば、バッジの金メッキははがれ落ちるはずです。
English: In a few years, the gold plating will flake off.

Japanese: ‥‥そのとき。きっと、あなたの本当の姿が見えるでしょう。
English: Then we'll see the real you.

Japanese: （ううう‥‥気軽な世間話ができないのかな、このヒト‥‥）
English: (Gah! What ever happened to innocent small talk!?)

Japanese: あなたの、３年後‥‥楽しみにしています。
English: Give it three years. Then we'll see what you have become.

Japanese: あの。これなんですけど‥‥
English: Would you mind taking a look at this?

Japanese: ‥‥１つ、教えてさしあげます。成歩堂弁護士。
English: I'll say this once and only once, Mr. Wright.

Japanese: 弁護士と検事が、法廷外で証拠品を論じるのは、タブーです。
English: Attorneys and prosecutors have no business showing evidence outside court.

Japanese: たがいに利害のからむ案件ならば、なおさら‥‥
English: It's taboo. Especially when the interests of both parties are involved.

Japanese: これは、キホンです。おぼえおいて、ソンはありません。
English: This is a basic principle, Mr. Wright. You'd do well to learn it.

Japanese: 怒られちゃいましたね。
English: She got pretty mad at you.

Japanese: ヒトに説教できる立場じゃないと思うけどなあ。
English: You wouldn't think she'd be in the position to lecture anyone right now.

Japanese: ‥‥さて。いかがかしら、成歩堂弁護士。
English: Well, Mr. Wright?

Japanese: は、はい？
English: E-excuse me?

Japanese: 私はこのとおり、“やった”と申し上げているのです。
English: As you can plainly see, I am admitting my guilt.

Japanese: この案件を引き受ける‥‥あり得ない選択、と断言できます。
English: I think it's safe to say... there's no way you can take this case. None.

Japanese: お、お姉ちゃん！
English: B-but, Lana!
You... you were always this way, weren't you?

Japanese: お姉ちゃんは、いつも自分のコトしか考えてないんだ。
English: You never think of anyone but yourself.

Japanese: ‥‥‥！...!あたし、知ってるのに。お姉ちゃんは、やってない、って。
English: I know you didn't do it, Lana, I know!

Japanese: なのに‥‥どうして、そんなコトを言うの！
English: So... So how can you say you did!?

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥‥‥...お姉ちゃんがいなくなったら‥‥
English: If I lose you...

Japanese: あたし、ひとりぼっちになっちゃうんだよ‥‥？
English: I'll be all alone!

Japanese: お姉ちゃん‥‥お姉ちゃんなんて、キライ！
English: I... I hate you, Lana!
Mr. Wright?

Japanese: は‥‥はい。
English: Y-yes?

Japanese: どうやら、この面会もここまで、のようですね。
English: I believe our discussion here is ended.

Japanese: あとは‥‥あなたにお任せいたします。
English: The rest... I leave to you.

Japanese: ‥‥！...!それって、つまり‥‥弁護の依頼、ということですか？
English: Um... you mean, you're requesting my services as your defense?

Japanese: むずかしく考えないコト。依頼人は、罪を認めているのです。
English: Don't lose any sleep over it. Your client has confessed, after all. The case is over.

Japanese: ‥‥わかりました。ぼくなりに、調べてみましょう。
English: Right... I'll do what I can to get to the bottom of this.

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥‥‥...（ともえさんは、罪を認めている。しかし‥‥）
English: (Lana has confessed to the crime, yes...)

Japanese: （１つだけ、どうしても気になるコトがあった）
English: (But something doesn't fit.)

Japanese: （あかねちゃんの、あの目‥‥）
English: (It's that look in Ema's eyes.)

Japanese: （何か‥‥この事件にはまだ、見えていない部分がある‥‥！）
English: (There's something else going on here... and I'm going to find out what!)

Japanese: ２月２２日　某時刻上級検事執務室?１２０２号
English: February 22 High Prosecutor's Office Room 1202

Japanese: ‥‥シャレてますね。
English: This... this is quite the place.

Japanese: うん。ニクらしいほどに。
English: You can say that again.

Japanese: でも。でも。成歩堂さんだって、これぐらいできるでしょ？
English: But, you know Mr. Wright, you could do your office up like this too!

Japanese: おカネとセンスさえあれば。
English: All you need is money and a little design sense...

Japanese: ‥‥両方とも、自信のない分野なんだよなあ。
English: I'm not doing so well in either of those areas.

Japanese: とにかく、おルスみたいですね。ここの検事さん。
English: In any case, it looks like the prosecutor is out.

Japanese: また、あとで来てみようか。
English: Let's come back later.

Japanese: なんだ、コレ。《盾》みたいだけど‥‥コワれてるぞ。
English: What's this? It looks like a shield of some kind... It's broken.

Japanese: チョコレートでできていて、少しカジったのかな。
English: Maybe it's made out of chocolate, and he took a bite out of it.

Japanese: どうやら‥‥ブロンズ製に金メッキ、ですね。
English: It appears to be made out of bronze with gold plating.

Japanese: 高めに見つもっても‥‥７０００円ぐらい、かな。
English: It's not worth much. $70 tops.

Japanese: （何かと値段をつけたがるな、この子は）
English: (This girl has a thing about pricing everything, doesn't she?)

Japanese: 《Ｋ》の文字が、なんとなくナゾめいていますよねー‥‥
English: I wonder what that big "K" means? Mysterious...

Japanese: ひゃあ！
English: Whoa!

Japanese: すごいながめですねー。１２階でしたっけ。ここ。
English: What a view! It must be nice to have an office on the 12th floor.

Japanese: たしかに‥‥なんか、エラくなった気分になるね。
English: I guess you would feel... important.

Japanese: ‥‥ちなみに。
English: Incidentally...

Japanese: ここから飛び降りたとき、地面に激突するまでの時間は‥‥
English: Were you to jump out this window, the time until impact with the ground would be...
Got it! Approximately 3.23 seconds!

Japanese: （‥‥だから、なんなんだ）
English: (That's handy to know...)

Japanese: シゴト机だ。さすが、キレイに片づいているなあ。
English: A work desk. It's quite tidy, as one might expect.

Japanese: いいですねー、この机。使いやすいけど、オシャレで。
English: What a nice desk! Easy to use, and easy on the eyes!

Japanese: カオが映るほど、よーくみがきこんでありますよ。
English: It's polished so high I can see my own reflection.

Japanese: ‥‥自分のカオを見つめながらシゴトをしたくないなあ。
English: I'd prefer not to have to look at myself while I work...

Japanese: うわあ‥‥これ、ゼンブ事件のファイルですか！
English: Whoa! These are all case files!?

Japanese: 天井まで、ギッシリ詰まってます！ハシゴもついていますね。
English: They're stacked up to the ceiling! There's even a ladder...

Japanese: ‥‥でも。こんなにギッシリ詰まっていたら‥‥
English: With them already packed in so tight like that...

Japanese: 新しい事件のファイルが増えたらどうするつもりなんだろ。
English: what's he going to do when he gets new case files?

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥‥‥‥‥...さすが、成歩堂さん！着眼点がザンシンですよね！
English: Splendid, Mr. Wright! What an unusual observation!

Japanese: （ホメられた‥‥）
English: (I think that was a compliment...)

Japanese: あ。チェスボードがありますよ！
English: Hey, a chessboard!

Japanese: チェスはよくわからないけど‥‥追いつめられてるね。青いコマが。
English: I'm not too up on my chess but it looks like Blue's in a bit of a tight spot.

Japanese: 赤いナイトたちが、よってたかって青いポーンを取りかこんでいます。
English: The Red Knights have surrounded the Blue Pawn...

Japanese: ‥‥どういうこと？
English: Huh?

Japanese: “ナイト”は“剣を持った騎士”“ポーン”は“歩兵”です。
English: Those horses are mounted knights. Their swords have really sharp "edges!" And check out that poor pawn, his head is kind of spiky... Kind of reminds me of you.

Japanese: （追いつめるのなら、ふつう“王様”じゃないのかな‥‥？）
English: (Yeah, I know, but isn't the point to surround the King?)

Japanese: きゃあ、カワイイ！ステキなティーセットですよ！
English: Oooh! Cute! What a pretty tea set!

Japanese: ‥‥ぼく、ティーバッグ派だから。
English: I go more for the instant tea bags myself.

Japanese: すごい！　下のタナに、茶葉と缶がギッシリ詰まってます！
English: Amazing! The drawer below is filled with packets of tea leaves!

Japanese: 産地別にキチンと並んでますよ。フレバリーも、種類ごとに！
English: They're all sorted by place of origin and flavor!

Japanese: お。このロイヤル、ミゴトなまでに暴力的なブレンディングです！
English: Look at this Royal blend! What an exquisitely splendid concoction!

Japanese: （‥‥シュミって、なんでも奥が深いものなんだなあ‥‥）
English: (There's such a thing as taking a hobby too far...)

Japanese: わあ。ゴーカな花ですねェ。
English: Wow! What an amazing bouquet!

Japanese: たしかにね。‥‥あ。カードが添えてあるぞ。
English: No kidding. Hey, there's a card on it...

Japanese: ‥‥《起死回生　　　カオル》
English: "Back from the Dead -- Wendy"

Japanese: （“カオル”‥‥どこかで聞いたような）
English: ("Wendy?"... I've heard that name somewhere before.)

Japanese: となりにあるのは‥‥巨大なトノサマンですっ！
English: And here beside it... A giant Steel Samurai!

Japanese: うわ、ほしいなあ。
English: Wow, I want one!

Japanese: ‥‥あ。足のウラ、何か書いてあるぞ。
English: Huh? There's something written on the bottom of his foot.

Japanese: ‥‥《青息吐息　　　カオル》
English: "Between a rock and a hard place. -- Wendy"

Japanese: この部屋の検事さんの名前‥‥“カオル”さん、なのかな。
English: Is the prosecutor in this office named "Wendy"?

Japanese: ‥‥たぶん、ちがうと思うよ。
English: Um... I don't think so.

Japanese: きゃあ、スゴくふかふかでキモチいいですよ！
English: Ooh! What a soft and comfy sofa!

Japanese: 座った瞬間、まさに“ソファ～”って感じです！
English: It makes you want to say, "sofa, so good!"
"Sofa"... "So far"...?

Japanese: いちおう、聞こえてるよ。（ビミョーなコトを言う子だな）
English: Yeah, I got it. (I'm glad she's not an aspiring comedienne...)

Japanese: が‥‥額の中に服がかざってありますよ！
English: There's a jacket framed on the wall like a painting!

Japanese: イタリア風の仕立て、最高級のカシミアビーバー織の服地。
English: The fabric is high-grade cashmere... Italian styling...

Japanese: シルクのヒラヒラに、トルコ石の飾りボタンと金糸のエリかざり。
English: Silk ruffles, turquoise buttons, and a gold thread collar.

Japanese: まあ‥‥５０万円ぐらい、でしょうか。
English: I'm guessing it's worth around $5,000.

Japanese: ごご、ごじゅうまん。
English: F-five thousand dollars!?

Japanese: ちなみに、成歩堂さんのスーツは‥‥
English: Speaking of which, your suit would be about...

Japanese: い、いいよ！そんな目で見ないでほしいな。
English: Th-that's enough of that. I don't need my life appraised thank you very much.

Japanese: ２月２２日　某時刻上級検事執務室?１２０２号
English: February 22 High Prosecutor's Office Room 1202

Japanese: いかにも“シゴトできます”って感じの部屋ですよねー。
English: This is the kind of room that just screams "I can do the job."

Japanese: 成歩堂さんの事務所とちがって。
English: Quite a change from your office, really.

Japanese: ‥‥うるさいな。
English: Thanks.

Japanese: ホラ。ホラ。なんか、トロフィーみたいなのがありますよ！
English: Look, look! There's a trophy or something here!

Japanese: （トロフィー‥‥って、あの《盾》か‥‥）
English: (A trophy? What, that shield?)

Japanese: こんなの、うれしそうにかざっちゃって。
English: It takes real nerve to display stuff like this.

Japanese: きっと、権力をハナにかけたイヤらしいヤツに決まってますよ！
English: Whoever's office this is, he must be a real stuck-up jerk!

Japanese: ‥‥成歩堂　龍一‥‥
English: Mr. Phoenix Wright...

Japanese: どこまでも、おせっかいなオトコだな、キミは。
English: You never tire of prying into other people's business, do you?

Japanese: （‥‥この声は‥‥やっぱり）
English: (That voice...!)

Japanese: ひさしぶり‥‥だな、御剣。
English: Long time, no see, Edgeworth.
Huh?

Japanese: あああああああああッ！み、み、御剣検事さん！
English: Aaaaaaaaaaaaah! M-M-Mr. Edgeworth!

Japanese: ‥‥！知ってるの？　コイツのコト。...!
English: You know him from somewhere?

Japanese: え、え‥‥だってあたし、大ファンなんです！
English: O-of course! I'm his biggest fan!

Japanese: お姉ちゃんに紹介されたコトもあるし‥‥いろいろと‥‥
English: My sister introduced us once, and....

Japanese: （そうか‥‥この子、主席検事の妹さんだもんな）
English: (Right... her sister was the Chief Prosecutor, after all.)

Japanese: それで？　なんの用かな。
English: Well? What brings you here?

Japanese: イヤミで権力をハナにかけたイヤらしい検事の部屋に‥‥
English: I'll warn you... I've been known to be a real stuck-up jerk...

Japanese: え！　あ、いえッ！それは、このヒトが勝手に‥‥
English: N-no! Did I...? No! It was just, Mr. Wright here, he...

Japanese: いやいや！ぼくのせいにするなよ。
English: Hey! Don't blame me!

Japanese: あの。あの。あたしたちはただ、殺人事件を調べてるだけなんです！
English: W-we're just here to investigate a murder case!

Japanese: ‥‥殺人事件‥‥？
English: Murder...?

Japanese: ホラ。イヤミでハデで、まっ赤なクルマから、死体が見つかった‥‥
English: A body was found in this nasty, bright red sports car in the parking lot...

Japanese: むう‥‥
English: Hmm?

Japanese: アレなら、私のクルマだが。‥‥それが、なにか？
English: That would be my car. What of it?

Japanese: うえええええええええええええッ！み、み、み、御剣検事さんのォォ！
English: Whaaaaaaaaaaaa!? Y-y-y-your car!?

Japanese: （いっそ、キモチいいぐらいの驚きっぷりだなあ‥‥）
English: (I'll say one thing, she certainly can scream.)

Japanese: ‥‥あのおッ！
スミマセン！　御剣検事ってヒト、いらっしゃるでありますかッ！
English: Excuse me! But is Mr. Edgeworth, uh... anywhere on the premises?

Japanese: ‥‥なんだろうか。私が御剣だが。
English: I'm Edgeworth. What is it?

Japanese: 本官！　警察局長のお使いで本官！報告書を持ってきたでありますッ！
English: I'm here, sir, at the request of the Chief, sir! I've got your report, sir!

Japanese: 報告書、だと‥‥？
English: Report?

Japanese: まさか！　宝月主席検事の件で、新しい手がかりが出たのかッ！
English: What? Did you find new evidence in the case against Chief Prosecutor Skye!?

Japanese: （‥‥目の前で、イヤなハナシをされてるなあ）
English: (I don't like the way this conversation is going at all...)

Japanese: ほーづき、でありますかッ？‥‥いえッ！
English: Er... Skye, sir? No, sir!

Japanese: そんな名前、本官！報告書にはありませんけれどもッ！
English: No name of that kind, sir! Not in this report, sir!

Japanese: ‥‥！...!（うわあ‥‥御剣のカンニン袋のヒモが切れる音が聞こえたぞ）
English: (I think I just heard Edgeworth's lid blow.)

Japanese: ゆ、ユルいですね‥‥御剣検事さんのカンニン袋。
English: Mr. Edgeworth's lid isn't on very tight, is it?

Japanese: 警察局には、言っておいたハズではないかッ！
English: I made a clear request to the Police Department, did I not?

Japanese: 明日の審理に集中したいから、よけいな書類は届けないように！
English: I need to focus on the trial tomorrow, so don't bring me anything unrelated!

Japanese: はッ！　でも。でも、だって本官ッ！
English: Sir! But, but sir!

Japanese: コレを持って行けって言われただけでありますからしてッ！
English: I'm just following orders, sir! They told me to bring this to you!

Japanese: ムズカシイことはコレ本官、わからないのでありますからし
English: I wasn't aware of the particulars of your arrangement with us, sir!

Japanese: ‥‥キサマの名前はッ！
English: Give me your name!

Japanese: あ、あのッ！　本官、あのッ！は、は。は。ハラバイです本官ッ！
English: U-uh, yes... yes, Sir! M-M-Meekins, sir. Officer Meekins!

Japanese: もういい、原灰巡査。報告書を持って、帰りたまえ。
English: Right. Officer Meekins? Take your report and leave.

Japanese: ‥‥来月の給与査定を楽しみにしておくことだ。
English: And good luck with that raise next month.

Japanese: ううう‥‥だって本官‥‥そんな、本官‥‥
English: *whimper* B-but, sir, I d-didn't know!

Japanese: （いるよなー。こういう、アタマも運もよくないヒト‥‥）
English: (Poor guy. Looks like he was absent on the day they gave out brains and good luck.)

Japanese: ‥‥成歩堂。
English: Wright.

Japanese: は、はいッ！　本官！（こういうときのコイツ、怖ェな）
English: Y-yes, sir!? (Gah! He caught me off guard!)

Japanese: 見てのとおり、私もヒマではない。
English: As you can see, I'm busy.

Japanese: ‥‥キミたちもそろそろ、帰ったほうがいいだろう。
English: You may leave now.

Japanese: そ、そうしましょうよ。成歩堂さん！
English: L-let's do what he says, Mr. Wright!

Japanese: 被害者は、捜査官‥‥今の巡査と同じく、警察の人間だ。
English: The victim was a detective from the same department as that patrolman just now.

Japanese: 警察局へ行ってみることだ。被害者のことは、そこで聞ける。
English: Go down to the Police Department. You can ask more there.

Japanese: あ、ああ。‥‥ありがとう。（キゲンが悪くなったみたいだな）
English: U-uh... Thanks. (He seems to have finally calmed down at least.)

Japanese: やっぱり、おまえのクルマだったのか‥‥あれ。
English: So, the body was found in your car?

Japanese: フッ‥‥思ったままを言えばいいだろう、成歩堂。
English: Go ahead, say it, Wright.

Japanese: 去年、せっかく助けてやったオトコが‥‥
English: You think I did it, don't you?

Japanese: ついに、本当に殺人に手を染めたか、と。
English: After you went through all that trouble to help me last year, no less.

Japanese: そ、そんな！御剣検事さんじゃありません！
English: N-no, we don't think you did it!

Japanese: だって、刺したのはあたしのお姉ちゃん‥‥
English: I mean, it was my sister who stabbed him...

Japanese: アレ。ええと、そうじゃなくて‥‥その。
English: Uh, wait, no, she didn't do that! I mean... Wait...

Japanese: あなたは、たしか‥‥主席検事の妹さん、でしたね。
English: So you're the Chief Prosecutor's little sister, then?

Japanese: は、はい！　宝月　茜ですっ！‥‥えーと、あの。
English: Y-yes, sir! Ema Skye! It, uh...

Japanese: そのセツは、ホントにどうも！
English: It's nice to meet you again!

Japanese: （とってつけたようなアイサツだな‥‥）
English: (Now that didn't sound forced at all...)

Japanese: ああ‥‥そうですね。すっかり、見ちがえました。
English: Ah, now I remember. You've... really grown.

Japanese: ‥‥しかし、この私もさすがに予想外だった。
English: I'll admit, it was a surprise to me, too.

Japanese: まさか、自分のクルマで殺人事件が起きようとは。
English: To think that my own car would become the scene of a murder.

Japanese: しかも‥‥
English: More surprising still...

Japanese: この手で、自分の上司の罪を立証することになろうとは。
English: Now I'm forced to prove my superior's guilt.

Japanese: ま。そうだろうなあ‥‥な、なんだって！
English: I can understand... W-wait!... What did you say!?

Japanese: 宝月　巴‥‥主席検事といえば、地方検事局のトップだ。
English: Lana Skye is the Chief Prosecutor... the top prosecutor in the district.

Japanese: ‥‥明日の法廷は、この私が担当することになった。
English: She can't prosecute herself, so I'll be the prosecutor at the trial tomorrow.

Japanese: そ、そんな！御剣検事さんが‥‥
English: You!? Mr. Edgeworth...

Japanese: 正直なトコロ‥‥
English: To be honest...

Japanese: ここにこうしていられるのが、フシギなくらいだ。
English: It's a bit of a miracle I'm still here at all.

Japanese: ‥‥どういうことだ？
English: What do you mean?

Japanese: ウワサ、だ。
English: Rumors.

Japanese: キミも聞いたことがあるだろう。私についてのウワサは。
English: You've heard the rumors about me, haven't you?

Japanese: （御剣　怜侍‥‥）
English: (Miles Edgeworth...)

Japanese: （たしかに、その名声の影では黒いウワサがウズを巻いている）
English: (It's hard to remember a time when there WEREN'T rumors about this guy...)

Japanese: （証拠品のねつ造や、偽証の取引、違法な裏づけ捜査、など‥‥）
English: (Forging evidence, arranging false testimony, illegal searches, you name it...)

Japanese: 年末の、あの事件。キミのおかげで無実が立証されたが‥‥
English: Thanks to you, my innocence was established in the trial at the end of last year.

Japanese: 今回の事件が、私のしわざであるというウワサも流れているようだ。
English: However, there are some who say I'm the one responsible for the current incident.

Japanese: ば、バカ言うなよ‥‥
English: Wh-what? That's crazy!

Japanese: フッ‥‥
English: Hmph.

Japanese: ヒトの悪意というものは、ふとしたキッカケであふれ出す。
English: Some people need very little excuse to think ill of others.

Japanese: それを止めることは、不可能なのだよ。
English: It's a fact of life. Impossible to stop.

Japanese: 御剣検事さん‥‥
English: But...

Japanese: その一方で、こんなオモチャを私に押しつけるヤカラもいる。
English: Some of them even go so far as to present me with toys like this...

Japanese: ‥‥コッケイ、だな。
English: They think it's funny.

Japanese: （ブロンズの盾‥‥か。何かいわくがあるのかな）
English: (Toys? That bronze shield? There's got to be a story behind that one...)

Japanese: 宝月主席検事とは、‥‥２年前。初めていっしょにシゴトをした。
English: Chief prosecutor Skye? Yes...We first worked together on a case two years ago.

Japanese: あれは、私にとっても初めての大きな事件だった。
English: It was my first big case.

Japanese: ‥‥そう、でしたね。
English: That's right, I remember.

Japanese: （２年前‥‥ぼくはまだ弁護士にもなってないな）
English: (Two years ago... I wasn't even a lawyer yet.)

Japanese: それ以来、何かと目をかけてくれている‥‥そう思っていたのだが。
English: Since then, I always felt that she was looking out for me...

Japanese: どうやら、私の思いちがいだったようだ。
English: It appears I was mistaken.

Japanese: お、“思いちがい”って、どうしてですか！
English: M-mistaken!? Why?

Japanese: たしかに、お姉ちゃんはあんな冷たいヒトだけど。
English: I mean, I know she's not the warmest person...

Japanese: きっと、御剣検事さんのコトは‥‥
English: But I'm sure she felt some responsibility for you!

Japanese: ならば‥‥なぜ。
English: Then... why?

Japanese: 彼女は、私のクルマのトランクでヒトを刺したのだろうか？
English: Why did she stab someone in the trunk of my car?

Japanese: しかも‥‥この私のナイフを使って。
English: Not only that... She stabbed him with my knife.

Japanese: ‥‥え！　ええええええッ！御剣検事さんの‥‥ナイフ！
English: Wha... Whaaaaaat!? Mr. Edgeworth! Your knife was the murder weapon!?

Japanese: 事件の凶器は、トランクの私の道具箱に入っていたナイフだ。
English: To be specific, it was the knife kept in the toolbox in the trunk of my car.

Japanese: 《御剣のナイフ》のデータを法廷記録にファイルした。
English: Edgeworth's Knife added to the Court Record.

Japanese: あのさ‥‥御剣。
English: Um... Edgeworth?

Japanese: なんだ。
English: What?

Japanese: もしかして、おまえ‥‥やったんじゃないの？
English: Are you sure you didn't do it?

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥‥‥‥‥‥...（‥‥やれやれ。ジョークのセンスがないヤツだよ）
English: (C'mon, can't he take a joke?)

Japanese: センスがないのは成歩堂さんのほうですッ！
English: You have a strange sense of humor, Mr. Wright!

Japanese: 事件当日‥‥きのうのこと、詳しく聞かせてくれないか？
English: Could you tell me more about yesterday... the day of the murder?

Japanese: きのうは‥‥検事局にとって、年に一度の大そうじがあったのだ。
English: Yesterday was the annual cleaning day at the Prosecutor's Office.

Japanese: 大そうじ‥‥ですか？
English: Cleaning day?

Japanese: 警察局と協力して、決着のついた事件の証拠やデータの整理をする。
English: Working with the Police Department, we sort and file all evidence for solved cases.

Japanese: 我々は、証拠品の《申し送り》と呼んでいるが。
English: We call it "evidence transferal."

Japanese: ふむふむ。たしかに“大そうじ”みたいですね‥‥
English: Wiping your hands of old cases, in other words.

Japanese: そして、もう１つ。警察局で、式典が行われた。
English: Oh, and another thing... A ceremony was held at the Police Department.

Japanese: 今年度の検事?捜査官たちの評価が発表、そして表彰されたのだ。
English: There's an annual review and awards for outstanding police officers and prosecutors.

Japanese: ‥‥ああ。それでこの《盾》をもらった、というワケか。
English: And that's when you got the shield?

Japanese: きのうの午後は、警察局にいた。戻ってきたのは‥‥５時１２分だ。
English: I was at the police department yesterday afternoon... I got back here at 5:12.

Japanese: ‥‥イヤにハッキリ覚えているな。
English: That's... very precise.

Japanese: 御剣検事さんとあたしは、成歩堂さんとはちがいますからね。
English: People like myself and Mr. Edgeworth pride ourselves on our precision, Mr. Wright.

Japanese: ‥‥いや。私はキオクをアテにしないことにしている。
English: No, I place little faith in my memory.

Japanese: このとおり、キチンと証拠があるのだよ。
English: The only thing I trust is solid evidence.

Japanese: 《御剣の駐車記録》のデータを法廷記録にファイルした。
English: Edgeworth's Parking Stub added to the Court Record.

Japanese: 地下駐車場が発行している、利用記録だ。
English: This is the parking stub from the underground lot.

Japanese: （事件が起こったのは、５時１５分ごろ‥‥）
English: (The murder took place around

Japanese: 5:15...)クルマで戻ってすぐに事件は起こったワケかあ。
English: So the murder happened right after you got back.

Japanese: ‥‥なんだ？　成歩堂。そのシツレイな目は‥‥
English: What, Wright? I'd appreciate it if you'd direct that suspicious glare elsewhere.

Japanese: 今は、明日の準備で忙しい。
English: I'm busy preparing for tomorrow.

Japanese: そしてそれ以上に、ムダなおしゃべりをする気分ではない。
English: And I'm not in the mood for idle banter.

Japanese: ‥‥相手がキミとなれば、なおさらだ。
English: Especially not with you.

Japanese: か、帰りましょう！成歩堂さん！
English: L-let's go, Mr. Wright!

Japanese: （やれやれ‥‥キゲンがなおるのを待つとするか）
English: (Fine. I'll just have to talk to him when he perks up.)

Japanese: さっきから気になってたんだけど‥‥なんだ、コレ。
English: I've been wondering... what the heck is this?

Japanese: 《Ｋ》って書いてありますね。
English: It has a big "K" on it.

Japanese: ‥‥オブ?ザ?イヤー‥‥
English: *mumbles* of Prosecutors...

Japanese: え？　なんだって？
English: Huh? What's that?

Japanese: 《検事?オブ?ザ?イヤー》だ。
English: It's the "King of Prosecutors" trophy.

Japanese: けけ、け。け‥‥けんじ?おぶ?ざ?いやー！
English: K-K-K... "King of Prosecutors"!?

Japanese: その年度でイチバン優秀な検事に贈られる、名誉ある像だ。
English: It's a great honor. They send that shield to the best prosecutor each year.

Japanese: ‥‥悪いかッ！
English: What! So?

Japanese: も‥‥もしかして、この《Ｋ》って‥‥
English: So that "K"... that's...

Japanese: “けんじ”の《Ｋ》ですか？
English: "K" stands for "King"?

Japanese: うるさいッ！
English: Yeah, you got a problem with that?

Japanese: 私がデザインしたワケではないのだッ！
English: I didn't design the thing!

Japanese: ひゃああ。ちょっと恥ずかしいですねー、コレは。
English: "King of Prosecutors"... Kind of like "Employee of the Month," only better!

Japanese: 《検事局賞の盾》のデータを法廷記録にファイルした。
English: King of Prosecutors Trophy added to the Court Record.

Japanese: 検事?オブ?ザ?イヤーの賞品ですね。《盾》のカタチの。
English: So that's the "King of Prosecutors" shield, huh?

Japanese: よかったな、御剣。検事?オブ?ザ?イヤー。
English: Well done, Edgeworth. You must be proud to be the King of Prosecutors!

Japanese: おめでとうございます！検事?オブ?ザ?イヤー。
English: Congratulations! King! Of! Prosecutors!

Japanese: たのむから‥‥何度も言わないでもらえるだろうか。
English: Please, stop saying that.

Japanese: （それにしても‥‥）
English: (That still doesn't explain one thing...)

Japanese: （この盾、なんで先っぽが欠けているんだろう‥‥）
English: (Why is the tip of this shield broken off?)

Japanese: シゴト机だ。さすが、キレイに片づいているなあ。
English: A work desk. It's quite tidy, as one might expect.

Japanese: いいですねー、この机。使いやすいけど、オシャレで。
English: What a nice desk! Easy to use, and easy on the eyes!

Japanese: カオが映るほど、よーくみがきこんでありますよ。
English: It's polished so well I can see my own reflection.

Japanese: （‥‥なぜだろう。イトノコ刑事のカオが浮かんだぞ‥‥）
English: (Strange... Why did I just picture Detective Gumshoe?)

Japanese: あのネームプレート、おみやげに持って帰ろうかなあ。
English: Maybe I'll take that name plaque as a souvenir.

Japanese: やめたほうがいいよ。起訴される。
English: Don't. He'll sue you.

Japanese: うわあ‥‥これ、ゼンブ事件のファイルですか！
English: Whoa! These are all case files!?

Japanese: 天井まで、ギッシリ詰まってます！ハシゴもついていますね。
English: They're stacked up to the ceiling! There's even a ladder...

Japanese: ‥‥おかしいな。アイツ、地震がニガテなはずだから‥‥
English: Odd...

Japanese: こんな危なっかしい本棚を使うとは思えないんだけど。
English: I thought Edgeworth wasn't good with heights.

Japanese: だいじょうぶです！
English: He must have someone get them for him.

Japanese: ものすごく大きなボルトで、カベにメリこむほど固定されています！すごいパワーだなあ。御剣検事さんがやったのかなあ。（‥‥なぜだろう。イトノコ刑事のカオが浮かんだぞ‥‥）
English: (Strange... Why did I just picture Detective Gumshoe?)

Japanese: でもさ。地震が起こったら、部屋中にファイルの雨が降るね。だいじょうぶです！
English: He must study these case reports so closely... He's so cool!

Japanese: ギュウギュウに詰まってますから、ちょっとやそっとじゃ抜けません！‥‥‥‥‥‥‥‥やっぱり、なんかおかしくない？　それ。
English: You wouldn't say that if you saw him sweating bullets up on that ladder.

Japanese: あ。チェスボードがありますよ！
English: Hey, a chessboard!

Japanese: チェスはよくわからないけど‥‥追いつめられてるね。青いコマが。
English: I'm not too up on my chess but it looks like Blue's in a bit of a tight spot.

Japanese: 赤いナイトたちが、よってたかって青いポーンを取りかこんでいます。
English: The Red Knights have surrounded the Blue Pawn...

Japanese: ‥‥どういうこと？
English: Huh?

Japanese: “ナイト”は“剣を持った騎士”“ポーン”は“歩兵”です。
English: Those horses are mounted knights. Their swords have really sharp "edges!" And check out that poor pawn, his head is kind of spiky... Kind of reminds me of you.

Japanese: 赤い“剣”が、青い“歩”を追いつめている‥‥御剣検事さん、チェス好きかあ。‥‥どうしました？　成歩堂さん。
English: Mr. Edgeworth must be an avid chess player! What's wrong, Mr. Wright?

Japanese: （気のせいだよな、きっと）
English: (Edges... surrounding a pawn with spiky hair... Nah... It's nothing.)

Japanese: わあ。ゴーカな花ですねェ。御剣検事さんに、ピッタリ！
English: My, my, my! What an amazing bouquet! Just right for Mr. Edgeworth.

Japanese: たしかにね。‥‥あ。カードが添えてあるぞ。
English: No kidding. Hey, there's a card on it...

Japanese: ‥‥《起死回生　　　カオル》
English: "Back from the Dead -- Wendy"

Japanese: （“カオル”‥‥どこかで聞いたような）
English: ("Wendy?"... I've heard that name somewhere before.)

Japanese: となりにあるのは‥‥巨大なトノサマンですっ！
English: And beside it... A giant Steel Samurai!

Japanese: うわ、ほしいなあ。御剣検事さん、マニアなのかなあ。
English: Wow, I want one!

Japanese: イヤだな、それ。‥‥あ。足のウラ、何か書いてあるぞ。
English: Huh? There's something written on the bottom of his foot.

Japanese: ‥‥《青息吐息　　　カオル》
English: "Between a rock and a hard place -- Wendy"

Japanese: カオルさんって‥‥御剣検事さんのフィアンセ、ですか？
English: Wendy? Is she Mr. Edgeworth's fiancee?

Japanese: ‥‥たぶん、ちがうと思うよ。
English: Um... I don't think so.

Japanese: さすが、御剣検事さん。ふかふかですよ、ソファ！
English: Mr. Edgeworth has such a comfy sofa!

Japanese: こんなリッパなソファ、座った瞬間に眠くなるよ。
English: Sofas like this make me want to curl up and take a nap.

Japanese: きっと、夜おそくまで資料を調べて、つかれきって‥‥
English: I bet he pours over his case files here until the wee hours of the morning...

Japanese: 上着をぬいで、シャツのソデなんかオシャレにまくっちゃって。
English: Then he takes off his jacket, rolls up his sleeves...

Japanese: ウデまくらで、ついウトウト寝ちゃうんですよねー。
English: And goes to sleep using his arms as a pillow!

Japanese: （彼女の目には、見えているらしいな‥‥オソロシイことに）
English: (I don't believe it. She's actually daydreaming about Edgeworth working...)

Japanese: 寝グセや、ホッペにソファのカドのアトがついちゃって。
English: I bet in the morning he has sofa hair, and little creases in his cheek from the seams!

Japanese: ‥‥カッコいいなあ。
English: He's so cool!

Japanese: ‥‥カッコいいかあ？
English: Sofa-hair is cool!?

Japanese: うわ！　なんか、いちだんとヒラヒラした服ですよ！
English: Wow! This jacket is even lacier than his usual ones!

Japanese: いわゆる“勝負服”かな。御剣検事さんの！
English: This must be his lucky trial jacket!

Japanese: 勝負服、ねえ‥‥。ぼくは見たことないけどな。
English: Lucky jacket, right... I've never seen him wear it.

Japanese: 額に入れてかざっちゃって。ナニか“いわく”がありそう！
English: I'm sure there's a story behind why it's in a frame!

Japanese: ちょっとシツレイして。写真、撮っちゃお！
English: Maybe I'll be naughty... and take a picture!

Japanese: （やれやれ。コーフンぎみだな‥‥）
English: (She's getting way too excited about this.)

Japanese: ‥‥ムカシ、あこがれていた時期があったものだ。
English: I once dreamed of being a defense attorney, a long time ago.

Japanese: え！　御剣検事さんが、弁護士に‥‥？
English: What? You wanted to be a defense attorney, Mr. Edgeworth?

Japanese: しかし、進むべき道が目の前にのびている以上‥‥
English: Yet, my path is laid out clearly before me...

Japanese: 今さら振りかえるわけにはいかないのだろうな。
English: I have no time to reflect on what might have been.

Japanese: 御剣、これなんだけど‥‥
English: Say, Edgeworth, I was wondering about this...

Japanese: あ、成歩堂さん！
English: M-Mr. Wright!

Japanese: ん？　なに？
English: Huh? What?

Japanese: そんなモノ、御剣検事さんに見せていいんですか？
English: Are you sure you should be showing that to Mr. Edgeworth?

Japanese: ‥‥あ。（カクジツに取りあげられるな）
English: Oh. (He'll take it for sure, won't he.)

Japanese: ‥‥はああ。
English: *sigh*

Japanese: ホントは御剣検事さんのミカタになりたいんだけど‥‥
English: I wish I could be on the same side as Mr. Edgeworth...

Japanese: お姉ちゃんが有罪になっちゃうからなあ‥‥
English: But then my sister would be found guilty!

Japanese: （深ぁいタメ息なんかついてるぞ‥‥）
English: (If she sighs any deeper I'm going to start getting depressed...)

Japanese: つまり。今年イチバンだったワケだよな。おまえが。
English: So, in other words, you were the best of the best this year, huh?

Japanese: ‥‥そのニヤニヤ笑いはやめてもらいたいな。
English: You can take that foolish grin elsewhere, Wright.

Japanese: それをもらうために、きのうはシゴトにならなかった。
English: I lost a day of work to receive that travesty.

Japanese: ‥‥？　どうしてですか。
English: Huh? Why's that?

Japanese: そのコワれた盾を受け取るため、警察局の式典に出席していたのだ。
English: I had to go to the Police Department ceremony to receive that broken shield.

Japanese: 警察局‥‥？
English: The Police Department?

Japanese: 警察署のトナリにある。署のほうはキミも行ったことがあるだろう。
English: Yes. Right next to the Police Station downtown. You've been there, correct?

Japanese: ああ‥‥イトノコ刑事がいる、あそこか。
English: Where Detective Gumshoe works? Yeah.

Japanese: あの‥‥この《盾》なんですけど。なんでコワれてるんですか？
English: Um, I was wondering something about your shield... Why is it broken?

Japanese: 大したイミはない。‥‥今となっては、ね。
English: What does it matter? I've got more important things to worry about.

Japanese: は、はあ‥‥
English: Oh. Right...

Japanese: （キョーミがないみたいだな、賞とか、そういうモノには‥‥）
English: (He doesn't seem too concerned about his award, for better or for worse.)

Japanese: とにかく‥‥きのうは、検事局が最も忙しい１日だったのだ。
English: Yesterday was a very busy day for the Prosecutor's Office.

Japanese: ‥‥もう少し聞いたほうがいいみたいですね。きのうのコト‥‥
English: Maybe... we should ask him more about yesterday?

Japanese: 弁護士と証拠について語るシュミはない。
English: It's against my policy to discuss evidence with the defense.

Japanese: ‥‥相手がキミとなれば、なおさらだ。
English: Especially with you.

Japanese: キラわれてるんですね、成歩堂さん。
English: He doesn't like you much, does he, Mr. Wright?

Japanese: （そういうコトじゃない）
English: (Nah. With Edgeworth it's never personal... It's all about winning tomorrow.)

Japanese: ２月２２日　某時刻検事局?地下駐車場
English: February 22 Prosecutor's Office Underground Parking Lot

Japanese: ここが、事件のあった駐車場か。
English: So this is the lot where it all happened?

Japanese: まだ、捜査中みたいですね‥‥
English: Looks like they're still investigating...

Japanese: （そういえば、検事局に来るのって、初めてだな‥‥）
English: (Funny that my first visit to the Prosecutor's Office should be like this...)

Japanese: みなさーん、ゴクローさまですッ！
English: Hey everyone! Keep up the good work!

Japanese: こ‥‥こらこら！ナニしてるんだよ！
English: H-hey! What are you thinking?

Japanese: あ、ホラ。あたしも３年後にはシゴト仲間になるわけですから。
English: Well, they are going to be my coworkers three years from now after all.

Japanese: ここはひとつ、ごアイサツを‥‥
English: No harm in saying hello...

Japanese: あのね。弁護士には、現場を捜査するケンリはないんだよ。
English: Actually, there is. You know attorneys aren't supposed to examine crime scenes?

Japanese: あんまりハデに目立つと‥‥
English: I'm trying to not stand out too much, here, see?

Japanese: ヘイ！
English: Hey there!

Japanese: それでジミを気取ってるつもりかい‥‥カウボーイ！
English: You 'specting to go unnoticed here, pardner?

Japanese: か、かうぼーい‥‥？
English: P-Pardner?

Japanese: どうやら‥‥
English: What do we have here?

Japanese: 牧場をはぐれたバンビーナが、おイタをしてるようだよ。
