Japanese: きゃああああああああああああああああああああああああああああッ！
English: NOOOOOOOOOO!!!

Japanese: ‥‥どさっ‥‥
English: *thud*

Japanese: まさか‥‥彼女の証言が、こんな結果をもたらすとは‥‥
English: I never imagined her testimony would lead to this...

Japanese: 被害者のイノチを奪ったのは、証人自身だった‥‥
English: So it was the witness who took the victim's life...

Japanese: そして、それを立証したのも‥‥証人自身！
English: and then proved so with her own testimony!

Japanese: まさに、前代未聞です！
English: This is unprecedented!

Japanese: ‥‥い、今になって‥‥何を言い出すのですか！
English: What... What are you saying!?

Japanese: ‥‥申しわけありません。でも。状況を考えると‥‥
English: I'm sorry, Ms. Skye, but given the circumstances...

Japanese: 罪門検事を殺害したのは、あの青影　丈なのです！
English: Joe Darke murdered Prosecutor Marshall!

Japanese: 妹が‥‥あかねが！その罪を着せられる、なんて‥‥
English: How can you think it was Ema!? How dare you try to pin the crime on her!?

Japanese: ヒニクなコト、だな。
English: Imagine that, coming from you.

Japanese: ！!《２年前、“ねつ造”があった》それを認めたのは、あなた自身だ。
English: If you recall, it was you who admitted to forging evidence two years ago.

Japanese: ‥‥罪門検事の死体が移動させられたのは‥‥
English: The reason you moved Prosecutor Marshall's body...

Japanese: ‥‥宝月　茜の“犯行”をかばうため、だったワケだろうか？
English: was to keep anyone else from finding out about what Ema did, wasn't it?

Japanese: くだらないオシャベリは、もうやめてッ！
English: I assure you, Mr. Edgeworth, I have no idea what you're talking about. If you hope to have anyone believe your insane allegations,

Japanese: そこまで言うのならば‥‥私は、証拠の提示を求めます！
English: I'm afraid you're going to have to have proof. Tell me...

Japanese: 妹が、罪門検事を“殺害”した‥‥その、決定的な証拠をッ！
English: Do you have any conclusive evidence that proves my sister killed Neil Marshall?

Japanese: しょ、証拠‥‥ですか！
English: E-evidence?

Japanese: 今さら、証明できるはずがないでしょうけどね‥‥
English: I'm willing to bet you don't.

Japanese: たしかに‥‥証拠による立証はムズカシイでしょうな。
English: Yes... it certainly would be difficult to prove this with evidence.

Japanese: 証拠がダメならば‥‥“証言”しかない、だろう。
English: If we don't have evidence, then we'll have to rely on testimony.

Japanese: ザンネンですけれど。それもムリ、みたいね。
English: I'm afraid that won't work in this case.

Japanese: 事件の当事者は全員、すでに死んでしまっているのですから。
English: Both parties involved in the incident are dead.

Japanese: ‥‥‥！...!（‥‥今となっては、死者に証言を求めることはできない）
English: (We certainly can't get dead people to testify.)

Japanese: （やっぱり‥‥ムチャなスイリだったのか‥‥！）
English: (This has all been a wild goose chase from the beginning!)

Japanese: ふう‥‥やれやれ。
English: Hmph. Touche, Ms. Skye.

Japanese: そうなれば‥‥もちろん。残る可能性は、１つしかない。
English: Of course... That only leaves us with one possibility.

Japanese: ‥‥！（まだ、あるのか？　可能性が！）...!
English: (You mean, there's still another possibility?)

Japanese: 御剣検事！　それは、いったい‥‥
English: What do you mean, Mr. Edgeworth?

Japanese: 死者が、すでにメッセージを残している、という可能性だ。
English: I mean, the possibility that the victim has left us a message.

Japanese: 幸か不幸か、罪門検事は即死ではなかった。
English: For better or for worse, Mr. Marshall did not die instantly.

Japanese: 自らのイノチを断った人物の名前を、残したかもしれぬ。
English: He may have left behind the name of the person who took his life...

Japanese: ‥‥なんらかの方法で。
English: somehow.

Japanese: そ、そんな、バカげた‥‥あり得ないわ！
English: That's... That's impossible!

Japanese: いかがですかな？　弁護人。これが、最後の可能性です。
English: Well, Mr. Wright? This is the only possibility left to you.

Japanese: 《死者のメッセージ》‥‥そのようなものが、存在するのか？
English: A message from the deceased... Does such a message exist?

Japanese: （もう一度‥‥法廷記録をよく思い出してみるんだ！）
English: (I've got to think back to the Court Record!)

Japanese: 被害者が残したかもしれない、《真犯人の名前》は‥‥
English: The real murderer's name that the victim may have left behind...

Japanese: （ダメだ‥‥）
（死者が、ハンニンの名前を語るなんて‥‥考えられない！）
English: (There's no way a dead person could tell the murderer's name!)

Japanese: どうやら‥‥ここまで、のようですな。
English: Well. It looks like this is as far as we can go with this.

Japanese: 成歩堂　龍一‥‥
English: Mr. Wright.

Japanese: やはり‥‥まだまだ、だな。感情に目をくもらされるとは。
English: You disappoint me. I never thought you the type to let feelings cloud your judgment.

Japanese: 感情に‥‥？
English: My feelings...?

Japanese: 最後のチカラをふりしぼって残された、死者のコトバ。
English: If we overlook the victim's message...

Japanese: 我々が読みとらなければ‥‥すべては闇の中なのだッ！
English: one he would have written with literally his last breath, then everything will be lost in darkness!

Japanese: （やはり‥‥“あれ”は手がかりだったのか‥‥）
English: (Perhaps that thing really is the clue I'm looking for.)

Japanese: （‥‥どうやら）
English: (This is it.)

Japanese: （今さら、目をそらすことはユルされないみたいだな）
English: (I can't afford any more mistakes.)

Japanese: 死者の残した“メッセージ”‥‥我々はすでに、手にしています。
English: This "message" from the deceased... is already in our possession.

Japanese: ‥‥成歩堂弁護士！
English: Mr. Wright!

Japanese: そこまでして‥‥あなたは、妹を殺人者にしたいのですか！
English: Will you stop at nothing to prove my sister a murderer?

Japanese: ゴカイしてもらっては困るな、主席検事殿。
English: Do not be mistaken, Ms. Skye.

Japanese: ‥‥‥！...!我々は、宝月　茜に、罪を着せようとしているのではない。
English: Our purpose is not to accuse Ema of any crime.

Japanese: わかっているはずだ。我々のシゴトは、ただ１つ。
English: There is only one thing we seek:

Japanese: ‥‥《真実》を見つけだす。
English: the truth.

Japanese: それが、どんなにツラいものであっても‥‥だ。
English: No matter how painful it may be.

Japanese: ‥‥それでは、弁護人。証拠の提示を求めます。
English: Now then, Mr. Wright.

Japanese: 死者の残した《メッセージ》‥‥それを示す、証拠品とはッ！
English: Please show us the piece of evidence... that conveys a message from the deceased!

Japanese: これこそが、死者からのメッセージですッ！
English: This is the message from the deceased!

Japanese: ‥‥なるほど。わかりました。
English: Right...

Japanese: それでは。これが、私からのメッセージです。
English: Now then. This is the message from the judge!

Japanese: うううう‥‥（どうやら、ちがったらしいな）
English: Oh... (I guess that wasn't right.)

Japanese: （死者がメッセージを残すとしたら、その“方法”は‥‥）
English: (If a dead person left behind a message...

Japanese: （何かに“書き残した”‥‥そう考えるのが、自然だろうな）
English: it would have to be in written form. That's the only logical conclusion.)

Japanese: キサマ自身が死者にならぬよう、メッセージには気をつけるコトだ。
English: You'd better be careful, or you might wind up deceased yourself.

Japanese: ‥‥もう一度、うかがいましょう。
English: Let's try this again.

Japanese: これこそが、死者からのメッセージですッ！
English: This is the message left by the deceased.

Japanese: これは‥‥先ほどの《タイホくん》ですね？
English: This is that "Blue Badger" from before, right?

Japanese: カレが、ハンニンの名を語る、というコトかしら？
English: Oh, is he going to just speak the killer's name?

Japanese: ‥‥そのカイブツに、コトバがあるものなら、な。
English: If that thing could, I'm sure it would.

Japanese: （‥‥みんな、コレがツボに見えなくなっているみたいだな）
English: (Looks like everyone's forgotten this is just a jar.)

Japanese: メッセージは残されていました。‥‥このツボの、表面に。
English: A message was left here... on the surface of this jar.

Japanese: どういうことですかな？
English: What do you mean?

Japanese: このツボには、かすかに血のアトが残っています。
English: If you look closely, you can see a faint trail of blood on this jar.

Japanese: どうやら‥‥ダレかにふき取られたようにも見えるが。
English: It looks like someone wiped the blood away.
Yes, but notice:

Japanese: カケラの一部分だけは、なぜか血がふきとられずに残っています。
English: for some reason... the blood on some of the fragments was not wiped away.

Japanese: たしかに‥‥アキラカに血で描かれた“線”があります！
English: Yes... there is a line here... drawn in blood!

Japanese: つまり‥‥こういうコト、か。
English: So what you're saying,

Japanese: これらの《点》は‥‥かつては《線》だった。
English: is these "dots"... were once lines!

Japanese: ‥‥罪門検事は、即死ではありませんでした。
English: Prosecutor Marshall did not die instantly.

Japanese: だから、自らの血を使ってメッセージを残すことができた。
English: He used the few precious moments left to him to leave behind a message!

Japanese: 何者かが、ぬぐい去ろうとしたようだが‥‥
English: One that someone apparently wiped away...

Japanese: “線”が折れる部分だけは、ツボに深くしみこんでしまったのだろう。
English: But blood must have seeped into the jar where the lines change directions.

Japanese: ‥‥つまり！その点をつなげれば‥‥
English: Precisely so! All we need to do is connect these points...

Japanese: 死者のメッセージが浮かび上がってくるのです！
English: and the victim's message will become apparent!

Japanese: な‥‥‥なんてこと‥‥‥
‥‥弁護人！
English: Mr. Wright!

Japanese: 被害者は、どんなメッセージを我々に残していたのですかッ！
English: What kind of message did the victim leave for us!?

Japanese: それは‥‥この《血痕》が語ってくれるでしょう！
English: Your Honor... I believe these blood stains will reveal to us the answer!

Japanese: （“点”と“点”をつないで、文字を作るんだ‥‥）
English: (I've got to connect these dots to make letters.)

Japanese: （あの状況で、被害者が書き残すとすれば‥‥それは、１つ！）
English: (There's only one thing the victim would have written, given the circumstances:)

Japanese: （‥‥“ハンニンの名前”しかないはずだ！）
English: (The murderer's name!)

Japanese: ‥‥いかがですか！こんなのが書けましたッ！
English: What do you think!? I came up with this message!

Japanese: そうですな‥‥
English: Hmm... Yes...

Japanese: ダメな生徒を持った、お習字教室のセンセイの気分です。
English: I feel like I'm teaching handwriting to a student who is all thumbs...

Japanese: 《もうしません》と１００回書くことをオススメしよう。
English: I think you should make him write out "I won't do it again" 100 times.

Japanese: （ニガテなんだよな、漢字。法廷記録、見てみるか‥‥）
English: (I never was much good at handwriting... I guess I'll check the court record...)

Japanese: （依頼人の無実を立証するのが、弁護士たちの任務だ）
English: (It's a defense attorney's duty to prove his client's innocence.)

Japanese: （だから‥‥ともえさんを救うことだけ、考えてきた）
English: (That's why all I've been thinking about is saving Lana.)

Japanese: （その結果、たどりついたコタエが‥‥‥これなのかッ！）
English: (After all my efforts, I never thought it would turn out like this!)

Japanese: ‥‥これが、罪門検事が残した最後のコトバ、というワケか‥‥
English: So this is the final message Prosecutor Marshall left behind.

Japanese: なんというコトでしょう‥‥
English: Of all people...

Japanese: 殺意はなかった。‥‥しかし。
English: She may not have meant it... but in the end, the one who took the victim's life...

Japanese: 結果的に、被害者のイノチをうばったのは‥‥宝月　茜さん、だったのです。
English: was Ema Skye.

Japanese: だから言ったでしょ？‥‥御剣ちゃん。
English: See, Worthy? Can't say I didn't warn you.

Japanese: が‥‥‥巌徒局長‥‥
English: Chief Gant...

Japanese: キミさ。わかってる？自分のやったコトの、イミ。
English: Do you understand the implications of what you've done?

Japanese: ど‥‥‥どういうコトだろうか‥‥？
English: What...? What are you talking about?

Japanese: ２年前の、あの事件。青影　丈って、処刑されたでしょ？
English: Two years ago, Joe Darke was sentenced to death.

Japanese: 裁かれたのは、カレの最後の犯行。検事は、たしか‥‥キミ、だっけ？
English: He was convicted because of his final murder. I believe you were the prosecutor in the case, were you not?

Japanese: ぐ‥‥ッ！
English: Ack!

Japanese: ‥‥そうなのよ、御剣ちゃん。
English: Yes, Worthy.

Japanese: キミ。無実のオトコをさ。チョイと処刑させちゃったワケ。
English: Because of you... an innocent man was sentenced to death.

Japanese: しかも‥‥。ねつ造された証拠を使って、ねェ‥‥
English: Not only that... but you used forged evidence to ensure his conviction!

Japanese: ぐ‥‥‥ぐッ！ぐおおおおおおおおおおおおおッ！
English: K-kkckck! KKCKRRAAAAHHHH!!!

Japanese: 青影　丈は、連続殺人犯だった！それは、たしかなハズでしょう！
English: But Joe Darke really was a serial murderer! That's undeniable!

Japanese: そんなコトはね。モンダイじゃないの。
English: I'm afraid that's not important.

Japanese: 知ってた？　ボクたち、正義の味方じゃないんだよ。
English: Didn't you know? We aren't defenders of justice.
What?

Japanese: ボクたちはね。法の番人なワケ。
English: We're merely keepers of the law.

Japanese: ひとりのオトコを処刑する‥‥それって、タイヘンなコトよね。
English: Sentencing a man to death... is no light matter.

Japanese: 現場に“ねつ造”があろうと“インペイ”があろうと‥‥
English: Even if there wasn't any cover-up or evidence forgery...

Japanese: 最終的なセキニンは、担当した検事にあるのよ。
English: ultimately the responsibility falls on the prosecutor in charge.

Japanese: ‥‥‥！!これだけはね。動かせないジジツなのさ！
English: Despite what anyone may say, this fact cannot be denied.

Japanese: ‥‥検事局って、いったいどうなってるんだよ！
English: What's going on at the Prosecutor's Office!?

Japanese: ‥‥ムジツのヤツが処刑されたかもしれないんだぞ！
English: They might have sent an innocent man to his death!

Japanese: ‥‥あのヒト、どうして平気なカオ、してられるの！
English: How can he just stand there, like it wasn't his fault!?

Japanese: 静粛に！　静粛に！　静粛に！静粛にィィィィィィィィィッ！
English: Order! Order! Order! ORDEEEEERRRRRR!!!

Japanese: ‥‥法廷内に、むなしく木槌の音がひびきわたった‥‥
English: The gavel's pounding fell on deaf ears...

Japanese: ‥‥さわぎはおさまらず、審理は一時、中断された‥‥
English: Unable to settle the crowd, the judge declared a recess.

Japanese: ‥‥法廷のゆくえは‥‥もう、ダレにもわからない‥‥
English: Where this trial is headed, no one knows...

Japanese: つづく
English: To be continued.

Japanese: そこまで！
English: That's enough! This court sees no reason to further prolong this trial.

Japanese: 本法廷は、これ以上の審議の必要性を認めません。さらに、被告人に対する判決を決める時間も、必要としません。
English: The defense has failed to give the court sufficient reason to doubt the prosecution's claim!

Japanese: これは、きわめて明白な事件です。疑問の余地はない！被告人、宝月　巴にたった今、判決を言いわたします！
English: This court finds the defendant, Ms. Lana Skye...

Japanese: 被告人はこれより身柄を司直に預けるものとし、
English: The accused will surrender to the court immediately,

Japanese: １ヶ月以内に、高等裁判所において通常裁判を受けることとします。
English: to be held pending trial at a higher court within a month from today's date.

Japanese: では、本日はこれにて閉廷！
English: That is all. The court is adjourned!

Japanese: 同日　午後１２時　６分地方裁判所　被告人第２控え室
English: February 25, 12:06 District Court Defendant Lobby No. 2

Japanese: ‥‥すまなかったな、御剣。
English: Sorry, Edgeworth.

Japanese: こんなところで、おまえ自身も知らなかった罪を暴いてしまって。
English: I didn't mean to get you in trouble...

Japanese: フッ‥‥かまわないさ。
English: Hmph. Don't worry about it.

Japanese: これは、私自身のモンダイなのだから、な。
English: This is my problem, not yours.

Japanese: シツレイするッス！
English: Hope I'm not interrupting anything, pals.
Oh... Guess I am. I'll come back later.

Japanese: あ‥‥イトノコ刑事！どうしたんですか？
English: Oh, Detective Gumshoe! What is it?

Japanese: アンタ！　まったく、シツレイしちゃうッス！
English: You've got a lot of nerve, pal! Making a detective run all around while on duty,

Japanese: 現職の刑事に使い走りさせた上、来てみれば、この空気！
English: and to top it off you call me here... I've seen happier people at funerals!

Japanese: ま、また、お使いですか。ともえさんの。
English: I take it Lana's having you run errands again.

Japanese: 今日の法廷が長びいて‥‥
English: Let me tell you, this is the last time, pal!

Japanese: １回でも、休憩を挟むコトになったら、アンタにわたせ、と。
English: Here. She asked me to give this to you if there was a break in today's trial.

Japanese: ‥‥“しょうこほう”？（たしか、２日前‥‥）
English: "Evidence Law"? (Edgeworth was talking about this just the other day...)

Japanese: 法廷における《証拠法》の２大原則‥‥知らないわけではあるまい。
English: You must know the two rules of evidence law!

Japanese: 《その１：警察局の認可のない証拠品の提示は認められない》
English: Rule 1: no evidence shall be shown without the approval of the Police Department!

Japanese: そ、そうなんですか！成歩堂さん！
English: I-is that right, Mr. Wright!?

Japanese: た‥‥たぶんね。
English: It seems so.

Japanese: もう！　《証拠法》ぐらいベンキョウしておきましょうよ！
English: You could at least study some evidence law! Really!

Japanese: ‥‥それから、もうひとつ。主席検事殿から、伝言があるッス。
English: The Chief Prosecutor also wanted me to give you a message.

Japanese: 伝言‥‥？
English: A message?

Japanese: 《あのヒトと戦うつもりならば‥‥この本が、きっと必要になる》
English: She said, "If you're planning to take HIM on, you're going to need this book."

Japanese: （“あのヒト”‥‥か）
（この本‥‥よく目をとおしておいたほうがいいな‥‥）
English: (I guess I'll need to give this book a thorough read...)

Japanese: ともえさんの《証拠法入門》をポケットにネジこんだ。
English: Securely slipped Evidence Law into pocket.

Japanese: でも‥‥今さら、必要ないかもしれないッスね。
English: Doesn't look like that book'll do you any good now though.

Japanese: もう、今度こそ‥‥主席検事殿に対する、判決しか残ってないッス。
English: All that's left now is the Chief Prosecutor's sentence.

Japanese: ‥‥それは、ちがうと思いますよ。イトノコ刑事。
English: That's where you're wrong, Detective.
Huh?

Japanese: ‥‥刑事。わからないのか？なぜ、この私が‥‥
English: Haven't you figured it out yet?

Japanese: ハジをさらしながらも、あの検事席にしがみついているのか‥‥
English: Why I'm still sitting in that prosecutor's seat... despite all these allegations being thrown at me?

Japanese: 御剣検事‥‥
English: Mr. Edgeworth...

Japanese: 本日の、本当の審理は‥‥ここから始まるのだ。
English: The real trial today... hasn't begun yet.

Japanese: でも！　この先に、いったい何があると言うッスかッ！
English: What!? What else is there left to do?

Japanese: 御剣検事は、やってもいない“ねつ造”でキズつけられて‥‥
English: Your credibility's been all but ruined with this forged evidence you were unaware of,

Japanese: 宝月　茜も、自分でも知らなかった“死”を思い知らされて‥‥
English: Ema Skye found out she unwittingly caused a man's death...

Japanese: アンタ！　アンタはいったい、何がしたいッスかッ！
English: And now you're telling me you want to do more!? You've gotta be kidding me, pal!

Japanese: だって‥‥‥
English: You're missing the point, Detective.

Japanese: ともえさんは、多田敷捜査官を殺害していませんからね。
English: Lana didn't murder Detective Goodman.

Japanese: ‥‥‥！...!彼女は‥‥被害者の死体にナイフを刺しただけ、なんです。
English: She merely stuck a knife into his dead body.

Japanese: つまり‥‥他にいるんですよ。《真犯人》が。
English: That means the real killer... is still out there.

Japanese: なんスッて‥‥！
English: What!?

Japanese: （そいつを引きずり出す‥‥かならず！）
English: (And we're going to expose him...)

Japanese: （みんなをキズつけた、この事件に決着をつけるために！）
English: (No matter what it takes!) (This case has hurt too many people. It's time to bring it to an end!)

Japanese: 同日　午後１２時５２分地方裁判所　第９法廷
English: February 25, 12:52 District Court Courtroom No. 9

Japanese: それでは、審理を再開します。あの‥‥御剣検事。
English: The court will now reconvene for the trial of Ms. Lana Skye. Mr. Edgeworth.

Japanese: なんだろうか。
English: Yes, Your Honor?

Japanese: あなたに対して、査問委員会はキビシイ処置をのぞんでいます。
English: The inquiry committee is planning to impose harsh penalties for your actions.

Japanese: それは、どうも‥‥
English: Thank you for the news, Your Honor.

Japanese: えーと。それで、その。通常ならば、あの。
English: Yes, well... *a-hem*

Japanese: これから、証人を呼んでいただくワケですが‥‥
English: Normally, this is where the prosecution calls forth a witness... but, er... *a-hem* *cough* *cough*

Japanese: マコトに言いにくいのですが。あなたが‥‥アレじゃないかと。
English: This isn't easy to say... You see, there is some concern that Mr. Edgeworth may have, ah...

Japanese: トリヒキ‥‥かな？
English: Struck a bargain?

Japanese: ‥‥！...!私が、証人を飼いならしているかもしれない、というワケだ。
English: You think I may have manipulated the witnesses.

Japanese: 私が言ったワケじゃありませんぞ！なんというか、みんながですね。
English: I didn't say that! It's just, you see... Everyone has been talking, and...
Very well, Your Honor.

Japanese: 私のほうから１つ、提案したいことがある。
English: I have a solution.

Japanese: な。なんでしょうかな？
English: A solution?

Japanese: 本日の証人の喚問を、すべて‥‥弁護側にゆだねよう。
English: That being the case, the prosecution will allow the defense to call forth all further witnesses.

Japanese: なんですとッ！しかし、それは‥‥
English: What!? But there's never been a case example...

Japanese: もちろん、トクベツな処置だ。しかし、それで証明できる。
English: Undeniably this is an unusual arrangement... but a very effective one.

Japanese: その証人が“トリヒキ”などしていない、というコトが‥‥
English: It would prove that I haven't struck any "deals" with the witnesses.

Japanese: ふむう‥‥いかがですかな？　弁護人。
English: Hmm... Well, Mr. Wright? What do you say?

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥‥‥（アイツが、道を作ってくれた）...
English: (Unbelievable.)

Japanese: （これが‥‥審理をつづけるための、１本しかない道だ！）
English: (Edgeworth has found a way to continue the trial!)

Japanese: わかりました。弁護側は、その提案を受け入れます。
English: Very well. The defense accepts the prosecution's proposal.

Japanese: それでは‥‥弁護人。
English: Then it's settled.

Japanese: 特例ということで、証人を呼んでいただきましょう。
English: The, uh... defense... may now call forth the next witness!

Japanese: わかっているな‥‥成歩堂。
English: Mr. Wright.

Japanese: ‥‥！...!これは、最後のチャンスだ。まちがった証人を選んだ時点で‥‥
English: You do realize this is your last chance? If you call the wrong witness...

Japanese: 審理は、終わる。
English: this trial is as good as over.

Japanese: ‥‥それでは、証人の喚問を要請します。
English: The defense calls...

Japanese: （今こそ‥‥《真犯人》を引きずり出すんだ！）
English: (The time's finally come to bring out the real murderer!)

Japanese: 見そこなったぞ‥‥成歩堂　龍一。
English: It appears I've overestimated you, Mr. Wright.
Huh?

Japanese: 一瞬とはいえ、キサマを信じようとした、私が愚かだったようだ。
English: For a moment, I actually thought you knew what you were doing. Hmph!

Japanese: 弁護人！　ここまで来て、テキトーはユルされませんぞッ！
English: Mr. Wright! This court has long since tired of your questionable antics!

Japanese: （今はまだ、決定的な証拠はない。でも‥‥）
English: (I still don't have any concrete evidence,)

Japanese: （今までの流れを考えれば‥‥もっとアヤシイ人物がいる！）
English: (but judging by the direction everything seems to be pointing...) (There's another person even more suspicious!)

Japanese: ‥‥巌徒　海慈氏を。証人として、入廷させてください。
English: Damon Gant. The defense calls Damon Gant to the stand!

Japanese: が、巌徒　海慈‥‥いったいなぜ、その名前がッ！
English: D-Damon Gant...? What does he have to do with anything!?

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥‥‥‥‥...巌徒氏は２年前まで、被告人とともに捜査官をつとめていました。
English: As the defendant's partner two years ago,

Japanese: ２年前の事件の関係者でもある。‥‥ハナシを聞くべきと考えます。
English: Mr. Gant has first-hand knowledge of the crime. I feel we should hear what he has to say about it.

Japanese: ふ、ふむう‥‥‥
English: Hmm...

Japanese: さいわい、彼はまだ裁判所にいるはずだ。そして‥‥
English: As luck would have it, he should still be in the courthouse.

Japanese: この世で最も“トリヒキ”から遠い場所にいる人物。
English: He would also be the least likely to have been manipulated by me in any way.

Japanese: ‥‥そうではないかな？
English: Wouldn't you agree, Your Honor?

Japanese: わかりました。
English: True...

Japanese: ‥‥それでは、係官！巌徒　海慈氏を証人席へ！
English: All right. Bailiff! Please escort Mr. Gant to the stand!

Japanese: ‥‥証人。名前と、職業をおねがいする。
English: Witness. Please state your name and occupation.

Japanese: あのさー。なんなのかな、コレって。
English: What is this, some kind of practical joke?

Japanese: ボク、おヒル食べに行こうと思ってたんだけどな。
English: I was just on my way to lunch!

Japanese: ‥‥名前と、職業をおねがいする。
English: Your name and occupation, sir.

Japanese: 御剣ちゃんさあ。こんなコトして、どうなるか‥‥
English: Worthy... Are you sure you want to do this?

Japanese: ‥‥名前と、職業だ。
English: Your name and occupation!

Japanese: ふうん‥‥そう来るんだ。
English: So... You want to play hardball, eh?

Japanese: お、おねがいします、局長。
English: P-please, Mr. Gant.
Fine.

Japanese: 巌徒　海慈。警察局の局長だよ。よろしくね。
English: My name is Damon Gant. I'm the acting Chief of Police.

Japanese: ‥‥それでは、巌徒局長。証言をおねがいします。
English: Now then, Chief Gant. The court requests to hear your testimony.

Japanese: お。ナルホドちゃん。どうしたの？　そんなカオして。
English: Oh, Wrighto. What's with the grim face?

Japanese: まずは‥‥《ＳＬ９号事件》をハッキリさせましょう。
English: First, let's clear up this SL-9 Incident.

Japanese: ああ。トモエちゃんの妹ちゃんが殺しちゃった、アレ？
English: Oh, you mean that time when Lana's sister murdered that prosecutor?

Japanese: もうハッキリしたんじゃないの？いいと思うな、ボク。アレでさ。
English: Personally, I think it's been made pretty clear already.

Japanese: ‥‥まだ、うかがっていないコトがあります。
English: There are still some things unaccounted for.

Japanese: ‥‥なんだろ。
English: Oh? Like what?

Japanese: あの事件で、あなたが演じた役割ですよ、モチロン。
English: Like the role you played in all of this.

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥‥‥‥‥‥...キミも‥‥そうとうイノチ知らずだな。
English: Son... Either you're very brave... or very foolish.

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥‥...知ってる？　警察局長には、いろんな武器があるって。
English: You are aware of course that a police chief has all kinds of weapons at his disposal?

Japanese: “ぶき”‥‥ですか？
English: "Weapons"...?

Japanese: たとえば‥‥こんな証言、キョヒしたっていいワケだし。
English: Sure. Take my testimony, for example. I don't have to give it if I don't want to.

Japanese: え！　そ。そうなんですか？
English: What? Is that true?

Japanese: たしかに‥‥警察局長には、《証言の拒否権》があります。
English: I'm afraid so... The Chief of Police has the right to refuse to testify.

Japanese: ‥‥モチロン、キョヒをすればそれなりのキケンもあるワケだが。
English: Of course, such an action carries with it certain risks...

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥‥‥‥‥‥...やだなー。ナニもボク、協力しない、なんて言ってないよ。
English: Don't worry. I'm not here to hinder your trial.

Japanese: ‥‥ただ。
English: Just remember...

Japanese: くだらない証言はゴメンだ、と。それが言いたいだけでね。
English: If this turns out to be a big waste of time, don't say I didn't warn you.

Japanese: それでは、巌徒局長！‥‥証言をおねがいします！
English: Very well. The witness may now begin his testimony.

Japanese: ～《ＳＬ９号事件》～
English: -- SL-9 Incident --

Japanese: あの日‥‥ボクと罪門ちゃん、カレの取り調べをしてたんだけど。
English: As I recall, Neil and I were questioning him that day.

Japanese: ドジ、やっちゃって。しかも、カミナリで停電でしょ？
English: To make a long story short, we slipped up. That power outage didn't help either.

Japanese: ボクがオフィスに行ったら、トモエちゃんがいてさ。
English: When I went to my office, I found Lana there.

Japanese: そのとき、すでに現場には、手が加えられてたんだね。
English: Apparently she had already... "arranged" the crime scene.

Japanese: ハッキリしてるよ。“ねつ造”なんて、ボクは関わってないもん。
English: As you can see, I had nothing to do with the "forgery."

Japanese: ふむう‥‥‥そこで、青影はタイホされたのですな？
English: Hmm... Is that when Darke was arrested?

Japanese: カレね。キゼツしていたワケ。
English: Him? He was lying on the floor unconscious.

Japanese: 妹ちゃんがナオトちゃんをつきとばしたとき‥‥
English: When Ema sent Neil flying... it seems Darke bumped his head.

Japanese: 青影ちゃんも、アタマを打ったみたいでさ。ナルホド‥‥。やはり、アキラカのようですな。
English: I see... Everything seems pretty clear-cut.

Japanese: （警察局長には、証言の拒否権があるみたいだ）
English: (If the police chief has the right to refuse to testify...)

Japanese: （‥‥一気にたたきつぶすしかない！）
English: (Then I'd better hit him hard and fast!)

Japanese: ～《ＳＬ９号事件》～
English: -- SL-9 Incident --

Japanese: あの日‥‥ボクとナオトちゃん、カレの取り調べをしてたんだけど。
English: As I recall, Neil and I were questioning him that day.

Japanese: ドジ、やっちゃって。しかも、カミナリで停電でしょ？
English: To make a long story short, we slipped up. That power outage didn't help either.

Japanese: ボクがオフィスに行ったら、トモエちゃんがいてさ。
English: When I went to my office, I found Lana there.

Japanese: そのとき、すでに現場には、手が加えられてたんだね。
English: Apparently she had already... "arranged" the crime scene.

Japanese: ハッキリしてるよ。“ねつ造”なんて、ボクは関わってないもん。
English: As you can see, I had nothing to do with the "forgery."

Japanese: （たしかに‥‥ともえさんは“ねつ造”のジジツを認めた）
English: (Lana did admit to forging evidence...)

Japanese: （‥‥しかし。真相は、それだけじゃないハズ‥‥）
English: (but that can't be the whole truth.)

Japanese: （‥‥なんとかして、アイツを事件に結びつけるんだ！）
English: (Somehow I've got to link Gant to the incident!)

Japanese: ２年前の、その日‥‥警察局では式典があったハズですね？
English: As I recall... a ceremony was held at the Police Department that day...

Japanese: ま。シゴト熱心だし。ボク。そもそも、それどころじゃなくて。
English: Yes, that's right. I guess you could say I'm a workaholic...

Japanese: ナオトちゃん、賞をもらってヤル気になっちゃっててさ。
English: After winning his award, Neil was all fired up too.

Japanese: それで、青影ちゃんもコワくなって、逃げたんじゃないかなー。
English: That's probably what spooked Darke, and made him run away like that.

Japanese: そのとき‥‥宝月　巴は、そこにいなかったのだろうか？
English: Was the defendant, Lana Skye, also present in the room?

Japanese: よく、おぼえてないけど‥‥
English: I don't quite remember...

Japanese: カレが逃げ出したときには、取調べ室にはいなかったね。
English: At the very least, she wasn't there when Darke ran for it.

Japanese: あなたたちは、すぐに青影のあとを追ったんですね？
English: So the two of you ran immediately after him, right?

Japanese: ちょうどさ。エレベータに逃げこんだところで。
English: That's right, but Darke made it to the elevator first,

Japanese: ナオトちゃんとね。手分けをしたワケ。
English: so Neil and I split up.

Japanese: カレは上の階、ボクは下の階。‥‥結果的には‥‥
English: He went upstairs and I went downstairs. I guess you could say...

Japanese: カレが“アタリ”をひいちゃったワケだけど。
English: he got "lucky."

Japanese: “停電”というのは‥‥？
English: What's this about a power outage?

Japanese: いやいや。まいっちゃうよね。
English: Oh, that.

Japanese: エレベータが急に止まっちゃって。どうしようかと思ったねー。
English: The elevator stopped all of a sudden, and I got the shock of my life.

Japanese: まあ‥‥
English: Well...

Japanese: そのとき、ナオトちゃんのほうは、心臓が止まっちゃったワケだけど。
English: Probably not as shocked as Neil was when that knife went into his heart, though.

Japanese: （‥‥笑えない）
English: (That's not funny...)

Japanese: ‥‥そのときのようすを聞かせていただけますか？
English: Could you tell us what you saw?

Japanese: アレはひどかったねー。
English: It was a shocking sight...

Japanese: ナオトちゃんと、殺人犯のカレがもつれるように倒れてるワケ。
English: Neil and that serial killer were lying in a heap on the floor, all tangled together.

Japanese: 青影も‥‥倒れていたんですか？
English: Darke was also lying collapsed on the floor?

Japanese: アタマでも打ったんだろうね。キゼツしてたよ。
English: Yes, apparently he hit his head and was knocked out.

Japanese: そして‥‥そのそばで、あの子たちがいたワケ。
English: Next to them were those two poor girls.

Japanese: （‥‥ともえさんと、あかねちゃんか‥‥）
English: (Lana and Ema...)

Japanese: トモエちゃん、必死に妹ちゃんを抱きしめてたっけね。
English: Lana was cradling Ema in her arms.

Japanese: ‥‥きっと、トモエちゃん‥‥
English: Looking back at it now...

Japanese: 妹ちゃんがナニをしたか、知ってたんだと思うよ。
English: She must have already known what her sister had done.

Japanese: ‥‥‥！...!なぜ、そんなコトが言えるのですか！
English: How can you know that!?

Japanese: ボクが見た、カレの死体‥‥もう、移動されたアトだったから。
English: Because of the victim's body. It had already been moved.

Japanese: と、言うと‥‥
English: So that means...

Japanese: あなたは、宝月　巴のデスクのそばで死体を見た、と？
English: you found the body near Lana's desk?

Japanese: そういうコトになるね。
English: That's right.

Japanese: さっきのハナシじゃ、あれでしょ？
English: I think you said earlier,

Japanese: カレをプッスリ刺しちゃったの、ボクのヨロイだったワケよね？
English: it was my suit of armor... that really stabbed the prosecutor?

Japanese: ‥‥そう、考えられます。
English: Yes...
Anyway...

Japanese: あなたがオフィスに行ったときには‥‥
English: So you're saying...

Japanese: すでに《ねつ造》が行われたアトだった、と言うのですか？
English: that the forgery had already taken place by the time you arrived at your office?

Japanese: 言うのですよ。そりゃね。
English: That's exactly what I'm saying.

Japanese: トモエちゃんのキモチもわかるケド。
English: I can understand how Lana must have felt,

Japanese: 死体を動かして‥‥証拠をかくすのは、よくないねェ。
English: but moving a body and hiding evidence are inexcusable no matter what the circumstances.

Japanese: （本当に‥‥そうだったのか？）
English: (Is that how it really went down?)

Japanese: 証人をニラみつけても、ムダだ。‥‥成歩堂。
English: Staring at the witness won't do you any good, Mr. Wright.

Japanese: ！!どうせニラむのならば。法廷記録にしておいたほうがいい。
English: If you're going to stare at anything... you'd be better off staring at the Court Record.

Japanese: さすが、御剣ちゃん。シャレたコト、言うじゃない。
English: Worthy, Worthy... Always the smooth talker.

Japanese: （巌徒局長と“ねつ造”をむすびつける証拠、か‥‥）
English: (But which piece of evidence ties Gant to the forgery...?)

Japanese: あなたが《ねつ造》と無関係なのであれば‥‥
English: If you really had nothing to do with the forgery...

Japanese: コイツは、いったいどう説明するつもりですかッ？
English: then how do you explain this?

Japanese: な、なんですか、それは！手形がついているようですが‥‥
English: W-what's that!? And what's that on it... a handprint?

Japanese: 巌徒局長、セツメイをッ！
English: Chief Gant! Your explanation, please!

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥‥‥...さあ、ねえ。ボクに聞かれてもなあ。
English: I don't know. You tell me, son.
Huh?

Japanese: あのさ、ナルホドちゃん。
English: My dear Wrighto.

Japanese: キミ‥‥《証拠法》の２大原則も知らないみたいだね。
English: Don't you know the second rule of evidence law?

Japanese: （うう、出たよ。《証拠法》‥‥）
English: (Uh-oh, not this again. Evidence law...)

Japanese: 《その２：未登録の証拠の提示には審議中の事件との関連性が必要》
English: Rule 2: "Unregistered evidence presented must be relevant to the case in trial."

Japanese: ボロキレと、この事件の“関連性”‥‥ボク、聞いてないけど？
English: Tell me, how is that rag relevant to this trial?

Japanese: どうやら‥‥弁護人。ジュンビ不足だったようですな。
English: It appears... the defense was not prepared.

Japanese: （この証拠品‥‥まだ、早すぎたみたいだな）
English: (I guess it's too early to use this piece of evidence.)

Japanese: 申しわけありませんが‥‥局長。最初から、おねがいできますかな？
English: Please accept my profound apologies, Chief. Would you mind giving the defense another chance?

Japanese: もお。しかたないな。今度だけよ、チョーさん。
English: Well, okay. I'll do it just this once. But only because you asked, Udgey.

Japanese: いやはや。メンボクない。ほっ、ほっ、ほっ‥‥
English: Thank you. I assure you the defense is terribly sorry. Ha ha ha...

Japanese: （くそお‥‥）
English: (Yeah. Sorry I didn't nail you...)

Japanese: あなたが《ねつ造》に関係していない‥‥
English: You claim you had nothing to do with the forgery...

Japanese: ザンネンですが、それは通りません。
English: but I'm afraid that is a claim you cannot back up.

Japanese: ‥‥どういうコト？
English: Explain yourself.

Japanese: あなたの部屋から、いくつかの証拠品が見つかったんです。
English: Several pieces of evidence were found in your office.

Japanese: たとえば‥‥このツボ。
English: Take this jar, for example.

Japanese: それは、さきほど提出された《タイホくん》‥‥
English: That's that Blue Badger you showed us earlier.

Japanese: この最後のカケラは、あなたの金庫から発見しました。
English: A piece of this jar was discovered in your safe.
Not only that,

Japanese: 先ほど提出した、証拠品リストがあります。
English: but the evidence list I presented earlier...

Japanese: ‥‥これもまた、あなたのデスクから発見されました！
English: was actually found inside your desk!

Japanese: な、なんですと！
English: It was found where!?

Japanese: あなたが《ねつ造》に関係していない‥‥
English: You claim you had nothing to do with the forgery...

Japanese: ザンネンですが、それは通りません。
English: but I'm afraid that is a claim you cannot back up!

Japanese: ‥‥どういうコト？
English: Explain yourself.

Japanese: あなたの部屋から、いくつかの証拠品が見つかったんです。
English: Several pieces of evidence were found in your office.

Japanese: たとえば‥‥このリスト。
English: Take this list, for example.

Japanese: それは、宝月　茜さんがラクガキをした‥‥
English: That's the list Ema Skye drew her picture on...

Japanese: こいつは、あなたのデスクから発見されました。
English: This was discovered in your desk.
Not only that,

Japanese: あなたの部屋にかざられていた、このツボがあります。
English: but a piece of this jar that was sitting in your office...

Japanese: ‥‥このカケラの１つが、あなたの金庫から発見されました！
English: was found inside your safe!

Japanese: な、なんですと！
English: It was found where!?

Japanese: いかがですか、巌徒局長。
English: You see, Chief Gant.

Japanese: あなたのオフィスから発見されたこれらの証拠品は‥‥
English: These articles of evidence uncovered in your office...

Japanese: あなたが不正捜査に関係している、何よりの証拠ですッ！
English: are both concrete proof... that you also played a part in the illegal investigation!

Japanese: 巌徒局長！こ。これは、いったい！
English: Chief Gant! What's the meaning of this!?

Japanese: ‥‥ふうん‥‥
English: Ho!

Japanese: コワいねえ。最近の弁護士も、なかなか。
English: Here's a defense attorney who may even rival Worthy!

Japanese: ‥‥認めるわけですね？《ねつ造》のジジツを。
English: So you admit to it, then? That you were involved in the forgery?

Japanese: え。ダレが？　ボク？それとも‥‥‥キミ？
English: Who, me? Or do you mean... you?

Japanese: な、なんで、ぼくなんですか！
English: Me? Why would I have anything to do with that!?

Japanese: だって‥‥さ。
English: Well...

Japanese: キミ。ボクのオフィス、勝手に荒らし回ったワケだよね？
English: You were the one who snuck into my office when you "found" this evidence.

Japanese: ‥‥！...!‥‥《ねつ造》をするのは、何も検察側だけじゃない。
English: Prosecutors aren't the only ones capable of forging evidence, you know.

Japanese: キミたちだって、できる。そうだよね？　ナルホドちゃん‥‥
English: Defense attorneys can do so too. Isn't that right, Wrighto?

Japanese: しかし！　その調査には、捜査官が立ち会ったハズだッ！
English: However! Detective Gumshoe was present during the investigation!

Japanese: 御剣ちゃんさァ。
English: Worthy, my boy.

Japanese: 違法は、違法なワケ。だから‥‥
English: Not even detectives are exempt from the law.

Japanese: ノコちゃんにも、キビシイペナルティが与えられるハズだよ。
English: Rest assured Dick will receive his due punishment.
Wh...

Japanese: なんですって！
English: WHAAAAT!?

Japanese: （これ以上、ナニが起こるんだ！イトノコ刑事の給料に‥‥）
English: (If Detective Gumshoe's salary drops any further, he'll end up paying to work!)

Japanese: とにかく‥‥あなたのオフィスから発見されたと言われる、証拠品。
English: Yes, well. In light of the Detective's presence...

Japanese: 現場の《ねつ造》もふくめて、詳しい証言をおねがいします！
English: please give us your testimony regarding these pieces of evidence found in your office, and their relation to the forgery that took place at the crime scene.

Japanese: やれやれ‥‥まったく。
English: My, my...

Japanese: 最近のコは“スジをとおす”ってコトを知らないからコマるよねェ。
English: Kids these days no longer know how to put two and two together...

Japanese: ～《証拠品》と《ねつ造》～
English: -- Evidence & Forgery --

Japanese: ツボのカケラと、リストだっけ？キミが持ちこんだんじゃないの？
English: Let's see, what was it now? A jar fragment... and a list? For all I know, you could have planted them in my office.

Japanese: そもそも、その証拠品が“いつ”発見されたか、立証できるのかな。
English: Anyway, you can't prove "when" those pieces of evidence were discovered.

Japanese: 青影の判決が下ってから見つかった証拠なら、無価値だよ。それって。
English: If they were found after Darke was convicted, then they're worthless.

Japanese: だいたい、ボクが《ねつ造》なんてするワケないでしょ？
English: There's no reason I'd participate in a forgery.

Japanese: 現場を偽装なんかしたって、なんのトクにもならないもんねー。
English: Rearranging the crime scene wouldn't help me out in any way.

Japanese: ふむう‥‥成歩堂くん。
English: Hmm... Mr. Wright.

Japanese: はい。
English: Yes, Your Honor?

Japanese: あなたの“調査”‥‥
English: When investigating the crime scene...

Japanese: もう少し、キチンと手順を踏むべきでしたね。
English: you should have been more careful to observe protocol.

Japanese: 相手は、警察局長よ？タダで済むワケ、ないじゃない。
English: You do understand that I am the Chief of Police, right? There will be consequences...

Japanese: うううう‥‥
English: Ooh...

Japanese: なんだったら、訴えてあげようか。今後のためにも、さ。
English: Indeed, I believe I will press charges... so you won't make the same mistake again.

Japanese: 申しわけありませんが、局長。明日にしてもらえますかな。
English: My apologies, Chief, but would you mind waiting until tomorrow for that?

Japanese: 今日はホラ。アレがありますから。
English: Today is... well, you know...

Japanese: じゃ、チョーさんのカオに免じて。でも、そのかわり‥‥‥
English: All right, Udgey. In return, though...

Japanese: わかっておりますぞ。あそこですな。ほっ、ほっ、ほっ。
English: I know! I know! That place, right?

Japanese: （‥‥オトナの会話が始まってしまった‥‥）
English: (Huh? What are these guys, telepathic?)

Japanese: ～《証拠品》と《ねつ造》～
English: -- Evidence & Forgery --

Japanese: ツボのカケラと、リストだっけ？キミが持ちこんだんじゃないの？
English: For all I know, you could have planted them in my office.

Japanese: そもそも、その証拠品が“いつ”発見されたか、立証できるのかな。
English: Anyway, you can't prove "when" those pieces of evidence were discovered.

Japanese: 青影の判決が下ってから見つかった証拠なら、無価値だよ。それって。
English: If they were found after Darke was convicted, then they're worthless.

Japanese: だいたい、ボクが《ねつ造》なんてするワケないでしょ？
English: There's no reason I'd participate in a forgery.

Japanese: 現場を偽装なんかしたって、なんのトクにもならないもんねー。
English: Rearranging the crime scene wouldn't help me out in any way.

Japanese: なんのメリットもないのに、ダレかに協力するハズもないしね。
English: I wouldn't be anyone's "accomplice" if there was nothing in it for me.

Japanese: （２年前‥‥罪門　直斗検事を殺害したのは、巌徒じゃない）
English: (Gant isn't the one who murdered Prosecutor Marshall two years ago.)

Japanese: （それなのに、“ねつ造”に関係しているというコトは‥‥）
English: (Yet I know he played a part in the forgery.)

Japanese: （だれかにたのまれて“共犯者”になった、ということか‥‥）
English: (That can only mean someone asked him to be an accomplice.)

Japanese: 巌徒　海慈が、だれかに協力‥‥考えにくいハナシだが、な。
English: Damon Gant... someone's accomplice? That sounds unlikely...

Japanese: バカなことを言わないでください！そんなコト、するわけが‥‥
English: I'd appreciate it if you'd stop making these ridiculous allegations.

Japanese: まあ、言われてみれば‥‥
English: Yes, you do have a point...

Japanese: そんなドキョウ、ないかぁ。ナルホドちゃんには。
English: You wouldn't have the guts to do something like that...

Japanese: そ、そんなコトありません！
English: What!?

Japanese: ぼくもムカシはこう見えて、フダつきのワルで‥‥
English: I'll have you know, back in the day I once broke into a cattle ranch, and tipped-

Japanese: な、ナニを言い出すのですかこんなところで！
English: M-Mr. Wright! What are you saying!?

Japanese: とにかく。“持ちこまなかった”っていう証拠はないよね？
English: Anyway, you can't prove you didn't carry in the evidence, can you?

Japanese: 今ごろ‥‥きっと、発見されているだろうし。
English: If you have proof to the contrary, you're going to need it later.

Japanese: “発見”‥‥？なんのこと、だろうか？
English: Later? What are you talking about?

Japanese: 決まってるでしょ？ナルホドちゃんの、指紋。
English: What else? I'm talking about when your fingerprints are found.

Japanese: ボクの金庫の内側にねェ‥‥‥違法捜査の証拠になるね、コレは！
English: Yes. If they're found inside my safe, they would prove your investigation was illegal.

Japanese: う‥‥‥（勝てるのか？　こんなのに‥‥）
English: Grrr... (I've never faced anyone as slimy as this guy!)

Japanese: どういうイミですか？
English: What do you mean by that?

Japanese: ‥‥あくまでも、仮定のハナシだけど‥‥
English: This is all purely hypothetical, of course...

Japanese: たとえば、ツボのカケラをボクが金庫にしまったとしようか。
English: but suppose I did place those items in my safe.

Japanese: ‥‥でもね。それが《ねつ造》になるとは、かぎらないワケね。
English: Such an act... wouldn't necessarily constitute forgery.

Japanese: 発見された証拠品をかくしたら、それはリッパな‥‥
English: If concealing evidence found at a crime scene isn't forgery...

Japanese: ハナシは最後まで聞こうよ。
English: I'm not through speaking yet, Mr. Wright.

Japanese: モンダイになるのは‥‥発見された“時期”ね。コレが。
English: It all depends on "when" the evidence was discovered.

Japanese: つまり、このカケラは‥‥
English: Are you saying this jar fragment...

Japanese: “初期捜査では発見されなかった”というコトですか？
English: wasn't discovered in the initial investigation?

Japanese: ま。そういうコトじゃないの？証拠品リストに、ないワケだし。
English: It would appear not. After all, it wasn't listed in the evidence list.

Japanese: 青影の裁判が終わった、次の日。ヒョイと出てきたのかもねー。
English: For all we know, it could have suddenly materialized the day after Darke was sentenced.

Japanese: そんなツゴウのいいハナシ、あるワケが‥‥
English: Oh, and wouldn't that be convenient...

Japanese: ‥‥成歩堂。
English: Wright.

Japanese: ！!局長は“可能性”の話をしている。それを完全に否定できない以上‥‥
English: The Chief is talking about a "possibility." So long as you can't rule that out...

Japanese: 何度、ツクエを叩いても、キミの手がイタいだけ、だ。
English: your remarks, however clever they may be, will only succeed in wasting time.

Japanese: （手のイタみは、自分がイチバンよく知っているよ！）
English: (Tell me something I don't know...)

Japanese: お。いいねー。そのクヤしそうなカオ。
English: Come now, Mr. Wright.

Japanese: ‥‥でもさ。考えてみてよ。
English: Think about it.

Japanese: なぜ、そんなコトが言えるんですか！
English: How can you look me in the eye and say that!?

Japanese: だって、ボク。ハンニンじゃないから。
English: Because I'm innocent.

Japanese: ‥‥！...!よーく、思い出してよ。ナオトちゃんを殺したのは、ダレ？
English: Remember? Who was it that murdered Neil?

Japanese: “殺した”という言い方はどうかと思いますが‥‥
English: I'm not sure I care for the word "murder" here...

Japanese: 結果的に、そのイノチをうばったのは‥‥宝月　茜さん、ですな。
English: but in the end the person responsible for Mr. Marshall's unfortunate demise... was Ema Skye.

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥‥‥‥‥...どうかな？　さすがに、わかってくれたでしょ？
English: Well? Now do you see?

Japanese: それは、どうでしょうか。‥‥巌徒局長。
English: Really, Chief Gant?

Japanese: 少なくとも、あなたは１つ、大きなトクをしているはずです。
English: At the very least, there is one very large benefit you've reaped from all this.

Japanese: へええ。知らなかったな。なんだろ。教えてくれる？
English: Oh? I wasn't aware. What is this "benefit"?

Japanese: “局長”のポジションですよ、モチロン。
English: That would of course be the position you have- Chief of Police.
Oh...

Japanese: 《ＳＬ９号事件》解決のおかげで、あなたは局長になった。
English: The resolution of the SL-9 Incident secured your promotion to Chief.

Japanese: それは、リッパな動機になるハズですッ！
English: That in itself is sufficient motive!

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥‥‥‥‥‥...あーっはっはっはっ。ナメられたもんだね。ボクも。
English: Ho! Ho! Ho! Oh, that's a good one!
Huh?

Japanese: キミ‥‥ボクがそんなに無能だと思ってたの？
English: Do you really think I'm that incompetent?

Japanese: ど。どういうことですか？
English: What do you mean?

Japanese: あの事件がなくてもね。ボクは次期局長に決まってたのね。
English: Even without that case, I was already in line to become the next chief.

Japanese: たしかに、あの件のおかげで就任は少し、早まったかな。
English: The resolution of SL-9 merely sped up the inevitable a little.

Japanese: ほ、ホントなのか？　御剣‥‥
English: Is that true, Edgeworth?

Japanese: う‥‥ム。たしかに‥‥そうなっていただろう。
English: Yes... He was going to be made Chief anyway.

Japanese: んぎゃあっ！
English: Gah!

Japanese: 人さし指でヒトをさすときはさ。刺し返されるコトもあるワケ。
English: Be careful when pointing that finger... or you might wind up being the one pointed at!

Japanese: ‥‥と、なれば。
English: So that means...

Japanese: あなたが《ねつ造》をする可能性は、１つしかない。
English: there's only one possible motivation for you to commit forgery.

Japanese: 自分のためではなく‥‥“他人のためだった”。
English: If you didn't do it for yourself... then you did it for someone else.

Japanese: バカだねー、御剣ちゃん。キミも知ってるでしょ？
English: Don't be silly, Worthy. You know me better than that.

Japanese: ‥‥ボクはね。自分のために生きてるの。
English: There are only three people I look out for: Me, Myself and I.

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥‥‥‥‥‥...いいや。この際、ハッキリさせておこうか。
English: There, it's out in the open now.

Japanese: ‥‥証言、ちょっと変えるね。チョーさん。
English: Udgey, would you mind if I changed my testimony a little?

Japanese: は、はあ。助かります‥‥
English: By all means, please do!

Japanese: “メリット”‥‥ですか。
English: "Nothing in it" for you?

Japanese: ワルいけど、ボクさ。キョーミがないから。自分しか。
English: Sorry, but the only person I care about is Yours Truly.

Japanese: あの子‥‥妹ちゃんだっけ？
English: That girl... Lana's little sister, was it?

Japanese: 彼女がカワイソウだから、同情した‥‥なんて考えてるなら、ムダよ。
English: If you think I felt sorry for her, you'd better think again.

Japanese: たしかに‥‥あなたは、同情などしない。
English: You're right... You don't feel sorry for anyone.

Japanese: 罪にキビしく、ヒトにキビしく。‥‥それが、ボクの生き方だから。
English: Be tough on crime and tough on people. That's how I was raised.

Japanese: さぞかし、アマいのだろうな。‥‥ご自分に対しては。
English: You seem to be lax enough on yourself, though.

Japanese: あーっはっはっはっ。
English: Ho! Ho! Ho!

Japanese: いや。おもしろいコト言うよね、御剣ちゃんてさ。
English: Oh, that's a good one, Worthy!

Japanese: （どうなんだろう‥‥“メリット”は存在したのか？）
English: (Hmm... Could there have been something in it for him?)

Japanese: （‥‥自分本位な局長が、だれかのために《ねつ造》を手伝った！）
English: (Given his selfishness, would he have helped someone out?)

Japanese: （‥‥ダメだ。“メリット”なんて、思いつかない！）
English: (Bah! I can't think of how it would help him!)

Japanese: （ということは‥‥）
English: (That means...)

Japanese: （巌徒局長は、ダレにも協力しなかった、ということか！）
English: (He wouldn't have helped out anyone.)

Japanese: ‥‥冷静になることだ。成歩堂　龍一。
English: Relax and take a deep breath, Mr. Wright.

Japanese: ‥‥！!キサマの得意分野だろう。‥‥“発想の逆転”ならば。
English: Try to think "outside the box." After all, that's what you're good at, isn't it?

Japanese: ‥‥発想の逆転‥‥（おまえに言われるとはな‥‥）
English: Think outside the box... (I never thought I'd hear him tell me that...)

Japanese: （“局長が、だれに協力したか”ではなく‥‥）
English: (The question isn't "Who would the Chief help"...)

Japanese: （“だれが、局長に協力を求めたか”‥‥？）
English: (It's "Who would ask the Chief for help?")

Japanese: （そこに、おのずと“メリット”は生まれる！）
English: (If someone did that, he'd be sure to find a way to benefit from that person!)

Japanese: どうやら‥‥弁護側の主張もこれまでのようですな。
English: It appears... the defense has nothing more to say.

Japanese: ‥‥局長。もう一度、最初から証言していただきましょう。
English: Chief, would you please repeat your testimony from the beginning?

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥‥‥...あなたは“ヒトのため”に助けることはないかもしれない。
English: True, you might not help out anyone for their sake.

Japanese: しかし‥‥“自分のため”ならば。だれかに手を貸すかもしれません。
English: But if it would benefit you... you might decide to assist someone.

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥‥‥...どうやら‥‥弁護人。どうしても、あなたは局長を‥‥
English: Mr. Wright.

Japanese: 人助けの大好きな、やさしいヒトにしたいようですな。
English: It appears you're positively determined to portray the Chief... as a nice man who likes to lend people a hand.

Japanese: （‥‥そういうコトじゃない）
English: (That's not what I mean...)

Japanese: それでは、うかがいましょう。いったい、どなたでしょうか？
English: Very well, I'll ask.

Japanese: ‥‥巌徒局長が、《ねつ造》に協力する可能性のある人物とは！
English: Who is this person you believe Chief Gant may have helped forge evidence?

Japanese: ‥‥あのさ。ナルホドちゃん。そこまでミジメだと‥‥
English: Wrighto, my boy. The look on your face...

Japanese: いっそ、キミに手を貸してあげたくなっちゃうよ。
English: is almost enough to motivate even me to help you out.

Japanese: ‥‥あなたの目を自由に泳がせておくのは、キケンなようですな。
English: I hope you understand that's not a compliment.

Japanese: （巌徒局長が“メリット”を求めた‥‥つまり）
English: (Chief Gant would have wanted something in return...)

Japanese: （“弱み”をニギりたかった人物、か‥‥！）
English: (He must have wanted to be able to blackmail the person he helped!)

Japanese: 宝月　巴主席検事‥‥ひ、被告人ですかッ！
English: Chief Prosecutor Lana Skye... Th-the defendant!?

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥‥‥...事件の流れを見れば、あまりにも、アキラカです。
English: I believe it's quite obvious in light of the circumstances.

Japanese: 宝月　茜さんが、不幸な事件に巻きこまれた。
English: Ema Skye fell victim to an unfortunate series of events.

Japanese: 彼女をイチバン助けたいのは‥‥姉の、宝月　巴さんです。
English: Who would want to help her more than her own sister, Lana?

Japanese: そして、巌徒局長は‥‥彼女になら、協力する理由がある。
English: And as for Chief Gant... he would also have a reason to help Lana if she asked him to.

Japanese: その理由とは、モチロン。“自分の利益”です。
English: That reason, of course, is... self profit.

Japanese: り、利益！いったい、どんな‥‥？
English: Self profit...? What do you mean?

Japanese: 《ＳＬ９号事件》に決着がついてから‥‥
English: After the SL-9 Incident was resolved...

Japanese: 宝月　巴さんは、主席検事として検事局へ配属されました。
English: Lana Skye was appointed Chief Prosecutor at the Prosecutor's Office.

Japanese: この異動を決めたのは‥‥巌徒局長。あなた、だそうですね。
English: The person who arranged this job change...was you, Chief Gant.

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥‥‥...し、しかし。それが、どういう利益に‥‥？
English: B-but... how would he profit from all of this?

Japanese: 警察局の局長室から、主席検事を自由に操ることができる‥‥
English: He would be able to use the Chief Prosecutor as his puppet!

Japanese: それは、つまり！　捜査に関する全権をニギることをイミするのだ！
English: Essentially... he would acquire unchecked authority over all investigations!

Japanese: あ‥‥！　もしかして‥‥つ。つまり、いわゆるアレですか。
English: Do you mean to tell me... that despite the Chief's formidable appearance,

Japanese: ‥‥捜査の操作ッ！
English: he plays with puppets!?

Japanese: あ！　いやッ。私は決して、そういうつもりではな
English: Oh, wait. You must mean "puppet" as in someone forced to do his bidding... Never mind!

Japanese: いかがですか、巌徒局長！
English: Admit it, Chief!

Japanese: あなたは、宝月　巴さんの《ねつ造》に手を貸したのです！
English: You assisted Lana Skye in forging evidence!

Japanese: 彼女を主席検事にして、自由に操作するためにッ！
English: Your motive: to appoint her as Chief Prosecutor so you could control her!

Japanese: ナルホドちゃん‥‥スキなコト、言ってくれるじゃない‥‥
English: Wrighto, my boy. You have quite an imagination.

Japanese: じゃ、聞くけどさ。
English: Let me ask you something.

Japanese: ‥‥なんでしょう。
English: What?

Japanese: トモエちゃんを“操った”だっけ？‥‥そんな証拠、あるのかな。
English: Do you have any proof of this? That I "controlled" Lana?

Japanese: たとえば、彼女自身がそう証言しているの？
English: For example, is Lana testifying that I've done such a thing?

Japanese: そ、それは‥‥
English: Lana...

Japanese: （彼女は、あかねちゃんのためにチンモクを守っているんだ‥‥）
English: (She's keeping quiet to protect Ema.)

Japanese: （証言なんか、するハズがない！）
English: (There's no way she'd testify against Gant!)

Japanese: ‥‥それでは、ハナシになりませんな。
English: I'm afraid without any proof, this all amounts to nothing more than mere conjecture.
Unless...

Japanese: それこそが‥‥今回の事件だったのかもしれぬ。
English: that is also what happened in this incident...

Japanese: “今回の”と言いますと‥‥あの。どの事件ですかな？
English: "This" incident...? Er... which one would that be?

Japanese: モチロン‥‥
English: Of course I'm talking about...

Japanese: 多田敷　道夫捜査官の殺害だ。
English: the murder of Detective Bruce Goodman.

Japanese: この事件における、彼女の行動は‥‥一貫して、不自然だった。
English: The Chief Prosecutor has been acting strange throughout this entire trial.

Japanese: まるで‥‥何者かに“操られている”かのごとく！
English: Almost as if... someone has been "controlling" her!

Japanese: ‥‥御剣ちゃんさあ。今の、キミの発言‥‥
English: Worthy... You'd better watch your tongue...

Japanese: どれだけキケンなものか、わかってるのかな‥‥？
English: I wouldn't want you to get hurt.

Japanese: ど‥‥どういうことですか？
English: Just what do you mean?

Japanese: つまり、こういうことです。
English: What he means, Your Honor,

Japanese: 巌徒局長は、多田敷捜査官の殺害に関与している。そして‥‥
English: is that Chief Gant is involved in the murder of Detective Goodman. Not only that...

Japanese: 宝月　巴さんを操って、事件の《いんぺい》工作をさせた！
English: but the Chief is now making Lana take the rap to cover up his involvement!

Japanese: な‥‥‥‥‥‥‥‥なんですッとオオオォォォォォッ！
English: Wha... wha... wha... WWWWHHHHAAAAAAAAATTTTT!!!?

Japanese: 静粛に！　静粛に！　静粛に！静粛に！　静粛に！　静粛にィッ！
English: Order! Order! Order! I SAID... ORDEEEEERRRR!!!

Japanese: 弁護人！　今の、あなたの発言‥‥ホンキなのですかッ！
English: Mr. Wright! You... You can't be serious!

Japanese: え！
English: Huh?

Japanese: これは‥‥この国の司法のトップに対する、挑戦です！
English: This... This is an affront to the highest ranking officer in our law enforcement agency!

Japanese: 警察局長を、告発！《殺人》‥‥ならびに《脅迫》で！
English: To accuse the Chief of Police of blackmail... and murder!!!?

Japanese: あ。あ。あ。あ。あ。あ。あ。あ。あ。あ。あ。‥‥あり得ませんッ！
English: That's i.... i... i-i-i-i-i-i-i-IMPOSSIBLE!!!
Your Honor, I was merely reiterating...

Japanese: 御剣検事さんが言ったことを、わかりやすくセツメイしただけ‥‥
English: what Mr. Edgeworth said, in easier-to-understand language.

Japanese: もう、おそい。弁護人。
English: It's too late, Mr. Wright.

Japanese: ！!‥‥すでに我々は、前に進むしかないのだッ！
English: There's no turning back for us now.

Japanese: （どうやら‥‥カクゴを決めたのはアイツのほうだったみたいだな）
English: (It looks like he's the one who's decided to go through with this.)

Japanese: ‥‥弁護人。あなたは、立証できるのですか？
English: Can you prove this, Mr. Wright?

Japanese: この国の司法を代表する局長が、殺人事件に関係している‥‥と！
English: That the Chief, a high-ranking officer of the law, is involved in this murder!?
(Good question...)

Japanese: ‥‥名前の前に肩書きがあろうと、立っているのは、ヒトリの男だ。
English: Regardless of his rank or title, Chief Gant is just a man.

Japanese: 犯罪者か、そうでないか？証拠品がそれを語るだろう。
English: The question is, is he a criminal? I believe the evidence will tell.

Japanese: わかりました。
English: I see.

Japanese: それでは‥‥提示していただきましょう！
English: Alright, then. Show us this evidence that ties Chief Gant to the murder of Detective Goodman.

Japanese: 巌徒局長と、捜査官の殺害をむすびつける、決定的な証拠を！
English: Just remember... It better be good!

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥‥‥いかがですかな？　巌徒局長。...
English: Well, Chief?

Japanese: モノを投げたいの、コッチだね。ショージキなトコ。
English: So long as Mr. Wright is tossing out things regardless of their relevance...

Japanese: ボクもクツ、投げていいかな。チョーさんめがけて。
English: mind if I toss something too, Udgey? This shoe should do nicely. I'll chuck it right at you!

Japanese: いやいや。いつもながら、キビしいジョークですなあ。
English: You always were quite the joker, weren't you?

Japanese: （やれやれ‥‥ちがったみたいだな）
English: (Oops... Looks like I got it wrong.)

Japanese: “やれやれ”じゃありません！私のクツ、くらわせますぞッ！
English: Don't "oops" me! Or I'll be the one throwing my shoe next!

Japanese: 事件が多くてコンランしがちだが‥‥思い出すのだ。
English: I know it's hard with all that's gone on during this trial,

Japanese: ‥‥今から４日前。警察局の証拠保管室で起きた事件、だ。
English: but try to think back to what happened in the evidence room four days ago.

Japanese: （そういえば‥‥きのうの調査で見つけたハズだ）
English: (Now that he mentions it, we did find something out in yesterday's investigation...)

Japanese: （事件当日、巌徒があの部屋に入った、という証拠を‥‥）
English: (Something that proves Gant entered that room...)

Japanese: なんか‥‥まだ足りないみたいだよ。チョーさんのクツ。
English: Looks like he wants another one of your shoes, Udgey.

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥‥‥...これは‥‥事件当日、証拠保管室に入ったヒトたちの‥‥
English: This is the ID card list...

Japanese: ＩＤカードの記録です。きのうもモンダイになりました。
English: Yes, the one that shows who entered the evidence room on the day of the crime.

Japanese: ただひとつ、持ち主が不明だった《７７７７７７７》のＩＤ‥‥
English: There was one ID on the list we couldn't determine the owner of yesterday...

Japanese: 7777777.ワルいけど。このカードのナンバーかどうかは‥‥
English: Sorry, but there's no way you can prove that's my card number.

Japanese: あなたのナンバーです。巌徒局長。
English: It's your number.

Japanese: ‥‥！!な、なぜ！　そんなコトがわかるのですか！
English: What!? How do you know that!?

Japanese: 局長の部屋の金庫には、暗証番号が必要なのです。‥‥７ケタの。
English: The safe in Chief Gant's Office requires a code to open. A seven-digit code...

Japanese: “なななけた”‥‥ま。まさかッ！
English: Seven digits... You don't mean...

Japanese: “な”が１つ多いようです。裁判長‥‥
English: I'm afraid so, Your Honor. The code was "7777777"...

Japanese: 《７７７７７７７》‥‥ＩＤカードの番号と一致しました。
English: The same as the remaining ID card number on that list!

Japanese: 巌徒局長！　あなたは事件当日、証拠保管庫に入ったのです！
English: Chief Gant! You entered the evidence room on the day of the crime!

Japanese: 静粛に！　静粛にッ！
English: Order! Order!

Japanese: 巌徒局長！　いかがですかッ！
English: Chief Gant! What do you have to say!?

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥‥‥いかがも何も、さあ‥‥...
English: Nothing.

Japanese: こんな捜査、認められると思ってるのかなあ‥‥
English: The defense's search of my office was in violation of regulations.

Japanese: その件については、私が全力で弁護士の罪を追求しよう。
English: And I will demand Mr. Wright be punished to the maximum extent of the law.

Japanese: しかしッ！今は、セツメイをおねがいしたい。
English: But right now, this court demands an explanation from you...

Japanese: この、ＩＤカードの使用についてッ！
English: about the use of this ID card!

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥‥‥‥‥...巌徒局長！あなたは‥‥認めるのですか！
English: Chief Gant! So you admit it?

Japanese: あの日、事件当日。証拠保管室に入ったことを！
English: You entered the evidence room... on the day of the crime!?

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥‥‥‥‥...あのね。ボク、局長なワケ。
English: What about it? I'm Chief of Police. Whether it's the evidence room or the bathroom,

Japanese: 保管室ぐらい、スキに入らせてよ。トイレ行くのと、いっしょでしょ？
English: what's the difference? I can go anywhere I want.

Japanese: ‥‥ひとつ、カクニンしたい。
English: Tell me.

Japanese: 証拠保管庫に入ったとき‥‥ヒトリ、だったのだろうか？
English: When you entered the room... were you alone?

Japanese: トイレと保管室はね！ヒトリで行くコトにしてるんだよ。
English: I always go to the bathroom alone... as I do with the evidence room.

Japanese: ‥‥もしかしたら、多田敷捜査官といっしょ、だったのでは？
English: Detective Goodman wouldn't have happened to be with you that day... would he?

Japanese: そ。そんなワケ、ないでしょ。ここ数日、カレとは会ってないね！
English: O-of course not! Why would he be? I hadn't seen him in days!

Japanese: ‥‥巌徒局長。今の発言は大きなミスでしたね。
English: You hadn't seen him... "in days"? Chief Gant... I'm afraid you've just undone yourself.

Japanese: あの日。あなたは、多田敷捜査官に会っていなければならないのです！
English: On that day, you had to have met with Detective Goodman!

Japanese: いったい、これはどういうコトですか！
English: What do you mean!?

Japanese: ここは、宝月　巴の罪状を審理する場のハズですッ！
English: This trial's purpose is to determine Lana Skye's guilt!

Japanese: ‥‥それはちがう、裁判長。
English: No it isn't, Your Honor.

Japanese: ここは、《真実》をアキラカにする場なのだッ！
English: This trial's purpose is to determine the truth.

Japanese: 事件当日、巌徒局長が被害者と会ったのであれば‥‥
English: If Chief Gant met the victim on the day of the crime,

Japanese: そこに“何があったか”‥‥我々は、最後まで追求するッ！
English: then we need to determine one thing: what transpired during that meeting!

Japanese: それならば、弁護人！証拠品の提示を求めます！
English: In that case, Mr. Wright! I'm going to have to ask you for evidence!

Japanese: 事件当日！　被害者が、局長に会いに行ったというコンキョは！
English: Show us proof that the victim went to meet Chief Gant on the day of the crime!

Japanese: （‥‥ちがう！　もっと、ハッキリした証拠があるはずだ！）
English: (No, there's got to be something more solid than this!)

Japanese: （あの日、多田敷捜査官は‥‥）
English: (On that day...)

Japanese: （どうしても、巌徒局長に会わなければならなかった！）
English: (Detective Goodman had to go see Chief Gant!)

Japanese: ‥‥ヒトリぼっちの反省会は終わったようですね。
English: Sorry to interrupt your mumblings...

Japanese: では、シツレイして。
English: but the court is waiting.

Japanese: （うううう‥‥）
English: (Oops...)

Japanese: キミも明日、ボクに会いにくる？‥‥始末書でも書いて、さ。
English: Why don't you come meet with me tomorrow? Perhaps we could write up your letter of... termination?

Japanese: と、とにかく！　あなたは多田敷さんに会っているんです！
English: Regardless, you did see Detective Goodman that day!

Japanese: 多田敷さんは、事件当日。ＩＤカードを紛失していました。
English: Detective Goodman lost his ID card on the day of the crime.

Japanese: むしろ‥‥罪門　恭介によって盗まれた、と言うべきだろう。
English: Or to be more accurate, Jake Marshall stole it.

Japanese: ‥‥だから、彼は《遺失物届》の書類を書いた。
English: So Detective Goodman filled out a lost item report.

Japanese: その、提出先は‥‥あなたですね、巌徒局長！
English: He would have had to give that report... to the Chief of Police!

Japanese: でもさあ。その書類をキミが持ってる、ってコトは‥‥
English: Yet you are in possession of the report...

Japanese: ジッサイに、それを提出したかどうかは‥‥
English: which means you can't be sure if he filed it.

Japanese: 提出したのです。なぜなら、多田敷さんは‥‥
English: He filed it. How do I know, you ask?

Japanese: あの日。どうしても、保管室に入る“必要”があったからです！
English: Because he needed to enter the evidence room that day.

Japanese: “必要”‥‥というのは？
English: He needed to?

Japanese: モチロン、証拠品の《申し送り》です。
English: Yes. To transfer the evidence out.
Oh...

Japanese: 多田敷捜査官は、書類を持ってあなたのところへ報告に行った。
English: Detective Goodman took the form to you, Chief Gant.

Japanese: そして‥‥あなたは、そのとき。
English: Then...

Japanese: 多田敷捜査官といっしょに、証拠保管庫へ入ったのです！
English: you accompanied the detective to the evidence room!

Japanese: ボクと、アイツが‥‥いっしょに？
English: I "accompanied" him?

Japanese: それ以外に、多田敷さんが保管室に入る方法は、ありません。
English: There's no other way the murderer and Detective Goodman could have entered the room!

Japanese: つまり‥‥キミは、あくまで。こう主張するつもりなんだ。
English: Hold on. Let me guess what you're going to say next.

Japanese: ‥‥このボクが、多田敷ちゃんを殺害した、と。
English: I, the Chief of Police, murdered poor Goodman!
Exactly.

Japanese: しかし！　“いっしょに入った”とは、かぎらないのでは‥‥？
English: But wait! The Chief didn't necessarily need to accompany him to the evidence room.

Japanese: 巌徒局長は、ＩＤカードを“貸した”だけかもしれません。
English: He could have just lent him his ID card.

Japanese: あ。言われてみれば‥‥たしかに、そんな気もするような
English: Yes... Now that you mention it, I believe I might have done something of the sort.

Japanese: ザンネンだが‥‥それはもう、通らない。
English: Sorry, but that's not possible.

Japanese: ！!使用記録によれば、あなたのカードは、一度しか使われていない。
English: According to the record, your card was only used once.

Japanese: しかし。先ほど、あなたはご自分のＩＤカードを見せた。
English: But you showed us your ID card earlier.

Japanese: ‥‥もし。多田敷捜査官に“貸した”のであれば‥‥
English: If you had really "lent" it to Detective Goodman,

Japanese: そのカードは、彼の死体から発見されるハズではないかッ！
English: it would have been found on his body!

Japanese: ‥‥く‥‥クソがァァァァァァァァァァァッ！
English: N... NNNNNOOOOOOOOOO!!!

Japanese: ま、まさか‥‥巌徒局長！あ。あなたが‥‥多田敷捜査官を！
English: Chief Gant! Y-you didn't...!

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥‥‥...おそらく‥‥事件は、突発的に起こったのでしょう。
English: The murder was most likely committed on the spur of the moment. No one in their right

Japanese: 事件の舞台に、警察局内を選ぶ者はいませんからね。
English: mind would choose the Police Department as a place to commit murder.

Japanese: 犯行後‥‥あなたは、検事局のともえさんに連絡をとりました。
English: After the murder, you contacted Lana at the Prosecutor's Office.

Japanese: モチロン‥‥多田敷さんの死体を“かたづけさせる”ためです。
English: Why else? To dispose of Detective Goodman's body.

Japanese: しかし‥‥
English: However.

Japanese: 多田敷捜査官の死体は、検事局の地下駐車場で発見された。
English: The victim's body was discovered in the Prosecutor's Office's parking lot.

Japanese: いったい‥‥どうやって、死体を移動させたと言うのか？
English: How did he manage to move it there?

Japanese: ちなみに。ボクあの日、警察局を離れなかったんだけどね。
English: I was at the Police Department the entire day, you know.

Japanese: 宝月主席検事も、式典後はずっと検事局にいたことがわかっている。
English: And everyone's aware that Lana stayed at the Prosecutor's Office after the ceremony.

Japanese: それは知りませんでした。‥‥しかし、巌徒局長。
English: Everyone except me, it seems...

Japanese: あなたは、警察局長です。‥‥部下を使うことができる。
English: Still, you're the Chief of Police. You have an entire police force at your disposal.

Japanese: なに？　警官をつかまえてボクが命令した、ってワケ？
English: Oh, so you think I just ordered an officer to do it?

Japanese: 『あ。キミ。チョイと検事局に、死体を届けてね』‥‥なんて。
English: "Hey you. Take this here dead body over to the Prosecutor's Office." I don't think so...

Japanese: 巌徒局長。検事局に死体を運んだ《方法》‥‥
English: Chief Gant. You left all the evidence we need...

Japanese: あなたはしっかり、手がかりを残してくれているんですよ。
English: to prove how you moved the body to the Prosecutor's Office.

Japanese: （‥‥そう。一見、なんのイミもない“手がかり”を‥‥）
English: (And all this time I thought it was a useless clue just taking up space...)

Japanese: いったい‥‥どうすれば！死体を運ぶことができたのか？
English: How could the Chief have moved the body!?

Japanese: 弁護人に、証拠品の提示をおねがいしましょう！
English: Mr. Wright! Show us this evidence!

Japanese: 巌徒局長は、死体を運ぶために‥‥コイツを使ったのです！
English: To move the victim's body... Chief Gant used this!

Japanese: さあ、いかがですかッ！
English: Well!?

Japanese: そうだねー‥‥じゃあ、今度はさ。
English: I see...

Japanese: それを使って運んであげるよ。‥‥ナルホドちゃんの死体を。
English: Maybe I'll use this later... to move your dead body, Mr. Wright.

Japanese: え。
English: Huh?

Japanese: そして、その死体は私がかたづけてあげましょう。
English: And I'll help you dispose of it!

Japanese: （‥‥あの日、ともえさんと局長は検事局、警察局から離れてない）
English: (Chief Gant stayed at the P.D. and Lana stayed at the Prosecutor's Office...)

Japanese: （だとすれば、死体が運ばれた方法は、１つしかない）
English: (That leaves only one possible way he could have moved the body.)

Japanese: （モンダイは‥‥どうやって、それを“やらせたか”？）
English: (The only problem is, how did he get him to do it?)

Japanese: （一見、なんのイミもない証拠品‥‥やっぱり、イミがあった！）
English: (Maybe that piece of evidence really did have something to do with this case!)

Japanese: ‥‥それでは、もう一度うかがいましょう。
English: I'll ask you again, Mr. Wright.

Japanese: ‥‥これが、多田敷さんの死体を運んだ方法です！
English: This is how he moved Detective Goodman's body!

Japanese: それは‥‥ドライバー、ですかな？
English: What's that? A screwdriver?

Japanese: いったい、この事件になんのカンケイが‥‥
English: But what does that have to do with this case?

Japanese: 御剣検事。思い出してください。事件当日のコト‥‥
English: Mr. Edgeworth. Think back to the day of the crime.

Japanese: なぜ、このドライバーがここにあるのか‥‥？
English: What is this screwdriver doing here?

Japanese: そ、それは‥‥
English: It's here because...
A-AAAHHHH...!

Japanese: ただ‥‥たのまれただけだ。巌徒局長に。
English: I... was asked to go. By Chief Gant no less.

Japanese: 検事局のオフィスで保管しておいてほしい、と言われた。
English: He told me he wanted me to keep it at the Prosecutor's Office.

Japanese: とにかく‥‥事件の日、私はこれを持って戻ってきたのだ。
English: In any case, on the day of the stabbings, I brought this back to the Prosecutor's Office.

Japanese: あの日‥‥警察局の式典のあと。
English: After the ceremony ended that day,

Japanese: 私は、検事局へは戻らないつもりでいた。
English: I didn't plan to return to the Prosecutor's Office.

Japanese: しかし‥‥きみは戻った。“巌徒局長にたのまれた”から。
English: But you did. Because Chief Gant asked you to.

Japanese: ま、まさか‥‥ッ！
English: You mean I... I...

Japanese: 死体は、御剣検事のクルマの後部トランクから発見されました。
English: The body was found in the trunk of Mr. Edgeworth's car.

Japanese: ‥‥これ以上、カンタンなハナシはありません。
English: I think it's obvious what happened.

Japanese: 死体は、そのクルマで運ばれたのです！
English: The body was moved by that car!

Japanese: ‥‥これが、多田敷さんの死体を運んだ方法です！
English: This is how he moved Detective Goodman's body!

Japanese: 検事局の《駐車記録》ですか。『ミツルギ　レイジ』‥‥
English: A parking stub? "Miles Edgeworth"...

Japanese: ま、まさか‥‥ッ！
English: You mean...!

Japanese: 死体は、御剣検事のクルマの後部トランクから発見されました。
English: The body was found in the trunk of Mr. Edgeworth's car.

Japanese: ‥‥これ以上、カンタンなハナシはありません。
English: I think it's obvious what happened.

Japanese: 死体は、そのクルマで運ばれたのです！
English: The body was moved by that car!

Japanese: こ‥‥この私が‥‥死体を運んだ、だとッ！
English: You mean I... I... I carried the victim's body!?

Japanese: ‥‥そのとおり。
English: Precisely so.

Japanese: し、しかし！キミも知っているハズだ！
English: But wait! Even you know...

Japanese: あの日‥‥警察局の式典のあと。
English: I didn't plan on returning to my office...

Japanese: 私は、検事局へは戻らないつもりでいたのだ！
English: after the ceremony finished that day.

Japanese: しかし、キミは検事局に戻った！‥‥いったい、なぜだ？
English: But you did return! Tell me, why?
A-AAAHHHH...!

Japanese: ただ‥‥たのまれただけだ。巌徒局長に。
English: I... was asked to go. By Chief Gant no less.

Japanese: 検事局のオフィスで保管しておいてほしい、と言われた。
English: He told me he wanted me to keep a screwdriver at the Prosecutor's Office.

Japanese: とにかく‥‥事件の日、私はこれを持って戻ってきたのだ。
English: In any case, on the day of the incident, I brought this to the Prosecutor's Office.

Japanese: ‥‥もう、おわかりでしょう。
English: So you see...

Japanese: 検事局に届けたかったのは、“ドライバー”ではなかった。
English: what you really took to the Prosecutor's Office wasn't the screwdriver...

Japanese: 後部トランクに積みこまれた、“死体”だったのです！
English: but the victim's body in your trunk!

Japanese: 御剣検事のクルマに‥‥多田敷捜査官の、死体がッ！
English: Detective Goodman's body... was carried in the trunk of Mr. Edgeworth's car!

Japanese: それとも‥‥セツメイできますか？　巌徒局長。
English: Yes. Unless, of course, you have another explanation, Chief?

Japanese: なぜ、半年も前に解決した事件の証拠品を、わざわざ運ばせたか？
English: Why else would you have asked Mr. Edgeworth to carry evidence from a closed case?

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥‥‥‥‥...そのコタエは、１つしかない。死体を、検事局に届けるため‥‥
English: There's only one plausible explanation:

Japanese: そこで待っている、協力者‥‥宝月　巴に引きわたすためです！
English: to transport the body to your accomplice... Ms. Lana Skye!

Japanese: 静粛に！　静粛に！　静粛にッ！これは‥‥どういうことですか！
English: Order! Order! Order! What's going on here!?

Japanese: 苦しまぎれに聞こえた、弁護人の主張には‥‥反論の余地がない！
English: Is there no room for rebuttal to the defense's outrageous accusations!?

Japanese: ‥‥おキョウさんが撮影した、この写真を思い出してください！
English: Think back to the photograph Ms. Starr took at the Prosecutor's Office.

Japanese: これは、死体をトランクにつめて運び出すところではなかった。
English: This was a not a photo of the body being stuffed in the trunk to be taken away.

Japanese: むしろ、その“逆”‥‥
English: It was exactly the opposite...

Japanese: トランクから、死体を取り出そうとしているところだったのかッ！
English: It is a photo of the body being taken from the trunk!

Japanese: 巌徒局長！‥‥何か、おっしゃるコトは！
English: Chief Gant! Please, say something!

Japanese: ‥‥そう、ねえ‥‥“時間切れ”ってトコ、かな。
English: I believe... your time's up.

Japanese: な。なんですか‥‥？
English: My "time's up"?

Japanese: ワルいんだけどさあ。これから、ボクね。
English: Sorry, Wrighto, but I'm having lunch with the District Attorney General after this.

Japanese: ゴハン食べに行くワケよ。地方検事局長クンとね。
English: We have to get going if we're to make it in time for the early bird special.

Japanese: 何を言ってるんですか！まだ、尋問の途中で‥‥
English: B-but... The cross-examination isn't finished yet!

Japanese: ‥‥あのね、ナルホドちゃん。はじめから、言ってるよね。
English: Remember what I told you earlier?

Japanese: 警察局長には‥‥いろんな武器がある、ってさ。
English: A police chief... has all kinds of weapons at his disposal.

Japanese: ‥‥ま。まさか‥‥
English: ..."Weapons"...?

Japanese: 《証言の拒否権》‥‥とかね。ボク、使わせてもらうから。
English: Like the right to refuse to testify. I'm invoking that right now.

Japanese: な。なんですと‥‥！
English: What!? That is not a right to be casually invoked.

Japanese: しかし‥‥局長。　証言をキョヒする、ということは‥‥それなりの“キケン”もあるワケですが‥‥
English: There are certain risks to be considered!

Japanese: ここまで来て、逃げるつもりかッ！　巌徒局長！
English: So you're going to just run away after all this!?

Japanese: “逃げる”‥‥‥？笑わせないでよ、御剣ちゃん。
English: "Run away"? Don't make me laugh, Worthy.

Japanese: ‥‥！!『ボクが、多田敷ちゃんを刺した』‥‥そんなコト、言ってたよね？
English: "I stabbed ol' Goodman." That's what you're saying, right?

Japanese: 決定的な証拠があるんなら、さ。見せるヒマはあったでしょ？
English: But if you had any conclusive evidence, you would have presented it by now.

Japanese: そ、それは‥‥
English: Well I...

Japanese: ボクが、トモエちゃんに“死体”の始末をたのんだ？
English: You think I had Lana dispose of the body?

Japanese: だったらさあ。さっさと、その証拠を見せなよ。
English: If so, then show your proof and get it over with!

Japanese: ‥‥ふむう‥‥
English: Hmm...

Japanese: 何度も言いますが、巌徒氏は、現職の警察局長です。
English: I'll say it again, Mr. Wright. Damon Gant is the current Chief of Police.

Japanese: 決定的な証拠のない告発など、ユルされるワケがありません。
English: This court will not tolerate any accusations against him without concrete proof.

Japanese: ‥‥弁護人。
English: Well, Mr. Wright?

Japanese: は‥‥はい。
English: Y-Your Honor?

Japanese: いかがですか？決定的な証拠はあるのですかな？
English: Do you have any concrete proof?

Japanese: “巌徒局長が、多田敷捜査官を殺害して、死体を始末させた”
English: Proof that Chief Gant murdered Detective Goodman and made Ms. Skye dispose of his body?

Japanese: （決定的な“証拠”‥‥どうなんだろう‥‥？）
English: (Do I have any concrete "proof"...?)

Japanese: （アイツに逃げられてしまってはどうしようもない‥‥）
English: (I can't let him just squirm his way out of this!)

Japanese: （ここは、なんとかしてたたきつけてやるしかない！）
English: (I've got to keep the pressure on!)

Japanese: ‥‥わかりました。証拠品をお目にかけましょう。
English: Yes, Your Honor. I do have such evidence.

Japanese: それでは、証拠品の提示をおねがいしましょう。
English: Then please hurry up and present it. Just remember,

Japanese: 巌徒局長が、多田敷捜査官を殺害した‥‥決定的な証拠とは！
English: it better prove Chief Gant murdered Detective Goodman beyond a shadow of a doubt!

Japanese: ‥‥それで。この証拠は、なんですかな？
English: So... Er, what exactly is this evidence?

Japanese: イチかバチか‥‥投げつけてみました！
English: It's proof. As to whether or not it's enough to demonstrate the Chief's guilt... I'll let you be the judge!

Japanese: “イチ”のほうですか？　それとも“バチ”のほう、ですかな？
English: But... I am the judge.

Japanese: そうですね‥‥どちらかと言えば“バチ”寄り、でしょうか。
English: Oh, right... Well, what do you think, Your Honor?

Japanese: ‥‥それならば。“バチ”を与えましょう！
English: What I think, Mr. Wright, is I'm going to be late for my meal.

Japanese: （やっぱり‥‥ちょっと、自信がなかったんだよな‥‥）
English: (I guess it wasn't enough...)

Japanese: も、もう一度！考えなおさせてください！
English: Please, Your Honor! Give me just a little longer to consider!

Japanese: （‥‥カクシンのない証拠をつきつけても、イミがない‥‥）
English: (It's no use showing evidence I'm not even sure of myself...)

Japanese: 現時点では‥‥決定的な証拠は、ありません。
English: No, Your Honor. At present I have no conclusive evidence.

Japanese: フン！　どうよ？聞いたでしょ、チョーさん。
English: Hmph! See, Udgey?

Japanese: ‥‥どうやら‥‥
English: In that case...

Japanese: 本法廷は、あなたに重大なペナルティを与えざるを得ません。
English: This court is forced to penalize you for your allegations against the Chief.
What?

Japanese: ボクね。負ける勝負はしないことにしてるワケ。
English: I don't gamble unless the stakes are high!

Japanese: やっぱり‥‥勝利の女神は今回も、ボクのミカタ、だったみたいだね。
English: It seems that Lady Luck was on my side again today.

Japanese: ‥‥じゃ、チョーさん。あと、ヨロシクね！
English: Okay Udgey, I'll leave the rest to you!

Japanese: 最初に言ったはずです。
English: I warned you earlier, Mr. Wright.

Japanese: これは‥‥この国の司法のトップに対する挑戦だ、と！
English: This...is an affront to a senior officer in our nation's law law enforcement agency.

Japanese: う‥‥‥‥‥‥‥‥...うおおおおおおおおおおおおおッ！
English: WAAAAAHHHHHH!!!

Japanese: ‥‥《勝利の女神》‥‥か。いいコトを言ってくれた。
English: "Lady Luck," hm? Maybe we should have a word with her.

Japanese: ど、どういうことですか！‥‥御剣検事ッ！
English: Mr. Edgeworth? What do you mean!?

Japanese: この審理の行方を知っている、《女神》‥‥
English: There's one "Lady" who knows the real truth behind this trial...

Japanese: 我々はまだ、彼女とコトバをかわす光栄を、味わっていないようだ。
English: We haven't yet had the honor of hearing her testimony.

Japanese: （審理の行方を知っている《女神》‥‥つまり、《証人》！）
English: (A lady who knows the truth... Another witness!)

Japanese: 決定的な証拠がない以上‥‥残る立証の手段は《証言》だ。
English: In the absence of conclusive evidence, the only other method of proof is testimony.

Japanese: でも！　巌徒局長は、《証言の拒否権》を‥‥！
English: But Chief Gant has invoked his right to refuse to testify!

Japanese: ひとりだけ、いるはずだ。
English: There's still someone else. One more witness who can answer all the questions

Japanese: この状況に、決着をつけるコトができる証人が‥‥ここに！
English: raised in this trial. Someone right in this very room!

Japanese: いったい‥‥ダレですか！御剣検事ッ！
English: Mr. Edgeworth! Who is this person!?

Japanese: フッ‥‥‥お忘れのようだ、裁判長。
English: Hmph... Why are you asking me, Your Honor?

Japanese: 本日、この法廷においては‥‥
English: Have you forgotten?

Japanese: 証人の選定は、すべて。弁護側にゆだねたことを。
English: The defense is the one calling witnesses today.

Japanese: ‥‥弁護人。そのような証人がはたして、存在するのですかな？
English: Mr. Wright. Does such a witness exist?

Japanese: （“彼女”が真実を語ってくれるかどうかは、わからない‥‥）
English: (She may not be willing to tell the truth...)

Japanese: （しかし‥‥ここで立ち止まることは、できない！）
English: (But we can't just stop now!)

Japanese: ‥‥わかりました。最後の証人の喚問を要請します！
English: Yes, Your Honor! The defense calls forth...

Japanese: キサマとは、同じ道をたどってここまで来た‥‥そう思ったが。
English: I thought we were walking the same path together, Mr. Wright.

Japanese: ‥‥どうやら、そうではなかったようだ。
English: It appears somewhere along the way you got lost.

Japanese: 早くしないと、おいて行きますぞッ！
English: You'd better find your way back, Mr. Wright, or you'll be left behind!

Japanese: （ううう‥‥いつの間にかとり残されていたみたいだ‥‥）
English: (There's another "witness" who can expose Chief Gant's crime...)

Japanese: （巌徒局長の罪を暴くことのできる《証人》‥‥）
English: (One whom we've all let stand at the sidelines...)

Japanese: （スナオに考えれば‥‥もう、ひとりしかいない！）
English: (Now that I think about it,) (there's only one possible person!)

Japanese: 被告人‥‥宝月　巴さん、ですか！
English: The defendant... Ms. Lana Skye!?

Japanese: ２月２１日の、５時１５分。事件が“起こった”時間‥‥
English: She was in the underground parking lot at 5:15 PM on February 21.

Japanese: 彼女は、あの地下駐車場にいた。死体を“始末”するために！
English: Her task: to dispose of the victim's body...

Japanese: そう。‥‥“ある人物”の指令によって！
English: in accordance with a certain someone's orders!

Japanese: ふ、ふむう‥‥いかがですか、御剣検事！
English: Hmm... Mr. Edgeworth...?

Japanese: ‥‥検察側に、特に異議はない。
English: The prosecution has no objections, Your Honor.

Japanese: ‥‥ここで、最後の休憩をとりたいと思います。
English: Very well. The court will now take its final recess for the day.

Japanese: １５分後、被告人?宝月　巴さんの証言を聞きましょう。
English: In 15 minutes, we will reconvene to hear the defendant's testimony.

Japanese: それでは、これより休憩に入ります
English: This court is now in re-

Japanese: ちょっと。いいかなあ。
English: Hold on!

Japanese: が‥‥‥巌徒局長！ゴハンに行ったのでは‥‥？
English: Huh? Chief Gant! I thought you were going to eat.

Japanese: ‥‥聞こえてるよねえ？トモエちゃん！
English: Listen good, Lana!

Japanese: （ともえさんに‥‥語りかけている‥‥？）
English: (He's talking to Lana!)

Japanese: わかってると思うけどさ。弁護士の主張を認めたら‥‥
English: I don't think you need me to tell you this, but if you accept Mr. Wright's claim...

Japanese: トンでもないコトになっちゃうからね。
English: there will be terrible consequences.
That's right...

Japanese: 罪門　直斗を殺したの‥‥‥妹ちゃん、ってコトになるから。
English: Your sister will be found guilty... for Neil Marshall's murder!

Japanese: あ‥‥ッ！（し、しまった‥‥！）
English: Ah! (This isn't good!)

Japanese: バカげたコトだと思うけどさ。
English: Of course, you'd never support such outrageous claims anyway... right?

Japanese: まあ‥‥よく考えたほうがいいんじゃないかなァ‥‥
English: Just something to think about...

Japanese: じゃッ、そういうことで。
English: All right, then.

Japanese: ボク、ちょっとゴハン食べてくるから。
English: I've got a lunch date to meet.

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥‥‥‥‥‥あ。で、では。そういうコトで！...
English: Okay. If there aren't any further objections...

Japanese: これより、１５分間の休憩に入ります！
English: this court is now in recess!

Japanese: 同日　午後　２時　４分地方裁判所　被告人第２控え室
English: February 25, 2:04 PM District Court Defendant Lobby No. 2

Japanese: なんとか、ここまでつなぐことができたな。
English: Looks like we managed to stay in the game.

Japanese: ‥‥ああ。おまえのおかげだよ、御剣。
English: Yeah. Thanks to your help, Edgeworth.

Japanese: それにしても‥‥さすが、局長殿ッスね。
English: That Chief... He's something else, eh pals?

Japanese: あ！　‥‥イトノコ刑事！
English: Detective Gumshoe!

Japanese: ハッハッハッ。もう、“刑事”じゃないッス。
English: Ha ha ha. I'm not a "detective" anymore.

Japanese: すみません！　ぼくのせいで‥‥
English: Oh yeah. Sorry about that...

Japanese: 気にするコト、ないッス。自分は、強く生きていくッス。
English: Ah, don't worry. I've already decided where to work now!

Japanese: ‥‥アンタの事務所でッ！
English: At your office!

Japanese: え。
English: My office...?

Japanese: 自分が、かわりになるッス。‥‥あの、チョンマゲの子のッ！
English: Sure! I'll take the place of that top-knotted girl you used to work with!

Japanese: （もしかして‥‥真宵ちゃんのコト、か‥‥？）
English: (Could he mean... Maya?)

Japanese: ‥‥でも。今度こそ、もう手の打ちようがないッスよねえ。
English: Still... Looks like we're all out of moves now.

Japanese: あの局長に、バッチリ！先手を打たれちまったッス。
English: Chief Gant's done it again. How is it he always gets the upper hand!?

Japanese: 証言を拒否するコトができる、なんて‥‥反則ですよ！
English: It's not fair he has the right to refuse to testify!

Japanese: ‥‥フッ。落ちつくことだ、成歩堂。
English: Hmph. Settle down, Wright.

Japanese: 思い出してみたまえ。裁判長も言っていただろう。
English: Remember what the judge said?

Japanese: しかし‥‥局長。　証言をキョヒする、ということは‥‥
English: But Chief! That is not a right to be casually invoked.

Japanese: それなりの“キケン”もあるワケですが‥‥
English: There are certain risks to be considered!

Japanese: “キケン”‥‥か。でも。どういうことだ？
English: "Risks"... What did he mean by that?

Japanese: カンタンだ。
English: It's simple.

Japanese: “すべての証言を、拒否できる”これを“逆”に考えれば‥‥
English: If the Chief refuses to testify... the opposite also holds true.

Japanese: いっさい“発言できない”‥‥ってコト、ですね！
English: You mean, he forfeits his right to say anything too!

Japanese: あかねちゃん！‥‥もう、大丈夫なの？
English: Ema! Are you okay?

Japanese: あたし、医務室で目がさめて‥‥さっきは、傍聴席にいたんです。
English: Yeah. When I came to, I was in the medical office. I've been listening to the trial from the gallery.

Japanese: （聞いていたのか‥‥あの裁判）
English: (So she heard everything that's been going on.)

Japanese: あの‥‥ゴメンね。ヒドいコト、言っちゃって。
English: Um, Ema... I'm sorry for what I said before.

Japanese: いいんです。ゼンブ、ホントのコトだから。
English: No, don't be. It was the truth.

Japanese: それに、フシギ。あたし‥‥ちょっとだけ、ウレシイんです。
English: You know, it's funny. I almost feel somehow... relieved.

Japanese: “ウレシイ”‥‥？
English: "Relieved"?

Japanese: ‥‥やっと。ホントのコトが、わかったから。
English: Yeah... Now I finally know what really happened.

Japanese: お姉ちゃんは‥‥この２年間、ずうっと。
English: To think that all this time...

Japanese: あの局長さんの、言いなりになっていたんですね。
English: my sister was being blackmailed by that terrible man!

Japanese: あの‥‥あたしのために。
English: And she did it all... just to protect me.

Japanese: 主席検事として検事局に異動してきてから‥‥
English: Ever since her appointment as Chief Prosecutor,

Japanese: “宝月　巴は、ヒトが変わった”‥‥彼女を知る者は、そう言う。
English: everyone who knew her... said she changed.

Japanese: おそらく‥‥そのほうが、やりやすかったのだろう。
English: Perhaps... it was easier that way for her.

Japanese: “やりやすい”というのは‥‥？
English: What do you mean?

Japanese: 巌徒局長の指令だ、モチロン。《ねつ造》や《トリヒキ》‥‥
English: What do you think I mean? To follow Chief Gant's orders.

Japanese: 任務をこなすには、冷たい仮面をかぶっていたほうがいい。
English: She must have shut herself up deep inside... to force herself to do anything and everything the Chief told her to do.

Japanese: （そういうことか‥‥）
English: (That must be why she became so cold...)

Japanese: ゼンブ‥‥あたしのせい、だったんですね。
English: It was all my fault.

Japanese: ‥‥あたしが、罪門さんを‥‥死なせちゃったから。
English: It's all because I... I murdered Mr. Marshall.

Japanese: アンタがワルいワケじゃないッス！ワルいのは‥‥その。
English: Hey. Don't go blaming yourself, now.

Japanese: 世の中ッス！
English: If you want to blame anyone, blame society, pal!

Japanese: 巌徒局長も、あたしのココロまで《ねつ造》するコトは、できない。
English: Chief Gant may be able to fool everyone else with his "forgery,"

Japanese: あたし‥‥やっぱり。つき飛ばしたんです。
English: but he can't fool my memory. I remember now. I knocked Mr. Marshall into that armor.

Japanese: そう‥‥なんだ。
English: I... I see.

Japanese: さあ！　そろそろ、時間ッス。サイゴの幕が上がるッス！
English: Well, we'd better get back. It's time for the final act!

Japanese: じゃあ、あかねちゃんはここで‥‥
English: Ema, why don't you wait h-

Japanese: あたしも行きます。
English: No. I'm going with you.

Japanese: ！!あたし‥‥この目で、見たいから。
English: I want to be there...

Japanese: お姉ちゃんが、ホントのコトを言うのを。
English: when Lana tells the truth.

Japanese: ‥‥行こう、成歩堂。今度こそ、最後だ。
English: Let's go, Wright. It's time to end this.

Japanese: つづく
English: To be continued.

Japanese: そこまで！
English: That's enough! This court sees no reason to further prolong the trial.

Japanese: 本法廷は、これ以上の審議の必要性を認めません。さらに、被告人に対する判決を決める時間も、必要としません。
English: The defense has failed to give the court sufficient reason to doubt the prosecution's claim!

Japanese: これは、きわめて明白な事件です。疑問の余地はない！被告人、宝月　巴にたった今、判決を言いわたします！
English: This court finds the defendant, Ms. Lana Skye...

Japanese: 被告人はこれより身柄を司直に預けるものとし、
English: The accused will surrender to the court immediately,

Japanese: １ヶ月以内に、高等裁判所において通常裁判を受けることとします。
English: to be held pending trial at a higher court within a month from today's date.

Japanese: では、本日はこれにて閉廷！
English: That is all. The court is adjourned!

Japanese: 同日　午後　２時２１分地方裁判所　第９法廷
English: February 25, 2:21 PM District Court Courtroom No. 9

Japanese: それでは‥‥
English: Now then...
