Japanese: 被告人?宝月　巴さん。‥‥証人席へおねがいします。
English: Will the defendant, Ms. Lana Skye, please take the stand?

Japanese: 宝月　巴さん。‥‥あなたは、主席検事だ。
English: Ms. Lana Skye. You are the Chief Prosecutor.

Japanese: この場で、何を求められているか‥‥よくご存じだろう。
English: I'm sure you're aware of what is required of you.

Japanese: ‥‥御剣検事。
English: But Mr. Edgeworth...

Japanese: その私が、この２年間。いったい、何をしてきたか‥‥
English: you already know everything...

Japanese: すべて、ご存じなのでしょう？
English: You know all that I've done these past two years.

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥‥‥‥‥...これから、あなたに証言をしていただきます。
English: Please provide the court with your testimony, Ms. Skye.

Japanese: ‥‥真実を述べるように。
English: And remember... you are under oath. We want to hear the truth.

Japanese: ええ。真実を、ね‥‥
English: Of course... The truth...

Japanese: お姉ちゃん！　あたし。味方だからね！　何があっても‥‥
English: Lana! No matter what happens, I'll always be your sister!

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥‥‥...‥‥それでは、証言していただきましょう。
English: Now then, your testimony, if you will.

Japanese: 最初に‥‥巌徒局長との“関係”について。
English: First, tell us about your relationship with Gant.

Japanese: （すべては‥‥あなたのコトバにかかっている）
English: (Everything hinges on your testimony.)

Japanese: （巌徒を追いつめられるのは、あなたしかいない！）
English: (You're the only chance we have to get Gant!)

Japanese: ～巌徒局長と《ねつ造》～
English: -- Gant & The Fabrication --

Japanese: 巌徒局長とは長い間、いっしょに仕事をさせていただきましたが‥‥
English: I worked alongside Gant for years...

Japanese: 脅迫のジジツなど、まったくありませんでした。
English: There's no truth to this "blackmail" theory.

Japanese: ２年前の《ねつ造》も‥‥私ひとりが、やったことです。
English: I fabricated the evidence two years ago all by myself.

Japanese: 罪門検事さんの死体を見つけて‥‥証拠をデッチ上げてしまった。
English: When I found Prosecutor Marshall's body, I rearranged the crime scene.

Japanese: 青影を、有罪にしたかっただけ。‥‥妹は、関係ありません。
English: My only motivation was to get Darke convicted. It had nothing to do with Ema.

Japanese: ‥‥ふむう‥‥本当に、まちがいありませんな？
English: Hmm... Are you sure about this testimony?

Japanese: 《重罪》を自供しているのです。まちがうハズ、ありませんわね。
English: Your Honor. I'm confessing to a capital offense. Of course I'm sure.

Japanese: お、お姉ちゃん‥‥！
English: But Lana...!

Japanese: ‥‥これが真実ならば、やはり。巌徒局長は無関係というコトです。
English: If this is true, then that means Chief Gant has nothing to do with this.

Japanese: 最初から、そう申し上げているハズですけれど‥‥？
English: That's what I've been telling you from the beginning.

Japanese: な‥‥成歩堂さん、おねがい！あたしのせいで、お姉ちゃんが‥‥
English: Please, Mr. Wright. You've got to help her! She's sacrificing herself because of me...

Japanese: （でも！　もし彼女が、ホントのコトを言っていたら‥‥？）
English: (But what if she's telling the truth?)

Japanese: ‥‥あたし、わかったんです。
English: She's not. I know my own sister.

Japanese: お姉ちゃんが、ていねいなコトバで話しているとき。いつも‥‥ホントのコトを押しかくしているんです。
English: Whenever she speaks stiffly like that, she's hiding something inside.

Japanese: ホントは、悲鳴を上げたいぐらいツラいのに！
English: Deep down, she's really screaming in agony!

Japanese: ‥‥！...!（たしかに‥‥ぼくが迷って、どうするんだ！）
English: (Yeah... This is no time to start second-guessing myself!)

Japanese: ‥‥それでは、弁護人。尋問をおねがいします！
English: The defense may now begin its cross-examination.

Japanese: ～巌徒局長と《ねつ造》～
English: -- Gant & The Fabrication --

Japanese: 巌徒局長とは長い間、いっしょに仕事をさせていただきましたが‥‥
English: I worked alongside Gant for years...

Japanese: 脅迫のジジツなど、まったくありませんでした。
English: There's no truth to this "blackmail" theory.

Japanese: ２年前の《ねつ造》も‥‥私ひとりが、やったことです。
English: I fabricated the evidence two years ago all by myself.

Japanese: 罪門検事さんの死体を見つけて‥‥証拠をデッチ上げてしまった。
English: When I found Prosecutor Marshall's body, I rearranged the crime scene.

Japanese: 青影を、有罪にしたかっただけ。‥‥妹は、関係ありません。
English: My only motivation was to get Darke convicted. It had nothing to do with Ema.

Japanese: 青影のナイフの刃先を折って埋めこみ、死体を移動させました。
English: I broke off the tip of Darke's knife, planted it inside the wound, then moved the body.

Japanese: 凶器の刃先が、体内から見つかれば‥‥決定的な証拠になりますから。
English: I knew the tip of the weapon found buried in his body would be all the proof we needed.

Japanese: 犯行の瞬間に割れたツボのカケラがジャマだったものですから‥‥
English: The pieces of the jar that shattered during the events threatened my plan.

Japanese: 成歩堂さん！　お姉ちゃん‥‥ウソをついてます！
English: Mr. Wright! My sister's lying!

Japanese: あくまでも、きみをかばうつもりなんだ。
English: Looks like she's determined to protect you to the end.

Japanese: 《ねつ造》は、自分ヒトリでやったことだ、って‥‥
English: She insists she fabricated the evidence by herself.

Japanese: お姉ちゃんに、そんなコトできるハズがありません！
English: There's no way she could have done it alone!

Japanese: （とにかく‥‥ともえさんにしゃべらせるしかない）
English: (I've got to get Lana to talk more.)

Japanese: （それが“ウソ”ならば‥‥“ムジュン”が生まれる！）
English: (If she's lying, then she's bound to slip up and make a contradiction!)

Japanese: それは‥‥何年ぐらい、ですか？
English: How many years, exactly?

Japanese: そうですね。２４才のとき、上級捜査官になって以来‥‥
English: Ever since I made senior detective.

Japanese: ５年ほど、でしょうか。
English: Let's see, I was 24 then... so that would be five years.

Japanese: 巌徒捜査官と、宝月捜査官‥‥すばらしいコンビでしたな。
English: Detective Gant and Detective Skye were legendary partners.

Japanese: 証言台で、何度もお見かけしたものです。
English: I personally saw them testify in numerous cases.

Japanese: （さすが、千尋さんのセンパイ‥‥ヤリ手なんだな）
English: (She must have been good, coming from the same school as Mia...)

Japanese: 巌徒　海慈は、ソンケイできる捜査官でした。ですから‥‥
English: Damon Gant was a respectable detective. That's why...

Japanese: ‥‥よく考えてください、ともえさん！
English: But think about it, Ms. Skye!

Japanese: 多田敷捜査官の“殺人事件”‥‥あなたは、やっていない！
English: You didn't murder Detective Goodman!

Japanese: きのう、留置所でそう言っていたハズです！
English: You told me as much yesterday in jail!

Japanese: 成歩堂弁護士‥‥まだまだ、アマいようですね。
English: You still don't get it, do you Mr. Wright?

Japanese: 法廷に提示できない証言など、“おしゃべり”と同じ、なのです。
English: Any testimony you cannot present in court is as useless as idle gossip.

Japanese: ‥‥！...!私は‥‥多田敷捜査官のカラダにナイフを突き立てました。
English: I stabbed Detective Goodman with a knife.

Japanese: そして‥‥
English: And...

Japanese: ‥‥妹さんを助けるため、ですか？
English: Did you do so to help your sister?

Japanese: 青影　丈は、連続殺人事件のハンニンだったのです。
English: Joe Darke was a serial killer.

Japanese: あの日。妹も、その被害者のヒトリになるところだった。
English: My sister almost became his last victim that day.

Japanese: ‥‥そんなコトで、彼女の人生を狂わせたくなかったのです。
English: I didn't want that incident to ruin her life.

Japanese: でも！　あかねちゃんのしたコトは、《正当防衛》です！
English: But what she did was justifiable self-defense!

Japanese: “罪”にはならないハズ‥‥
English: She wouldn't have been charged with anything...

Japanese: モンダイは、そんなコトではありません。
English: That's not the point.

Japanese: あの子のココロに、キズが残る。くだらないオトコのために‥‥！
English: She was traumatized that day, all because of that creep!

Japanese: それが、ユルせなかったのです。
English: That's why I couldn't forgive him.

Japanese: お姉ちゃん‥‥
English: Lana...

Japanese: それでは、２年前。やはり、やはりあなたは《ねつ造》を‥‥？
English: So that's why you fabricated the evidence two years ago?

Japanese: あなたが、ヒトリでやったと言うのですか？
English: You say you did this all by yourself?

Japanese: ‥‥ええ。
English: Yes.

Japanese: ２年前、現場で何を見たのか？‥‥お聞かせねがおうか。
English: Would you mind telling us what you found when you arrived at the crime scene?

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥‥‥‥‥‥‥...どうやら、私は‥‥最初に現場を発見したようです。
English: It seems I was the first person to discover the scene.

Japanese: 死体には‥‥《検事局賞》の、折れた剣が刺さっていました。
English: The broken prosecutor award knife was stuck in the victim's body.

Japanese: 待ってください！　罪門検事のイノチをうばったのは‥‥
English: What!?

Japanese: ‥‥不幸な“事故”だったハズです！
English: But Prosecutor Marshall died from an unfortunate "accident"!

Japanese: ‥‥それは、成歩堂弁護士。あなたの、勝手なスイソクです。
English: That's only a situation you dreamed was "possible."

Japanese: ‥‥！...!罪門検事のイノチをうばったのは、妹ではない。
English: The reality is, it wasn't my sister who took the prosecutor's life!

Japanese: この主張を変えるつもりはありません。‥‥ゼッタイに！
English: Fantasize all you want, Mr. Wright, but I'll never change this statement!

Japanese: つまり‥‥罪門検事は、青影の返りうちにあった、と？
English: You mean, Prosecutor Marshall wound up being killed by Darke?

Japanese: きっと‥‥そういうコト、なのでしょうね。
English: Something like that...

Japanese: ‥‥そうなると。もう１つの凶器はどうなったのだろうか？
English: If that is so, what happened to the other murder weapon?

Japanese: 青影　丈は、飛び出しナイフを持っていたはずだが。
English: Darke was carrying a switchblade knife.

Japanese: ああ‥‥そのナイフは、少し離れたところに落ちていました。
English: Oh, that was lying on the floor a little distance away.

Japanese: 格闘の際に飛ばされたのでしょうね。
English: It was probably knocked away in the struggle.

Japanese: （‥‥ちがう！　彼女はまだウソを重ねようとしている‥‥）
English: (That's not how it went down! She's trying to cover up her lies with more lies!)

Japanese: ‥‥あたしを“殺人者”にしないため、なんですね‥‥
English: All just to protect me...

Japanese: それで、あなたは‥‥現場で、何をされたのかな？
English: So when you found the scene like this, what did you do?

Japanese: 重要なのは、その１点です。証言をおねがいしましょう！
English: After all, this is what everything boils down to!

Japanese: ‥‥はい。
English: Yes...

Japanese: あなたは、現場に細工をした。しかし、その理由は‥‥
English: So you rearranged the crime scene...

Japanese: 妹さんを“殺人者”にしないため、だったのでは？
English: Are you sure you didn't do this to keep Ema from looking like the "murderer"?

Japanese: ‥‥しつこいのですね。まだ、わからないのですか？
English: How many times do I have to tell you, Mr. Wright?

Japanese: 妹は“何もしてない”‥‥私は、それが言いたいだけ。
English: Ema didn't do it. Period.

Japanese: （そのためならば‥‥）
English: (Are you so desperate to hide that fact...)

Japanese: （どんな“罪”も引き受けるつもり、なのか！）
English: (you're willing to risk the death sentence?)

Japanese: ウソだ！　お姉ちゃん‥‥あたしを巻きこまないために！
English: She's lying! She did it so I wouldn't be blamed for what happened!

Japanese: ‥‥いずれにせよ‥‥
English: In any case...

Japanese: 私は、検事としてユルされざるコトをしたのです。
English: as a prosecutor, what I've done is unpardonable.

Japanese: ‥‥それは、どんなコトをしてもつぐなえるコトではありません。
English: There's nothing I can do to make up for my actions.

Japanese: “青影　丈のナイフの刃先を死体に埋めこんだ”‥‥！
English: You planted the tip of Darke's knife in the victim's wound?

Japanese: “罪門　直斗の死体を移動させた”‥‥！
English: And then you moved the body?

Japanese: いったい、なぜ！そのようなことを‥‥
English: But why!? Why would you do that...?

Japanese: ‥‥御剣くんならわかるのではないですか？
English: You of all people should know, Edgeworth.

Japanese: アタマがよろしいのですからね。
English: You've always had a good head on your shoulders.

Japanese: ‥‥！...!（ぼくもアタマはいいけど‥‥念のため、聞いてみるか？）
English: (My head isn't that bad... but maybe I ought to ask for the sake of the others...)

Japanese: いったい、なぜ！ナイフを刺したりしたんですか！
English: But why did you do that!?

Japanese: そんなカオをしなくても‥‥おわかり、なのでは？
English: Come now, Mr. Wright. Even you should be able to figure it out.

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥‥‥...それでは、証言に加えていただきましょう！
English: Very well, let's add this to the witness's testimony!

Japanese: 《ナイフの細工》をした、その理由を！
English: The reason Ms. Skye fabricated the knife!

Japanese: あなたが現場を発見したとき、被害者の死体は、どこに‥‥？
English: When you showed up on the scene, where exactly was the victim's body?

Japanese: あなたが立証したとおり。巌徒局長のデスク付近です。
English: It was where you deduced it was - by Chief Gant's desk.

Japanese: しかし‥‥我々が死体を見たのは、あなたのデスクの近くだった。
English: But the body was found by your desk.

Japanese: なぜ、その死体は移動されたのか？
English: Why did you move it there?

Japanese: カンタンなコトです。その理由は‥‥
English: The reason for that is simple...

Japanese: それでは、証言に加えていただきましょう！
English: Let's have the witness explain this in more detail.

Japanese: 《死体を移動させた》‥‥その理由を！
English: The reason Ms. Skye moved the body!

Japanese: あなたの主張によれば‥‥
English: According to your testimony,

Japanese: “凶器”は、罪門検事の《折れた剣》だったハズですね？
English: Prosecutor Marshall's broken knife was the murder weapon, right?

Japanese: そうです。でも‥‥
English: Yes, and leaving it at that might point the blame away from Darke.

Japanese: それでは、青影のシワザにはならないかもしれません。あのオトコを、カクジツに追いつめるためには‥‥
English: I felt the most effective way to get him convicted,

Japanese: 彼のナイフの先が、被害者の体内から見つかるのが、イチバン。
English: would be by having the tip of his knife found inside the victim's body.

Japanese: だから‥‥
English: So you...

Japanese: あなたが、被害者のキズグチに埋めこんだのですな？
English: you buried it inside the victim's stab wound?

Japanese: ‥‥そうです。ナイフの刃を、ヘシ折って。
English: Yes.

Japanese: 私は、青影の犯罪をニクんでいたのです。
English: Because I hated Darke for what he did.

Japanese: ふ‥‥ふむう‥‥
English: Hmm...

Japanese: “ツボのカケラ”というのは、やはり‥‥？
English: "Pieces of the jar..." You mean...?

Japanese: ‥‥ええ。ヒトさわがせなツボのコトです。
English: Yes. That wretched jar Mr. Wright showed us earlier.

Japanese: 青影の犯行に見えるために《工作》をするには‥‥
English: In order to show that Darke committed the crime...

Japanese: 死体を動かしたほうが、作業がやりやすかったのです。
English: I felt it would be more expedient to move the body.

Japanese: と、いうことは‥‥
English: So...

Japanese: あなたが死体を発見したとき、すでに、ツボは‥‥？
English: when you first found the body, the jar was already...

Japanese: ええ、モチロン。割れていましたわ。
English: Of course. It had been shattered to pieces.

Japanese: 現場の状況を見れば‥‥何があったかは、アキラカです。
English: If you looked at the crime scene, it would be clear right away what happened.

Japanese: 罪門検事は命を落とし、青影もキゼツしていた‥‥
English: Neil Marshall was dead, and Darke was lying unconscious...

Japanese: つまり。ツボは、２人の格闘中に割れたことになるのです。
English: In other words, the jar must have been broken during their struggle.

Japanese: なるほど‥‥わかりました。
English: I see.

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥‥‥‥‥‥...どうしたの？　あかねちゃん。
English: What's the matter, Ema?

Japanese: ‥‥あのツボが、“犯行の瞬間に割れた”‥‥
English: Apparently the jar shattered at the time the crime was committed.

Japanese: そんなハズ、ないと思うんです！‥‥なんとなく。
English: But I have a feeling there is more to it than that.

Japanese: （どうやら‥‥ここに。ムジュンがあるみたいだな！）
English: (There must be a contradiction here somewhere!)

Japanese: ‥‥とにかく。《ねつ造》は私ひとりがやったコトです。
English: Anyway, I committed this fabrication completely alone.

Japanese: ‥‥ともえさん。あなたのキモチはわかります。
English: Ms. Skye, I understand how you feel.

Japanese: ２年前‥‥妹さんを守るため、あなたは“罪”を犯した。
English: You committed that "crime" two years ago to protect your sister.

Japanese: 罪門　直斗殺害現場の《ねつ造》‥‥ですか。
English: You mean the forgery at the scene where Neil Marshall was murdered?

Japanese: 今。その真相が暴かれたら‥‥この２年間が、ムダになる。
English: If that truth were to be exposed now, the past two years of your life

Japanese: ‥‥それでも、ぼくたちは追求しなければならない！
English: will have been useless. Even so, I am compelled to bring to everyone's attention

Japanese: あなたの証言にひそむ、大きなムジュンを！
English: a significant contradiction within your testimony!

Japanese: わ。私の証言に“ムジュン”が‥‥？
English: A contradiction... in my testimony?

Japanese: ‥‥あなたは、こう証言しました。
English: You testified, and I quote,

Japanese: 《割れたツボのカケラがジャマだった》‥‥と。
English: "The pieces of the jar that shattered during the events threatened my plan."

Japanese: え‥‥ええ。
English: That's right...

Japanese: ‥‥何か、モンダイが？
English: Do you have a problem with that?

Japanese: タンジュンな見落としです。
English: It's a simple oversight, really.

Japanese: このツボには‥‥被害者の血で、メッセージが残されていました。
English: You see, a message was written on this jar with the victim's blood.

Japanese: 罪門検事が、最後のチカラをふりしぼって、書いたのでは？
English: Yes. The prosecutor must have written it in his final moments.

Japanese: ‥‥そう。その時点ですでにムジュンしてるのです。
English: Exactly so. And this is where the contradiction lies.

Japanese: ！!被害者が、ツボにメッセージを書くことができたのであれば‥‥
English: In order for the victim to be able to write his message on the jar,

Japanese: 犯行後、ツボはまだ“割れていなかった”コトになる！
English: it must not yet have been broken before he died!

Japanese: あ‥‥ッ！
English: Ah...

Japanese: そう！　割れたツボに、名前を書くのは、不可能なのですッ！
English: He couldn't have written Ema's name on a shattered jar!

Japanese: ‥‥静粛に！　静粛にッ！
English: Order! Order!

Japanese: 裁判長！　どうやら‥‥
English: Your Honor. It would appear...

Japanese: さらなる情報が必要のようだ。‥‥そのツボと、血の文字。
English: more information is needed in regard to this jar, and its bloody message!

Japanese: 我々は、とんでもない見落としをしていた可能性がある！
English: We may be missing something critical here!

Japanese: （とんでもない‥‥“見落とし”？）
English: (Something critical...?)

Japanese: ‥‥主席検事。
English: Chief Prosecutor.

Japanese: どうやら、あなた自身にもまだ、見えていないようだ。
English: It seems you're as in the dark as we are...

Japanese: これから、我々がたどりつく‥‥《真相》に。
English: about the truth towards which we're headed.
What...?

Japanese: ‥‥あなたは、見たことをそのまま、証言すればよい。
English: Just tell us exactly what you saw.

Japanese: あとは‥‥我々が、正しい道をたどるだろう。
English: We'll piece together the information to arrive at the truth.

Japanese: それでは、証人！つづけて、証言をおねがいします！
English: Very well! The witness may now continue her testimony!
-- Jar & Message in Blood --

Japanese: ツボのカケラに、血のアトがあるのは、すぐに気がつきました。
English: I immediately noticed the blood traces on the jar,

Japanese: でも、部屋は暗くて‥‥調べている時間は、なかった。
English: but it was dark in the room and I didn't have time to check it out.

Japanese: だから、私。すべてのカケラの血のアトをふき取ったのです。
English: To be safe, I wiped away the blood.

Japanese: カケラは大きかったから、見落としはありませんでした。
English: The fragments were large, so I'm sure I got them all.

Japanese: 『見つかる前に、消してしまおう』‥‥それだけを考えていました。
English: All I could think about was wiping them clean before they were discovered.

Japanese: あなたが‥‥ふき取った！この、血の文字を‥‥
English: You mean you were the one who wiped away this message in blood!?

Japanese: あのときの私は、主席検事ではなかったのです。
English: I wasn't Chief Prosecutor at the time.

Japanese: お姉ちゃん‥‥真犯人が青影だとは、思わなかったんだ。
English: She didn't think Darke was the real murderer.

Japanese: だから‥‥ツボの文字を消しちゃったんですね‥‥
English: That's why she tried to erase the "real evidence."

Japanese: それでは、弁護人。尋問をおねがいしましょう。
English: Very well. The defense may now begin its cross-examination.

Japanese: ～ツボと血の文字～
English: -- Jar & Message in Blood --

Japanese: ツボのカケラに、血のアトがあるのは、すぐに気がつきました。
English: I immediately noticed the blood traces on the jar,

Japanese: でも、部屋は暗くて‥‥調べている時間は、なかった。
English: but it was dark in the room and I didn't have time to check it out.

Japanese: だから、私。すべてのカケラの血のアトをふき取ったのです。
English: To be safe, I wiped away the blood.

Japanese: カケラは大きかったから、見落としはありませんでした。
English: The fragments were large, so I'm sure I got them all.

Japanese: 『見つかる前に、消してしまおう』‥‥それだけを考えていました。
English: All I could think about was wiping them clean before they were discovered.

Japanese: お姉ちゃんの証言‥‥“ムジュン”が増えてきましたね。
English: More contradictions have surfaced in her testimony.

Japanese: ともえさんは、必死に戦っているんだよ。
English: Your sister's really putting up a fight.

Japanese: ‥‥きみのために。
English: She must really care about you.

Japanese: でも。それは、まちがっているんだ。
English: Still, she's not doing this the right way...

Japanese: ‥‥‥！...!（この証言の《ムジュン》‥‥やっと、見えてきた気がする）
English: (I think I've finally figured out the contradictions in her testimony.)

Japanese: （すべてをひっくり返す、最後の“可能性”が‥‥）
English: (There's one final "possibility" that might turn everything around!)

Japanese: やっぱり、割れていたんですね？あのツボは。
English: So the jar was already broken?

Japanese: それまでに割れていないのが、フシギなくらいでしたわね。
English: It's a miracle that thing hadn't broken earlier.

Japanese: まあ‥‥あなたのベンゴなみにあぶなっかしいツボですな。
English: It certainly looks as feeble as the defense's case...

Japanese: （裁判長の判決ほどじゃないと思うぞ‥‥）
English: (But not as feeble as the judge's judgment...)

Japanese: 証人。‥‥抜け目のない、あなたのコトだ。
English: You were an ace detective who never missed a detail. Do you really expect us to believe

Japanese: カケラに何が書いてあるか、調べなかったのだろうか‥‥？
English: you didn't investigate what was written on the jar pieces?

Japanese: ‥‥たしかに。いつもの私なら、そうしたでしょうね。
English: Normally I would have,

Japanese: じゃあ‥‥カケラに《茜》と書かれていたコトは‥‥？
English: So you didn't know your sister's name was written on the jar?

Japanese: 知りませんでした。もし、知っていたら‥‥
English: No. If I had known...

Japanese: カケラを踏みつぶして、コナゴナにしていたでしょう。
English: I would have gathered all the pieces and ground them to dust.

Japanese: （やれやれ‥‥助かったな）
English: (Well, that helps my case...)

Japanese: ‥‥なんだか、あたし。ちょっと、うれしいです。
English: Lana... you'd do that for me?

Japanese: どうやら‥‥お姉さんとは仲なおり、できそうだね。
English: It seems you two might make up yet.

Japanese: ‥‥とにかく。１秒を争う状況でした。
English: Anyway, I just barely had enough time to move the body as it was.

Japanese: ダレかが現場に来れば‥‥証拠を“消す”チャンスも消える。
English: If someone happened upon the scene, you'd lose your chance to erase the evidence.

Japanese: あなたは、行動を急いだのだろう。
English: You must have been in a hurry.

Japanese: ‥‥そのとおりです。あのときの私は‥‥
English: I was.

Japanese: 証拠を《インメツ》することしかアタマにありませんでした。
English: I knew I had to destroy the evidence before anyone came.

Japanese: ‥‥なんというコト、でしょう。
English: This is rather shocking...

Japanese: その行動が‥‥すべてを物語っていますね。
English: I'm afraid this action of yours reveals what really happened.

Japanese: ‥‥どういうコト、ですかな？
English: What do you mean?

Japanese: あなたが本当に、ハンニンを《青影》だと思っていたのなら。
English: If you really thought Darke killed Prosecutor Marshall,

Japanese: カケラの“血”を消すハズがないのです。
English: you wouldn't have wiped away the blood.

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥‥‥...あの状況で‥‥いったい、他に何ができたと言うの？
English: What else could I have done in that situation?

Japanese: ‥‥お姉ちゃん‥‥
English: Lana...

Japanese: 時間がなかった。
English: I only had a few moments.

Japanese: とにかく、私はすべてのカケラを集めたのです。
English: There wasn't enough time for me to do anything else but gather up the pieces.

Japanese: しかし‥‥部屋は停電中で、暗かったはず、では‥‥？
English: But how could you see with the power out? It should have been pitch black in that office.

Japanese: これでも、ウデききの刑事たちとわたり合う立場にいるのです。
English: A detective is always prepared, Mr. Wright.

Japanese: 小型のライトや、カメラ‥‥いつでも、身につけています。
English: Even now I always carry a pocket light and a camera with me.

Japanese: あたしだって！　おフロに入るときも、コレだけは‥‥！
English: Even I carry my a bottle of emergency Luminol wherever I go!

Japanese: 私に、見落としはありません。すべてのカケラを集めました。
English: I never miss anything. I got every last piece.

Japanese: あなたは‥‥現場を、不正に操作したのですね？
English: So you illegally rearranged the crime scene?

Japanese: ‥‥そのとおりです。イイワケする気はありません。
English: Yes. I don't have any excuse for my actions.

Japanese: ゴメンね‥‥お姉ちゃん。あたし、知らなくて‥‥
English: I'm so sorry, Lana. I didn't know...

Japanese: いつも。ヒドいコトばっかり、言ってた‥‥
English: I've treated you so badly all this time...

Japanese: （‥‥まだ、遅くない）
English: (It's not too late.)

Japanese: （仲なおりする時間なら、たっぷり、あるはずだ）
English: (There's still plenty of time to make up...)

Japanese: （この事件に、すべて決着がついたら‥‥）
English: (After we've gotten to the bottom of this incident!)

Japanese: 検事局にとって、今日という日は歴史的な１日になりそうですな。
English: No doubt this day will leave a permanent stain on the history of the Prosecutor's Office.

Japanese: きっと‥‥あなたも知らなかった事実があります。
English: Ms. Skye. I believe this jar conceals a truth even you were unaware of.
What?

Japanese: この、最後のカケラ‥‥巌徒局長の金庫から発見しました。
English: We found the final piece of this jar in Chief Gant's safe.

Japanese: 局長の‥‥‥？どうして、そんなところに‥‥
English: In the Chief's safe? But how...?

Japanese: （やはり‥‥‥ともえさんは知らなかった！）
English: (I knew it! She really didn't know!)

Japanese: ここに、ひとつ。フシギなことがあるのです。
English: There's something even more disturbing about that final piece:

Japanese: そのカケラにだけ‥‥ハッキリ。“血のアト”が残っていたのです！
English: There was still blood on it.

Japanese: し、しかし！　さきほど、証人は断言しましたぞ！
English: But the witness just testified...

Japanese: “カケラは、１つ残らず集めて血をふき取った”‥‥と！
English: that she gathered every last piece and wiped the blood off of them!

Japanese: だからこそ‥‥‥１つの“コタエ”が見えてくる。
English: Yes, which leaves us with only one explanation.

Japanese: つまり‥‥あの晩！
English: On the night Prosecutor Marshall was murdered...

Japanese: 最初に現場を発見したのは、宝月　巴さんではなかった！
English: you were not the first one to show up on the scene!

Japanese: そう、巌徒局長だったのです！
English: Chief Gant got there before you!

Japanese: ですが！　被告人が、カケラを見落とした、という可能性もあるの
English: But couldn't the defendant have simply missed a piece?

Japanese: このカケラは、ひとつひとつがかなり大きい。
English: I'm afraid that's unlikely.

Japanese: 見落とすなど、考えられません！
English: The pieces are too big for anyone to miss, let alone an ace detective!

Japanese: ですが！　私などは、その。
English: That may well be, but everyone makes mistakes. Even I once wasted an entire day

Japanese: クチに入れた入れ歯を探して、１日をムダにしたコトがあったりす
English: looking for my dentures. They were in my mouth all along! Ha! Can you believe that!?

Japanese: お忘れだろうか、裁判長。
English: Have you forgotten, Your Honor?

Japanese: この証人が、現場に行ったとき。ツボは、すでに割れていた。
English: When this witness arrived at the scene, the jar was already broken.
Oh, that...

Japanese: 割れたツボに、名前を書き残せるワケがない‥‥つまり！
English: There's no way a name could have been written on a shattered jar.

Japanese: 証人よりも先に、現場を“発見”した人物がいるのだッ！
English: Another person discovered the scene prior to the witness!

Japanese: ですが！　巌徒局長は、あの日、逃走した青影を追っていました！
English: I hope you're not implying this "person" was Chief Gant. At the time, he was looking

Japanese: 先に現場を発見しても、不自然だとは言い切れないと考えら
English: for Darke downstairs. Besides, even if he was there first, why would he break the jar?

Japanese: それならば、なぜ！２年間もダマっていたのですかッ！
English: The question is, if he did arrive there first, why did he hide that fact for two years!?

Japanese: そ。それは、アレで...さあッ！どうなんですか、裁判長ッ！
English: Well, Your Honor? Can you answer us that!?
Nnnnn... NNNNGHAAAAAAA!

Japanese: もうしまッせええええええええええええええええええええええええん！
English: Noooooooooooo!

Japanese: ‥‥ココロならずも、裁かれる立場を味わってしまいました。
English: Wait, I'm not the one on trail here!

Japanese: 巌徒　海慈は、あの日。証人よりヒトアシ先に、現場に現れた。
English: Damon Gant arrived at the crime scene prior to the witness.

Japanese: そして、自らツボを割り‥‥カケラの１つを、故意にかくした。
English: He proceeded to break the jar, and purposefully hid one of the broken pieces.

Japanese: ‥‥さて。この行為を、なんと呼ぶか？
English: Question: What is this action called?

Japanese: 《ねつ造》‥‥そして、《インペイ》！
English: Fabrication...

Japanese: しかし！　いったい、なぜ！巌徒局長は、そんなコトを‥‥！
English: B-but why would Chief Gant do that!?

Japanese: それは‥‥その後の展開を見れば、アキラカなのではないだろうか。
English: In light of what happened afterwards, isn't it clear?

Japanese: その後の展開‥‥？
English: What happened afterwards...?

Japanese: 現場を“発見”した被告人は、それが妹の“犯行”だと考えた。
English: Discovering the scene, Lana Skye believed her sister Ema killed the victim.

Japanese: 彼女は、巌徒局長に助けを求めた。‥‥妹を助けたい、一心で。
English: Determined to help her sister, she sought Gant's aid.

Japanese: 巌徒は、彼女に“協力”して、青影に対する証拠を作りあげた。
English: Lending her his "aid," Gant helped her create evidence that incriminated Darke,

Japanese: そして、宝月　茜は救われた。そう！　これは、すべてッ！
English: sparing Ema. And here is the reason!

Japanese: あなたを言いなりに動かすための計画だったのだ！
English: The reason why Ms. Skye became the Chief's puppet!

Japanese: ‥‥‥ッ！...!!そ、そんな‥‥！私は！　私は、ひとりで‥‥
English: N-no... I... I did it on my own...

Japanese: お姉ちゃん！　もう‥‥局長さんをかばうのはやめて！
English: Please, Sis! Stop trying to protect the Chief!

Japanese: あたしのせいで、これ以上‥‥
お姉ちゃんが苦しむの、見たくないよ！
English: I can't watch you suffer any more for my sake...

Japanese: ち、ちがう！　あなたじゃない！あなたは、だれも殺してないの！
English: No, you didn't! It wasn't you, Ema! You didn't kill anyone!

Japanese: 弁護士の言うコトなんて、信じてはダメ！
English: Don't believe anything Mr. Wright says!

Japanese: 依頼人のためならば、平気でハレンチなウソをつく連中なのよ！
English: Defense attorneys make up the he most foul lies to defend their clients!

Japanese: はれんち‥‥（依頼人、本人に言われたぞ）
English: "Foul lies"...? (Imagine that, coming from my own client!)

Japanese: なんだか、急にあなたがはれんちに見えてきました。
English: Hmm... I guess you do seem the type who likes to twist the truth.
(Wait a minute...)

Japanese: （もしかしたら‥‥まだ）
English: (What if...)

Japanese: （ぼくたちは、ワナの中にいるのかもしれない！）
English: (we're still smack dab in the middle of Gant's trap!?)

Japanese: ど‥‥‥どうかしましたか？弁護人‥‥
English: Is something wrong, Mr. Wright?

Japanese: ‥‥ともえさんが‥‥正しかったのかもしれません。
English: Lana... may be right after all.

Japanese: どういうことだ、成歩堂！!
English: What do you mean, Wright?

Japanese: やっぱり、はれんちなんですか？成歩堂さん！
English: So you do tell foul lies then, Mr. Wright?

Japanese: ともえさん！もう一度！　証言してください！
English: Ms. Skye! Please testify once more!
But...

Japanese: ‥‥あなたの知らないところで《ねつ造》があったのならば。
English: If evidence was "fabricated" behind your back...

Japanese: 《罪門　直斗のイノチをうばったのは、宝月　茜である》‥‥
English: then Ema's accidental killing of Prosecutor Marshall...

Japanese: それ自体が、まやかしだった可能性がありますッ！
English: might also be a lie!

Japanese: で、でも。あたし‥‥たしかに。つき飛ばしたようなキオクが‥‥
English: B-but, I do remember knocking over Mr. Marshall...

Japanese: ともえさん！‥‥おねがいします！
English: Ms. Skye! If you will!

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥‥で、でも。やっぱり、私は‥‥...
English: I... I can't...

Japanese: 今は、恐れるべきではない。
English: There's nothing to be afraid of anymore.

Japanese: ‥‥！...!尋問の結果‥‥やはり、何も変わらないかもしれない。
English: This cross-examination may not change a thing.

Japanese: しかし！　可能性は、ある。あなたが‥‥《真実》を語れば！
English: However, there is a possibility that it will, if you tell the truth!
Very well.

Japanese: 証言させてください。私の見たコトを‥‥そのまま。
English: I'll testify... about what I really saw.

Japanese: それでは‥‥最後に、もう一度。証言をおねがいしましょう！
English: All right. The witness may testify once more, for the final time!

Japanese: ～本当に見たこと～
English: -- Actual Crime Scene --

Japanese: 現場では‥‥‥罪門検事の死体がヨロイの剣に刺さっていました。
English: When I arrived, I found Mr. Marshall's body impaled on that suit of armor's sword.

Japanese: そして‥‥その横に、あかねと青影がキゼツして、倒れていた‥‥
English: Ema and Darke were lying unconscious on the floor nearby.

Japanese: あの状況を見て、私。あの子が殺した‥‥そう思ったのです。
English: When I saw what had happened, I thought she... did it.

Japanese: だから、妹に結びつきそうな証拠はすべて、消し去りました。
English: That's why I erased all the evidence that linked her to the murder.

Japanese: 巌徒局長に手伝っていただいて、死体を剣から抜いて、運んで‥‥
English: I had Chief Gant help me remove the body from the sword and carry it...

Japanese: でも、すべてが《ねつ造》ならば。妹は、無実かもしれないのですね！
English: But if it all really was a fabrication, Ema might be innocent!

Japanese: 信じられない光景です。死体が‥‥ヨロイの剣に！
English: Unbelievable! The body was impaled on the armor's sword?

Japanese: ‥‥ザンネン、だ。
English: You were the only one who saw that. If only you had proof...

Japanese: それを見たのは、あなただけ。何か、証拠でもあれば‥‥‥‥‥‥‥‥‥‥...じつは‥‥あるのです。証拠ならば。
English: Actually, I do have proof.

Japanese: ‥‥！!今朝、コッソリおわたししました。‥‥成歩堂弁護士に。
English: I gave it to Mr. Wright just this morning.

Japanese: え！　ぼ、ぼくですか？
English: What? To me!?

Japanese: ２年前の、あの日。私が撮影した《現場写真》です。
English: It's a picture I took of the crime scene as I encountered it.

Japanese: もしかしたら‥‥必要になるかもしれないと思って。
English: I thought it might be needed.

Japanese: でも！　そんなもの、受け取ったおぼえは‥‥
English: But I don't remember receiving a picture like that...

Japanese: きっと、お姉ちゃん‥‥予想していたんだと思います。
English: Lana must have known...

Japanese: 成歩堂さんのチカラを、信じていたから‥‥
English: See. Mr. Wright? She really does have faith in you!

Japanese: それでは、弁護人。その《写真》を提出してください！
English: Very well, Mr. Wright. Please present this "picture"!

Japanese: （ともえさんから、写真なんて受け取ったおぼえはない‥‥）
English: (I don't remember receiving any pictures from Lana...)

Japanese: お姉ちゃん、“今朝、わたした”って言ってましたよね。
English: Lana said she gave it to you this morning, right?

Japanese: 今朝‥‥‥か。たしかに。何か、もらったような‥‥
English: I seem to remember getting something from her then...

Japanese: その証拠品！　もう一度、よく調べてみましょうよ！
English: Let's check that evidence again!

Japanese: きっと、どこかに。写真があるハズです！
English: There must be a picture in there somewhere!

Japanese: ‥‥こんなところに、写真が‥‥
English: Hey... There's a picture here!

Japanese: ひ‥‥‥ひどい‥‥‥
English: Oh... Oh my...

Japanese: これが‥‥本当の、サツジン現場！
English: This is... the actual crime scene...!

Japanese: ‥‥この光景を、ジッサイに見た捜査官は、存在しません。
English: No other detective saw the crime scene like this.

Japanese: すべて‥‥細工をしてから、刑事課に連絡を入れましたから。
English: That's because I contacted Criminal Affairs only after I rearranged the scene.

Japanese: 証拠品《巴の写真》を法廷記録に挟んだ。
English: Lana's Picture inserted in the Court Record.

Japanese: あッ、成歩堂さん！
English: *gasp* Mr. Wright!

Japanese: あの、ムネの切りぬかれた部分。もしかして‥‥‥！
English: That piece cut out from his vest! Could that be...

Japanese: （巌徒局長のキンコで見つけた、あの布きれ‥‥‥か！）
English: (The cloth we found inside Chief Gant's safe!)

Japanese: ‥‥なんだ？　コレ‥‥
English: What's this?

Japanese: これ、って‥‥手のアト、ですよね。
English: It's... a handprint.

Japanese: あの布きれ‥‥たしか、指紋がついてましたよね。
English: That cloth... It had fingerprints on it!

Japanese: その指紋のヌシこそが！きっと、ハンニンですよッ！
English: Whosever fingerprints those are must be the real murderer!

Japanese: （な‥‥‥なんてコトだ！あの布キレの指紋は‥‥）
English: (What!? But those fingerprints...)

Japanese: （あかねちゃん！キミのもの、なんだよ‥‥！）
English: (They're yours, Ema!)

Japanese: どうしたんですか？クチビル、ムラサキ色ですよ。
English: Why are your lips turning all purple, Mr. Wright?

Japanese: ‥‥とにかく、すべては尋問でアキラカになるだろう。
English: Anyway, let's get on with the cross-examination.

Japanese: あなたが、真実を述べていれば‥‥《真犯人》をいぶり出せる！
English: So long as you tell the truth, we should be able to flush out the real murderer.

Japanese: それでは、弁護人。‥‥尋問をおねがいします。
English: Very well. The defense may now begin its cross-examination.

Japanese: ～本当に見たこと～
English: -- Actual Crime Scene --

Japanese: 現場では‥‥‥罪門検事の死体がヨロイの剣に刺さっていました
English: When I arrived, I found Mr. Marshall's body impaled on that suit of armor's sword.

Japanese: ‥‥イイカゲンにしてくれないかなあ。
English: Come now, Udgey.

Japanese: こんな裁判、さあ。聞いたことがないよ。
English: This is the poorest excuse for a trial I've ever seen!

Japanese: 巌徒局長‥‥！
English: Chief Gant...

Japanese: ナニ？　ボクをワルモノに仕立てあげよう、ってワケかな。
English: What, now you want to make me out as the bad guy too?

Japanese: それは、ちょっとボクにも言わせてもらいたいなぁ。
English: If so, I'd like to put in a word or two in my defense.

Japanese: ‥‥引っこんでいてもらおうか。
English: I'm afraid it's too late for that.

Japanese: なんだって‥‥？
English: What?

Japanese: あなたは、この審理における証言を、すでに拒否している。
English: You already declined testimony.

Japanese: 逆に言えば‥‥あらゆる発言の権利を、自ら捨てたのだよ。
English: That means you forfeited your right to make statements of any sort.

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥‥‥...（発言拒否の“キケン”‥‥‥こういうコトだったのか！）
English: (This must be the "risk" the judge was talking about...)

Japanese: まあ、ゆっくり座って、だまって聞いていていただこうか。
English: Just sit back, relax...

Japanese: ご自分のクビにかかったロープが、ゆっくり締まっていく音を‥‥
English: and enjoy the sound of the noose tightening around your own neck.

Japanese: く‥‥‥く。くそおおおおおおッ！‥‥‥‥‥‥‥‥‥‥なんて、ね。
English: GGGCCKCKCCKKKK...! ... Ah, so what? You think I'm worried?

Japanese: それで‥‥‥ナニ？追いつめたつもり？　ボクをさ。‥‥！...!ザンネンだけどね。そうは行かないんだよ。
English: Sorry to disappoint you,

Japanese: だって‥‥ボク。発言する必要なんて、ないから。
English: but I don't need to make any statements.

Japanese: ‥‥どういうコト、だろうか。
English: What do you mean?

Japanese: 《証拠品》‥‥さ。
English: The evidence will do all the talking for me.

Japanese: 発言はできなくても、証拠品を提示するコトは、できる。
English: Even if I can't testify, I can still present evidence.

Japanese: それは、そうですが‥‥‥あ！　まさか、巌徒局長ッ！
English: Yes, that's true... Wait! You mean...

Japanese: あなたはまだ、何か決定的な証拠品を‥‥
English: you still have some conclusive evidence?

Japanese: 持ってないよ、ボク自身は。‥‥今は、もう。
English: No, I don't. But someone does.

Japanese: “あなた自身は”‥‥？
English: "Someone"...?

Japanese: でも‥‥‥ねえ。ナルホドちゃんさあ？
English: So then... what's your excuse Wrighto?

Japanese: ‥‥！...!さっきから‥‥なんでダマってるのかな？
English: Why have you been keeping quiet about it?

Japanese: ‥‥キミさァ。たしか、持ってるハズだよね？
English: You do have something to show us, right?

Japanese: 《ナオトちゃんをつきとばしたのがいったい、ダレなのか‥‥？》
English: Something that proves who knocked over Neil Marshall, causing his death.

Japanese: ハッキリ立証している、決定的な証拠を、さァ！
English: Conclusive evidence, that leaves no room for doubt!

Japanese: そ。そうなのですか、弁護人！
English: I-is this true, Mr. Wright!?

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥‥‥...（今、“あれ”を提出すれば、まちがいなく‥‥）
English: (If I show that piece of evidence now...)

Japanese: （罪門検事を殺害したのは、《宝月　茜》というコトになる！）
English: (Ema's sure to be made out as the murderer!)

Japanese: 弁護人！　証拠品があるのならば、提出を命じます！
English: Mr. Wright! If you have any more evidence, present it now! And if you try to conceal

Japanese: 不当にかくそうとした場合‥‥重罪に問われるコトになりますぞ！
English: anything... you will be the one appearing before the Board of Inquiries!

Japanese: （‥‥どうする‥‥）
English: (What do I do now!?)

Japanese: （ここは、最も重要なポイントだ。ミスは、ゆるされない‥‥！）
English: (I'd better think this through carefully. I can't afford to make the wrong decision!)

Japanese: （ぼくは、あの証拠品を提示するべきなのか‥‥？）
English: (Should I present that piece of evidence?)

Japanese: （罪門　直斗検事を殺害した《真犯人》を示す証拠品‥‥）
English: (The one that shows who really killed Prosecutor Marshall?)

Japanese: ‥‥わかりました。証拠品を提出します。
English: Yes, Your Honor. I do have further evidence.

Japanese: いよいよ、“アレ”の出番ですね！もしかしたら‥‥
English: All right! The time's finally come to show it to them!

Japanese: あの指紋、局長さんのモノかもしれないですよ！
English: Those prints have got to be the Chief's!

Japanese: それでは！　証拠品の提示をおねがいします！
English: Now then, let's see this "conclusive" evidence!

Japanese: 罪門検事を殺害した人物を示す《決定的な証拠》とは！
English: The evidence that shows who actually murdered Prosecutor Marshall!

Japanese: （‥‥ダメだ！　やっぱり‥‥）
English: (No... I can't! I can't show them that yet!)

Japanese: （今、あれを提出するワケにはいかないッ！）ど、どうして！カンタンじゃないですか！
English: Why not? You know which item to show!

Japanese: ‥‥成歩堂。
English: Mr. Wright.

Japanese: 迷いがあるのならば‥‥もう一度、よく考えることだ。
English: If you're not sure, then think it through again...

Japanese: その、証拠品。いかにして、使うか？
English: How can you use that evidence to your advantage?

Japanese: これは‥‥カワの衣服の一部、ですかな？
English: That's... a piece of leather clothing?

Japanese: おそらく、被害者のムネから切り取られたものです。
English: Yes. It most likely was cut from the victim's vest, near his chest.

Japanese: な‥‥‥なんだコレはッ！
English: What's this!?

Japanese: ハッキリと、《手のアト》が残っているではないかッ！
English: There's a big handprint on it!

Japanese: つき飛ばしたときに、付着した‥‥そう考えるべき、だよね。
English: Surely it must have been left on the cloth by whoever shoved the victim into the sword.

Japanese: いったい！この指紋のヌシは‥‥！
English: What? Who's fingerprints are on this!?

Japanese: トーゼン‥‥調べたよね？ナルホドちゃん‥‥
English: I'm sure Wrighto has checked, haven't you?

Japanese: ‥‥‥！...!ダレなのですかッ、成歩堂くん！
English: Well? Whose are they!?
...They're Ms. Skye's. Ms. Ema Skye's.

Japanese: え‥‥‥ッ！あ。あたし‥‥‥？
English: What!? They're mine...?

Japanese: やっぱり‥‥あたし、だったの‥‥！
English: So I really did do it...

Japanese: ね‥‥‥？あったでしょ。決定的な証拠が‥‥さ。
English: See? I told you it was conclusive.

Japanese: し。しかし！　これは、あなたの金庫の中にあった！
English: But this was found in your safe!

Japanese: あなたの《ねつ造》である可能性が‥‥！
English: That means it's possible you forged it!

Japanese: ‥‥なに、それ。
English: I don't know what you're talking about.

Japanese: ！!ボクの金庫に、そんな布きれが入っていたって？
English: I don't remember any cloth in my safe.

Japanese: ‥‥そんなの。このボクが認めるワケ、ないじゃない。
English: Do you really expect me to believe that?

Japanese: で、でも‥‥！
English: But...!

Japanese: ‥‥おしまいだ、成歩堂。ここまで来て、ザンネンだが。
English: Give it up, Mr. Wright. It's over.

Japanese: ！!この《証拠品》は、提出するべきではなかった。
English: You shouldn't have presented that...

Japanese: ‥‥それによって、キミは認めてしまったのだよ。
English: By presenting that evidence,

Japanese: 罪門　直斗の死と、宝月　茜の《関連性》を‥‥
English: you tied Ema Skye to Neil Marshall's death.

Japanese: そんな‥‥‥
English: No...

Japanese: ついに‥‥‥ここまで、のようですな。
English: It appears we have our killer.

Japanese: し‥‥‥‥‥‥‥‥しまったぁぁぁぁぁぁぁぁぁぁッ！
English: NNNO...NOOOOOO!!!

Japanese: ‥‥それが、すべての《ポイント》だった‥‥
English: Everything hinged on that point.

Japanese: ‥‥ともえさんは、すべての罪を、ヒトリで引き受けて‥‥
English: In the end, Lana was found guilty.

Japanese: 現時点で‥‥提出できる証拠はありません。
English: Your Honor, I don't have any evidence I can present at this point in time.

Japanese: バカなッ！ふ‥‥ふざけるなッ！
English: What!? You lie!

Japanese: 巌徒局長‥‥？
English: Chief Gant?

Japanese: お。オマエ、ボクの金庫を開けたじゃないか！
English: You... you opened my safe! I know you took what was inside!

Japanese: あの中に‥‥あの中に、入っていたはずだ！決定的な証拠がァァァァァッ！
English: The conclusive evidence!

Japanese: ‥‥なんのことでしょう？
English: I don't know what you're talking about.

Japanese: な、成歩堂さん！　どうして！
English: Mr. Wright! Why don't you show them?

Japanese: あたしたち、見つけたじゃないですか！
English: We found it together!

Japanese: そうか‥‥わかった！‥‥オマエ、知ってるんだな？
English: Oh, I see. It's because you know the truth, don't you?

Japanese: あの《指紋》のイミを。だから、提出しないワケか‥‥
English: You know whose fingerprints are on it. That's why you won't present it!

Japanese: 巌徒局長ッ！　いったい‥‥なんのコトだッ！
English: What are you talking about, Chief Gant!?

Japanese: ‥‥聞くまでもないだろう？この現場写真、よく見てみなよ。
English: Can't you figure it out? Take a good look at this picture.

Japanese: 被害者のジャンパー‥‥その、ムネの部分をさぁ。
English: See the victim's vest? Notice anything odd about the chest area?

Japanese: ‥‥どうやら、切り取られているようですが‥‥
English: It looks like part of it's been cut off for some reason.

Japanese: もしや、これは‥‥！あなたのシワザ、なのかッ！
English: You mean, you had this...? In your safe?

Japanese: ま、まさかッ！　それこそ、不正捜査ではないですかッ！
English: What!? That means you... the Chief of Police... concealing evidence!

Japanese: 警察局長が、そんな‥‥‥大スキャンダルですぞッ！
English: This is going to be the biggest scandal in the history of the Police Department!!!
Impressive...

Japanese: 正直なトコロ、ここまでやるとは思わなかったよ。
English: To be honest, I didn't think you had the gall, Wrighto.

Japanese: ‥‥！...!でも‥‥さすがにね。殺人犯にされちゃ、たまらないでしょ？
English: Well, I can't just let you pin me up as the murderer.

Japanese: ‥‥いいかな。話してあげちゃおうか。ゼンブ。
English: I'll tell you what really happened.

Japanese: な、なんというコトでしょう‥‥あなたが‥‥認めるとはッ！
English: What!? You mean you admit to it!?

Japanese: たしかに‥‥ボクはあの日、最初に現場を発見したんだよ。
English: I was the first person to arrive at the crime scene that day.

Japanese: そして‥‥あの状況を利用して、彼女を操るシナリオを考えたんだ。
English: It then occurred to me that I could use the situation to control Lana.

Japanese: やっぱり‥‥‥あなたが、お姉ちゃんを！
English: So you really were manipulating her!

Japanese: ‥‥あのとき。ボクには、カクシンがあったよ。
English: I knew Lana.

Japanese: そこの妹ちゃんに、ウタガイがかかるようにしておけば‥‥
English: If I made it look like the blame lay with her sister...

Japanese: 現場を見つけた宝月　巴は、必ずボクに相談してくる、って。
English: That when she saw the scene, she would ask me for my aid.

Japanese: それで、あなたは‥‥宝月　巴に“協力”したワケか！
English: So you "assisted" Ms. Skye!

Japanese: 彼女に指示して、すべての証拠を作りかえたんだ。
English: I told her to arrange all the evidence.

Japanese: ナイフの刃を埋めこんだり、死体を移動させたり‥‥
English: I had her plant the knife tip in the victim's body, and move the body across the room.

Japanese: その証拠を使って、この私は‥‥青影　丈の有罪を立証したのかッ！
English: And I ended up using that evidence to get Joe Darke convicted!

Japanese: ‥‥でもね。あの《工作》をしたとき‥‥
English: When we rearranged the crime scene,

Japanese: ボク、２つだけ。証拠品をかくしておいたんだよ。
English: I hid two pieces of evidence.

Japanese: ‥‥トモエちゃんが現場に現れる前に、ね。
English: I did this before Lana arrived at the scene.

Japanese: “２つの証拠品”‥‥それが、あの金庫の中身ですね！
English: Two pieces of evidence... You mean those items in your safe!

Japanese: なぜ、そのようなことを！
English: But... why?

Japanese: 決まってるでしょ？‥‥“保険”だよ。
English: For insurance, of course.

Japanese: ほ‥‥“ホケン”？
English: "Insurance"...?

Japanese: 計画には自信はあったケド。万が一、ということがあるよね？
English: I was sure my plan would work, but it's always best to be prepared for the worst.

Japanese: イザとなって、妹ちゃんの罪を着せられちゃ、たまんないから。
English: I wasn't about to let anyone blame me for a murder that girl committed.

Japanese: 《ねつ造》をしながら‥‥そこまで考えていたのですかッ！
English: You mean you were calculating that far ahead while forging the evidence!?

Japanese: ‥‥ダレだと思ってるのかな。警察局長にまで、なったオトコよ？
English: Who do you take me for, a fool? I didn't make police chief by dumb luck.

Japanese: ホラ。この、ツボのカケラ。
English: See this jar fragment?

Japanese: 《茜》という文字の、イチバン目立つ部分をかくしておいたわけ。
English: I hid the most legible part of Ema's name.

Japanese: まさか彼女が、血をふきとっちゃうとは思わなかったけどねー。
English: I didn't expect Lana to go and wipe the blood off all the pieces.

Japanese: しかし！　すべてが証人の《ねつ造》であるならば！
English: But if you fabricated all the evidence...

Japanese: このツボの文字も、ホンモノの手がかりとはかぎらないッ！
English: what's to say you didn't fabricate the message on this jar, too?

Japanese: ‥‥クックックッ‥‥
English: Ho ho ho...

Japanese: やだね。そうやって、なんでもヒトのせいにするヤツ‥‥
English: Some people just don't know when to quit, do they?

Japanese: そんなのもいると思ってさ。‥‥もう１つの《保険》があるの。
English: That's why I kept one more item for "insurance."

Japanese: それが‥‥切り取られた布きれ、かな？
English: You mean that piece of cloth?

Japanese: そういうワケね。
English: Come on, Wrighto. Cough it up, already. I know you have it.

Japanese: ‥‥もう、いいからさ。提出しちゃいな。ナルホドちゃん！‥‥‥‥‥‥‥‥‥...成歩堂さん！出しちゃいましょうよ！
English: What are you waiting for, Mr. Wright?

Japanese: ‥‥巌徒局長。あなたは‥‥認めるのですか？
English: So you admit to it then, Chief Gant?

Japanese: ご自分の金庫の中に、切り取った布きれをかくしていた‥‥と。
English: That you were hiding the cloth you cut off the victim's vest in your safe?

Japanese: ‥‥さすがに、ボクも殺人者にはなりたくないからねー。
English: Yes, I admit it. I didn't want to have to do that, being Chief and all,

Japanese: 局長のイスは、おしいけど‥‥認めてあげるさ。《ねつ造》を。
English: but it's a lot better than being portrayed as a murderer!

Japanese: 弁護人！　どうなのですか！たしか、先ほどは‥‥
English: Well, Mr. Wright? What do you have to say for yourself?

Japanese: 《提出できる証拠はない》とハッキリ、言っていたハズです！
English: Just a moment ago, you said you didn't have any evidence you could present.

Japanese: ‥‥ホント、バカだよねェ‥‥さっさと出せばよかったのに。
English: Foolish move, Wrighto. You should have shown it then before it was too late.

Japanese: （長い戦いだった‥‥でも）
English: (It's been a long battle...)

Japanese: （ここが、正念場だ！この一瞬こそが、勝負！）
English: (but the moment of truth has finally arrived!)

Japanese: （ここで、判断をまちがわなければ‥‥これで、勝てるハズ！）
English: (As long as I don't mess up here, victory is mine!)

Japanese: （ダメだ！　今さら、証拠を提出できるハズがない！）
English: (No! It's too late to show the evidence now!)

Japanese: （それに、今。“アレ”を提出してしまったら‥‥）
English: (Besides, even if I did...)

Japanese: （あの子の《指紋》があかるみに出るだけだ！）
English: (it'd just expose that poor girl's fingerprints!)

Japanese: ‥‥成歩堂。状況を、よく考えるといい。
English: Wright. Think hard over the circumstances.

Japanese: じょ、状況‥‥？
English: The circumstances...?

Japanese: “さっき”と、“今”‥‥
English: I'm talking about "then" and "now."

Japanese: １つだけ、ハッキリちがうポイントがある。
English: There's one major difference between the two.

Japanese: キミは、その“ポイント”を待っていたのではないか？
English: Weren't you waiting for that difference?

Japanese: （見抜いていたのか！　ぼくのたった１つの《作戦》‥‥）
English: (Edgeworth figured out my plan!)

Japanese: 成歩堂さん！　もう‥‥今しか、ないみたいですよ！
English: Mr. Wright! This is your only chance!

Japanese: ‥‥わかりました。証拠品を提出します！
English: Your Honor, I do have evidence to present now.

Japanese: それでは！　証拠品の提示をおねがいしましょう。
English: All right then, let's see this "conclusive" evidence!

Japanese: 罪門検事を殺害した人物を示す《決定的な証拠》とは！
English: The evidence that shows who actually murdered Prosecutor Marshall!

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥‥‥‥‥いかがですか、御剣検事。...
English: Mr. Edgeworth? Your opinion, please?

Japanese: ‥‥冗談はウマくなったが、弁護はヘタになったようだ。
English: I think Mr. Wright's humor has improved... at the expense of his defense.

Japanese: ベンゴのウデをみがくようにッ！
English: Better polish up your defense, Mr. Wright!

Japanese: か‥‥カンタンじゃないですか！
English: Come on, Mr. Wright!

Japanese: ハンニンの手のアトがついてる、“アレ”ですよね？
English: You remember that thing with the murderer's handprint on it, don't you?

Japanese: まわり道は、もうゴメンです。もう一度だけ、うかがいますよ。
English: There's no way around it, Mr. Wright. I'll ask you just one more time.

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥‥‥...‥‥もう一度だけカクニンします。
English: Let me verify this once more.

Japanese: この布きれは、事件当日。あなた自身が、その手で‥‥
English: On the day of the crime,

Japanese: 被害者のベストから切り取ったもの、なんですね？
English: you personally cut out this piece of the victim's vest?

Japanese: あー、そうだともさ！ついに、出しちゃったなあ、それ。
English: Oh, yes! At last you've finally brought it out into the open.

Japanese: こ、この《布きれ》‥‥手のアトが残っていますッ！
English: There's a handprint on this piece of cloth!

Japanese: 裁判長！　至急、それを鑑識に回していただきたいッ！
English: Your Honor! The prosecution requests that be immediately sent to the lab for analysis!

Japanese: カワの布地に残った、この“手のアト”‥‥
English: This handprint on the leather...

Japanese: かなりの衝撃がなければ、こんなにハッキリ残らない！
English: There must have been a strong impact for it to be left so clearly!

Japanese: と、いうことは！この、布きれは‥‥？!
English: You mean...

Japanese: 《ねつ造》の入る余地のない、決定的な証拠と断言できるッ！
English: It could not have been forged. It must be authentic, conclusive evidence!

Japanese: ‥‥クックックッ‥‥
English: Ho ho ho...

Japanese: アマい。御剣ちゃん、まだまだ、アマいなー。
English: You're as slow on the uptake as ever, Worthy!

Japanese: な。なんだろうか。
English: What?

Japanese: 考えてみなよ。ナルホドちゃんが、どうして‥‥
English: Think about it. Wrighto had all this time to present this evidence...

Japanese: 今まで、コイツを提出しようとしなかったのか‥‥？
English: Yet he was reluctant to do so. Why would that be?

Japanese: そ‥‥そう、か！キミは、知っているのだな？...!
English: You mean you already know?

Japanese: この《布きれ》に付着した指紋のヌシが‥‥ダレなのか！
English: You know whose fingerprints are on that?

Japanese: そうなんですか！　成歩堂さん！
English: M-Mr. Wright... Do you really know?

Japanese: この指紋のヌシこそは、罪門検事を殺害した、真犯人です！
English: Whoever the fingerprints belong to must be the real murderer!

Japanese: いったい‥‥ダレの指紋だったのですかッ！
English: Whose fingerprints are they!?

Japanese: ‥‥わかりました。ご報告します。
English: Very well. I'll tell you.

Japanese: （大丈夫‥‥のハズ、だ。ここまでは‥‥予想どおり！）
English: (It should be okay now. Everything's proceeding as predicted.)

Japanese: その布きれに付着した、指紋の持ち主は‥‥ッ！
English: The person whom these fingerprints belong to are...

Japanese: （ダメだ！　こんなところでウソをついても‥‥）
English: (No, it's no use lying here.)

Japanese: （調べれば、イッパツでバレてしまう！）
English: (They'll discover the truth the second they analyze this.)

Japanese: どうしたの？　ナルホドちゃん。早く、教えてほしいなあ‥‥
English: What's the matter, Mr. Wright? Hurry up and tell us!

Japanese: ‥‥弁護人。あなたにもわかっているハズでしょう。
English: You do know, don't you, Mr. Wright?

Japanese: ほ‥‥‥‥‥‥‥‥宝月　茜さん、ですかッ！
English: Ema? Ema Skye!?

Japanese: え‥‥‥‥ええええええッ！　あ、あたし‥‥
English: What!? They're mine...!?

Japanese: ‥‥ゴメン、あかねちゃん‥‥
English: I'm sorry, Ema...

Japanese: 成歩堂さん。どうして‥‥
English: But why...

Japanese: どうして、今まで教えてくれなかったんですかっ！
English: Why didn't you tell me!?

Japanese: あーっはっはっはっはっ！
English: Oh ho ho ho!

Japanese: まったく、キミはケッサクだよ。ナルホドちゃん！
English: You're really something, Wrighto!

Japanese: その子がやったってコト、知っていたクセにさぁ。
English: You knew this girl did it all along,

Japanese: このボクに、そのツミを押しつけようなんてねェ‥‥
English: and you still tried to pin the murder on me!

Japanese: 非常に、ザンネンですが‥‥
English: So it's true. Tragic, but true.

Japanese: やはり、その少女が‥‥罪門検事をつき飛ばしたようです。
English: This girl really did shove Prosecutor Marshall to his death.

Japanese: なんてヒトなの、成歩堂弁護士！
English: How could you!? You... you monster!!!

Japanese: ‥‥ひ、被告人‥‥
English: Ms. Skye...

Japanese: あなたは、指紋のコトを知っていた！　それなのに‥‥
English: You knew whose fingerprints those were all along, yet you...

Japanese: さも、希望があるようなコトを言って‥‥
English: you acted like she really didn't...

Japanese: ともえさん。‥‥まだ、なんですよ。
English: Ms. Skye. It's not over yet...
What!?

Japanese: まだ‥‥‥この法廷は終わっていない。
English: I said this trial isn't over yet.

Japanese: ハッ！　ザンネンだけどさ。もう、終わったんだよ。ゼンブ。
English: Ha! But I'm afraid it is over, boy! Not only this trial...

Japanese: この法廷も‥‥キミ自身の、弁護士生命も、ね。
English: but your career too!

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥‥‥...キミは、この決定的な証拠品を“故意に”かくそうとしたよねェ。
English: You purposely concealed this conclusive evidence.

Japanese: それは、リッパな犯罪なワケ。しかも‥‥重罪だ。
English: That, my friend, is a serious offense.

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥‥‥...楽しみだねえ‥‥判決のあと。キミを訴えてやるさ。
English: I'm looking forward to pressing charges after the defendant is convicted.

Japanese: かならず、バッジを取りあげてやるよ！
English: I'll have your badge, boy!

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥‥‥...どうしたのかな？ダマりこんじゃってさあ。
English: What's the matter, cat got your tongue?

Japanese: ゼヒ、感想を聞かせてほしいんだけどね。
English: Aren't you going to tell us how it feels?

Japanese: 自分の手で、カワイイ妹ちゃんを殺人者にした、その感想をさあ‥‥
English: How it feels to be the one who single-handedly turned a poor little girl into a murderer!?
Before I do that...

Japanese: ひとつだけ、ハッキリさせておきましょうか。
English: there's just one little thing I have to clear up.

Japanese: な、なんだよ？
English: Oh? And what's that?

Japanese: もちろん‥‥‥《真犯人》ですよ。罪門　直斗殺害事件の、ね。
English: Who really killed Prosecutor Neil Marshall.

Japanese: な、なんですって！
English: What!?

Japanese: ‥‥巌徒局長。たしかに、あなたは正しい。全面的に。
English: Chief Gant, you are absolutely right.

Japanese: コイツは、完全に立証している。ダレが、罪門　直斗を殺害したか？
English: This piece of cloth proves who the real murderer is. Who killed Neil Marshall, you ask?

Japanese: だから！それは、宝月　茜さん、では‥‥？
English: It was Ema Skye, wasn't it?

Japanese: ‥‥あり得ないんですよ。なぜならば。
English: I'm afraid that's not possible.

Japanese: この布きれは、決定的に‥‥《ムジュン》しているからです。
English: You see, this piece of cloth contains a critical contradiction!

Japanese: ば、バカなッ！ムジュン‥‥だと？
English: What!? A contradiction!?

Japanese: こ、このオトコ‥‥‥今さら、ナニを言い出すんだ！
English: What is this fool babbling about!?

Japanese: そう、《ムジュン》です！そして、それこそが‥‥
English: I'm talking about a contradiction. One that proves...

Japanese: まちがいなく、真犯人を指し示しているッ！
English: who the real killer is!

Japanese: な‥‥成歩堂くん！この、布きれが‥‥
English: M-Mr. Wright! This piece of cloth...

Japanese: いったい！　ナニとムジュンしているのですかッ！
English: What could it possibly contradict!?

Japanese: （巌徒局長‥‥これが、トドメの一撃だ！）
English: (Chief Gant, your tyrannical reign ends here!)

Japanese: この布きれと《ムジュン》する証拠品‥‥ごらんくださいッ！
English: Behold! The piece of evidence that contradicts this cloth!

Japanese: あーっはっはっはっはっ！
English: Oh ho ho ho!

Japanese: やっぱり、キミは楽しいね。ナルホドちゃん！
English: It's fun watching you squirm, Wrighto!

Japanese: たしかに‥‥この証拠品。ムジュンしているようですな。
English: This piece of evidence indeed seems to contradict something...

Japanese: そう。あなたのイイカゲンな発言とッ！
English: like your outward appearance of mental sanity!!!

Japanese: （ナニをやってるんだ！）
English: (What am I doing!?)

Japanese: （この布きれ、罪門検事が死んだ“後”に切り取られたなら‥‥）
English: (If this cloth was cut from Prosecutor Marshall's vest after he died...)

Japanese: （アキラカに、オカシイところがあるじゃないか！）
English: (Then something's definitely not right about it!)

Japanese: ‥‥ここまで来たら、今度こそ後もどりはできない‥‥
English: It's too late to turn back now.

Japanese: 決着をつけることができるのはキミだけだ。成歩堂　龍一！
English: You're the only one who can put an end to this, Wright!

Japanese: こ、これは‥‥‥？
English: And what exactly is this supposed to be...?

Japanese: ともえさんが撮影した写真です。‥‥よく見てください。
English: This is the picture Ms. Skye took. Take a good look at it.

Japanese: 被害者‥‥罪門さんのベストが切り取られた部分を！
English: See where the piece of his vest was cut out?

Japanese: これは‥‥‥シャツ、が見えていますな。
English: Yes... His shirt is showing underneath.

Japanese: 血でよごれていて、よくわかりませんが‥‥
English: It's hard to make out with all the blood on his vest, though.

Japanese: ‥‥そう。胸もとが血でよごれている‥‥当然です。
English: Exactly my point. His chest is soaked with blood. That's only natural.

Japanese: 肺を刺されたのでしょう。クチから、血が流れ出しています。
English: His lungs no doubt were punctured. Blood poured out of his mouth.

Japanese: あ！　で、でも！その布きれ‥‥見せてください！
English: Oh! But that piece of cloth...

Japanese: 血が‥‥‥‥ついていない！
English: Wait... There's no blood on it!

Japanese: あ‥‥‥ああああああっ！
English: AAAAAAAHHHHHH!!!

Japanese: この《布きれ》に、宝月　茜の指紋がついている以上‥‥
English: Since Ema Skye's fingerprints are on this cloth,

Japanese: 彼女が、検事をつき飛ばしたのはまちがいないでしょう。
English: there's no doubt that she shoved the prosecutor aside!

Japanese: ‥‥しかし。
English: However!

Japanese: このとき！　罪門検事は剣には刺さらなかったのです！
English: Mr. Marshall was not impaled on the sword!

Japanese: ちょ‥‥ちょっと待てッ！なんだよ、それ‥‥
English: No! Th-this is nonsense!

Japanese: さて。‥‥ここで、巌徒局長。あなたにお聞きしましょう。
English: Now then, Chief Gant. Let me ask you something.

Japanese: つき飛ばされた罪門検事は、剣には刺さらなかった。
English: Prosecutor Marshall was not impaled when he was shoved aside.

Japanese: おそらく、床にアタマを打ちつけて、キゼツしたのだろう。
English: He most likely hit his head on the ground and was knocked out.

Japanese: ‥‥それならば‥‥いったい、ダレなのでしょうか？
English: If so, then tell me. Who could it have been?

Japanese: 宝月　巴さんが現場に現れるよりも、“前”。
English: Who could have arrived at the scene before Ms. Skye,

Japanese: キゼツした罪門　直斗の身体を床から持ち上げて‥‥
English: picked up the unconscious prosecutor,

Japanese: ヨロイの剣に、つき刺すことができたのはッ！
English: and impaled him on the armor's sword?

Japanese: う‥‥‥ううううう‥‥ッ！
English: Hnnngngghgghh...

Japanese: また、その“ハンニン”は、宝月　茜に罪を着せるため‥‥
English: Then, to make it look like Ema was responsible for the prosecutor's death,

Japanese: 被害者の血で、ツボに《茜》の文字を書いた。
English: said person proceeded to write her name on the jar with the victim's blood.

Japanese: そして‥‥そのツボを割り、“手がかり”を残しておいた。
English: Then he broke the jar on purpose, to leave behind a clue,

Japanese: 宝月　巴に、《妹の犯行》だと思いこませるために！
English: and make Lana believe her sister did it!

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥‥‥‥...おぼえていますね？あなたは、ハッキリ認めている。
English: Remember what you admitted only moments ago?

Japanese: この、血のついていない布きれを、その手で“切り取った”ことを。
English: That you personally cut out this bloodless piece of the victim's vest?

Japanese: ‥‥つまり！
English: Ironic, isn't it?

Japanese: あなたは、自らに“保険”をかける、その行動で‥‥
English: Through the very act of creating "insurance,"

Japanese: 自らがハンニンであることを、カンペキに立証したのですッ！
English: you proved that you were the actual murderer!

Japanese: ぬ‥‥‥‥‥‥‥‥‥‥‥‥‥‥おおおおおおおおおおおおおおおおおおおおおおおおおおおおおッ！
English: NNNNNNOOOOOOOOOOOOO OOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOO OOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOO!!!

Japanese: （‥‥終わった‥‥か）
English: (It's finished...)
Heh...

Japanese: ‥‥フッフッフッ‥‥
English: Heh heh heh!

Japanese: あーっはっはっはっはっはっはっ！
English: Oh ho ho ho ho ho ho!

Japanese: 惜しかったよ‥‥ナルホドちゃん！もう少し、だったのにねェ‥‥
English: That was close, Wrighto! You almost had me!

Japanese: ‥‥‥！!ザンネンだけど‥‥ダメだよ。そ、そうさ。認められないよ。
English: Sorry, but you'll have to do better than that. I refute your allegations.

Japanese: どういうイミ、でしょうか。“認められない”‥‥？
English: What do you mean, you "refute" his allegations?

Japanese: その布きれ、さァ。‥‥そいつは《違法な証拠》だッ！
English: You see, that piece of cloth... is illegal evidence!

Japanese: 静粛に！　静粛にッ！な、ナニを言い出すのですかッ！
English: Order! Order! What nonsense is this!?

Japanese: い‥‥違法な証拠を使って、断罪するコトは、認められない！
English: Illegal evidence cannot be used to convict a suspect!

Japanese: お、おぼえてるだろ、チョーさん！
English: Remember, Udgey?

Japanese: コイツ‥‥このベンゴシは、一度！アレをかくそうとしたんだ！
English: Earlier, ol' Wrighto here concealed that piece of cloth!

Japanese: でも‥‥‥ねえ。ナルホドちゃんさあ？
English: So then... what's your excuse Wrighto?

Japanese: キミ。持ってるはずだよね？‥‥決定的な証拠品を、さァ。
English: You do have some conclusive evidence, don't you?

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥‥‥...現時点で‥‥提出できる証拠はありません。
English: Your Honor, I don't have any evidence I can present at this point in time.

Japanese: た‥‥‥たしかにッ！
English: Well, that's true...

Japanese: 弁護人は一度、証拠の提示を拒否していますッ！
English: The defense did refuse to present evidence!

Japanese: その時点で‥‥その布きれは、《正当な証拠》にはならないさ！
English: At that moment, that piece of cloth ceased to be "legal evidence"!

Japanese: そ‥‥そんなああ‥‥ッ！
English: But that's not fair...!

Japanese: うふ。うふふ。うふふふふふふふ。ザンネンだったよねえ‥‥
English: Hoo hoo hoo hooooo! Did you actually think you could best me in court?

Japanese: ‥‥このボクに、法廷戦術で勝てると思っていたのかなァ。これが本当の、《最後の大逆転》ってヤツだね！
English: It looks like the last laugh's on you, son!

Japanese: ザンネンながら。巌徒氏の主張は、法律的には、カンペキに正しい。
English: I'm afraid Mr. Gant's claim is legally correct.

Japanese: いかがですかな？　御剣検事。
English: Well, Mr. Edgeworth?

Japanese: たしかに‥‥違法な証拠で容疑者を断罪することはできない。
English: True... Illegal evidence cannot be used to convict a person...

Japanese: 本当に、それが“違法なら”のハナシだが‥‥
English: assuming, of course, that the evidence is indeed illegal.

Japanese: ふ、ふむう‥‥‥？いかがですかな、弁護人！
English: Hmm? Well, Mr. Wright?
(It seems... at last...)

Japanese: （今回の作戦の“真意”を明かすときが、来たみたいだな‥‥）
English: (The time for me to reveal my plan has finally arrived.)

Japanese: ‥‥弁護人。あなたは、認めるのですかな？
English: Mr. Wright. Do you admit to it?

Japanese: 故意に、あの《布きれ》をかくしていたコトを‥‥！
English: That you purposefully and illegally concealed this piece of cloth?

Japanese: （‥‥ダメだ！　ここで“違法”だと認めたら‥‥）
English: (No! If I admit to that...)

Japanese: （今までの“布石”がすべて、ムダになってしまう！）
English: (all my planning will have been for nothing!)

Japanese: どうやら‥‥
English: It seems...

Japanese: 弁護人は、自分の行動のイミをわかっていなかったようですな。
English: Mr. Wright didn't understand the implication of his conduct.

Japanese: ‥‥体勢を立てなおすならば。今しかないぞ、成歩堂‥‥！
English: If you're going to change your position, this is your only chance, Wright!

Japanese: （そ‥‥そうだな）
English: (He's right...)

Japanese: たしかに‥‥ぼくは一度、証拠品の提示を拒否しました。
English: Certainly, I refused to present evidence at one point.

Japanese: ホラね！　ホラね！　つまり！その証拠品は《違法》に決まってる
English: Aha! So the evidence is illegal!

Japanese: ‥‥ちがうんですよ、巌徒さん。
English: No it isn't, Mr. Gant.
Huh?

Japanese: ぼくは、あのとき‥‥“提出しなかった”のではない。
English: It's not that I "didn't" present evidence then...

Japanese: “提出できなかった”のです。
English: it's that I "couldn't."

Japanese: ど‥‥どういうコトですかな？“提出できなかった”‥‥
English: What do you mean, you "couldn't"?

Japanese: 弁護側には、そのコンキョを提示する用意があります！
English: There are certain procedures involved when presenting evidence!

Japanese: ウソだッ！　チョーさん！こんなタワゴト、聞いちゃダメだ！
English: No, Udgey! Don't listen to his lies!

Japanese: ヒキョーなオトコなんだよ！だから、こんなヤツの言うコトは聞
English: He's nothing but a coward! You can't let him?

Japanese: この法廷‥‥‥最後に残された問題は、ただ１つ。
English: There is only one issue left to be resolved in this trial:

Japanese: この証拠品は、《違法》か、そうでないか？
English: Is this evidence legal or not?

Japanese: それでは‥‥今度こそ、これが最後です。
English: Very well. Let us settle this once and for all.

Japanese: あなたは一度、証拠品の提示を拒否しました。
English: Earlier you refused to present evidence.

Japanese: それが《違法》でなかったという、“コンキョ”を示してください！
English: If you can prove your conduct was not in violation of the law, then do so now!

Japanese: ‥‥どうやら‥‥
English: It seems...

Japanese: あなたの存在自体が《違法》だったようですな。
English: your very existence is "illegal."
Huh...?

Japanese: ‥‥キミには失望したぞ、成歩堂　龍一‥‥
English: You've lost it, Wright. All this time I thought you were using the Chief's

Japanese: 巌徒‥‥法律のプロのウラをかき、ワナにかけたと思っていたのだが。
English: knowledge of the law to trap him. It looks like you're the one who got trapped.

Japanese: やっぱり‥‥慣れないコト、しちゃダメなんですね。
English: I guess I should just stick to what I'm used to.

Japanese: ‥‥弁護士のセリフとは思えませんな。
English: I never expected to hear that coming from a defense attorney.

Japanese: （相手は、法律のスキマを抜ける、プロ‥‥）
English: (Gant's a pro at slipping through loopholes in the law...)

Japanese: （それに対抗する、武器は‥‥‥やはり、“法律”しかない！）
English: (Still, the law's the only weapon I can use to beat him!)

Japanese: ‥‥これが、そのコンキョです。《よくわかる証拠法入門》‥‥
English: This is my proof, Your Honor: "Evidence Law."

Japanese: ど‥‥どういうことだ‥‥
English: What's this?

Japanese: ぼくもベンキョウした、ということですよ‥‥局長さん。
English: I've done my homework too, Chief.

Japanese: たしかに、コイツには宝月　茜の指紋が残っていました。
English: Indeed, Ema Skye's fingerprints were on this piece of cloth.

Japanese: ‥‥しかし！
English: However!

Japanese: その時点では、これはただの《布きれ》にすぎなかったのです。
English: At that point in time, this was merely a piece of cloth, nothing more.
What?

Japanese: この本に、ハッキリ書いてある！証拠法の《２大原則》‥‥ですよ。
English: You see, it's written right here in this book: The second rule of evidence law!

Japanese: な。ナニを、バカな‥‥‥...!《その１：警察局の認可のない証拠品の提示は認められない》
English: Rule 1: no evidence shall be shown without the approval of the Police Department!

Japanese: これは、ぼく自身が発見しました。‥‥あなたの金庫から。
English: I found this piece of evidence myself... inside your safe.

Japanese: したがって、当然‥‥警察局の認可はありません。
English: It goes without saying I did not have approval from the Police Department.

Japanese: 《その２：未登録の証拠の提示には審議中の事件との関連性が必要》
English: Rule 2: unregistered evidence presented must be relevant to the case in trial.

Japanese: そう‥‥ここが、ポイントでした。
English: And here is the crux of the matter.

Japanese: 《布きれ》と《ＳＬ９号事件》‥‥
English: You see, at the time it was impossible for me to prove the relevance...

Japanese: その２つの《関連性》を立証するのは‥‥不可能だったのです。
English: between the cloth and the SL-9 Incident.

Japanese: バカなッ！　そ、そんな‥‥そんなタワゴト、通用するかッ！
English: What!? What kind of nonsense is this!?

Japanese: そんな《関連性》なんか！この写真を見れば、イッパツで‥‥
English: You want "relevancy"? Just take one look at this picture and-

Japanese: ザンネンですが‥‥思い出してください。
English: Sorry, but can you recall... when was that picture presented?

Japanese: この写真が提出されたのは、いつのことだったでしょうか？たしか‥‥‥つい、さっきのコトです！
English: That was shown only a few moments ago!

Japanese: そ‥‥‥そんな、バカな‥‥‥
English: No...
He's right.

Japanese: 今朝、１０時。この法廷が開廷した時点では‥‥
English: At the beginning of today's trial,

Japanese: たしかに、その布きれは無価値だったのだ。
English: that piece of cloth was still meaningless.

Japanese: そこに“証拠品”という価値を与えてしまったのは‥‥
English: The person who gave it value as evidence...

Japanese: ‥‥巌徒　海慈さん。あなた自身、だったんですよ。
English: was you, Damon Gant.

Japanese: ‥‥‥！...!あなたは‥‥自分から、ある“ジジツ”を認めてしまった。
English: You yourself confessed to a certain "truth."

Japanese: ‥‥もう一度だけ、カクニンします。
English: Let me verify this once more.

Japanese: この布きれは、事件当日。あなた自身が、その手で‥‥
English: On the day of the crime,

Japanese: 被害者のベストから切り取ったもの、なんですね？
English: you personally cut out this piece of the victim's vest?

Japanese: あー、そうだともさ！
English: Oh, yes!

Japanese: あ‥‥‥ああああああ‥‥‥ッ！
English: NNNNOOOOO!!!

Japanese: ‥‥あの瞬間。あなた自身が、認めたのだ。
English: It was then that you approved this cloth...

Japanese: この布きれが、決定的な《証拠品》である、と。
English: as conclusive evidence.

Japanese: そう！　警察局長である、あなた自身が‥‥認めたのだッ！
English: Yes! You, the Chief of Police, personally approved this cloth!

Japanese: 被害者のムネから、これを切り取ることができたのは‥‥
English: The only person who could have cut this from the victim's vest...

Japanese: 罪門　直斗の死の直前、その目の前に立っていた人物。
English: is the one who stood before Prosecutor Marshall in his final moments.

Japanese: つまり、《真犯人》です。そして、それは‥‥
English: In other words, the real murderer! And there's only one person who that could be...

Japanese: 巌徒　海慈！　あなた以外にはあり得ないのです！
English: Damon Gant, the killer was you!
N... N... Mmph...

Japanese: ‥‥フッ‥‥あああああああああああああああッはッはッはッはッはッはッはッはッ
English: WA HA HA HA HA HA HA HA HA HA HA HA HA HA HA HA HA HA HA HA HA HA HA HA

Japanese: ひッひッひッひッひッひッひッひッふッふッふッふッふッふッふッふッ
English: HEH HEH HEH HEH HEH HEH HEH HEH HEH HEH HEH HEH HEH HEH HEH HEH HEH HEH HEH HEH HEH

Japanese: ヘッヘッヘッヘッヘッヘッヘッヘッほッほッほッほッほッほッほッほッ
English: HO HO HO HO HO HO HO HO HO HO HO HO HO HO HO HO HO HO HO HO HO HO HO HO

Japanese: かかかかかかかかかかかかかかかかきききききききききききききききき
English: UH HUH HUH HUH HUH HUH HUH HUH HUH HUH HUH HUH HUH HUH HUH HUH HUH HUH HUH HUH HUH

Japanese: くくくくくくくくくくくくくくくくけけけけけけけけけけけけけけけけ
English: HEE HEE HEE HEE HEE HEE HEE HEE HEE HEE HEE HEE HEE HEE HEE HEE HEE HEE HEE HEE HEE

Japanese: ここここここここここここここここコココココココォォォォォォォッ！
English: HAW HAW HAW HAW HAW HAW HAW HAW HAW HAW HAW HAW HAW HAW HAW HAW HAW!!!

Japanese: やっぱり‥‥‥アイツはカタづけておくべきだったかな‥‥
English: I knew I should have gotten rid of him...

Japanese: あの、できそこない‥‥‥ホント、シツコイ野郎だったよ！
English: That good-for-nothing scum!

Japanese: ボクのまわりを、ムダにカギ回ってさあ。‥‥２年間も！
English: For two years he's been snooping around the department trying to get something on me!

Japanese: 新しい犯罪は毎日、起こっているというのにねェ‥‥
English: Crimes are being committed everyday, yet he insisted on hounding me!

Japanese: ‥‥あなたのしたことも、リッパな犯罪だったのだ。
English: Well, your crime wasn't exactly petty.

Japanese: アイツ‥‥あくまでも、事件を調べなおそうとしたんだ。
English: He wanted to reinvestigate the case.

Japanese: 市ノ谷　響華を仲間に引き入れて、多田敷　道夫も説得していた‥‥
English: He recruited Angel Starr, then convinced Bruce Goodman...

Japanese: 多田敷捜査官を‥‥？
English: Detective Goodman?

Japanese: ‥‥そうなんだよ！聞いてくれる？　ナルホドちゃん！
English: Yeah, that's right.

Japanese: 《申し送り》が終わったら、もうチャンスがない！
English: If the evidence is transferred I'll lose my only chance to find out the truth!

Japanese: たのむ、多田敷！オレに協力してくれッ！
English: Please, you've got to help me!

Japanese: 多田敷ちゃんは、断った。‥‥トーゼンだよね。
English: Goodman turned him down, as he ought to.

Japanese: ‥‥でもね。罪門　恭介はあきらめなかったんだ。
English: Still, Jake Marshall didn't know when to quit.

Japanese: ＩＤカードを盗みだして、証拠品を強奪しようとした‥‥
English: He stole Goodman's ID card and tried to take the evidence!

Japanese: あの、運命の日‥‥‥
English: Goodman came to me that day.

Japanese: ボクの執務室に、多田敷が来た。《遺失物届》の書類を持って。
English: He wanted to file a lost item report.

Japanese: なんか、イヤな予感がしてさ。カレと保管室に行ったんだけど‥‥
English: I went with him to the evidence room.

Japanese: カレね。トンでもないコトを言いだしやがったのよ。
English: Then all of a sudden he had to speak out!

Japanese: な‥‥‥ナニ、言い出すのかな。タダシキちゃん‥‥
English: What are you talking about, Goodman?

Japanese: もう一度！　調べさせてもらえないでしょうか、局長！
English: Can you please reopen the investigation, Chief?

Japanese: このまま《申し送り》なんて‥‥やっぱり、オカシイ！
English: We can't transfer the evidence out. There are too many questions left unanswered!

Japanese: カレ‥‥保管庫を開けて、証拠品を取り出して‥‥言ったよ。
English: He... told me to open up the evidence room and take it out.

Japanese: 《今からでも遅くない。これを、罪門にわたします！》
English: It's not too late. I'll hand this to Marshall!

Japanese: いやー。ボクもさァ。正直なトコ、アセっちゃって。
English: Well, to be honest, I was a little panicked too.

Japanese: ワルい予感はあったけど、これほどまでとは、ね。
English: I had a bad feeling about it, but never knew it would come to this.

Japanese: そのとき‥‥目に入ったんだ。あの‥‥のろわれたナイフが。
English: That's when I saw it... that accursed knife.

Japanese: ‥‥とにかく、ナイフを傷口から抜くワケにはいかなかった。
English: I couldn't just pull it out.

Japanese: 抜けば‥‥出血がはげしくなり、始末できなくなっていただろう。
English: You would only increase the amount of blood and you couldn't finish what you started.

Japanese: それでも、けっこうな血が出て。こっちの血の気が引いたよ。
English: Even so, the blood was just pouring out.

Japanese: ダレが入ってくるかわからない。大急ぎで血をふきとったけど‥‥
English: I didn't know who might stumble in, so I was wiping it up.

Japanese: 床ばっかり気にして、トンでもない見落とし、しちゃったし。
English: I was worrying so much about the floor, I didn't realize my mistake.

Japanese: それが‥‥イトノコ刑事の保管庫に残された“手のアト”ですか。
English: Detective Gumshoe's bloody hand print...

Japanese: “犯罪のコンピュータ”‥‥そんなふうに呼ばれたモンだけど。
English: I used to be known as the "crime computer"...

Japanese: だれでも、やる方は初心者でしょ？ボクも、アセっちゃって。
English: But everyone has to start somewhere I guess. I was too nervous.

Japanese: ホント。ガラにもないコト、するモンじゃないよね。
English: I had no business doing any of it.

Japanese: それで‥‥私のクルマに、その死体を‥‥？
English: Then you put the body in my car?

Japanese: ゴメン！　他に、死体を運びだす方法を思いつかなくてさ。
English: I'm sorry! We couldn't think of any other way to move the body.

Japanese: トランク、コワしちゃったけど。ベツに、かまわないよね？
English: We broke the trunk, but what's the big deal?

Japanese: いっぱいもらってるんだから。刑事クンたちとちがって。
English: You pull down a lot more than us detectives!

Japanese: うむむむむ‥‥
English: Grr...

Japanese: そ、それにしても！
English: W-what does this have to do with anything?

Japanese: ともえさんを巻きこむなんて‥‥ヒキョウじゃないですかッ！
English: You're horrible! How could you get Ms. Skye involved in all of this!?

Japanese: ま。真実があかるみに出て困るのは、彼女もいっしょだから。
English: Well, she had as much to lose as I did if the truth came out.

Japanese: ‥‥多田敷捜査官の保管庫の証拠品も、あなたが‥‥？
English: So you took the evidence from Detective Goodman's locker?

Japanese: ワルいとは思ったけどね。
English: I feel bad for having to do it.

Japanese: 選んでるヒマもなかったから。持てるかぎり、ガッて行ったよ。
English: I couldn't sit around and pick and choose what to take.

Japanese: まあ‥‥‥ツボのカケラと手袋が残ってましたけどね。
English: Well... you left the jar fragment and gloves.
Yeah...

Japanese: 犯罪を犯すよりも、捜査するほうがハダに合うみたいだね。ボクも‥‥
English: It looks like I was better off being an investigator of crimes than a committer.

Japanese: ‥‥みんなして、ボクのジャマをしてくれちゃってさァ。
English: They all did their best to get in my way...

Japanese: ホントに‥‥‥優秀なヤツらだよ。ニクらしいぐらいにねェ‥‥
English: I've got to hand it to them, they do their jobs well... much to my dismay.

Japanese: ‥‥ねじ曲げられた証拠品には、かならず、ホコロビが生まれる。
English: Fake evidence doesn't hold up very well upon close examination.

Japanese: あなたならば。そのぐらい、ご存じだっただろうに‥‥
English: You must have known that...

Japanese: ‥‥‥ねえ、御剣ちゃんさ。
English: Tell me, Worthy.

Japanese: キミは、なんのために法廷に立っているんだい？
English: What are you doing in court?
Me?

Japanese: キミは、ニクんでいるはずだ。犯罪者を‥‥どうしようもなく。
English: You despise criminals.

Japanese: ボクにはわかる。キミは‥‥‥ボクと同じニオイがするからね。
English: I can feel it. You and me... we're the same.

Japanese: ‥‥！!いずれ、キミにもわかるよ。‥‥かならず。
English: One day you'll understand.

Japanese: たったヒトリでヤツらと戦うためには‥‥
English: If you want to take them on alone...

Japanese: “何が必要なのか”をね‥‥
English: you'll figure out what's needed!

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥‥‥‥‥...じゃあ‥‥これで。サヨナラだね。ショクン。
English: Well, looks like it's time to say goodbye.

Japanese: あ。チョーさん、さ。
English: Oh, Udgey.

Japanese: な、なんですかなッ！
English: Wh-what?

Japanese: 来週の、あのヤクソク。なんか。ダメになっちゃったね。
English: Looks like we'll have to cancel that lunch date.

Japanese: いやはや。ゴメンね！
English: Sorry old friend!

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥‥‥‥‥‥‥‥‥...まことに、ザンネンです。巌徒　海慈さん。
English: I'm sorry too, Damon Gant.

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥‥‥‥‥‥...私は、ムカシのあなたをよく知っています。
English: I knew you as you used to be, long ago.

Japanese: かつては、我々の世代を代表する、優秀な捜査官だった。
English: You were once a fine investigator, and an example to others on the force.

Japanese: それだけに‥‥本当に、ザンネンです。
English: I'm sorry to learn that you are no longer that person.

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥‥‥‥‥‥ムカシのコトだよ。ありがとね。チョーさん。
English: Those days are gone now, Udgey. Thanks for all the memories, though...

Japanese: なあに、大丈夫だって！
English: Don't worry, you'll be fine!

Japanese: ナルホドちゃんに‥‥
English: Now you have Wrighto here...

Japanese: ‥‥御剣ちゃん。
English: and Worthy.

Japanese: この、ふたりがいれば。きっと‥‥‥さ。
English: With these two around, you can't go wrong...

Japanese: ‥‥だって、今。ボクにはね。
English: You see, if I listen carefully, I can hear it right now...

Japanese: “始まり”のメロディが聞こえているんだからさァ！
English: The sound of a new beginning!

Japanese: ‥‥あなたにカクニンしておく点が、２つあります。
English: There are two things I want you to understand.

Japanese: ‥‥はい。
English: Yes?

Japanese: １つ。妹の宝月　茜さんは、だれもキズつけていない。
English: First, your sister never hurt anyone.

Japanese: ２つ。巌徒　海慈は、最初からあなたをウラ切っていた。
English: Second, Damon Gant betrayed you from the beginning.

Japanese: つまり‥‥ともえさん。
English: You see, Ms. Skye...

Japanese: あなたがチンモクを守る理由は、もう何もないんです。
English: you no longer have any reason to keep silent.

Japanese: ‥‥ええ。
English: You're right.

Japanese: この裁判が終わったら‥‥すべて、お話しします。
English: When this trial is over, I'll tell everything.

Japanese: あの事件で、巌徒の協力を得て現場を《ねつ造》してから‥‥
English: All that I've done these past two years...

Japanese: この２年。私が、何をしてきたか‥‥すべて。
English: from the time I had Gant help me forge evidence, up until today.

Japanese: どうやら‥‥ここに。
English: So...

Japanese: すべてのナゾは、姿を消したようです。
English: it seems all the questions raised in this trial have been answered.

Japanese: すみませんでした、ともえさん。やっぱり‥‥
English: I'm sorry, Ms. Skye... I couldn't get you out of all your trouble.

Japanese: あなたを完全に救うことは、できなかったみたいです。‥‥‥‥‥‥...思い上がらないことね。‥‥新米弁護士さん。
English: My, my. What high standards you have... for a rookie.

Japanese: ‥‥‥！...!さすが‥‥綾里　千尋さんが見こんだだけのコトはあります。
English: I can see why Mia thought so highly of you.

Japanese: そう‥‥あと３年もすれば。きっと、最高の弁護士になるわ。
English: Who knows? A few years from now, you just might make it to the top.

Japanese: 本当に、どうもありがとう。‥‥成歩堂くん。
English: I owe you my thanks, Mr. Wright.

Japanese: ともえさん‥‥
English: Ms. Skye...

Japanese: そして‥‥‥御剣くん。
English: And to you too, Mr. Edgeworth.

Japanese: ‥‥‥！...!この３日間‥‥あなたは、私と同じ苦しみを味わった。
English: You've suffered every bit as much as I have over these past few days.

Japanese: それが、どれほどのものか‥‥私には、よくわかります。
English: Believe me, I know how much of an ordeal it's been for you.

Japanese: そ、そんな‥‥！大したコトではないッ！
English: Hmph! It was nothing.

Japanese: （ウソ、つけ‥‥）
English: (Liar...)

Japanese: いつ、くじけてもおかしくない‥‥そう思っていました。
English: I was worried the pressure might break you.

Japanese: それなのに、あなたは‥‥
English: And yet...

Japanese: 最後まで、成歩堂くんをみちびいてくれました。
English: you rose above it all and guided Mr. Wright to victory.

Japanese: ‥‥よく、がんばりましたね。
English: You've done well, Mr. Edgeworth.

Japanese: やめていただきたいッ！当然のコト、だ。
English: S-stop it! I only did my job!

Japanese: 今回の事件を通じて、我々は‥‥
English: In light of this case...

Japanese: 我々自身の“あり方”を考えなおす必要があるようですね。
English: it seems a good self-examining is in order for all of us.

Japanese: ‥‥被告人。
English: Ms. Skye.

Japanese: はい。
English: Yes, Your Honor?

Japanese: 殺害の容疑に関して、あなたは無実です。‥‥しかし。
English: You are innocent of murder. However...

Japanese: 脅迫されていたとはいえ、あなたは巌徒氏と《共犯関係》にあった。
English: Although the Chief blackmailed you, the fact is you still acted as his accomplice.

Japanese: ‥‥それについては、後日。改めて審理が必要になるでしょう。
English: A trial will be scheduled for these crimes at a later date.

Japanese: ‥‥ええ。わかっております。
English: Yes. I understand, Your Honor.

Japanese: ‥‥なんですかな？そのハレヤカなニコニコ顔は。
English: Is there something amusing about all of this? Why are you smiling?

Japanese: ひさしぶりなんです。
English: It's been a long time, Your Honor.

Japanese: この、重いクサリから解きはなたれたような気分‥‥‥
English: A long time since I've felt free of these heavy chains...

Japanese: ‥‥それでは。
English: Well, this trial has gone on far too long already.

Japanese: この法廷も、いささか長くなりすぎたようです。
English: Regarding the charge of murder,

Japanese: 罪状を《殺人》にかぎって、今。判決を言いわたします！
English: this court finds the defendant, Ms. Lana Skye...

Japanese: ‥‥では、本日はこれにて閉廷！
English: That is all. The court is adjourned!

Japanese: そこまで！
English: That's enough! This court sees no reason to further prolong this trial.

Japanese: 本法廷は、これ以上の審議の必要性を認めません。さらに、被告人に対する判決を決める時間も、必要としません。
English: The defense has failed to give the court sufficient reason to doubt the prosecution's claim!

Japanese: これは、きわめて明白な事件です。疑問の余地はない！被告人、宝月　巴にたった今、判決を言いわたします！
English: This court finds the defendant, Ms. Lana Skye...

Japanese: 被告人はこれより身柄を司直に預けるものとし、
English: The accused will surrender to the court immediately,

Japanese: １ヶ月以内に、高等裁判所において通常裁判を受けることとします。
English: to be held pending trial at a higher court within a month from today's date.

Japanese: では、本日はこれにて閉廷！
English: That is all. The court is adjourned!

Japanese: 同日　午後　５時　３分地方裁判所　被告人第２控え室
English: February 25, 5:03 PM District Court Defendant Lobby No. 2

Japanese: （ついに‥‥すべてが、終わった‥‥）
English: (At long last... It's finally over...)

Japanese: あ。あかねちゃん‥‥？
English: E-Ema?

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥‥‥‥...な。なんで、そんなカオしてるのかな。
English: Why the long face?

Japanese: たしかに‥‥お姉さんは、完全に無実にはならなかったけど。
English: I'm sorry your sister didn't get completely off the hook,

Japanese: 少なくとも、やってもいないサツジンの罪だけは‥‥
English: but at least she wasn't convicted for a murder she didn't commit!

Japanese: そうじゃないの！
English: No, that's not it.

Japanese: さっき。裁判が、終わったとき‥‥
English: Just now, after the trial ended...

Japanese: さすが‥‥綾里　千尋さんが見こんだだけのコトはあります。
English: I can see why Mia Fey thought so highly of you.

Japanese: 本当に、どうもありがとう。‥‥成歩堂くん。
English: I owe you my thanks, Mr. Wright.

Japanese: そして‥‥‥御剣くん。
English: And to you too, Mr. Edgeworth.

Japanese: ‥‥‥！...!この３日間‥‥あなたは、私と同じ苦しみを味わった。
English: You've suffered every bit as much as I have over these past few days.

Japanese: ‥‥よく、がんばりましたね。
English: You've done well.

Japanese: ‥‥あたしだって。あたしだって、がんばったのに。
English: You know, I did my best too.

Japanese: お姉ちゃん、あたしには‥‥なんにも、言ってくれなかった。
English: But... Lana didn't say a single word to me.

Japanese: シツレイするッス！
English: Hope I'm not interrupting anything.
Oh... Guess I am. I'll come back later.

Japanese: あ‥‥イトノコ刑事！どうしたんですか？
English: Ah, Detective Gumshoe! What is it?

Japanese: アンタたち！　ちょっと、ワザとやってないッスか？
English: You're doing this on purpose, aren't you? Making a detective run all around while on duty,

Japanese: 現職の刑事に使い走りさせた上、来てみれば、この空気！
English: and to top it off you call me here... I've seen happier people at funerals!

Japanese: ‥‥なんて。ちょっと、言ってみたッス。
English: Hey, lighten up, pals. I'm only kidding!

Japanese: あの‥‥。また、お姉ちゃんのお使い、ですか？
English: Oh... Are you here because of my sister again?

Japanese: いやー‥‥今度ばかりは、ちょっとチガうッスね。
English: Nope, not this time!

Japanese: なんというか‥‥ある種。そう、アンタのお使いッス。
English: I came today because of you, pal!

Japanese: あ。あたしの‥‥？
アンタのために‥‥つれてきてあげたッスよ！
English: That's right. I thought you'd like to see someone.

Japanese: お‥‥お姉ちゃん‥‥！
English: Lana!

Japanese: い、いいんですか？いちおう、ともえさんは‥‥
English: Should you be doing this?

Japanese: まだ、拘束中‥‥ですけど。
English: She's still under arrest, you know.

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥‥‥みんなにはヒミツッス。...
English: Well, I won't tell if you won't.

Japanese: あかね‥‥あなたに、ひとこと。あやまっておこうと思って。
English: Ema... I owe you an apology.

Japanese: い。いいよ、ベツに。あたし‥‥気にしてないから。
English: It's okay, Sis. Don't worry about it.

Japanese: ‥‥２年前の、あのとき。
English: That day, two years ago,

Japanese: ‥‥‥！...!生まれて初めて‥‥私は、何も考えるコトができなかった。
English: was the first time in my life I ever panicked.

Japanese: 叫び出しそうなキモチを必死で押さえて‥‥ただ、１つだけ。
English: It was all I could do to keep myself from screaming.

Japanese: 《あなたを、巻き込みたくない》それだけ、アタマをかけめぐった。
English: All I could think about was keeping you from getting wrapped up in that mess.

Japanese: ‥‥お姉ちゃん‥‥
English: Sis...

Japanese: そして、巌徒のチカラを借りて《真実》をかくしとおしたの。
English: I asked Gant to help me cover up the "truth."

Japanese: それが、あなたのためだ‥‥ずっと、そう思っていたわ。
English: I thought I was doing it for your sake...

Japanese: でも‥‥‥そうじゃなかった。私は、まちがっていたのね。
English: But now I realize I was wrong.

Japanese: え‥‥‥...あの事件が終わって‥‥私は、変わったわ。
English: I changed after that day.

Japanese: 変わらなければ‥‥この２年間、とても、耐えられなかったから。
English: I had to... It was the only way I could make it through the past two years.

Japanese: あなたが、悲しんでいるのもよく知っていたわ。でも‥‥！
English: I knew how much I was hurting you by distancing myself...

Japanese: そんなあなたに、私は向かい合うコトができなかった。
English: but I couldn't bring myself to tell you what I did.

Japanese: だって、私は‥‥怖かったの。あなたの、その目を見るのが。
English: I... I was scared. Scared that you'd look at me with those eyes of yours.

Japanese: あなたに《真実》を知られるのが、怖かった‥‥
English: I was scared of how you'd react if you knew...

Japanese: でも！　お姉ちゃんは、あたしのために‥‥
English: But Sis! You were only doing it for me...

Japanese: ‥‥ちがうわ。
English: No...
Huh?

Japanese: 私は、あのとき‥‥あなたのキモチに、目をつぶっただけ。
English: I turned my back on you that day.

Japanese: 《真実》をかくすことは‥‥あなたをあざむくだけだったのに！
English: In hiding what I believed to be the truth, I was deceiving you!

Japanese: お姉ちゃん‥‥
English: Sis...

Japanese: ホント、バカみたい。今になって‥‥やっと、気づいた。
English: I'm such a fool... It took me all this time to realize it.

Japanese: ‥‥あかね。ごめんなさいね。
English: Ema... I'm so sorry.

Japanese: おねがい、お姉ちゃん！あ。あやまらないで。
English: But Sis! You don't have to apologize!

Japanese: だって‥‥あたし。うれしいから。
English: I'm happy now!
You're... happy?

Japanese: お姉ちゃんは‥‥やっぱり。
English: Of course!

Japanese: あたしがよく知ってる、お姉ちゃんだったよ！
English: You know, Sis,

Japanese: ‥‥あたし、信じていたから。いつか、かならず‥‥
English: I always knew that one day you'd come back.

Japanese: 帰ってきてくれる、って！
English: And now you have!
Oh, Ema... Ema!

Japanese: ‥‥過去を変えるコトはできない。
