English: No one can change the past.

Japanese: そして‥‥あやまちはつぐなわなければならない。
English: The only thing we can do is strive to make up for our mistakes.

Japanese: では‥‥なぜ、つぐなわなければならないのか？
English: Why must we make up for our mistakes, you ask?

Japanese: それは、きっと‥‥
English: Because in so doing...

Japanese: その先に、まだ“道”がつづいているから。
English: we can find the way back to our path. And once we've found our path,

Japanese: 過去を断ち切って、今度こそ。前を見て、歩いて行くために‥‥
English: we can move on from our past mistakes toward a brighter future.

Japanese: ‥‥ふたりを見ていてぼくは、そんなコトを思った。
English: At least that's what I felt, looking at those two sisters make up.

Japanese: 成歩堂くん。イトノコくん。
English: Mr. Wright. Mr. Gumshoe.

Japanese: は‥‥‥はッ！
English: M-Me?

Japanese: 本当に、ありがとう。
English: Thank you both, for all that you've done.

Japanese: また、いつか‥‥どこかで。きっと、また会えるはずです。
English: I'm sure we'll meet again someday.

Japanese: ‥‥そうよね？　御剣くん。
English: Isn't that right, Edgeworth?

Japanese: え。み、御剣‥‥‥？
English: E-Edgeworth...?

Japanese: そんなところにかくれてないで、出ていらっしゃいな。
English: Stop hiding and come over here.

Japanese: （どこにかくれていたんだよ！）
English: (Where was he hiding!?)

Japanese: ヒトコトだけ‥‥どうしても、お祝いを申し上げたかった。
English: I just came to say...

Japanese: おめでとうございます。
English: Congratulations.

Japanese: ‥‥わざわざ、ありがとう。
English: Thank you.

Japanese: ありがとうございました！御剣検事さん！
English: Thank you, Mr. Edgeworth!

Japanese: では‥‥‥その。シツレイするッ！
English: Right. Well... I'll be going now!

Japanese: 今回の事件‥‥気に病む必要はないわ。御剣くん。
English: Mr. Edgeworth. I hope you don't blame yourself for what happened.

Japanese: ‥‥！...!まちがっていたのは、私たち。あなたじゃないわ。
English: We were the ones who acted corruptly, not you.
It's too late for me.

Japanese: ‥‥！...!だれがなんと言おうと‥‥私は、知ってしまったのだ。
English: No matter what anyone may say,

Japanese: 私自身が犯した、アヤマチ‥‥それは、変えることはできないッ！
English: I realized today that I can't change my own mistakes!

Japanese: 御剣検事さん‥‥
English: Mr. Edgeworth...

Japanese: それに‥‥私は、恐ろしいのだ。局長の言った、あのコトバ‥‥
English: Not only that, but I don't even trust myself anymore. Chief Gant was right...

Japanese: キミは、ニクんでいるはずだ。犯罪者を‥‥どうしようもなく。
English: You despise criminals I can feel it. You and me... we're the same.

Japanese: ボクにはわかる。キミは‥‥‥ボクと同じニオイがするからね。‥‥！!いずれ、キミにもわかるよ。‥‥かならず。
English: One day you'll understand. If you want to take them on alone...

Japanese: たったヒトリでヤツらと戦うためには‥‥“何が必要なのか”をね‥‥
English: you'll figure out what's needed!

Japanese: たしかに‥‥私は犯罪者をニクんでいる。
English: I do despise criminals.

Japanese: この生涯をかけて。彼らと戦うつもりでいた。
English: I planned to dedicate my entire life to fighting them.

Japanese: 『たったヒトリで犯罪と戦うには、《武器》が必要』‥‥
English: But in order to fight crime alone, one needs a "weapon."

Japanese: 恐ろしいコトだが‥‥かねてから、私も、同じことを考えていた。
English: It's scary, but I've been thinking the same thing for quite some time now.

Japanese: ‥‥そう、だったのか‥‥
English: But Edgeworth...

Japanese: もしかしたら‥‥“いつか、この私も同じコトを”
English: Who knows? Given enough time, I might have tried to pull something like Chief Gant did.

Japanese: そう考えると‥‥このまま、検事をつづけられるハズがないッ！
English: That thought terrifies me. That's why I can't continue on as a prosecutor!

Japanese: ‥‥御剣くん。なぜ、わからないのかしら。
English: Edgeworth... Don't you understand?

Japanese: 巌徒　海慈も‥‥‥あなたの師匠、狩魔　豪も。
English: Damon Gant and your mentor, Manfred von Karma...

Japanese: ‥‥‥！...!ふたりとも、ソンケイすべき、本当にすばらしい戦士だったわ。
English: were both the best of the best when it came to fighting crime.

Japanese: でも‥‥彼らは。ある１点で、まちがっていたのよ。
English: But they both made the same mistake.

Japanese: え‥‥‥...『たったヒトリで犯罪と戦うには、《武器》が必要』‥‥
English: You said, "in order to fight crime alone, one needs a weapon.'"

Japanese: たしかに、そうかもしれないわね。でも‥‥よく、思い出して。
English: That may be right, but think back to today's trial.

Japanese: あなたは、ヒトリじゃない。
English: You weren't alone.

Japanese: ‥‥！...!今回の法廷だって、そう。あなたと、成歩堂くん‥‥
English: You were working together with Mr. Wright.

Japanese: ２人がいて初めて、成立した《証拠》があるじゃない。
English: And because of that partnership, you were able to present evidence that otherwise would have gone undiscovered.

Japanese: そうだったでしょう？‥‥成歩堂くん。
English: Isn't that right, Mr. Wright?

Japanese: え！　‥‥は、はい！（いきなり言われても‥‥）
English: Huh? What? Oh, uh... yeah. (What is this, a pop quiz?)

Japanese: 成歩堂さん！　御剣検事さんに見せてあげましょうよ！
English: Come on, Mr. Wright! Show him what Lana's talking about!

Japanese: （‥‥御剣と、ぼく‥‥）
English: (Evidence...)

Japanese: （ひとりでは、見つけることができなかった《証拠》とは‥‥）
English: (that neither Edgeworth nor I would have been able to find on our own?)

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥‥‥...おそらく‥‥キミにとっては、深いイミがあるのだろうが。
English: That might mean something to you,

Japanese: ザンネンながら、私には理解するチカラが足りないようだ。
English: but I don't see how it had anything to do with our partnership.

Japanese: ‥‥あれ？
English: Huh?

Japanese: 成歩堂くん。
English: Mr. Wright.

Japanese: まだまだ‥‥あなたもベンキョウが必要みたいね。
English: It seems you still have a lot to learn as well.

Japanese: （ど。どうやら‥‥ちがったみたいだな。証拠品）
English: (I guess that wasn't the right piece of evidence.)

Japanese: こ、これは‥‥あたしが描いた、絵‥‥！
English: That's the picture I drew!

Japanese: すべての“反撃”は、ここから始まった。
English: Our counterattack began with this.

Japanese: おまえが持っていたリストと、ぼくが見つけた、リスト‥‥
English: You had one half of the evidence list, and I had the other.

Japanese: どちらが欠けても、この絵は完成しなかったよ。
English: Apart, we wouldn't have been able to completely restore Ema's picture.

Japanese: それは、きっと‥‥グーゼンではないわ。御剣くん。
English: That didn't just happen by "chance," Edgeworth.
It's time for me to go.

Japanese: 御剣検事さん‥‥
English: Mr. Edgeworth...

Japanese: それでは‥‥私は、シツレイしよう。
English: If you'll excuse me...

Japanese: あとかたづけが残っているのでね。
English: there are still some loose ends that need wrapping up.

Japanese: 宝月主席検事。それでは‥‥お元気で。
English: Take care, Chief Prosecutor.

Japanese: ‥‥御剣！これから、どうするんだ？
English: Edgeworth! What will you do now?

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥‥‥‥...わかっているよな。今回の、この事件‥‥
English: Well, whatever you do, just remember.

Japanese: 《検事》としての、お前自身を“生かす”のも、“殺す”のも‥‥
English: What happened in this trial can either make or break you as a prosecutor.

Japanese: 決められるのは、オマエしかいないんだ！
English: In the end, it's up to you.

Japanese: ‥‥ああ。わかっているさ。
English: I know... It seems I owe you my thanks too, Wright.

Japanese: キミにも礼を言うよ。‥‥成歩堂。あとは‥‥私自身のモンダイだ。
English: But what I face now... is my problem.

Japanese: 御剣‥‥法廷で待っているぞ。
English: Edgeworth... I'll be waiting for you in court.

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥‥‥‥‥‥それでは、シツレイする。...
English: Farewell.

Japanese: それじゃあ‥‥私もそろそろ、行かないと。
English: I'd better be getting back too.

Japanese: ‥‥うん。あたし、会いに行くから。
English: Okay. I'll come visit you!

Japanese: おたがい、もっともっとベンキョウしなくちゃね。
English: It seems we both still have a lot to learn.

Japanese: じゃあ‥‥これ。あなたに、プレゼントよ。
English: Here, this is a little something for you.

Japanese: 《よくわかる科学捜査入門》‥‥
English: "Scientific Investigation"...

Japanese: 私が初めて買った本なの。よく、読んでおきなさい。
English: It's the first book I ever bought. Study it well.

Japanese: あ。ありがとう、お姉ちゃん！ベンキョウしておくからね！
English: Thanks, Sis! I will!

Japanese: ‥‥こうして１つの事件は、幕を閉じた。
English: And so, another case came to a close.

Japanese: しかし‥‥ぼくは、信じている。
English: As for the sisters... I have faith.

Japanese: あかねちゃんと、ともえさん‥‥
English: Faith that their lives...

Japanese: ふたりの物語は‥‥きっと。これから、また始まるんだろう。
English: have only just begun.

Japanese: ‥‥そして、ぼくも。
English: And as for me...

Japanese: ぼく自身の“再会”の物語に向けて。
English: I think it's time I started on a new journey of my own.

Japanese: 明日から、新しいキモチで‥‥また、歩き出そう！
English: A journey to rediscover myself.

Japanese: あ。歩き出す前に、ちょっといいッスか。
English: Well, don't go trekking off just yet, pal!

Japanese: な。なんですか、イトノコ刑事！
English: Huh? What is it, Detective?

Japanese: アンタもわかってると思うッスが‥‥
English: There's just a little matter to be resolved about the Chief Prosecutor.

Japanese: 宝月　巴主席検事は、まだ、拘束中ッス。
English: You see, she isn't supposed to be out of jail like this.
But...

Japanese: だから、言ったじゃないですか。大丈夫なんですか、って。
English: I thought you said it was okay.

Japanese: 大丈夫なワケ、ないッス。
English: Yeah, well it may be "okay" with me, but the folks at the prison are a different story.

Japanese: え。
English: Huh?

Japanese: 『３０分だけ、見て見ぬフリをしてくれ』と‥‥
English: Basically, I had to bribe a guard in order to sneak her out for 30 minutes.

Japanese: 看守たちに、たんまりカネをニギらせたッス。
English: Believe me, it wasn't cheap either!

Japanese: え。え。
English: Huh?

Japanese: さすが、刑事さん！イキなコト、しますよね！
English: Way to go, Detective! I didn't know you had a wild side!

Japanese: いやー。ハッハッ。そういうコトで。
English: Yeah, well... ha ha! You see...

Japanese: そっちの支払いのほう、よろしくたのむッス！
English: Mr. Wright here's the one who'll be footin' the bill.

Japanese: え。え。え。
English: Huh? Huh?

Japanese: 自分の給料じゃ、とてもムリッスからねェ。‥‥あんな大金。
English: What, you think I could afford that with my salary? You gotta be kiddin' me, pal!

Japanese: え。え。え。え。
English: Huh? Huh? Huh?

Japanese: 本当に、どうもありがとう。成歩堂さん！
English: Thank you, Mr. Wright! You're the best!

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥‥‥‥‥...（どうやら‥‥もう一度だけ）
English: (Why is it...)

Japanese: （叫んでおいたほうがいいみたいだな‥‥）
English: (I suddenly feel like I want to scream?)

Japanese: せっかくだから、みんなで行っとくッスか。
English: Since we're all here, why don't we all go together?

Japanese: あ、それ！いい考えですね！
English: Yeah! That's a great idea!

Japanese: ‥‥それじゃあ、みなさん、ごいっしょにっ！
English: Come on, guys! Let's go!!!

Japanese: あかねは、アメリカの知人にあずけることにしました。
English: I arranged for a friend of mine in Europe to take care of Ema.

Japanese: 超一流の検視官ですから、いいシゲキになると思います。
English: She's a coroner. I think Ema will be pleased.

Japanese: 今回の事件で、私も検事局をはなれるコトになるでしょう。
English: As for me, this affair has pretty much ended my days at the Prosecutor's Office.

Japanese: でも。また、ちがう立場でかならず、現場にもどってきます。
English: Still, I'll manage to find my way back to the field somehow.

Japanese: いつか‥‥今度は、いっしょに事件を追いたいですね。
English: Then I'll be able to investigate crimes together with Ema.

Japanese: いやー、一時はどうなるコトかと思ったッスが‥‥
English: Yikes! I thought I was a goner for a moment there! In the end though,

Japanese: どうやら今回は、違法捜査のおとがめはナシ、というコトで。
English: they overlooked my unauthorized investigation of the Chief's office.

Japanese: 『これ以上のペナルティは、ある種、クビよりヒドい仕打ち』
English: "If we penalized you any more, it'd be worse than firing you!"

Japanese: ‥‥なんでも、そういうケツロンになったとか。
English: Yep. That's what they said.

Japanese: この、イトノコギリ！　これからもイッピキの刑事を、つらぬくッス！
English: It just goes to show... You can't shake me off that easily!

Japanese: 最近のシゴトはもっぱらコレ、正門の警備と、ビリーの相談相手！
English: My new mission is to guard the main entrance and take care of Billy!

Japanese: 決してコレ、ガードマンに格下げになったのではないのでありますッ！
English: Can you believe it!? I've been demoted to a security guard!

Japanese: 本日は、相棒が本官のかわりに正門を守っているでありますが‥‥
English: My partner's keeping an eye on the entrance for me today.

Japanese: でも、いつの日か、本官もコレッ！捜査をしてみたいでありますッ！
English: I'll show them, though! Someday I'm going to make detective!

Japanese: 本官の目標は、あのイノコリ刑事殿でありますからしてェェェェェッ！
English: Yes sir! Then I can be just like that Dick Gumshoe!

Japanese: なんの用だい？　ステーキ弁当と真昼の決闘を楽しんでたトコロさ。
English: What is it? Can't you see I'm havin' me a showdown with a steak lunch, pardner?

Japanese: おキョウからの差し入れさ。看守室に、カレがいるらしいぜ。
English: Miss Starr managed to sneak this in to me. She's seeing one of the guards it seems.

Japanese: あの、カウボーイ‥‥さすがだな。やっぱり、やってくれたかい。
English: Well, cowboy... It looks like you did it.

Japanese: バンビーナにも、とびきりのエガオを取り戻してやったみたいだしね。
English: You even gave Bambina back her smile.

Japanese: ついでに、ビリーたちに水をやっておくよう、言っといてくれ。
English: Can you make sure Billy and the gang get their water?

Japanese: これで、しばらく‥‥おキョウのカオともお別れだねェ。
English: Looks like we won't be seeing each other for a while...

Japanese: さあ。このたび新発売になった成歩堂弁当、よろしかったかしら？
English: As a farewell gift, I put a new meal on the menu: The Wright Way Lunch.

Japanese: 一段目は、苦ぁい敗北の味。二段目は、甘ぁい勝利の味。
English: The top layer tastes as bitter as defeat, but the bottom layer's as sweet as victory.

Japanese: 大逆転の味わいが、ユメ見がちな受験生たちに大ウケですのよ。
English: Kids seem to dig the turnabout theme. It's a hot seller around exam time!

Japanese: ‥‥くれぐれも、逆に食べませんように。
English: Just make sure not to eat it backwards!

Japanese: 今回の事件、弁護士の最後のコトバ‥‥私は一生、忘れないでしょう。
English: I'll never forget what that young defense lawyer said after the trial.

Japanese: あの、若き弁護士‥‥‥あれ。ナニ歩堂くん、でしたっけな？
English: Let's see, what was his name again? Mr. Left...?

Japanese: ええと。この私も、この道に入ってかれこれ‥‥あれ。何年でしたか。
English: Anyway, he said he's been doing, er, something or other for, uh... how many years...?

Japanese: と。とにかく！　そろそろ開廷の時間なので、このへんで‥‥
English: Well anyway! I've got another trial to get to, so I'd better be...

Japanese: あ！　木槌を忘れてきました！それでは、シツレイしますぞッ！
English: Huh? Oh no! I forgot my gavel! Sorry, gotta go!

Japanese: うーん。やっぱり、里の空気はいいなあ。
English: Aaaaah! Nothing soothes the soul like fresh, country air!

Japanese: 正直、たまに聞きたくなりますよ。なるほどくんの『異議あり！』
English: Still, sometimes I do miss hearing Nick and his "objections"...

Japanese: でも！　まだ、ダメなんです！霊媒師として、一人前になるまで。
English: Still, I can't go back until I'm a full-fledged spirit medium!

Japanese: ‥‥真宵さまー。午後の修行が始まりますよー。
English: Maya! Afternoon training's about to begin!

Japanese: うん！　今、行くよー。
English: Coming!!!

Japanese: じゃ、あたしもがんばるから。またね、なるほどくん！
English: Well, see you around Nick!

Japanese: ‥‥御剣サマ。
English: Mr. Edgeworth.

Japanese: あの。御剣検事サマ？
English: Uh, Mr. Edgeworth?

Japanese: いつもの、ストレートティーをお持ちいたしましたが‥‥
English: I brought you your tea...
What's going on?

Japanese: わざわざ、お見送りありがとうございました！
English: Thanks for coming to see me off!

Japanese: なんか、急にアメリカに行くコトになっちゃって。
English: I can't believe I'm going to Europe.

Japanese: 成歩堂さん。本当に‥‥本当に、ありがとう！
English: Thank you, Mr. Wright! Thank you so much for everything!!!

Japanese: やっぱり、さみしいけど‥‥あたし。がんばります！
English: I'm a little sad, but I'll be all right!

Japanese: この本を開けば、いつでも‥‥お姉ちゃんに会える気がするから。
English: Whenever I want to see Lana, all I have to do is open this book...

Japanese: へえ‥‥バッジのウラって、こうなってるんですね！
English: So this is what the back of the badge looks like!

Japanese: あたし、安全ピンだと思ってました！
English: And I always thought it had a safety pin!

Japanese: バッジには、ナンバーが刻まれているからね。
English: Each badge has a number carved into it.

Japanese: 他の弁護士がつけることはできないんだ。
English: That way, you can tell which attorney it belongs to.

Japanese: じゃあ、《ニセ弁護士あらわる！》‥‥なんてコト、ないんですね。
English: You mean you couldn't lend your badge to anyone?

Japanese: すぐにバレるんじゃないかな。ニセモノなんて。
English: No, I'd be found out right away.

Japanese: なーんだ。つまんないなあ‥‥
English: Well that's no fun!

Japanese: バッジのウラには、ナンバーが刻まれている。
English: A number is engraved on the back of the badge.

Japanese: ‥‥どうせなら、名前を刻んでくれればいいのに‥‥
English: I wish they'd put my name on it instead...

Japanese: 名前と、ＩＤナンバーが書きこまれているね。
English: A name and ID number are written here.

Japanese: 《捜査官：多田敷　道夫ＩＤ　：５８４２１８９》
English: "Detective Bruce Goodman, ID# 5842189"

Japanese: でも。どうして《ＩＤ》って番号ばっかりなんでしょうね。
English: I wonder why they only use numbers for IDs.

Japanese: ‥‥どういうこと？
English: What else would they use?

Japanese: あたし。『ひらがな』だって、人類の偉大な発明だと思うんです。
English: Letters, silly! They're the reason we have a written language in the first place!

Japanese: はあ。
English: True...

Japanese: 《捜査官：多田敷　道夫ＩＤ　：ぬむろふへもこ》
English: "Detective: Bruce Goodman, ID: YABADAB"

Japanese: ‥‥とか。どう思います？
English: See? Wouldn't that be better?

Japanese: 《ぬむろふへもこ》‥‥なんか、おぼえやすいね。
English: "YABADAB"? Well, it does have a certain ring to it...

Japanese: ですよね！　ですよね！‥‥ふふん。
English: Exactly my point! Tee-hee!

Japanese: （ゴキゲンになった）
English: (It doesn't take much to amuse her.)

Japanese: 名前と、ＩＤナンバーが書きこまれている。
English: His name and ID number is written on it.

Japanese: 《捜査官：多田敷　道夫ＩＤ　：５８４２１８９》
English: "Detective Bruce Goodman, ID# 5842189"

Japanese: ‥‥被害者のヒトの血、ですよね。
English: This must be the victim's blood, right?

Japanese: リンゴをむいてて、指を切ったワケじゃなさそうだね。
English: Either that, or Edgeworth cut himself peeling an apple.

Japanese: それにしても、御剣のヤツ。ブッソウなナイフを持ってるなあ。
English: What's Edgeworth doing with a knife like this anyway?

Japanese: 週末は、ヤマにこもって『サバイバル』してるのかも！
English: Hey! Maybe he spends his weekends roughing it in the wild!

Japanese: たき火のそばに座って、リンゴむいたりしてね。
English: Edgeworth? In the wild? I think my fruit-peeling theory is more likely.

Japanese: もう！　御剣検事さんといえば、もっとワイルドなイメージですっ！
English: Are you kidding? I always pictured him as an outdoorsman!

Japanese: （そうかなあ‥‥）
English: (Now there's a scary thought...)

Japanese: 被害者?多田敷　道夫の血液が付着している。
English: The victim Bruce Goodman's blood still remains on the blade.

Japanese: ‥‥このナイフのニギリ‥‥服の色と合わせているのかな。
English: I wonder if Edgeworth got this knife because the handle matches his clothes...?

Japanese: こんなところに、金属のプレートがハマってますよ！
English: Hey, check it out! There's a metal plate here!

Japanese: どうやら、歴代の受賞者の名前が刻まれているみたいだ。
English: Hm. It looks like the names of all the previous recipients are engraved on it.

Japanese: ‥‥なんか、やたら同じヒトの名前が並んでますね。
English: Wow. One guy's listed a bunch of times!

Japanese: なんて読むんでしょうね、コレ。えーと。《かり‥‥ま》かな？
English: "von Karma"... I guess he must be a foreigner?

Japanese: まあ‥‥そんなトコじゃないかな。
English: Uh, yeah. That's probably it.

Japanese: わあ！　すごいんですねー。かりま検事さん。
English: Well wherever he's from, he must have been an amazing prosecutor!

Japanese: 一度、会ってみたいです！かりま検事さんに。
English: I'd like to meet this Mr. "von Karma" sometime!

Japanese: （‥‥連呼されると、ちょっとおもしろいな。かりま検事）
English: (When she says it, his name does have kind of a ring to it...)

Japanese: 《検事?オブ?ザ?イヤー》歴代の受賞者が刻まれている。
English: "King of Prosecutors." The names of past recipients are etched on the plate.

Japanese: ならんでいる“狩魔　豪”の名前‥‥なんか、ヒニクに見える。
English: Manfred von Karma's name appears here too... How ironic.

Japanese: うわ。ジミなストラップですねー。
English: Man, what a boring strap!

Japanese: いいんじゃないかな。好きずきだし。
English: What's wrong with it? Everyone has different tastes, you know.

Japanese: ちなみに。あたしのストラップ、《小江戸剣士?ヒメサマン》です！
English: Here, check out mine. It's a Pink Princess strap!

Japanese: なかなか手に入らないんですよ！いいでしょ。
English: These are hard to come by, you know.

Japanese: （やれやれ。あいかわらず、はやってるみたいだな‥‥）
English: (I see he's as popular as ever with the kids...)

Japanese: ちなみに、ぼくの携帯電話にはストラップがついていない。
English: Come to think of it, I don't have a strap on my mobile phone at all.

Japanese: ‥‥よけいなものをつけるのがメンドくさいんだよな。
English: I hate having useless things dangling off it.

Japanese: （‥‥もう、押す必要はないだろう）
English: (There's no need to push this again.)

Japanese: どうしたんですか？ムズカシイ顔、して。
English: What's wrong? You look like I do during finals!

Japanese: ‥‥いや。なんでもないよ。
English: Never mind. It's nothing.

Japanese: リダイヤルボタン。最後に通話したナンバーを呼び出す。
English: The redial button. It dials the number of the last person called.

Japanese: ‥‥あかねちゃんだ。いったい、何を話したのか‥‥？
English: The last call was to Ema. I wonder what they spoke about...?

Japanese: ‥‥あれ。なんか、文字を消したアトがありますよ。
English: Hey, look here! It looks like something's been erased.

Japanese: へえ。イトノコ刑事あての手紙かなんか、だったのかな。
English: Maybe it was a letter or something to Detective Gumshoe.

Japanese: ええと‥‥《賞与：２４００円在中》
English: Let's see... "Annual bonus: $20."

Japanese: あの。コレ‥‥ゼロが何個か足りませんよね。
English: Um... I think a couple zeros are missing.

Japanese: こんなモンじゃないのかな。（あの刑事さんの場合‥‥）
English: No, that sounds about right. (At least in that detective's case...)

Japanese: ‥‥オトナになるのがイヤになってきました。...
English: Maybe I should rethink my career as an investigator.

Japanese: この、血‥‥お姉ちゃんのもの、なんですね。
English: This blood... It's my sister's, right?

Japanese: そうみたいだね。
English: It appears so.

Japanese: （留置所で見た、ともえさんの右手の包帯‥‥）
English: (Lana's right hand was bandaged when I saw her in jail.)

Japanese: （あのとき、キズを負ったんだろうな‥‥）
English: (She must have cut herself at the time of the crime...)

Japanese: お姉ちゃん‥‥
English: Poor Sis...

Japanese: 白いエナメル地に、細かく血が飛んでいる。
English: Blood is splattered over the white enamel surface.

Japanese: ‥‥ともえさんのものだ。右手の包帯が、そうなんだろう。
English: It must be Lana's blood, from the cut on her right hand she now has bandaged.

Japanese: きゃあっ！こんなトコロにも、ぺったり血がついてますッ！
English: AAAH! There's blood here too!

Japanese: クツの底、か‥‥被害者自身の血、だろうね。
English: On the sole of the shoe? It's got to be the victim's.

Japanese: どうやら‥‥血だまりの中を歩いたみたい、だね。
English: He must have stepped in a puddle of his own blood.

Japanese: ううう‥‥ナマナマしいんですね。サツジン事件って。
English: All this blood... It's horrible!

Japanese: （足のウラに、血のアトがあるということは‥‥）
（当然、血だまりの上を歩いたことになる、よな‥‥）
English: (This blood might be an important clue...)

Japanese: こちらの血痕は、被害者自身‥‥多田敷　道夫のものだ。
English: These blood traces are from Bruce Goodman, the victim.

Japanese: 刺されたあと、逃げようとしたのだろうか。
English: He must have tried to run after he was stabbed.

Japanese: これ‥‥血のアト、ですよね？
English: This is a bloodstain, isn't it?

Japanese: 飛び出しナイフから付着したんだろうね。
English: Yeah. It was probably left behind from the switchblade.

Japanese: このマフラー‥‥まちがいなく、ともえさんのモノ、なの？
English: Are you absolutely sure this is your sister's muffler?

Japanese: は、はい‥‥
English: Yes...

Japanese: （やはり‥‥このナイフをかくしたのは、彼女のようだ）
English: (So she really did hide the knife.)

Japanese: （いったい、なぜ‥‥？）
English: (But why...?)

Japanese: きゃあああ！　びび、ビックリさせないでくださいっ！
English: EEEEEEE!!! Don't scare me like that!

Japanese: ビックリしたの、こっちだよ！
English: Scare YOU? I'm the one who was scared!

Japanese: ‥‥この、ナイフの刃‥‥先が欠けてますね。
English: Hey... The tip of this knife is missing!

Japanese: （それに、この赤黒いシミ。“血”かな、やっぱり‥‥）
English: (There's also a dark red stain here. It looks like blood...)

Japanese: とにかく‥‥このナイフはカガク的に、重要な証拠品です！（たしかに‥‥他にも手がかりがあるかもしれない）（このナイフ‥‥もっとよく調べたほうがいいな！）ナイフに、小さなタグがついてますね。
English: There's a small tag on the knife.

Japanese: 《ＳＬ－９》‥‥そう書かれているみたいだ。
English: It says, "SL-9 2."

Japanese: どういうイミかなあ‥‥
English: What's that supposed to mean?

Japanese: （似たヒビキのコトバなら聞いたことがあるんだけど‥‥）
English: I'm not sure... (But it reminds me of a similar code...)

Japanese: （ＤＬ６号‥‥“事件”）
English: ("DL-6." Maybe it's a case number?)

Japanese: でも‥‥なんだか。
English: That's weird.

Japanese: ？　どうしたの？
English: What?

Japanese: なんか‥‥どこかで見たような気がするんですよねー。
English: I don't remember where, but I think I've seen something like this before.

Japanese: この、タグの文字と‥‥“よく似た”文字を‥‥最近。
English: Something similar to what's written on this tag. It wasn't that long ago either...

Japanese: （法廷記録‥‥もう一度、カクニンしてみたほうがいいな）
English: (Maybe I should check the Court Record again.)

Japanese: 小さなタグには、《ＳＬ－９》と書かれている。
English: "SL-9" is written on a small tag attached to the knife.

Japanese: ２年前の、あの事件の凶器で、多くの被害者の血を吸ったナイフ。
English: It was the weapon used in that incident two years ago; and has known many victims.

Japanese: （油性ペンで名前が書いてある）
English: (Someone used a marker to write their name on this.)

Japanese: 《１－３ほうづき　あかね》
English: "Ema Skye"

Japanese: もしかして‥‥学校にも持っていってるの？　これ。
English: Don't tell me you bring this with you everywhere you go.

Japanese: そりゃもう。いつ、何が起こるか、わかりませんからね！
English: Well, you never know where something might go down!

Japanese: （いつ、何が起こると思ってるんだろう‥‥）
English: (Just what kind of a world do you think we live in...?)

Japanese: 油性ペンで名前が書いてある。《１－３》‥‥か。
English: Ema Skye is written on the side in permanent marker.

Japanese: この《１》が《小学１年》だったらイヤだなあ‥‥
English: She seems awfully possessive about a can of luminol.

Japanese: 《ＡＩ１６号》‥‥そう書かれているみたいだ。
English: The tag says, "AI-16."

Japanese: それで‥‥なんなんですか、このドライバー。
English: What's that got to do with anything?

Japanese: それは、その。関係ないんじゃないかな。
English: Nothing, apparently.

Japanese: 御剣も、そう言ってたし。
English: At least, that's what Edgeworth said.

Japanese: でも、“関係ない”って言われるほど、気になりますよねー。
English: Hm. That makes it seem all the more suspicious.

Japanese: きっと、トンでもなく重要なイミがあるんですよ。コレ。
English: Who knows? This might turn out to be the clue that breaks the case!

Japanese: これ以上、ヘンな事件をかかえこみたくないなあ‥‥
English: Wouldn't that be nice...

Japanese: 《ＡＩ１６号》‥‥そう書かれているみたいだ。
English: "AI-16" is written on the tag.

Japanese: 今回の事件とは、関係ない証拠品ということだ。
English: That means it has nothing to do with the current case.

Japanese: この部分‥‥かすかに、赤黒いシミがありますけど。
English: Hey, look here! It's hard to make out, but there's a dark red stain here.

Japanese: やっぱり‥‥“血”みたいだね。
English: Hmm... Looks like blood.

Japanese: 多田敷さんが刺されたときについたんでしょうか？
English: Do you think Detective Goodman's blood somehow got on it when he was stabbed?

Japanese: どうも‥‥そうじゃないみたいだね。
English: Not likely.

Japanese: ずいぶん、古いものに見えるよ。ほとんど、消えかかってるし。
English: This blood looks like it's been here for months, maybe longer.

Japanese: （このツボは、《ＳＬ９号事件》という事件の証拠品だった‥‥）
English: (This jar was evidence in the SL-9 Incident...)

Japanese: （血は、そのときについたのかもしれないな）
English: (That might be when the blood got on it.)

Japanese: 赤黒い“血痕”‥‥ずいぶん、古いものみたいだ。
English: There's a trace of old blood on this jar.

Japanese: このツボは、《ＳＬ９号事件》という事件の証拠品だった‥‥
English: This was evidence in the SL-9 Incident...

Japanese: 血は、そのときについたのかもしれないな。
English: The blood must be from back then.

Japanese: あ、あれ！ここ、底がありませんよ！
English: Huh? This thing doesn't have a bottom!

Japanese: ホントだ。これじゃあ‥‥
English: That's weird.

Japanese: どっちが下か、わかりませんね。
English: I wonder which side is "up"?

Japanese: いやいや。それより、そもそも底がなかったら‥‥
English: Better yet...

Japanese: コレ、何に使うんだろう？
English: What's the purpose of a bottomless jar?

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥‥‥‥‥...うーん。底の知れないツボですねえ‥‥
English: At least it doesn't collect dust inside, right?

Japanese: ポッカリ穴が空いている。底なしのツボだ。
English: The jar has no bottom, so the hole goes right through.

Japanese: ‥‥ホントにツボなのかな。あるいは、天体望遠鏡、とか‥‥
English: I wonder if it's really a jar, or some kind of weird telescope...?

Japanese: この部分‥‥かすかに、赤黒いシミがありますけど。
English: Hey, look here! It's hard to make out, but there's a dark red stain here.

Japanese: やっぱり‥‥“血”みたいだね。
English: Hmm... Looks like blood.

Japanese: でも。なんかヘンですね。この、巌徒局長のカケラ‥‥
English: This piece the Chief has is different, though.

Japanese: 他の血は、みんな消えかけた“点”なのに‥‥
English: The blood stains on the other pieces are just spots,

Japanese: この１枚だけ、“線”になってますよ！
English: but this one's a line!

Japanese: （どういうことだろう‥‥）
English: (That is odd...)

Japanese: この赤黒いシミは‥‥２年前の血痕だろう。
English: The dark red stains must be blood traces from the incident two years ago.

Japanese: 巌徒局長の部屋から見つかったカケラだけ、“線”が残っている。
English: Only the fragment found in Chief Gant's office still has lines remaining on it.

Japanese: ビデオテープにラベルが貼ってありますね。
English: This tape has a label on it.

Japanese: どれどれ。記録した時間なんかが書かれているんじゃないかな。
English: Let me see. Maybe it lists the time it was recorded.

Japanese: 《永久保存版！湯ケムリ紀行?旅まみれ》
English: "Hackoholics Anonymous: Twelve Steps to Recovery"

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥‥‥...新しいテープがなかったんですね、きっと。
English: They must have recorded over an old tape.

Japanese: そ。それにしても、警察が提出した証拠だぞ！
English: Yeah, but this evidence is from the police department!

Japanese: 《永久保存版》がつく番組って、ロクなモノがないですからねー。
English: Why would they have a tape like that anyway?

Japanese: ‥‥なんか、急に旅に出たくなってきたよ。
English: Let's just hope they got it off a criminal.

Japanese: 《永久保存版！湯ケムリ紀行?旅まみれ》
English: "Hackoholics Anonymous: Twelve Steps to Recovery"

Japanese: この事件が解決したら、温泉でも行ってみようか。
English: I wonder what the 12 steps are...?

Japanese: タグには《ＳＬ９号－１１番》と書かれているね。
English: The tag says, "SL-9 11."

Japanese: これも、例の事件の証拠品らしいなあ。
English: I guess this is another piece of evidence from that case.

Japanese: あたし、ムカシからキライなんですよ。“タグ”ってコトバ。
English: You know, I never did care for the word, "tag." It's confusing.

Japanese: へえ‥‥。なんで？
English: Huh? What's so confusing about that?

Japanese: だって。見た目がまぎらわしいじゃないですか。
English: Do you know how many other words sound like it? "Bag," "Gag," "Nag," "Lag," "Xag"...

Japanese: まぎらわしい‥‥？
English: "Xag"...? Is that a word?

Japanese: “タグ”か“クダ”か“ゲタ”か“タダ”か、はたまた“多い”か。
English: Do you challenge me...?

Japanese: “多い”はないよ、あかねちゃん。
English: What, are we playing a word game now?

Japanese: タグには《ＳＬ９号－１１番》と書かれている。
English: "SL-9 11" is written on the tag.

Japanese: これも、例の事件の証拠品らしいな。
English: It seems this is more evidence from that incident.

Japanese: 《ミツルギ　レイジ１７：１２》‥‥
English: "Miles Edgeworth - 17:12"...

Japanese: 日付は、事件当日だ。
English: This is dated the day of the crime.

Japanese: 入庫してから、３分後に事件が起きたわけですね。
English: The murder took place three minutes after Edgeworth parked his car.

Japanese: どこかで２、３回、信号に引っかかっていたら‥‥
English: If only he was held up at a couple extra red lights...

Japanese: 事件に巻きこまれなくてすんだかもしれないのに。
English: he wouldn't have been caught up in this whole affair.

Japanese: そういう考えかたもあるね。
English: Perhaps...

Japanese: 成歩堂さんも、信号はキチンと守りましょうね！
English: It just goes to show you never know what'll happen when you run a yellow light!

Japanese: 《ミツルギ　レイジ１７：１２》‥‥
English: "Miles Edgeworth - 17:12"...

Japanese: 日付は、事件当日だ。
English: It's dated the day of the murder.

Japanese: これ‥‥手のアト、ですよね。
English: This is a handprint, right?

Japanese: カワの布地に、アブラのシミみたいに付着している。
English: Yeah. Someone left behind an oil stain on the fabric.

Japanese: アブラっぽい手をしたヒトだったのかな。
English: Whoever it was, they must have had a really oily hand.

Japanese: 成歩堂さん。カガク的に考えてみましょう！
English: Mr. Wright, think about it scientifically!

Japanese: ど。どういうコト？
English: Huh?

Japanese: つまり。こういうコトです。
English: A more likely explanation is...

Japanese: ツルツルにワックスをかけたばかりのろうかで、転んで‥‥
English: the person slipped and fell on a freshly waxed floor,

Japanese: 手が、ワックスでべたべたになった、とか。
English: getting wax all over their hand.

Japanese: なるほど‥‥意外に、イイ線かもしれないね。
English: Well, that would account for this amount of oil I guess.

Japanese: （どこが“カガク的”かわからないけど）
English: (I don't see what's so "scientific" about it though.)

Japanese: あたしの学校、こないだワックスをかけたばっかりだし。
English: It happens, you know. I always slip on the floors at school after they're waxed.

Japanese: カワの布地に、ハッキリ手のアトが残っている。
English: A clear handprint can be seen on the leather cloth.

Japanese: 指紋のヌシは、あかねちゃんだ。‥‥彼女にはヒミツにしておこう。
English: The print belongs to Ema... I think I'll keep this information to myself.

Japanese: 《周知の事情により、辞職を決意した。よろしくおねがいしたい》
English: "Due to recent events I hereby announce my resignation as public prosecutor."

Japanese: （ほ。本当に、辞表だぞ‥‥）
English: (He really wrote a resignation letter...)

Japanese: さすが、御剣検事さん。ちょっぴり、エラそうですね。
English: Wow! Even when resigning, Mr. Edgeworth is cool and concise!

Japanese: でも‥‥御剣検事さんは、ワルくないのに‥‥
English: Still, it wasn't his fault...

Japanese: ダレかに、セキニンをとらせる。それが、オトナの世界なんだよ。
English: Someone has to be held responsible. That's how it is in the grown-up world.

Japanese: えー！　でも、辞めちゃったら“セキニン”も何もないですよ？
English: Yeah, but that responsibility means nothing if he just quits!

Japanese: ま。まあ、たしかに‥‥
English: Well, not everyone sees it that way.

Japanese: セキニンを取るのなら‥‥タダで５年はたらく、とか！
English: To truly take responsibility you should have to work the rest of your life for no pay.

Japanese: （オトナの世界はキビシイな‥‥）
English: (Sometimes the "grown-up" world can be tough...)

Japanese: 刑事さん‥‥あんまり、よくないですね。写真うつり。
English: Detective Gumshoe isn't very photogenic, is he?

Japanese: そんなコト言っちゃワルいと思うよ。本人に。
English: Whatever you do, just don't say that to his face.

Japanese: だって、ホラ。目が半分、閉じちゃってますよ！
English: Look, his eyes are half shut!

Japanese: そのかわり、クチが半分開いちゃってるね。
English: Yeah... and his mouth is half open.

Japanese: うわ。シャツのボタン、１個ずつズレてますよ！
English: Hey! Each of his shirt buttons is off a notch!

Japanese: このネクタイ、細いほうが前に来てるぞ。
English: And he's got the narrow end of his tie in front.

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥‥‥...“写真うつり”とはちょっとちがうみたいだね。
English: I think this goes beyond being a "photogenic" issue.

Japanese: キンチョーのあまり、エガオがひきつっている。
English: He's wearing a really nervous smile in this picture.

Japanese: めずらしく、ヒゲがつるつるにそり上げられている。
English: At least for once he's got a clean-shaven face.

Japanese: うわ、おいしそう！
English: Wow, my mouth is watering!

Japanese: 今にも、ニクの焼ける音がジューッ、て聞こえそうです！
English: I can almost hear the sound of steak frying on the grill!

Japanese: ジッサイは、冷えてコチコチだけどね。
English: No doubt it's all cold and tough by now.

Japanese: きっと、大丈夫です！
English: No, I'm sure it's delicious!

Japanese: おキョウさんが、まごころでヤキを入れてますから。
English: Ms. Starr poured her heart into making this!

Japanese: ‥‥その表現、なんかコワいよ。
English: So long as she didn't put any other organs in there...

Japanese: えーと。原材料は‥‥と。『にく』だって。
English: Let's see... "Ingredients: Meat"

Japanese: も。モチロン、牛肉ですよね。ハッキリ書いてありませんけど。
English: She must mean "beef." She probably just wrote it generically.

Japanese: そ。そりゃそうだよ！ハッキリ書いてないけどね。
English: Uh... yeah. Let's hope so.

Japanese: カガク者としては、やはり。食品添加物をチェックしないと。
English: Of course, as a scientist I have to check what additives she used.

Japanese: マニアックだなあ。
English: Go to town.

Japanese: 『まごころ』‥‥だそうです。
English: Huh? It says here... "Hours of sweat and labor."

Japanese: うーん‥‥自分で書くかあ。
English: So that's why the sauce is so salty...

Japanese: な、なんだ‥‥？（リストのウラに、ラクガキが！）
English: I wonder what this is? (It looks like someone drew some kind of sketch here!)

Japanese: なんですか！　ナニかありましたか？　成歩堂さん！
English: What is it? Did you find something?

Japanese: （なんとも判断しにくい絵だけど‥‥今は、ヒミツにしておこう）
English: (I can't make it out. I'd better keep quiet about it for now.)

Japanese: い、いや。なんでもないよ。
English: Huh? Oh. No, it's nothing.

Japanese: 成歩堂さん、目が泳いでます‥‥
English: Why are your eyes moving about like that, Mr. Wright?

Japanese: （この絵のコト‥‥覚えておいたほうがいいな！）
English: (I'd better not forget about this picture!)

Japanese: 証拠品リストのウラに、マジックで描かれた絵。
English: A picture is drawn on the back of the evidence list in magic marker.

Japanese: ‥‥なんか、とてつもなくイヤな予感がする‥‥
English: I've got a very bad feeling about this...

Japanese: うわ。こんなところに反応しましたよ！
English: Wow! We got a reaction!

Japanese: キレイに血がついてるね。サボテンのトゲのまわりに。
English: Hm. There's clearly blood around the thorns here.

Japanese: この部屋、ちらかってるから。何かにつまづいて‥‥
English: This room's pretty messy. Someone must have tripped over something...

Japanese: ぐさっと、ヒタイでも突き刺さったんじゃないかな。
English: and planted their head right in these thorns.

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥‥‥‥‥...あるイミ、サツジンよりイタい事件ですよね。
English: I think that might be more painful than being murdered.

Japanese: きゃあ！こんなに、たくさん‥‥
English: Whoa! This area must have been covered in blood!

Japanese: やっぱり、あの事件の‥‥？
English: Is this from that incident?

Japanese: 罪門　直斗検事さんが殺害されたときのもの、ですよね。
English: It must be. When Prosecutor Neil Marshall was murdered.

Japanese: ２年もたっているから、かなり反応がウスくなっています。
English: Two years have passed, so the reaction's kind of dull.

Japanese: （この場所で事件が起こった、というワケか‥‥？）
English: (So a murder really did take place here.)

Japanese: こ。こんなところにぽたぽたと、血がっ！
English: What? It looks like some blood has dripped down here!

Japanese: この、量‥‥鼻血、かもしれないね。
English: Judging by the amount, my guess is it's from a nosebleed.

Japanese: はなぢ‥‥
English: A nosebleed, hm?

Japanese: もしかしたら、御剣検事さん。
English: Maybe in his wrath,

Japanese: デキのワルい部下のほっぺをビンタした、とか。
English: Mr. Edgeworth slapped someone for their incompetence.

Japanese: （デキのワルい部下‥‥思い当たる人物が１人、いるな）
English: (Why does Detective Gumshoe's face come to mind...?)

Japanese: ‥‥‥！こ。こんなところに、反応が！...!
English: Why am I getting a reaction here?

Japanese: 犯人がフツウに逃げたとしたら‥‥こんなところ、さわるハズがない！
English: There's no reason for the murderer to touch this spot if he fled out the door.

Japanese: （何か、重要な手がかりになる‥‥そう考えるべきだ！）
English: (This just might be something significant!)

Japanese: あ、アンタ‥‥すげェクスリ、持ってるッス！
English: Hey... That's some pretty amazing stuff you got there, pal!

Japanese: あ、これですか？
English: What, this?

Japanese: 《ルミノール試薬》っていうんですよ！
English: It's called "Luminol Testing Fluid"!

Japanese: どこでッ！　いったい、どこで売ってるッスかッ！
English: Where'd you get your hands on that!?

Japanese: え。
English: Huh?

Japanese: 自分も買うッス！
English: I'd like to get some too!

Japanese: 局長に５０００円借りて、買ってくるッス！
English: I'll just borrow 50 bucks from the Chief!

Japanese: どこで売ってるの？　これ。
English: Where do you get this, Ema?

Japanese: ‥‥さあ。あたし、いつも通信販売で買ってますから。
English: I always buy it by mail order.

Japanese: （とりあえず、上面図に書きこんでおこう‥‥！）
English: (Well, I'd better jot this down on the floor plans.)

Japanese: あ‥‥、やっぱり！これって右手のアト、ですね！
English: I knew it! This is someone's right handprint!

Japanese: なんッスとほほおおおおおおおッ！
English: WHAAAAAAT!!?

Japanese: ど、どうしたんですか？
English: What's the matter, Detective?

Japanese: コレ‥‥この、保管庫。よりによって、自分のッス！
English: Th-this locker... It's mine!

Japanese: え。刑事さんの‥‥？
English: It's yours?

Japanese: あ、アンタ！　自分が‥‥自分がタイホされたときは！
English: Please! You have to help me... When they come to take me away...

Japanese: ちゃんと証言してほしいッス！“ニクめないヤツだった”‥‥と！
English: Promise you'll testify that I wouldn't harm a fly! You'll do that for me, won't you, pals!?

Japanese: （‥‥これは、重要な手がかりだ！上面図に書きこんでおこう‥‥）
English: (This is an important clue! I'll jot it down on the floor plans...)

Japanese: ケーサツは信用できないッス！ヨロシク証言、たのんだッスよ！
English: I'm counting on you guys! Believe me, you can't trust the police!

Japanese: 刑事さんが言わないでください！
English: What? But you're a detective!

Japanese: かなり出血があったみたいだ。（こんなの、初めて見るぞ‥‥）
English: There must have been a massive amount of blood here. (I've never seen so much before...)

Japanese: どう思いますか？　イトノコ刑事。ぼく、シロートですから‥‥
English: I'm not a professional. What's your opinion, Detective?

Japanese: うーム‥‥‥‥青白い血‥‥ッスか。
English: Hmm... Pale blue blood...

Japanese: 多田敷刑事は、もしかしたら‥‥宇宙人、だったッスかッ！
English: Maybe Detective Goodman... was actually an alien?

Japanese: とにかく、やっぱり‥‥事件はこの保管室で起こったんですね。
English: This proves that something really happened in front of this locker.

Japanese: いちおう、上面図に書きこんでおこうか。
English: I'll make a note of it on the floor plans.

Japanese: 自分のスイリはほったらかしッスかッ！
English: Hey! If you didn't want my opinion, you shouldn't have asked!

Japanese: この、血‥‥やっぱり、ともえさんが‥‥
English: This blood must be from when Lana...

Japanese: お‥‥お姉ちゃんはハンニンじゃありません！
English: No! My sister isn't the murderer!

Japanese: でも‥‥きみに電話をかけてきたんだよね？
English: But she did call you, didn't she?

Japanese: 事件が起こった時間に。
English: At the time of the crime?

Japanese: うううう‥‥...ジッサイ、右手に包帯を巻いていたわけだし。
English: And her right hand is bandaged.

Japanese: もう！　成歩堂さん、どっちのミカタなんですかっ！
English: Hey! Just whose side are you on!?

Japanese: そ、そういうモンダイじゃないよ‥‥
English: This has nothing to do with "taking sides."

Japanese: （ともえさんの手‥‥血でよごれていたことになる）
English: (So... This means that Lana's hand had blood on it.)

Japanese: （やっかいだな‥‥）
English: (This just keeps getting worse...)

Japanese: （‥‥他の指紋を試してみたいな）
English: (I think I'd like to try one of the other fingerprints.)

Japanese: じゃあ、ベツの“指”に変えてみましょうか！
English: Okay, let's move on to a different finger!

Japanese: うーん‥‥いちおう、検出してみたけど。
English: Hmm... I gave it my best shot.

Japanese: これじゃ、さすがに照合のデータになりませんね。
English: That kind of result won't be any good for matching prints, will it?

Japanese: でも。この指紋、これ以上キレイに検出するのは、ムリみたいだよ？
English: But it doesn't look like we'll get a clearer result from this print.

Japanese: じゃあ。気分をかえて、他のユビを調べてみましょうか！
English: Ok, let's try a different finger, then!

Japanese: ここまでのクリアデータを保存しますか
English: Save cleared stage data up to this point?

Japanese: 現在の状況を保存してゲームを中断しますか
English: Save game and suspend play? Do not turn off the power.

Japanese: ‥エピソードを選んでください
English: Select an episode.

Japanese: セーブに失敗しました
English: Save failed.

Japanese: すべてのデータを消去して初期状態に戻しますか
English: Erase all data and restore default settings?

Japanese: 新しいエピソードが追加されました。
English: A brand new Episode has been added.

Japanese: ゲームを中断します
English: Suspending game.

Japanese: ロードに失敗しました
English: Load failed.

Japanese: 手がかりになるものはない。
English: No clues here.

Japanese: ‥‥今は、この血のニオイが気になる！
English: I smell blood... and that can't be good!

Japanese: 千尋さん‥‥所長の無事をたしかめないと！
English: I have to check and see if Mia... the Chief's okay!

Japanese: 今の発言は、この証拠品のデータとムジュンしています！
English: Your Honor! That statement contradicts this evidence!

Japanese: ‥‥？...?どこがですか？特におかしいところはないが‥‥
English: It does? I don't see anything contradictory...

Japanese: え。そうですか？
English: Huh? Really?

Japanese: 異議は認められません。
English: Objection overruled.

Japanese: 弁護人は、もっと慎重に発言してください！
English: Try to think before you make accusations, Mr. Wright!

Japanese: （しまった！失敗したみたいだ‥‥）
English: (Whoops! That didn't go so well.)

Japanese: 今の証人の発言は、あきらかにヘンです！
English: The witness's statement is clearly faulty, Your Honor!

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥‥‥...残念だが、私にはヘンなところは見あたりません。
English: I'm sorry, but I can see nothing faulty.

Japanese: ‥‥やむを得ません。弁護人にはペナルティを与えます。
English: Unfortunately, I will have to penalize you, Mr. Wright.

Japanese: （うう。ぼくのカン違いだったのか‥‥？）
English: (Ugh. I must be on the wrong track?)

Japanese: この証拠品は、今の証言のムジュンをハッキリ示しています！
English: This evidence clearly reveals the contradiction in that statement, Your Honor!

Japanese: ‥‥その証拠品と今の発言が、どう関係するのですかな？
English: How exactly are that evidence and the statement just now related...?

Japanese: ‥‥しませんかね、やっぱり。
English: They aren't, are they...

Japanese: 全然しませんね。
English: Not at all.

Japanese: 弁護人。もっと、ちゃんと考えてから発言するように。
English: Mr. Wright, please think the facts over before making accusations.

Japanese: （‥‥裁判長の心証が悪くなったみたいだ‥‥）
English: (I don't think that won me any points with the judge...)

Japanese: 裁判長！　今の証人の発言をどう思われますか！
English: Your Honor! What do you think about the witness's statement?

Japanese: え‥‥。どうと言われても‥‥
English: Uh... I'm not sure I follow?

Japanese: あきらかに、その、ムジュン‥‥してると‥‥思うんですけど‥‥
English: It clearly, er, contradicts the... um... I thought...

Japanese: そんな顔で言われても説得力に欠けた感じがしますね。
English: You don't sound very convinced, Mr. Wright.

Japanese: 異議は却下します。
English: Objection overruled.

Japanese: （‥‥裁判長の心証が悪くなったみたいだ‥‥）
English: (I don't think that won me any points with the judge...)

Japanese: ダミー‥‥ともえさんから情報を聞こう。それで、調査は終了だ！
